parent_admin_h_backup.title: Data backup
parent_admin_h_backup.content:
# Data backup

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Data backup

While working with EEC, the following data are changed:

  * Data maintenance file (EOX file),
  * Resources,
  * User preferences (Workspace).



Particularly in the case of an update, the modified data must be applied.

parent_admin_h_backup_export_current_mod.title: Exporting the current information model
parent_admin_h_backup_export_current_mod.content:
# Exporting the current information model

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exporting the current information model

The entire information model is exported to an XML file, and subsequently imported into an empty database of the new installation (note: The XML format is database-neutral and allows for the switch between different database products and versions).

The entire information model is exported to an EOX file, and subsequently imported into an empty database of the new installation.

  1. Click File > Export....

The Export dialog opens.

  2. Navigate to Model > Model data (EOX).
  3. Click [Next >].

The EOX export dialog opens.

  4. Enter a file name and storage location for the export file.
  5. Use the Libraries option to select all libraries and the Projects option to select the complete model, or subsequently deselect entries that should not be exported.
  6. Click [Finish].

parent_admin_h_backup_export_store_impor.title: Exporting, backing up and importing data for a Client-Server installation
parent_admin_h_backup_export_store_impor.content:
# Exporting, backing up and importing data for a Client-Server installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exporting, backing up and importing data for a Client-Server installation

The steps to export, back up and import data are not markedly different form those of the Stand-Alone installation.

The only differences are:

  * During the client-server installation you export the settings for each individual user.
  * Back up the ec.ini file and copy it back to the installation folder after the new installation.



In particular, proceed as follows:

  1. Export and back up the data from the existing installation as you would for a stand-alone installation (see [Exporting the current information model](admin_h_backup_export_current_model.htm)).
  2. Back up the file ec.ini (located in the EEC installation folder).
  3. Remove the existing installation.
  4. Install the new EEC version.
  5. Copy the backed-up ec.ini back to the EEC installation folder.
  6. Apply the resources from the compressed resource folder (see [Restoring resources](admin_h_backup_recover_resources.htm)).
  7. Apply the settings (see [Restoring settings](admin_h_backup_recover_user_settings.htm)).



### Note

The settings have to be applied for each user separately.

parent_admin_h_backup_export_user_settin.title: Exporting user preferences
parent_admin_h_backup_export_user_settin.content:
# Exporting user preferences

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exporting user preferences

Upon installation of a new EEC version, the default user preferences are loaded. To apply the familiar user preferences to the new installation, you have to export them first. The user preferences are stored in a compressed format.

This is how you export the user preferences:

  1. Click File > Export....

The Export dialog opens.

  2. Navigate to General > Settings.
  3. Click Next >.
  4. Select the Export all option.
  5. Enter a file name and storage location.
  6. Click Finish.



Result:

The user preferences are stored in an .EPF file.

parent_admin_h_backup_recover_resources.title: Restoring resources
parent_admin_h_backup_recover_resources.content:
# Restoring resources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Restoring resources

Resources previously backed up via a compression program are to be made available again. The resources can be stored, in a discipline-specific manner, in various folders.

Proceed as follows:

  1. If necessary, delete the resource folders.
  2. Select the zipped file.
  3. Click 7-Zip > Extract files... in the context menu.
  4. Select whether to restore the directory structure, and by which method.

Full pathnames restores the files with absolute path information.

Relative pathnames restores files with folders based on the folder that contains the zip file.

No pathnames restores files in the folder that contains the zip file.

  5. Confirm your selection with [OK].



The progress is shown during the extraction. Subsequently, the restored resources will be available.

parent_admin_h_backup_recover_user_setti.title: Restoring settings
parent_admin_h_backup_recover_user_setti.content:
# Restoring settings

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Restoring settings

Proceed as follows to restore settings:

  1. Start EEC
  2. Click File > Import....

The Import dialog opens.

  3. Navigate to General > Settings.
  4. Click Next >.

The Import settings dialog opens:

  5. Select the setting file previously exported.
  6. Select the Import all option.
  7. Click Finish.



Subsequently the settings of the previous installation are available again.

parent_admin_h_backup_resourcefolder.title: Backing up resources folders
parent_admin_h_backup_resourcefolder.content:
# Backing up resources folders

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Backing up resources folders

You can back-up the resource folder with any compression software.

Since a separate resource folder can be specified for each discipline, several folders may have to be backed up.

The example describes compression using 7-Zip.

  1. Select the resource folder.
  2. In the popup menu click 7-Zip > Add to archive....
  3. Enter a file name for the zipped file, and confirm with [OK].

parent_admin_h_installation_automated.title: Installation with command line parameters
parent_admin_h_installation_automated.content:
# Installation with command line parameters

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Installation with command line parameters

Installation can be carried out in a shell with the specification of parameters.

The syntax is: setup_EEC_<version information> /Parameter<=argument> /Parameter<=argument>

The following command line parameters can be used:

Parameter | Description  
---|---  
/SILENT | Performs the installation without user interface, but with progress display for the EEC installation and the license-client installation. Default settings are used.  
/VERYSILENT | Like /SILENT, however, without progress display for EEC installation, but with progress display for license client installation.  
/LANG=de|en | Specifies the language for the installation. You can choose either de for German or en for English.  
/DIR=<installation directory> | Specifies the target directory including the drive for the installation.  
/SAVEINF=<path to .inf file> | Saves the installation settings in the specified inf file  
/LOADINF=<path to .inf file> | Applies the settings of the specified inf file for installation.  
This parameter in combination with /SILENT or /VERYSILENT allows automated installation on distributed computers.

parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.title: Client-Server-Installation
parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.content:
# Client-Server-Installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Client-Server-Installation

Familiarize yourself first with the special characteristics of the Client-Server installation. For this, you will find an overview and background information in [Installation variants](admin_r_installation_variants.htm).

The installation is carried out as follows:

  1. Choose the distribution of the EEC parts adapted to your requirements and IT structure to one or several servers.
  2. Create the folders for resources.
  3. Share the created folders for remote access.



### Note

Mapped drives or UNC paths cannot be specified during the server installation. For the server installation the Setup file is to be copied to the server and there installed locally.

  1. Copy the installation file to the server on which the application is to be located later on.
  2. Start the installation file.
  3. Select the language of the installation wizard.
  4. Click [OK].
  5. Acknowledge the following information page with [Next >].
  6. Accept the license agreement.
  7. Click [Next >].
  8. Select the installation variant Client-Server-Installation.
  9. Click [Next >].
  10. Enter the target folder for the installation.
  11. Click [Next >].
  12. Enter the folder in which the resources are to be saved.
  13. Click [Next >].
  14. Complete the installation overview with [Install].



### Note

Please note that localhost always points to the computer on which the process is currently executed.

If the UNC paths are not available, these can be edited subsequently in the initialization file (for example ec.ini).

Afterwards EEC has to be licensed, bevore it can start (see [Licensing](admin_h_installation_licensing.htm) and [Starting](admin_h_installation_client_server_start.htm)).

### Note

Never start a Client-Server installation without the correct setting of the folders Workspace and Configuration in the file ec.ini!

Every client has its own ec.ini!

If this has already happened, follow the instructions in the chapter [Repairing a Client-Server installation](admin_h_maintenance_client_server_installation_repair.htm).

Optimizations:

Property | Comment  
---|---  
RAM | See initialization parameter [-xmx](admin_r_vmargs_xmx.htm)

parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.title: Uninstalling
parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.content:
# Uninstalling

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Uninstalling

To uninstall EEC from the client, proceed as follows:

Click Start > All Programs > Eplan > Engineering Configuration > Build-ID > Uninstall Eplan Engineering Configuration...

Runtime data created at the start must be deleted manually.

The files on the server must be uninstalled by deleting them manually.

parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.title: Starting
parent_admin_h_installation_client_serve.content:
# Starting

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Starting

The EEC is started as a client-server installation on the workstation by opening the link of ec.exe.

parent_admin_h_installation_job_server.title: Job Server installation
parent_admin_h_installation_job_server.content:
# Job Server installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server installation

The installation of the Job Server is based on the Stand-Alone installation with data backend via EOX file (see [Stand-Alone installation](admin_h_installation_stand_alone.htm)).

The optional function (Optional Feature) has to be installed for the Job Server (see [EEC Job Server Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserver.htm)).

### Note

The Job Server can be set up as a Windows server and can be run without a user interface.

### Note

Up to four Workers together with a Job Server can be run on a workstation or server to create schematics and 3D graphics with Eplan Electric P8 and Eplan Pro Panel. To do this at least four different ports have to be specified for P8 (see [Open ports for Eplan P8 connection](admin_h_maintenance_p8_port_registration.htm)).

parent_admin_h_installation_jobserver_pk.title: Configuring a Job Server with a public key certificate
parent_admin_h_installation_jobserver_pk.content:
# Configuring a Job Server with a public key certificate

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Configuring a Job Server with a public key certificate

For companies with distributed structures, a secure connection to the Job Server by means of a signed public key certificate is usually required. This section describes the creation of the certificate and the configuration of the Job Server with a signed public certificate.

Preconditions:

  * The Job Server is installed on a server.



### [Creating a keystore and certificate with Java Keytool](javascript:void\(0\);)

  1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
  2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the keytool.exe file is located.
  3. Enter the command to create the keystore and certificate using the following syntax:
    
        keytool -genkeypair -dname "cn=<JOB-SERVER-URL>, ou=<DEPARTMENT>, o=<COMPANY-NAME>, l=<LOCATION-TOWN>,c=<STATE>" -alias <JOB-SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> -validity 712 -keyalg RSA -keysize 4096 -ext ExtendedKeyUsage=serverAuth -ext KeyUsage=digitalSignature,keyEncipherment -ext SubjectAlternativeName=DNS:<JOB-SERVER-URL> -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks

  4. Replace <JOB-SERVER-URL>, <DEPARTMENT>, <COMPANY-NAME>, <LOCATION-TOWN>, <STATE>, <JOB-SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> and <KEYSTORE-NAME> with your own specifications.
  5. Enter the password for the keystore.
  6. The file .keystore is located in the same folder as the file keytool.exe.




A private certificate is now stored in the new keystore.

### [Creating a Certificate Signing Request (CSR file) with Java Keytool](javascript:void\(0\);)

  1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
  2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the keytool.exe file is located.
  3. Enter the command to create the Certificate Signing Request using the following syntax:
    
        keytool -certreq -alias <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks -file <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>.csr -ext SubjectAlternativeName=DNS:<TOMCAT-SERVER-URL>

  4. Replace <SERVER ALIAS-NAME>, <KEYSTORE-NAME>, <SERVER ALIAS-NAME> and <TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> with your own specifications.
  5. Enter the password for the keystore.
  6. The <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>.csr file is located in the same folder as the keytool.exe file.




Send the CSR file to the appropriate authorized issuing authority.

A signed certificate (CRT file) is returned from the authorized issuing authority.

The signed certificate contains a certificate chain. A certificate chain can be recognized when looking at the contents by the fact that it contains at least two blocks that start with BEGIN CERTIFICATE and end with END CERTIFICATE.

### [Example of a signed certificate with a certificate chain](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
    
    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

    MIIGVzCCBT+gAwIBAgITbQA6dz5jedRlyde5UgAAADp3PjANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQsF

    ADBYMRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDaW50MRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDZmxnMRIw

    

    	...

    

    2Km685y4zOhD4a2Z4GgZbDiiUscDt/cPFK833PN+fOJG+nM3J/C2Ld3WuFgz2Fkg

    Re4wyba9MPOTZTmRBcgsLDqmLi0zc8Adq4JeNB8Z9x8b/U6YcD95rEwDcA==

    -----END CERTIFICATE-----

    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

    MIIXqAYJKoZIhvcNAQcCoIIXmTCCF5UCAQExADCCBm4GCSqGSIb3DQEHAaCCBl8E

    AQsFADBYMRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDaW50MRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDZmxn

    

    	...

    

    gUX8SnZNOw/VnA81WXcSg3lZBi2I3QC10bbKFP4S41z+2Cu8T8Q4U4ewu0VeGakt

    l9XPQ8pEDClKmzEA

    -----END CERTIFICATE-----

### [Importing a signed certificate into Keystore](javascript:void\(0\);)

  1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
  2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the files keytool.exe and <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks are located.
  3. Enter the command to import the certificate into the keystore with the following syntax:
    
        keytool -importcert -file <CERTIFICATE-NAME>.crt -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks -alias "<SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>"

  4. Replace <CERTIFICATE-NAME>, <KEYSTORE-NAME> and <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> with your own specifications.
  5. Enter the password for the keystore.
  6. The signed certificate is then successfully stored in the keystore.




### [Configuring the connection with the Job Server](javascript:void\(0\);)

  1. On the server open the initialization file (ec.ini) of the Job Server installation with an editor.
  2. Add the following runtime options:
    
        -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.disablehttps=false

    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.httpsPort=<SSL PORT>

    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.port=<PORT>

    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl=https://<JOB-SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>

    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.private.aliasurl=http://<JOB-SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>:<PORT>

  3. Replace <SSL PORT> and <JOB-SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> with your own specifications.
  4. The configuration of the initialization file is complete.




### [Entering keystore and password in the preferences](javascript:void\(0\);)

  1. Start the Job Server.
  2. Open the menu Window > Settings.
  3. Navigate to the entry Job Server.
  4. Enter the file name and the password for the keystore in the HTTPS support section.



### Note

To use the secure connection you have to restart the Job Server.

parent_admin_h_installation_jobserver_wo.title: Job Server with Job Server Worker installation
parent_admin_h_installation_jobserver_wo.content:
# Job Server with Job Server Worker installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server with Job Server Worker installation

The installation of the Job Server with Worker is based on the Stand-Alone installation (see [Stand-Alone installation](admin_h_installation_stand_alone.htm)).

The optional functions (Optional Features) for the Job Server and Job Server Worker have to be installed (see [EEC Job Server Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserver.htm) and [EEC Job Server Worker Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserverworker.htm)).

To enable the Job Server to communicate with the Workers, the initialization file (for example ec.ini) should contain an argument with the Port number, where the Job Server listens, for example 8686 (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.port](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_port.htm)):
    
    
    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.port=8686

### Note

The specifications for -Dde.eplan.eec.jobsrever.port and -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl should match so that the communication between the administration website and the Job Server is ensured. The port specifications can be different when a reverse proxy is interconnected.

Example scenario for different port specifications:

The Job Server has the settings de.eplan.eec.jobserver.port=80 and ‑Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl=https://www.company.de (port number 443 is the default).

A reverse proxy transfers the HTTPS request to the Job Server with http://www.company.de (port number 80 is the default).

In order to make the Job Server accessible for Workers, the initialization file (for example ec.ini) has to contain a private.aliasurl entry, for example http://jobserverstation:8686 (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.private.aliasurl](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_privatealiasurl.htm)). Workers that also have this address entered in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) can register themselves automatically during the start:
    
    
    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.private.aliasurl=http://jobserverstation:8686

In order to make the Job Server accessible for clients (for example browsers), the initialization file (for example ec.ini) can contain the aliasUrl argument, for example http://jobserverstation:8686 (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_aliasurl.htm)).
    
    
    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl=http://jobserverstation:8686

To enable the Job Server to receive queries from Workers the initialization file (for example ec.ini) should contain an argument with the Port number, where the Worker listens, for example 8989 (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.port](admin_r_vmargs_worker_port.htm)):
    
    
    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.port=8989

The Worker is started automatically when EEC is started, if the value for autostart is set to the value true in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart](admin_r_vmargs_worker_autostart.htm)):
    
    
    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart=true

You can also specify in the initialization file the language in which the user interface of the Worker is to be started, for example German (see [-osgi.nl](admin_r_eclipse_args_nl.htm)):
    
    
    -Dosgi.nl=de_DE

Further settings can be made in the settings for the Worker:

  * A directory (UNC path is possible), where the job definitions for the Worker are stored.
  * The maximum number of parallel jobs.
  * E-mail preferences, to send e-mails to particular addresses in case of successful or failed jobs.

parent_admin_h_installation_licensing.title: Licensing
parent_admin_h_installation_licensing.content:
# Licensing

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Licensing

As a matter of principle, the licensing for a Stand-Alone installation is identical to that for a Client-Server installation. In case of a client-server installation, the licensing must be done separately for each workstation. The licensing depends on three different cases. In each case, the process starts after the installation.

On workstations with an installed and licensed Eplan product, the licensing program is not installed again, but started by launching the EEC while keeping the [Shift] key pressed.

### Note

The key to be pressed at the start can be configured. Possibly, a different key may have to be used to start the licensing program.

The installation of the License Manager is described in the separate documentation Eplan License Manager Installation Instructions. That documentation is available as a PDF file upon request.

parent_admin_h_installation_optionalfeat.title: EEC Job Server Add-On
parent_admin_h_installation_optionalfeat.content:
# EEC Job Server Add-On

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## EEC Job Server Add-On

The optional function EEC Job Server Add-On provides the functions to extend a stand-alone installation with Job Server capabilities.

To install the optional function:

  1. Start EEC
  2. Open Help > Install new software....
  3. In the Use field select the \--All available sites-- or de.eplan.eec.install.optfeatures entry from the drop-down list.
  4. Expand the EEC Optional Features path.
  5. Select the EEC Job Server Add-on entry.
  6. Click [Next >].



The function to be installed is only listed so that the selection can be checked.

  1. Click [Next >].
  2. Accept the license terms.
  3. Click [Finish].

parent_admin_h_installation_optionalfeat.title: Optional functions
parent_admin_h_installation_optionalfeat.content:
# Optional functions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Optional functions

The installation can be extended with optional functions.

Currently available optional functions:

Name | Function  
---|---  
[EEC Diagnostics Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_diagnostics.htm) | Provides the diagnostics functions for EEC  
[EEC Job Server Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserver.htm) | Provides the Job Server functions for EEC  
[EEC Job Server Worker Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserverworker.htm) | Provides the Job Server Worker functions for EEC  
[EEC P8 Integration Service Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_p8_integration.htm) | Provides a function which makes it possible to open the corresponding discipline component in EEC from a P8_Macro.  
[EEC SAP RFC Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_sap.htm) | Provides functions for SAP RFC support using JCo for EEC.

parent_admin_h_installation_sap_javaconn.title: Installation of the SAP Java connector
parent_admin_h_installation_sap_javaconn.content:
# Installation of the SAP Java connector

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Installation of the SAP Java connector

Requirements:

  * EEC 2.4 or higher
  * Installation of the optional function [EEC SAP RFC Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_sap.htm)



Because the Java Connector provided free by SAP may not be passed on to third parties due to licensing reasons, the customers must obtain this themselves.

The package is provided for download in the customer area of the SAP Service Marketplace.

Requirement:

An SAP account is required to access the SAP customer area.

Here are the steps to download the SAP Java Connector.

  1. Start Microsoft Internet ExplorerÂ©.
  2. Navigate to SAP Support Portal (service.sap.com/connectors).
  3. Click SAP Java Connector > Tools & Services > Download SAP JCo Release 3.1.
  4. Download the SAP JCo 3.1.x files in accordance with your operating system.



The downloaded files include the file sapjco3.dll. This file has to be unpacked and copied to the following directory:

<EEC installation folder>\plugins\de.eplan.sap_<Build-Nummer>\os\win32\x86_64

parent_admin_h_installation_stand_alone.title: Stand-Alone installation
parent_admin_h_installation_stand_alone.content:
# Stand-Alone installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Stand-Alone installation

The installation is carried out as follows:

  1. Start the installation file.
  2. Select the language of the installation wizard.
  3. Click [OK].
  4. Acknowledge the following information page with [Next >].
  5. Accept the license agreement.
  6. Click [Next >].
  7. Select the installation variant Stand-alone installation.
  8. Click [Next >].
  9. Enter the target folder for the installation.
  10. Click [Next >].
  11. Indicate whether an entry is to be created in the start menu and/or desktop symbol.
  12. Click [Next >].
  13. Complete the installation overview with [Install].



Installation with database data management

### Note

This option is discontinued and cannot be used for new projects.

The documentation for the database data management is only available in the versions up to Version 2024.

  1. Open a Windows console.
  2. Navigate to the EEC installation file.
  3. Start the installation routine with the following command:



setup.exe /show-deprecated-features

  1. Select the language of the installation wizard.
  2. Click [OK].
  3. Acknowledge the following information page with [Next >].
  4. Accept the license agreement.
  5. Click [Next >].
  6. Select the installation variant Stand-alone installation.
  7. Click [Next >].
  8. Enter the target folder for the installation.
  9. Click [Next >].
  10. Indicate whether an entry is to be created in the start menu and/or desktop symbol.
  11. Click [Next >].
  12. Complete the installation overview with [Install].
  13. After the successful installation the administration tool for the database is available.



### Note

A corresponding license is required for operation with database data management.

parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec.title: WebÂ EEC installation
parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec.content:
# WebÂ EEC installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Web EEC installation

This section describes the installation and configuration of the web-based EEC variant. For further information on the products used, for example, Tomcat, please refer to the documentation of each product.

Overview:

The Web EEC is a web application based on Eclipse Rich Ajax Platform (RAP), which makes it possible to put EEC models on the web.

The following figure gives an overview of the system configuration:

Browser:

The Web EEC is a web application that the user calls up in a web browser. The graphical interface of the application is displayed and executed in the browser window.

Web EEC Server:

The Web EEC requires as an execution environment a web server like Tomcat that implements the servlet specification. The essential Web EEC Serverâs function is to supply to the client and / or browser Form-UI-based user interfaces, process user requests, send function calls to the EEC Server, and process their results accordingly.

EEC Server:

The operation of a Web EEC application requires, apart from the Web EEC Server, an EEC Server. Its essential task is to manage the data and models, as well as provide fundamental EEC functions, such as the execution of commands.

Perform the installation in the following sequence:

  1. [Installing EEC servers](admin_h_installation_web_eec_eec_server.htm)
  2. [Installation and configuration of the Web EEC Server](admin_h_installation_web_eec_server.htm)
  3. [Installing and configuring Web EEC](admin_h_installation_web_eec_configuration.htm)

parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_call.title: Calling the Web EEC
parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_call.content:
# Calling the Web EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Calling the Web EEC

The Web EEC is called in a browser with the following syntax:
    
        http://<Hostname>:<Portnummer>/webec/main

## [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

If, for the Web EEC, as described before, it was installed in the folder webapps\webec of a Tomcat server that accepts http requests at http://localhost:8181:

<http://localhost:8181/webec/main>

parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_conf.title: Installing and configuring Web EEC
parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_conf.content:
# Installing and configuring Web EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Installing and configuring Web EEC

### [Requirement](javascript:void\(0\);)

The Tomcat web server service is stopped.

Variant 1 to stop the Tomcat web server service:

  1. If required, open the hidden symbols in the task bar.
  2. Right-click the symbol of the Tomcat Monitor.
  3. Select Stop service from the popup menu.
  4. The symbol  in the task bar indicates that the Tomcat web server service is stopped.



Variant 2 to stop the Tomcat web server service:

  1. Open the Windows Explorer task manager.
  2. Open the Services tabs.
  3. Navigate to the Tomcat9 service.
  4. Right-click the name and select Stop from the popup menu.
  5. The Status column shows the Tomcat web server service as Finished.



The Web EEC is provided as a Web archive with the file extension .war in the <EEC installation folder>\install\webec folder. This file format represents a JAR and / or ZIP archive, and can be opened and edited using a compression program, for example, 7-ZIP.

  1. Unpack the file webec.war into a new folder called webec.
  2. Open the file \webec\WEB-INF\web.xml in an editor.
  3. Change the following configuration parameters:
Configuration parameter | Description  
---|---  
com.mind8.formui.aliasUrl | Modifies the URL of the download link.  
com.mind8.formui.eox | Name of the model EOX file without file extension. This information must match the information in the file ec.ini of the EEC Server.  
com.mind8.formui.perspective.object | Path to the project component that contains the Form-UI to be displayed initially.  
com.mind8.formui.perspective.form-id | The ID of the Form-UI to be displayed initially.  
com.mind8.formui.host | Host of the EEC Server.  
com.mind8.formui.port | Port of the EEC Server. This information must match the information in the file ec.ini of the EEC Server.  
com.mind8.formui.timeout | Waiting time until a session-timeout is triggered after inactivity (in seconds).  
  
### Note

Parameter values to be entered by the user are identified by a comment:

<!-- TODO: Comment text -->.

The complete comment, including <!-- and \--> has to be replaced by a parameter value.

Example for a parameter value before the change by the user:

<param-value><!-- TODO: ExampleEOX (without file type .eox) --></param-value>

Example for a parameter value after the changes by the user:

<param-value>TutorialModel</param-value>

### Example of a configuration
    
        <web-app id="WebApp">

    ...

    	<context-param>

    		<param-name>com.mind8.formui.eox</param-name>

    		<param-value>**Tutorial_model** </param-value>	

    	</context-param>

    	<context-param>

    	<param-name>com.mind8.formui.perspective.object</param-name>

    	<param-value>**Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder** </param-value>

    	</context-param>

    	<context-param>

    		<param-name>com.mind8.formui.perspective.form-id</param-name>

    		<param-value>**feeder** </param-value>

    	</context-param>

    	<context-param>

    		<param-name>com.mind8.formui.host</param-name>

    		<param-value>**localhost** </param-value>

    	</context-param>

    	<context-param>

    		<param-name>com.mind8.formui.port</param-name>

    		<param-value>**7171** </param-value>

    	</context-param>

    ...

    </web-app>

  4. Copy the folder \webec into the folder <Tomcat installation folder>\Tomcat <Tomcat version>\webapps.
  5. Delete the contents of the folder <Tomcat installation folder\Tomcat <Tomcat version>\work.



Start the Tomcat web server service.

Variant 1 to start the Tomcat web server service:

  1. If required, open the hidden symbols in the task bar.
  2. Right-click the symbol of the Tomcat Monitor.
  3. Select Start service from the popup menu.
  4. The symbol in the task bar indicates that the Tomcat web server service is started.



Variant 2 to start the Tomcat web server service:

  1. Open the Windows Explorer task manager.
  2. Open the Services tabs.
  3. Navigate to the Tomcat9 service.
  4. Right-click the name and select Start from the popup menu.
  5. The symbol  in the task bar indicates that the Tomcat web server service is started.

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Installing EEC servers

#### Requirements

     * A folder that contains the basic model exists.
     * A folder in which the generated models are stored exists.

Installation of the EEC Server can be carried out in two variants:

    1. Select the EEC server installations option in the installation wizard.
    2. Select the Stand-alone installation in the installation wizard and then edit the installation file.

### [Variant 1](javascript:void\(0\);)

Installation:

    1. Start the installation file.
    2. Select the language of the installation wizard.
    3. Click [OK].
    4. Acknowledge the following information page with [Next >].
    5. Accept the license agreement.
    6. Click [Next >].
    7. Select the installation variant EEC server installation.
    8. Click [Next >].
    9. Enter the same value for the server port that you have already specified in the web.xml file (see [Installing and configuring Web EEC](admin_h_installation_web_eec_configuration.htm)).
    10. Click [Next >].
    11. Specify a folder for the storage location of the basic models in which the EOX file is located as a back-end.
    12. Click [Next >].
    13. Specify a folder for the storage location of the session models in which the EOX files of each session are stored.
    14. Click [Next >].
    15. Enter the target folder for the installation.
    16. Click [Next >].
    17. Indicate whether an entry is to be created in the start menu and/or desktop symbol.
    18. Click [Next >].
    19. Complete the installation overview with [Install].

### [Variant 2](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Start a stand-alone installation.
    2. Deactivate the option Start EEC.
    3. If required, deactivate the option Open 'Feature Releases' list.
    4. Complete the installation with [Finish].
    5. Open the initialization file <EEC installation folder>\ec.ini in an editor (for example Notepad++).
    6. Add the following lines to the end of the file:
    
        -Dcom.mind8.ecserver.port=<Port des EEC-Servers z.B. 7171>

    -Dcom.mind8.ecserver.baseEOXPath=<absoluter Pfad zum Ordner der Basismodelle, z.B. .\eox>

    -Dcom.mind8.remote.server.repository.file=<absoluter Pfad zum Ordner der Sessionmodelle, z.B. .\sessionmodels>

    1. Save the initialization file.

### Note

This port number must match that in the file web.xml.  
The folder for basic models contains models that are available for the Web EEC. This specification is mandatory! The path must be specified using forward slashes /, and not backslashes \\. The specific model is stated in the file web.xml without the file extension .eox. This is also true of the system model.  
The folder of the session models contains the model files that are created for a new session. This specification is mandatory!   
The resources required for the model must be provided, and configured on the side of the EEC Server.

parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_serv.title: Installation and configuration of the Web EEC Server
parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_serv.content:
# Installation and configuration of the Web EEC Server

Path to the project This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

## Installation and configuration of the Web EEC Server

The Web EEC Server used is Apache Tomcat. This can be downloaded from the following web site in different versions: https://tomcat.apache.org/ Please note the requirements for compatibility with the Java Development Environment (JRE) or the Java Development Kit (JDK) on the page https://tomcat.apache.org/whichversion.html. [Software and hardware requirements > Eplan Engineering Configuration > Apache Tomcat Server for Web EEC Server](../../../../../../../en-US/Infoportal/Content/swreqs/content/htm/swreqs_k_eecpro.htm#TOMCAT)

### Note

The following entries in the initialization file (ec.ini) prevent the successful start of the Apache Tomcat server version 9: -Dorg.eclipse.rap.workbenchAutostart=false -Dorg.osgi.framework.os.name=win Apart from the regular program version, which is started conventionally, there is also a Windows Service Installer, which installs Tomcat as a Windows system service, and also provides an installation wizard. Follow the installation wizard. Note a change to the installation folder from Tomcat Version 9 to Tomcat Version 10: Tomcat Version 9 is installed in the <Tomcat installation folder>\Tomcat <Version>\webapps folder. Tomcat Version 10 and more recent versions are installed in the <Tomcat installation folder>\Tomcat <Version>\webapps-javaee folder. After the successful installation, the configuration of the Tomcat web server starts.
    1. To avoid port conflicts with the EEC Server, a value different from the default 8080 must be entered for the HTTP1.1 Connector Port if the Tomcat web server and the EEC Server are to be installed on the same host.
    2. Confirm your input with [Next >].
    1. Enter the installed version of the Java Virtual Machine.
    2. Confirm your input with [Next >].
    3. Enter the folder for the installation of the Tomcat server.
    4. Complete the installation with [Install].
You must start the Tomcat Monitor to configure the Tomcat web server:
    1. Right-click the symbol of the Tomcat Monitor on the taskbar.
    2. Select Configure from the popup menu.
The Tomcat Monitor opens.
    3. Open the Java tab.
    4. Select the Java Virtual Machine.

### Note

After each update of the Java installation, the Tomcat Monitor has to be opened again, to customize the path to the vm.dll to the update.
    1. Confirm your input with [OK].

### [Updating Apache Tomcat](javascript:void\(0\);)

Apache Tomcat can be installed in parallel for different versions. Each version installs its own service, which differs in the naming. An installed service cannot be renamed. To set up a new service with the same name, the existing one has to be terminated and uninstalled beforehand. Exit the existing Apache Tomcat service:
    1. Open the Task Manager.
    2. Open the Services tab.
    3. Navigate to the entry Tomcat9.
    4. From the popup menu for Tomcat9 select the menu item Stop.
Uninstall the existing Apache Tomcat service:
    1. Open a command prompt (console) with administrator rights.
    2. Enter the following command:
sc delete <Service name>
    1. Replace <Service name> with the name of the service, for example Tomcat9.
    2. In the case of success, the command prompt reports success.
Restart Windows for this change to take effect. Install a different version of Apache Tomcat as described above.

parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_serv.title: admin_h_installation_web_eec_server_pkc_configuration
parent_admin_h_installation_web_eec_serv.content:
# admin_h_installation_web_eec_server_pkc_configuration

## Configuring a Web EEC Server with a public key certificate

For companies with distributed structures, a secure connection to the Web EEC Server by means of a signed public key certificate is usually required. This section describes the creation of the certificate and the configuration of the Web EEC Server with a signed public certificate. Requirements:
     * The Tomcat web server service is installed on a server.
     * Web EEC and Web EEC server are installed on the same server.

### Creating a keystore and certificate with Java Keytool

    1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
    2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the keytool.exe file is located.
    3. Enter the command to create the keystore and certificate using the following syntax:
        
                keytool -genkeypair -dname "cn=<TOMCAT-SERVER-URL>, ou=<DEPARTMENT>, o=<COMPANY-NAME>, l=<LOCATION-TOWN>,c=<STATE>" -alias <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> -validity 712 -keyalg RSA -keysize 4096 -ext ExtendedKeyUsage=serverAuth -ext KeyUsage=digitalSignature,keyEncipherment -ext SubjectAlternativeName=DNS:<TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks

    4. Replace <TOMCAT-SERVER-URL>, <DEPARTMENT>, <COMPANY-NAME>, <LOCATION-TOWN>, <STATE>, <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>, <TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> and <KEYSTORE-NAME> with your own specifications.
    5. Enter the password for the keystore.
    6. The file .keystore is located in the same folder as the file keytool.exe.
Additional information about the command syntax of keytool is available at https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/specs/man/keytool.html.

### Example

<TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> = webserver.de.flg.int, <DEPARTMENT> = development, <COMPANY-NAME> = EPLAN GmbH & Co. KG, <LOCATION-TOWN> = Stuttgart, <STATE> = DE, <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> = eec-webserver, <TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> = webserver.de.flg.int, <KEYSTORE-NAME> = webserver_keystore The following command to create the keystore and the certificate results from these specifications:
    
        keytool -genkeypair -dname "cn=webserver.de.flg.int, ou=development, o=EPLAN GmbH & Co. KG, l=Stuttgart,c=DE" -alias eec-webserver -validity 712 -keyalg RSA -keysize 4096 -ext ExtendedKeyUsage=serverAuth -ext KeyUsage=digitalSignature,keyEncipherment -ext SubjectAlternativeName=DNS:webserver.de.flg.int -keystore webserver_keystore.jks

A private certificate is now stored in the new keystore.

### Creating a Certificate Signing Request (CSR file) with Java Keytool

    1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
    2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the keytool.exe file is located.
    3. Enter the command to create the Certificate Signing Request using the following syntax:
        
                keytool -certreq -alias <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks -file <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>.csr -ext SubjectAlternativeName=DNS:<TOMCAT-SERVER-URL>

    4. Replace <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>, <KEYSTORE-NAME> and <TOMCAT-SERVER-URL> with your own specifications.
    5. Enter the password for the keystore.
    6. The <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>.csr file is located in the same folder as the keytool.exe file.
Send the CSR file to the appropriate authorized issuing authority. A signed certificate (CRT file) is returned from the authorized issuing authority. The signed certificate contains a certificate chain. A certificate chain can be recognized when looking at the contents by the fact that it contains at least two blocks that start with BEGIN CERTIFICATE and end with END CERTIFICATE.

### Example of a signed certificate with a certificate chain
    
        -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

    MIIGVzCCBT+gAwIBAgITbQA6dz5jedRlyde5UgAAADp3PjANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQsF

    ADBYMRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDaW50MRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDZmxnMRIw

    

    	...

    

    2Km685y4zOhD4a2Z4GgZbDiiUscDt/cPFK833PN+fOJG+nM3J/C2Ld3WuFgz2Fkg

    Re4wyba9MPOTZTmRBcgsLDqmLi0zc8Adq4JeNB8Z9x8b/U6YcD95rEwDcA==

    -----END CERTIFICATE-----

    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

    MIIXqAYJKoZIhvcNAQcCoIIXmTCCF5UCAQExADCCBm4GCSqGSIb3DQEHAaCCBl8E

    AQsFADBYMRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDaW50MRMwEQYKCZImiZPyLGQBGRYDZmxn

    

    	...

    

    gUX8SnZNOw/VnA81WXcSg3lZBi2I3QC10bbKFP4S41z+2Cu8T8Q4U4ewu0VeGakt

    l9XPQ8pEDClKmzEA

    -----END CERTIFICATE-----

### Importing a signed certificate into Keystore

    1. Open a console with administrator rights on the server.
    2. Navigate to the folder of the Java installation where the files keytool.exe and <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks are located.
    3. Enter the command to import the certificate into the keystore with the following syntax:
        
                keytool -importcert -file <CERTIFICATE-NAME>.crt -keystore <KEYSTORE-NAME>.jks -alias "<SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>"

    4. Replace <CERTIFICATE-NAME>, <KEYSTORE-NAME> and <SERVER-ALIAS-NAME> with your own specifications.
    5. Enter the password for the keystore.
    6. The signed certificate is then successfully stored in the keystore.

### Configuring the connection with the TomCat server

    1. Open the <Tomcat installation folder>/conf/server.xml file with an editor.
    2. Navigate to the entry for an SSL/TLS HTTP/1.1 Connector.
    3. Replace the existing entry with the following lines:
        
                <!-- HTTPS configuration -->

        	<Connector port="443" protocol="HTTP/1.1" SSLEnabled="true" maxThreads="150" scheme="https" secure="true" keystoreFile="<PATH-TO-KEYSTORE>/<KEYSTORE-FILENAME>" keystorePass="<PASSWORD>" clientAuth="false" sslProtocol="TLS" >

        	</Connector>

    4. Replace <PATH-TO-KEYSTORE>/<KEYSTORE-NAME> and <PASSWORD> with your own specifications.
    5. The configuration is complete. Activate the configuration by restarting the Tomcat service.

### Activating the configuration

    1. Open the Task Manager.
    2. Open the Services tab.
    3. Navigate to the entry Tomcat9.
    4. From the popup menu for Tomcat9 select the menu item Restart.
    5. The URL for calling up the Web EEC in the browser has changed through the configuration to [https://<SERVER-ALIAS-NAME>/web/main](https://localhost:443/webec/main)

### Note

To use the secure connection you have to restart the browser.

parent_admin_h_installation_worker.title: Job Server Worker installation
parent_admin_h_installation_worker.content:
# Job Server Worker installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

# Job Server Worker installation

The installation of the Worker is based on the Stand-Alone installation with EOX data storage (see [Stand-Alone installation](admin_h_installation_stand_alone.htm)). The optional function (Optional Feature) for the Job Server Worker has to be installed (see [EEC Job Server Worker Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserverworker.htm)). Further settings can be made in the settings for the Worker:
     * If the Worker should start automatically after EEC has been started.
     * A directory (UNC path is possible), where the job definitions for the Worker are stored.
     * The maximum number of parallel jobs.
     * E-mail preferences, to send e-mails to particular addresses in case of successful or failed jobs.

## [Setting up a Job Server Worker as a service](javascript:void\(0\);)

The following entries in the initialization file (ec.ini) are additionally required to set up a Job Server Worker as a service:
    
        -Dde.eplan.eec.headless=true

    -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart=true

Constraints: If the Job Server Worker is set up as a service, you cannot use connected drives for job definitions and paths in the model. The Word discipline can generate the structure and the document, but it cannot update the fields nor generate and open a PDF file.

parent_admin_h_maintenance_client_server.title: Repairing a Client-Server installation
parent_admin_h_maintenance_client_server.content:
# Repairing a Client-Server installation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

## Repairing a Client-Server installation

If EEC was started on a server without first stating the folders workspace and configuration correctly in the ec.ini file, the following steps must be taken to render the EEC operable again:
    1. Shut down EEC.
    2. In the ec.ini file, enter the correct paths for the folders workspace and configuration.
    3. Replace the content of the folder configuration with the content of the archive configuration.bak.zip from the folder install/configuration.

### Note

By starting without the correct settings in ec.ini, a configuration folder was created that is then no longer used. This can be deleted.

parent_admin_h_maintenance_p8_port_regis.title: Open ports for Eplan P8 connection
parent_admin_h_maintenance_p8_port_regis.content:
# Open ports for Eplan P8 connection

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

## Open ports for Eplan P8 connection

The default port for the Eplan P8 connection is port 80. If this port is already used by another service, the port for the connection to Eplan P8 must be changed.

### Note

If Eplan has never been installed on a host before, Eplan URL registration starts during the installation as of Version 2024. You can change the default port with the Eplan URL registration program and add further ones. The program is located in the folder <Eplan Installation folder>\Bin\EplanUrlActivatoru.exe. The program lists all existing port assignments of the Eplan services. The options known from the platform are available for editing the list. To change the ports via script, the Url keys of the following registry entries must be changed:
    
        HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\EPLAN\Services\<Text>

    HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\**Wow6432Node** \EPLAN\Services\<Text>

In addition to the registry entries, the URLs must be registered with netsh.
    
        netsh http add urlacl url=http://+:<Port>/EPLAN_EEC<Text> user=<Domain>\<User>

    netsh http add urlacl url=http://+:<Port>/EPLAN_EEC<Text> user=<Domain>\<User>

parent_admin_h_maintenance_version_updat.title: Change to Current EEC Version
parent_admin_h_maintenance_version_updat.content:
# Change to Current EEC Version

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

## Change to Current EEC Version

To transfer the user data and settings from an older EEC version to the current one, different steps are required depending on the installation variant.

## General

These steps have to be performed for all installations:
    1. Export settings from the old EEC and import them into the new EEC.
    2. Modify settings for suitable disciplines.
    3. Modify the initialization data (ec.ini).
    4. Copy self-produced plugins into the folder \dropins.
    5. Copy resources to the new EEC (possibly an existing export has to be modified accordingly).

## ECAD

    1. Copy license default setting file (when simultaneously switching to current Eplan Electric P8 version).
    2. Create macros, basic project templates and macro project templates with the compatible P8 version.
    3. Synchronize discipline components.

## Job Server

    1. Install EEC for Job Server and/or Job Server Worker.
    2. Install plugins for Job Server and/or Job Server Worker.
    3. Customize the initialization file (ec.ini) for Job Server and/or Job Server Worker.

## Windows Service

    1. Creating and configuring the license default setting file.
    2. Terminate and uninstall the old service (with PowerShell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass.\uninstallService.ps1).
    3. Install and start the new service (with PowerShell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass.\installService.ps1).

## Web EEC

    1. Install the new version of Tomcat.
    2. Unpack the webec.war file in the Tomcat folder.
    3. Customize the web.xml file or import from the old Tomcat.
    4. Start Tomcat.
    5. Import settings from the old initialization file (ec.ini).
    6. As of Version 2.9 the Remoteserver plugin is installed automatically.

parent_admin_k_backup_strategies.title: Data backup strategies
parent_admin_k_backup_strategies.content:
# Data backup strategies

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here: 

## Data backup strategies

Backing up resource folder via a compression program: The resource folder can be backed up using a compression program, for example, 7-ZIP (see [Backing up resources folders](admin_h_backup_resourcefolder.htm)). By assigning the proper names to the zipped files, the backed-up files can be allocated easily. Benefit: Compression can be automated, and does not require any further intervention on the userâs part. Drawback: All resources are always compressed, which creates redundant data collections. Differential backup of the resource folder: Initially, all resources are backed up onto an external storage medium. Then, all changes since that backup are stored. Benefit: Can be automated, and does not require any further intervention on the userâs part. To restore the files, only the full backup and the most recent differential backup will be required. Drawback: Higher memory usage than with incremental backups Incremental backup of the resource folder: Initially, all resources are backed up onto an external storage medium. Then, only the changes since the last backup are stored. Each time, a new folder is created so as to have a transparent chronology. Benefit: Can be automated, and does not require any further intervention on the userâs part. Low memory usage; if necessary, the data can be compressed on a backup medium. No redundant data. Drawback: More effort in restoring files than with differential backups. Exporting user preferences: User preferences are exported to an EPF file via the export function of the user interface. The process can be automated by means of a script. If named accordingly, the EPF file can be identified easily. Exporting the information model: The information model is exported to an EOX file via the export function of the user interface. The process can be automated by means of a script. If named accordingly, the EOX file can be identified easily.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_application.title: -application
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_application.content:
# -application

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## -application

Eclipse argument | Values | Usage  
---|---|---  
-application | applicationId | Optional  
**Annotation**  
The application executed by the call of ec.exe is defined by a specific composition of plug-ins. Based on the plug-ins installed, alternative applications consisting of a different composition of plug-ins and configuration data can be launched. The default application is specified in the file config.ini in the configuration folder. It contains the entry eclipse.product=de.eplan.engineeringconfiguration.workbench A different application can be selected via the argument -application. EEC defines the following applications | applicationId | Description  
---|---  
de.eplan.engineeringconfiguration. DatabaseAdminApplication | Used for managing databases:  
Creating databases  
Importing models  
Updating models  
Deleting databases  
de.eplan.engineeringconfiguration. XMLExportApplication | Allows for the export of the model to an XML file in batch mode operation.  
  
Generally, in combination with this argument, an alternative configuration has to be provided also for ec.ini, see argument âpluginCustomization.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_clean.title: -clean
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_clean.content:
# -clean

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## -clean

Eclipse argument | Values | Usage  
---|---|---  
-clean | - | Optional  
**Annotation**  
During the booting of Eclipse, all plug-ins are analyzed to obtain an overview of the available plug-ins. Eclipse can cache this list in order to accelerate the boot-up time (default behavior). The parameter -clean forces an update of the cache at each boot-up. If users do not change the plug-in structure with Eclipse resources, but, for example, by copying further plug-ins directly to the plug-in folder, this change will never be recognized while the cache is enabled.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_configuratio.title: -configuration
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_configuratio.content:
# -configuration

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## -configuration

Eclipse argument |  Values |  Usage  
---|---|---  
-configuration |  - |  Optional  
**Annotation**  
Specifies the path to the configuration folder where the platform configuration file ec.ini is located. By default, the configuration folder is called configuration, and is located in the execution folder of ec.exe. The explicit output of this folder is necessary if the user cannot write to the default folder in the Eclipse installation. A shared installation is one where ec.exe can be launched by different users. Typically work in an application server configuration or also in a local installation, alternating with the installation in various configurations.  
  
### Note

Typically, the option -configuration is used in combination with -data. If the configuration directory and the workspace are modified at the same time, an error message will result: Class not found. An error-free migration requires two steps:

    1. Only convert the workspace to the new directory.
    2. Then, convert the configuration.

### Note

In the case of Client-Server installations, only ini files may be in the configuration folder of the server installation. If this is not the case, EEC was launched at least once without the option configuration. In the event, EEC (due to a bug in Eclipse 3.2) will in future ignore the configuration setting stated at the start, and use the setting in the serverâs configuration folder. There, all subfolders of the configuration folder have to be deleted in this context.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_consolelog.title: -consoleLog
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_consolelog.content:
# -consoleLog

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## -consoleLog

Eclipse argument | Values | Usage  
---|---|---  
-consoleLog | - | optional  
**Annotation**  
With this option, a console window is opened automatically at the start of ec.exe. The application writes output to that window.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_data.title: -data
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_data.content:
# -data

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## -data

Eclipse argument | Values | Usage  
---|---|---  
-data | <dir> | Optional  
**Annotation**  
Specifies the path to the workspace folder. By default, the workspace folder is called workspace, and is located in the execution folder of ec.exe.  
  
### Note

Typically, the option -configuration is used in combination with -data. If the configuration directory and the workspace are modified at the same time, an error message will result: Class not found.  
An error-free migration requires two steps:

    1. Only convert the workspace to the new directory.
    2. Then, convert the configuration.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_launcher_ini.title: --launcher.ini
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_launcher_ini.content:
# --launcher.ini

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## \--launcher.ini

Eclipse argument | Values | Usage  
---|---|---  
\--launcher.ini | Name of the ini file | Optional  
**Annotation**  
This argument is used to reference a different initialization file. Example: ec.exe --launcher.ini user1.ini points to the initialization file user1.ini. Without this argument, EEC will use the initialization file of the same name. Examples: ec.exe uses ec.ini. ec_admin.exe uses ec_admin.ini.

parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_nl.title: -osgi.nl
parent_admin_r_eclipse_args_nl.content:
# -osgi.nl

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## -osgi.nl

The following EEC argument must be transferred with the following syntax:
    
        -D<EEC-Argument>=<Wert>

Eclipse argument |  Values |  Usage  
---|---|---  
-Dosgi.nl=<Language code>_<Country code> |  de_DE  
en_US |  Optional  
**Annotation**  
Sets the user interface language of EEC, the Job Server and the Workers. Currently, German and English are supported. Is entered below -vmargs.
    
        -Dosgi.nl=<Language code>_<Country code>

Examples:
    
        -vmargs

    -Dosgi.nl=de_DE
    
        -vmargs

    -Dosgi.nl=en_US
