parent_admin_r_installation_important_fi.title: Important folders and files
parent_admin_r_installation_important_fi.content:
# Important folders and files

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Important folders and files

In the following table, data and processes are assigned to specific folders, using a stand-alone installation as an example:

Folders and files | Read-/write rights | Explanations  
---|---|---  
ec.exe | R | Das EEC main applikation is ec.exe. A link to this file appears in the Start menu and, optionally, on the desktop.  
ec.ini | R | Initialization file for ec.exe.  
/configuration/ | RW | In this folder, Eclipse stores user-neutral configuration data that refer to the EEC installation. Like the workspace, this folder, too, belongs precisely to one running Eclipse application. In case of a Client-Server installation, this folder must therefore be located on the client computer. In the initialization file ec.ini this folder is specified with the -configuration argument.  
/plugins/ | R | All plugins of the EEC installation. The sum of plug-ins corresponds to the exe file of conventional applications  
/dropins/ | RW | The following is stored in this folder:
    1. Plug-ins created by the customer that are loaded like normal plug-ins at the start.
    2. Eclipse Extension Locations (dropins/myLocation/eclipse/, dropins/myLocation/eclipse/.eclipseextension, dropins/myLocation/eclipse/plugins or features or both)
    3. For further options, see <http://help.eclipse.org/helios/index.jsp?topic=/org.eclipse.platform.doc.isv/reference/misc/p2_dropins_format.html>  
/workspace/ | RW | Each user needs a private workspace where the user-specific configuration data, created documents, etc. are stored.  
/workspace/.metadata/ | RW | Eclipse stores the user-specific configuration data in this folder. These include the settings (Preferences) set up under Window > Settings, or other user-specific settings, such as the position and size of views, editors, perspectives, the editors most recently opened, etc.  
/resources/ | RW | This folder contains the âcontentsâ of the discipline-specific components, for example, EXF files, AWL files, etc.  
/db/ | RW | This folder contains the files of the database  
/install/ | RW | This folder contains the auxiliary data for the installation and / or use of skills  
/jre/ | R | Java runtime environment  
/jre/bin/javaw.exe | R | Application of the Java Virtual Machine  
  
*) Only available for hybrid installation.

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_clie.title: Client-Server
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_clie.content:
# Client-Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Client-Server

For the installation option Client Server you distribute the EEC components to at least two computers. A server is used to provide the model data (data back end) and/or the executable files of the EEC application. One or more workstation computers can execute the EEC and access the model data of the server.

The application and the resources do not necessarily have to be located on one server, but can be installed on two different servers:

Preparation A preparation or setup process step. (ISO) Down Arrow System-spec. generator Preparation A preparation or setup process step. (ISO) Down Arrow System-spec. generator Functional configuration generator Model Dimensioned 2D CAD Drawing generator generator Mechatronic components param Model Discipline-spec. configuration image/svg+xml EEC Workstation Link ec.ini configuration workspace Data access Refers to File server for application File server for resources resources >read/write< ec.exe ec.ini configuration plug-ins dropins proxy: EEC anyName.eox ../anyFolder >read/write< >read/write< May contain r/w r r/w

A file server provides the application with plug-ins and resources as Windows shares. Depending on whether the application or the resources are on the share, users will have either read-only or also write access to the respective share.

Another file server provides the resources that are accessed during the runtime.

The client-server installation involves two steps:

     * First, the servers are installed by starting the installation on the server that provides the share with the application. During the installation, the target location of the resources must be specified.
     * The data back end is then set up. Once all server-based steps are complete, the client installation is realized on each workstation. This is done via another setup wizard that is installed in the subfolder ec_client_setup of the application.

The installation of the client creates on each workstation a separate ec.ini file, as well as links to the application on the application share.

The above figure shows the folders and the process data traffic of the client-server installation. The read and write rights required for the individual areas are shown.

If EEC is started on the workstation, it reads the folders and files of the application, such as plugins, dropins, features or configuration. Locally, EEC creates caches, and stores the settings in a separate configuration folder. EEC has read and write access to the resources. For data storage with EOX files, it is possible to specify read-only or also write access. Client-computer-specific parts of the configuration folder and the workspace folder are always stored locally on the workstations in order to prevent noncompliance with Windows security policies, for example, regarding trusted assemblies (see Microsoft: [Code Access Security Policy Tool](http://msdn.microsoft.com/de-de/library/cb6t8dtz\(VS.80\).aspx)).

### Note

The installation, or parts of it, must not be moved, copied or renamed at the file system level. Such changes limit the operability of the application, or prevent the application from being executed.

Advantages of the Client-Server installation:

Lowest possible administration effort: All key data are stored centrally, and can be stored and updated there.

Disadvantages of the Client-Server installation:

When starting, application data (plug-ins) must be loaded from the server. This can lead to longer starting times. Each client computer requires a valid license.

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_job_.title: Job Server
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_job_.content:
# Job Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server

The installation option Job Server installation is created on a workstation computer or a server.

Stand-alone installation with EOX file or EECX file for data storage:

After the installation of the application as a Stand-Alone installation on the computer, further manual installation steps are required.

Manual installation steps:

     * Install [EEC Job Server Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserver.htm).

To run the Job Server, at least three folders must be set up that can be named in any manner (the following names have been chosen freely):

     * Job definitions: This folder must also be specified as the directory for JMX files in the settings. This is where job definitions in the JMX format are stored that trigger a defined job.
     * Successful jobs: In this folder, or its subfolders, the job definition is stored, as are the data created after successfully processing a job definition.
     * Failed jobs: In this folder, or its subfolders, the job definition is stored, as are the data created after a failed processing of a job definition.

If the generation of target data requires further resources, another, random, folder must be provided for this as well.

### Note

The Job Server can be set up as a Windows server and can be run without a user interface.

### Note

Up to four Workers together with a Job Server can be run on a workstation or server to create schematics and 3D graphics with Eplan Electric P8 and Eplan Pro Panel. To do this at least four different ports have to be specified for P8 (see [Open ports for Eplan P8 connection](admin_h_maintenance_p8_port_registration.htm)).

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_job_.title: Job Server with Worker
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_job_.content:
# Job Server with Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server with Worker

The installation option Job Server with Worker is created on a workstation computer or a server. The Job Server and Worker run in a joint process. If a restart of the Job Server is required, the Worker is also restarted and the other way around.

Stand-alone installation with EOX file or EECX file for data storage:

After the installation of the application as a Stand-Alone installation on the computer, further manual installation steps are required.

Manual installation steps:

     * Install [EEC Job Server Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserver.htm).
     * Install [EEC Job Server Worker Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserverworker.htm).

To run the Job Server with Worker, at least three folders must be set up that can be named freely (the following names have been chosen freely):

     * Job definitions: This folder must also be specified as the directory for JMX files in the settings. This is where job definitions in the JMX format are stored that trigger a defined job.
     * Successful jobs: In this folder, or its subfolders, the job definition is stored, as are the data created after successfully processing a job definition.
     * Failed jobs: In this folder, or its subfolders, the job definition is stored, as are the data created after a failed processing of a job definition.

If the generation of target data requires further resources, another, random, folder must be provided for this as well.

The Job Server with Worker can be set up as a Windows service and can be run without a user interface.

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_stan.title: Stand-Alone
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_stan.content:
# Stand-Alone

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Stand-Alone

You create the Stand-Alone installation option on a workstation computer.

After installing and licensing the application, you can start working immediately (see chapter [Starting](admin_h_installation_stand_alone_start.htm)).

### Note

**The installation, or parts of it, must not be moved, copied or renamed at the file system level.**   
Such changes limit the operability of the application, or prevent the application from being executed.

Stand-Alone installation with EOX file for data storage:

All parts of EEC are installed within a folder structure. An EOX file is used for data storage purposes.

The following image shows the installed folder:

The folders plugins and dropins must always be directly below the installation folder (ec).

After installation EEC has to be licensed, before it can start (see [Licensing](admin_h_installation_licensing.htm) and [Starting](admin_h_installation_stand_alone_start.htm)).

Advantages of the Stand-Alone installation:

There is virtually no installation effort. Without administrative efforts, several EEC versions can be installed easily in parallel on a computer in different folders.

Data storage via EOX file can be realized also as read-only, so that a specific status of the model is maintained.

Disadvantages of the stand-alone installation:

When extending the application area to a team, it becomes more complex to synchronize model data, for example.

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_web_.title: WebÂ EEC
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_web_.content:
# WebÂ EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Web EEC

The installation option Web EEC can be installed and distributed on different servers, or just on a single computer.

After the installation of the application as a stand-alone installation on the EEC Server, further manual installation steps are required (see [Installing EEC servers](admin_h_installation_web_eec_eec_server.htm)).

On the page of the EEC Server, the file ec.ini must be adjusted in such a way that the data of the user interface of a model provided can be sent to the Web EEC Server on the basis of an EOX file. In addition to the EOX file used, one must specify read-only or also write access. For communications, the host of the EEC Server, the port, and waiting time must be defined. The identical port number must be stated in the file web.xml of the Tomcat Server. The port must be different from the port for the communication between the clients and the Web EEC Server if the Web EEC Server and the EEC Server have been set up on the same computer.

On the page of the Web EEC Server, at first, a Tomcat web server must be installed (see [Installing and configuring Web EEC](admin_h_installation_web_eec_configuration.htm)).

Then, the port for client communications must be configured, and Java options set up.

The Web EEC Server and EEC Server can be installed on different computers, or on the same computer. If the servers are installed on different computers, then the created files must be located on a file system which is accessible by both computers.

parent_admin_r_installation_variant_work.title: Job Server Worker
parent_admin_r_installation_variant_work.content:
# Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server Worker

The installation option Job Server Worker is created on a workstation or on a server. Although Installed on one system, Job Server and Worker still run in different processes that can be terminated or restarted independently of each other.

Stand-alone installation with EOX file or EECX file for data storage:

After the installation of the application as a Stand-Alone installation on the computer, further manual installation steps are required.

Manual installation steps:

     * Install [EEC Job Server Worker Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_jobserverworker.htm).

### Note

Up to four Workers together with a Job Server can be run on a workstation or server to create schematics and 3D graphics with Eplan Electric P8 and Eplan Pro Panel. To do this at least four different ports have to be specified for P8 (see [Open ports for Eplan P8 connection](admin_h_maintenance_p8_port_registration.htm)).

parent_admin_r_installation_variants.title: Installation variants
parent_admin_r_installation_variants.content:
# Installation variants

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Installation variants

EEC is set up as a modular system, and can be installed and operating in various configurations. The license agreements for internal and external users applies to all of the installation variants.

     * Stand-Alone: Single workstation application Several modular system developers and / or users with Stand-Alone installations can exchange and / or synchronize their data via EOX files.
     * Client-Server: Multiple-workstation application for small group of users in a local network or in a separate virtual environment. Several modular system developers can work on the same model, without having to synchronize. Not recommended for a configuration of projects across distributed locations.
     * EEC Server: Like Stand-Alone installation, with EOX file for data storage on a Server (Apache Tomcat). Extension to EEC Server via plug-ins and modified ec.ini. Comes with http connection (also SSL-encrypted). Generates target data, as well as Form-UI elements of a project, and makes these available to the user via the Web EEC Server.
     * Job Server: As Stand-Alone installation. Extension to Job Server and/or Worker via plugins. Monitors a directory for the existence of job definitions, and, based on a loaded model executes project manipulations (Create new project, Instantiate components, Execute commands, Generate target data).
     * Web EEC Server: Multiple-workstation application for large groups of users to configure projects across distributed locations. Functional limitations possible with respect to certain disciplines; Graph2D currently not possible; Form-UI with some minor limitations (dialogs), because it is a web application. Requires an EEC server which is accessed remotely. SSL-encrypted communication possible.
     * Windows service: As Stand-Alone installation. Runtime environment for model of the development environment. Generates the target data of a project and makes these available to the user via the REST service.

The installation variants Stand-Alone and Client-Server are offered by the installation wizard, while Web EEC, EEC Server, Job Server and Windows Server are derived from these installation variants.

Caption:

Symbol | Meaning  
---|---  
| Installation file for Eplan Engineering Configuration.  
| Installation package for EEC client.  
| Installation file for EEC client.  
| Configuration file for EEC client installation.  
| EEC installation folder  
| EEC main application  
| Link to an executable file, e.g., main EEC application.  
| Initialization file for the main application  
| Data back end based on an EOX or an EECX file.  
| Folder with plugins for EEC. This corresponds to the DLL files of a classic Windows application.  
| Data storage in database: Folder with private user data, for example, user preferences, work status of created project documents, etc. Data storage with EOX: Folder containing information model, private user data, for example, user preferences, work status of created project documents, etc.  
| Folder with internal administrative data of the EEC, e.g., config.ini  
  
A table of [Important folders and files](admin_r_installation_important_files_folders.htm) can be found in the chapter of that name.

If this is not the case, the documentation goes beyond the scope of the purchased software, this means it also describes functions that are not available to you.

## Default comment

Path to model variable:

Disciplines

Specify a string value. The value determines a text that is used for all comments without content.

The comment is merely displayed for each object in the comment field. Once the comment for an object has been changed, it can no longer be overwritten by the default comment. Thus, a change of the default comment will only affect all the empty comments.

The value of the default comment can be created using a simple text editor (e.g., Edit) and pasted in the Value column via the clipboard. Multiline comments can be pasted only via the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [V].

parent_admin_r_modelvar_globalparameteru.content:
# Name of the unit for global parameters

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Name of the unit for global parameters

Path to model variable:

Model Variables > Disciplines

Specify a string value. The value determines the unit in which a search for parameters is first carried out while discipline resources are Import and synchronized before these are created in the unit for the respective discipline parameters.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

Requirements:

Definition of model variables:

Name | Value  
---|---  
Disciplines > Global Parameter Unit | Parameter pool  
Disciplines > ECAD Parameter Unit | ECAD.Parameter  
Disciplines > ECAD > Create parameters on demand |  true  
  
The Parameter pool unit contains at least one parameter with the Position name.

The ECAD.Parameter unit contains a parameter with the Location name.

An ECAD discipline component is created. This contains the parameters Position, Location and Page.

Result:

     * The Position parameter references the parameter by the same name in the Parameter pool unit.
     * The Location parameter references the parameter by the same name in the ECAD.Parameter unit.
     * The Page parameter is created in the ECAD.Parameter unit.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_iogen_ioassign.title: Name of the parameter for the I/O assignment
parent_admin_r_modelvar_iogen_ioassign.content:
# Name of the parameter for the I/O assignment

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Name of the parameter for the I/O assignment

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > IO Generator

Specify the name of a parameter. Add this parameter to the PLC discipline components. The value of the parameter in the discipline components defines whether the input/output addresses can be assigned to different input/output cards.

Possible parameter values are:

     * free: The assignment is free, meaning that the addresses of the sensors and actuators can be assigned to different input and output cards. This mode is the default behavior when a sensor or actuator does not dispose of the parameters for the I/O assignment. The Free mode creates the addressing previously carried out by the IO Generator.
     * IO separate: If a component disposes of both input and output signals, all the inputs are assigned to the same input card and all the outputs assigned to the same output card in this mode.
     * IO combined: If a component disposes of both input and output signals, all the inputs and outputs are assigned to the same control card in this mode. In this case the control card must offer both input and output addresses.

## Instance name parameter for discipline structures

Path to model variable:

Disciplines

Specify the name of a parameter. Add this parameter to the root projects of the disciplines. The value of the parameter in the discipline components defines the name of a discipline root project.

### [Example for the discipline ECAD](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Create the Disciplineroot parameter with the Stringtype.
    2. Add this parameter to a discipline component of the WiringDiagram type.
    3. For this parameter, enter the value Schematic.
    4. Create the project and create the ECAD discipline structure.

The root project with the name Schematic is located in the project structure below ECAD.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_order_of_discipl.title: Parameter name for the sort order
parent_admin_r_modelvar_order_of_discipl.content:
# Parameter name for the sort order

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameter name for the sort order

Path to model variable:

Model Variables > Disciplines

Specify the name of a parameter. Add this parameter to the discipline components. The value of the parameter in the discipline components defines the order in which the components of the discipline structure are sorted. The sorting is in ascending order. The directly subordinated discipline components of a parent discipline component are sorted. If subordinate discipline components with and without sorting parameter are found, then discipline components without parameter are sorted to the end (they have implicitly an infinite parameter value). The parameter is determined in the mechatronic structure before inserting the discipline component into the discipline structure. Formulas which determine the value in the discipline structure (e.g. dc, rdc, dos, etc.) cannot work.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8imagefolder.content:
# Image Folder

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Image Folder

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Folder

The path in which the initial search for P8 images is carried out is specified with the model variables Image Folder.

No default value is specified.

If a relative path is specified for the Image folder, the resulting absolute path is composed of the following settings:

    1. The specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath= in the initialization file (e.g. ec.ini).
    2. The specification Image Folder in the model variables.

An absolute path specification for the image folder overrides the specifications in the initialization file and the settings.

# Parameter Name For The Macro Parameter Structure

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameter Name For The Macro Parameter Structure

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8

Specify the name of a parameter. If the value of the model variables Write macro-parameter structure in parameter is set to true, the parameter with the specified name is created when a P8 macro is read in. All the relevant data are read from the P8 macro and stored as a list of maps in the parameter. The list contains, among other things, information about variants, text markers (plugs and sockets), variables, value sets, representation type, and others.

### Read more

     * [Write Macro Parameter Structure In Parameter](admin_r_modelvar_p8writemacrostructure.htm)

An absolute path specification for the macro folder overrides the specifications in the initialization file and the settings.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8script.title: Parameter Name For C# Script
parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8script.content:
# Parameter Name For C# Script

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameter Name For C# Script

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8

Specify the name of a parameter. Add this parameter to the P8 discipline components of the WiringDiagram type. The value of the parameter in the discipline components defines the absolute name of a C# script. The path to the C# script has to be specified with double backslashes, for example =c:\\\scripts\\\ecad\\\myP8Script.cs.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8scriptargs.title: Parameter name for the C# script arguments
parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8scriptargs.content:
# Parameter name for the C# script arguments

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameter name for the C# script arguments

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8

Specify the name of a parameter. Add this parameter to the P8 discipline components of the WiringDiagram type. The value of the parameter in the discipline components defines a list of arguments that are passed to a C# script, for example [refreshFSÂ»true,widthÂ»35,lengthÂ»100].

Currently, the data types string and int are supported for the arguments of the Start method. The order of the arguments does not have to be followed, because the assignment is realized via the parameter names.

As a further option, the parameter refreshFS can be entered. If the value of the parameter is true, the internal file system of the EEC is synchronized after execution of the script.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8skanlage.title: Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Function Designations
parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8skanlage.content:
# Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Function Designations

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Installation Sites

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Structure identifier management

With the Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Installation Sites model variable the name of a parameter is specified that stores the list of installation sites in a specific sequence.

To be able to specify a specific sequence of installation sites in the schematic, a parameter by the same name must be inserted in the component of the WiringDiagram super class.

The output value is a list that consists either only of designations or pairs that are composed of a designation and a description.

### [Example for List](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        =List{'Ground', 'Floor 1', 'Floor 2', 'Top'}

Result:

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | Ground |  |  | ? |   
2 | Floor 1 |  |  | ? |   
3 | Floor 2 |  |  | ? |   
4 | Top |  |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
  
### [Example for Map](javascript:void\(0\);)

The description can be specified in every available language. The syntax is as follows: <Language abbreviation>@<Description>;. After the last description a semicolon must be added!
    
        =List{Pair{'Ground', 'de_DE@Erdgeschoss;en_US@Ground Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Floor 1', 'de_DE@Erstes Geschoss;en_US@First Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Floor 2', 'de_DE@Zweites Geschoss;en_US@Second Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Top', 'de_DE@Obergeschoss;en_US@Top Floor;'},}

Result (set dialog language: de_DE):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Installation Sites

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Structure identifier management

To be able to specify a specific sequence of installation sites in the schematic, a parameter by the same name must be inserted in the component of the WiringDiagram super class.

The output value is a list that consists either only of designations or pairs that are composed of a designation and a description.

### [Example for List](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        =List{'Ground', 'Floor 1', 'Floor 2', 'Top'}

Result:

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | Ground |  |  | ? |   
2 | Floor 1 |  |  | ? |   
3 | Floor 2 |  |  | ? |   
4 | Top |  |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
  
### [Example for Map](javascript:void\(0\);)

The description can be specified in every available language. The syntax is as follows: <Language abbreviation>@<Description>;. After the last description a semicolon must be added!
    
        =List{Pair{'Ground', 'de_DE@Erdgeschoss;en_US@Ground Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Floor 1', 'de_DE@Erstes Geschoss;en_US@First Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Floor 2', 'de_DE@Zweites Geschoss;en_US@Second Floor;'},

    	Pair{'Top', 'de_DE@Obergeschoss;en_US@Top Floor;'},}

Result (set dialog language: de_DE):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | Ground | Erdgeschoss |  | ? |   
2 | Floor 1 | Erstes Geschoss |  | ? |   
3 | Floor 2 | Zweites Geschoss |  | ? |   
4 | Top | Obergeschoss |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
  
Result (set dialog language: en_US):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | Ground | Ground Floor |  | ? |   
2 | Floor 1 | First Floor |  | ? |

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Function Designations

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Structure identifier management

With the Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Function Designations model variables the name of a parameter is specified that stores the list of installation location designation in a certain sequence.

To be able to specify a certain sequence for location designations in the schematic, a parameter by the same name must be inserted in the component of the super class Schematic.

The output value is a list that consists either only of designations or pairs that are composed of a designation and a description.

### [Example for Map](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        = List {

    	Pair{'A1', List{

    		Pair{'1002', 'Description1 A1'},

    		Pair{'1007', 'Description2 A1'},

    		Pair{'1008', 'Description3 A1'},

    		Pair{'1009 1', 'Description Additional Field1 A1'},

    		Pair{'1009 2', 'Description Additional Field2 A1'},

    		Pair{'1009 3', 'Description Additional Field3 A1'}

    	}},

    	Pair{'A2', 'Description A2'}

    }

Result (set dialog language: en_US):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description <1002> | Original designation | Usage | Status | Structure description 2 <1007> | Structure description 3 <1008> | Structure description Supplementary field[1] <1009 1> | Structure description Supplementary field[2] <1009 2> | Structure description Supplementary field[3] <1009 3>  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | A1 | Description1 A1 | A1 | No |  | Description2 A1 | Description3 A1 | Description Additional Field1 A1 | Description Additional Field2 A1 | Description Additional Field3 A1  
2 | A2 | Production | A2 | No |  |  |  |  |  |   
3 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | * |  |  |  |  |  |   
  
### Read more

     * [Structure identifier management](../../../../Plattform/2026/Content/htm/pleditorgui_k_start.htm)

parent_admin_r_modelvar_p8skdokumentenar.content:
# Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Document Kinds

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Document Kinds

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Structure identifier management

The name of a parameter that contains the list of the document types saved in a certain sequence, is specified with the model variable Parameter Name For The Sequence Of The Document Kinds.

To be able to specify a certain sequence of document types in a project, a parameter by the same name must be inserted in the component of the WiringDiagram super class.

The output value is a list that consists either only of designations or pairs that are composed of a designation and a description.

### [Example for List](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        =List{'EPC', 'EMB', 'EFS', 'EPL'}

Result:

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | EPC |  |  | ? |   
2 | EMB |  |  | ? |   
3 | EFS |  |  | ? |   
4 | EPL |  |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
  
### [Example for Map](javascript:void\(0\);)

The description can be specified in every available language. The syntax is as follows: <Language abbreviation>@<Description>;. After the last description a semicolon must be added!
    
        =List{Pair{'EPC', 'de_DE@StÃ¼cklisten;en_US@Bills of material;'},

    	Pair{'EMB', 'de_DE@Verkabelungs- und Rohrleitungsdokumente;en_US@Wiring and tubing plans;'},

    	Pair{'EFS', 'de_DE@Schaltkreisdokumente;en_US@Schematics;'},

    	Pair{'EPL', 'de_DE@Ortslisten;en_US@Locations list;'},}

Result (set dialog language: de_DE):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | EPC | Bills of materials |  | ? |   
2 | EMB | Verkabelungs- und Rohrleitungsdokumente |  | ? |   
3 | EFS | Schaltkreisdokumente |  | ? |   
4 | EPL | Ortslisten |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
  
Result (set dialog language: en_US):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | EPC | Bills of materials |  | ? |   
2 | EMB | Wiring and tubing plans |  | ? |   
3 | EFS | Schematics |  | ? |   
4 | EPL | Locations list |  | ? |   
5 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |

parent_admin_r_modelvar_partsdb.title: Parts database
parent_admin_r_modelvar_partsdb.content:
# Parts database

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parts database

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Parts database

In order to work with Eplan Pro Panel, it must be possible to access the parts database. There are corresponding methods for this in the formula language, as well as model variables.

The advantage of the model variables is that the required data is not only determined during the generation.

### Note

These model variables are not assigned to parameter names, but the required values are directly entered.

### Note

Changes to the model variables for the parts database do not become effective until after a restart of the EEC because they are read in during the start. When P8 or Pro Panel is started, the model variables are transferred to the P8 or Pro Panel application respectively.

Model variable  
---  
SQL user name  
SQL catalog  
SQL password  
SQL Server  
  
### Note

If there is no specification for the model variables, the parts database that is specified in the options of P8 under User > Management > Parts is accessed.

Access to the database can be carried out either by a database user who has to carry out corresponding specifications for authorizations, or by a user who authorizes themselves through their Windows login. The following tables show which specifications are required respectively (values are only examples).

The following specifications are required in the case of a Windows authorization:

Model variable | Value  
---|---  
SQL user name |   
SQL catalog | DataBaseName  
SQL password |   
SQL Server | ServerName\SQLEXPRESS  
  
The following specifications are required in the case of an SQL Server Authentication:

Model variable | Value  
---|---  
SQL Username | LoginName  
SQL catalog | DataBaseName  
SQL password | ***  
SQL Server | ServerName\SQLEXPRESS  
  
The password is displayed represented by asterisks.

parent_admin_r_modelvar_plc_awx_codegen_.title: Generate comments in calls
parent_admin_r_modelvar_plc_awx_codegen_.content:
# Generate comments in calls

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Generate comments in calls

Path to model variable:

Disciplines > PLC > AutomationWorX > Code Generator

Select a Boolean value. The value determines whether block calls are provided with comments during the generation of the PLC code.

Valid values are true and false.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

Block call with comments (Value = true):
    
        Xaxis1(

    	moveForward := forward1,

    	moveBack := backward1,(* X-axis moves backward*)

    	inFront := inFront1,

    	inBack := inBack1,(* X-axis reached back position*)

    	forward => forward1,

    	Bus := Bus.No1(*Transportation*)

    	);

Block call without comments (Value = false):
    
        Xaxis1(

    	moveForward := forward1,

    	moveBack := backward1,

    	inFront := inFront1,

    	inBack := inBack1,

    	forward => forward1,

    	Bus := Bus.No1

    	);

PLC code of the resource:
    
        (*

    moveForward: BOOL; [* X-axis moves forward*]

parent_admin_r_modelvar_validationfilesy.title: File system name check
parent_admin_r_modelvar_validationfilesy.content:
# File system name check

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## File system name check

Path to model variable:

Validation

The NTFS file system allows a length of 255 characters for a file name, of which 4 characters are as a rule used for the file extension including the separating dot. In particular the absolute names of discipline components should be selected so that the created files do not exceed this length. The absolute names result amongst others from the depth of hierarchy levels. Components that do not affect the generation of target documents can have any length of name.

The restriction to 260 characters for the path length can be lifted for the Windows 10 operating system via the registry key HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\LongPathEnabled.

Select a value from the dropdown list to determine the type of message.

     * Ignore: Errors in the file system are ignored.
     * Warning: A warning is output for each error in the file system.
     * Error: An error message is output for each error in the file system.

Default value: Warning

parent_admin_r_modelvariables.title: Model variables
parent_admin_r_modelvariables.content:
# Model variables

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Model variables

Path to the dialog:

Model > Model variables

Discipline-specific variables can be configured in a user-specific manner as part of customizing EEC. Apart from parameter names, it is also possible to define the procedure for the import of external resources.

Model variables can be exported to files with the extensions .mdv and imported again to a model .

During the import, only the model variables are overwritten that are contained in the *.mdv file. If all other model variables are to be reset to their defaults, select the check box Reset model variables that are not contained in import file to default values (see the following figure).

There are two ways of doing this:

    1. Importing and exporting without open model variables editor:

Export: File > Export... > Model > Model Variables

Import: File > Import... > Model > Model Variables

All currently stored model variables are exported; during the import, all existing model variables are overwritten and stored.

    2. Importing and exporting in the model variables editor:

Export: using icon  or Popup Menu > Export

Import: using icon  or Popup Menu > Import

Only highlighted model variables are exported. This takes into account also model variables that have not yet been saved. In the import, only model variables are overwritten that were imported from the *.mdv file. The imported model variables are not saved automatically; this must be done separately.

parent_admin_r_prefs.title: Preferences
parent_admin_r_prefs.content:
# Preferences

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Preferences

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences

Preferences are used to control the appearance and behavior of the user interface. All preferences are default preferences that apply to most users.

The preferences of the category displayed are reset to the default values by using the [Restore default values] button.

The modifications become active immediately when the [Apply] button is clicked.

parent_admin_r_prefs_datasources.title: Data sources
parent_admin_r_prefs_datasources.content:
# Data sources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Data sources

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > Data sources

     * The Resources folder specifies which folder is initially searched for data files.

Default value: <EEC installation folder>\resources\DataSources

The absolute path is shown below the input field.

     * The Resources folder specifies which folder is initially searched for data files.
     * To allow the output of warnings of the OpenOffice Quickstarter, the Show OpenOffice Quickstarter warnings has to be marked. This option is enabled by default.
     * The Initial connection pool size indicates how many connections are allowed from the start of the EEC. The default is 1.
     * The specification of the Minimum size of the connection pool can be used to specify how many connections are allowed as a minimum. The default is 0.
     * To restrict the data traffic the specification of the Maximum size of the connection pool can be used to specify how many connections at a maximum can be permitted parallel. The default is 3.
     * If the number of connections has to be increased, this can be carried out in steps that are specified with the specification Increment for pool increase. The default is 2.
     * After the expiry of time for Maximum idle time of a connection in the pool (in seconds) the connection to a data source is closed. The default is 300.
     * After the expiry of time for Maximum age of a connection in the pool (in seconds) the connection to a data source is closed. The default is 28800.
     * After the indicated Number of connection attempts the resumption of a connection attempt is canceled and an error message output. The default is 5.
     * A Pause between connection attempts (in milliseconds) prevents a too rapid resumption of a connection attempt for connection problems. The default is 1000.
     * The option Protocoled SQL queries for quality feedback report activates the entry of warnings and errors in a protocol file to send these to Eplan. This option is not enabled by default.

parent_admin_r_prefs_debugging.title: Preferences for the formula inspector
parent_admin_r_prefs_debugging.content:
# Preferences for the formula inspector

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Preferences for the formula inspector

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Formulas > Formula Inspector

In the default setting, the branches of a formula are also calculated and represented in the Formula Inspector that are not relevant to the determination of the result. Results of non-relevant branches are represented in gray.

To suppress the evaluation in the formula inspector, the check box Enable inspection of non-evaluated conditional branches in the formula inspector must be deselected. Branches whose evaluation are not relevant to the result will then be marked as Not evaluated!.

Enter a Maximum number of drop-down levels so that only a limited number of terms can be opened in the formula inspector.

The default value is 20.

To avoid restricting the drop-down levels, enter a value of 0. In the case of very extensive formulas, this setting can result in longer waiting times.

parent_admin_r_prefs_ecad.title: ECAD
parent_admin_r_prefs_ecad.content:
# ECAD

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## ECAD

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > ECAD

     * The Path to ECAD resources specifies which folder is initially searched for Eplan Electric P8 macros.

Default value: <EEC installation folder>\resources\ECAD

The absolute path is shown below the input field.

Absolute local paths, absolute UNC paths as well as relative paths are all valid:

Absolute paths are used as specified.

Relative paths consist of the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) and this specification. If no path is specified for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file, the path consists of the EEC installation folder and the relative path.

### [Example for a local path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2_7_20180202-1530\resources

Specification of Path to ECAD macros = Tutorial\P8

Resulting path = C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2_7_20180202-1530\resources\Tutorial\P8

### [Example for a UNC path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=\\\myShare\resources\

Specification of Path to ECAD macros = Tutorial\P8

Resulting path = \\\myShare\resources\Tutorial\P8

### Note

Only existing folders are valid. Non-existing folders are not created by EEC.

### Read more

     * [Preferences for disciplines](admin_r_prefs_disciplines.htm)

parent_admin_r_prefs_ecad_p8.title: Eplan Electric P8
parent_admin_r_prefs_ecad_p8.content:
# Eplan Electric P8

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Eplan Electric P8

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8

     * Eplan Electric P8 executable specifies the executable file of the Eplan Electric P8 application (W3u.exe). The executable file does not have to be specified, if EEC is only used to create EDZ files.
     * Macro project template specifies the name of a macro project template (*.zw9 file) for generation of schematics. The macro project template does not have to be specified, if EEC is only used to create EDZ files.

The absolute path is shown below the input field.

     * Automatically start API in background starts the Eplan API on start of EEC in background. This option can only be used if valid specifications are contained n the fields Eplan Electric P8 executable and Macro project template.
     * Keep plug/socket text objects avoids deletion of function text for Plugs and Sockets in the created schematics.
     * Keep macro boxes in window macros has the effect that the macro boxes of window macros are retained in the created schematics. If a schematic page with a window macro that consists of several symbol macros is to be created for a new part of the parts database (EDZ file), this option is to be activated.
     * Keep macro boxes in symbol macros has the effect that the macro boxes of symbol macros are retained in the created schematics. If a schematic page with a symbol macro that consists of several window macros is to be created for a new part of the parts database (EDZ file), this option is to be activated.
     * Reuse existing project for schematic generation accelerates the renewed generation of the same project. The creating and deleting of P8 projects using API takes considerable time. Schematic generation has therefore been optimized so that a project already created is kept during repeated creating. If the project already exists when a new schematic is to be created, EEC checks whether the project template for the project has remained the same. If yes, EEC deletes only existing pages and leaves the project. As a secondary effect an error message is no longer created if the project is still opened in a P8. This is particularly useful, if only individual pages are to be checked in a schematic.
     * Convert macros into groups during schematic generation groups all macros, which are located in a created schematic.
     * Continue P8 generation in case of an error prevents that the creation of schematics is canceled by the occurrence of an error. If this option is active, imperfections during the creation of a schematic are skipped and then if necessary C#-Scripts executed.
     * Delete placeholders after generation removes all placeholders after the P8 project has been created.

### Note

The Delete placeholders after generation option can be used as of Version 2.6 HF4 of Eplan Electric P8. If this option is active, the optional function P8 Integration Service Feature cannot be used.

The Communication server section is displayed when the optional function P8 Integration Service Feature is installed. There the set port is displayed with which communication between Eplan Electric P8 and EEC is effected.

parent_admin_r_prefs_execution_time.title: Settings for profiling
parent_admin_r_prefs_execution_time.content:
# Settings for profiling

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Settings for profiling

Path to the settings:

Menu Window > Settings

Dialog Settings > Formulas > Profiling

If the Enable performance measurement option is selected, all the performances that exceed a specific threshold are logged.

A time value that has to be exceeded by a performance in order to log it is to be specified in the Performance measurement threshold (ms) field. 10 ms are specified as the default value. An execution is logged in the workspace\\.metadata\Formulatrace_<running number>.csv file if it is exceeded, for example workspace\\.metadata\Formulatrace_0.csv.

Each log entry contains the name of the object (Object), the name of the parameter (Parameter), the formula (Formula), the execution time (ExecutionTime) and the calculation time (ComputationTime).

parent_admin_r_prefs_js.title: Preferences for the Job Server
parent_admin_r_prefs_js.content:
# Preferences for the Job Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Preferences for the Job Server

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Job Server

     * A UNC path or a local path to a folder can be specified in the Repository folder field. This folder is used as basis for relative path specifications for the folders incomingFolder, outputFolder and failedFolder, as well as the folder, which contains the model (see[incomingFolder](refjobserver_r_incomingfolder.htm),[outputFolder](refjobserver_r_outputfolder.htm),[failedFolder](refjobserver_r_failedfolder.htm)and[jobdefinition](refjobserver_r_jobdefinition.htm)).

A central directory for the different storage locations of the Job Server is specified by means of the Repository folder.

If a UNC path is specified, it must be accessible both for the Job Server and for all Workers (read and write permissions). A local path can only be accessed by the Job Server and the local Workers.

Absolute path specifications for the Job Server can be made as well.

If this specification is missing and relative path specifications are provided in the job definitions, then error messages will occur.

### Note

If the Repository folder is set as a local path, jobs cannot be executed on remote Workers, but only on local Workers. So that this limitation can be recognized, this limitation is displayed at the start of the Job Server and when the job definitions are updated.

parent_admin_r_prefs_js_email.title: E-mail settings
parent_admin_r_prefs_js_email.content:
# E-mail settings

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## E-mail settings

After finishing a job certain e-mail recipients can be informed about the result. Different recipients, subjects and contents can be provided for successful and failed jobs. In addition the mail server preferences can be set.

Path to the settings:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Job Server > Job Server Worker > Email preferences

     * To enable messages in the event of success or an error, the check boxes Send mail on success and / or Send mail on failure must be selected.
     * The recipient of the message is specified along with the sender (Worker) and a subject line in Mail Settings.
     * To allow the e-mail to be sent, the following preferences have to be carried out in the area of the SMTP connection:
       * In the Connection security field, select one of the protocols None, STARTTLS or SSL/TLS.
       * In the Server field, specify the SMTP mail server that is used in your company to send the e-mail. The default is http://localhost.
       * Enter the appropriate port number for the selected protocol in the Port field. The default value for unencrypted mailing is 25, for encrypted mailing via STARTTLS is 587, via SSL/TLS is 465.
     * If the SMTP mail server requires authentication, the Use SMTP authentication check box must be selected and the User and Password must be entered in the fields with those names.

### Note

The specified port numbers are default values and can differ from the specifications for your mail server. Contact the responsible administrator of your company to obtain the combination in question.

parent_admin_r_prefs_js_worker.title: Preferences for the Job Server Worker
parent_admin_r_prefs_js_worker.content:
# Preferences for the Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Preferences for the Job Server Worker

The preferences for the Worker set up the following:

     * The starting behavior of the Worker.
     * The file folder where job definitions for this Worker are stored.
     * The maximum number of parallel jobs.

### Note

The preferences for a Worker can only be changed

     * When the Worker is not processing a Job.
     * When the Worker is stopped.

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Job Server > Job Server Worker

     * The Automatic Worker startup option specifies that the Worker is started automatically when EEC is started and that the Job Server perspective is displayed. If the input folder contains files that correspond to the file trigger, the Job Server immediately begins to process them.
     * A file folder where the job definitions for this Worker are stored is specified in the JMX file folder field. The folder may be specified as UNC path, e.g., \\\myShare\jobserver\jobdefinitions.
     * The maximum number of jobs to be processed in parallel is specified in the Maximum number of parallel jobs field. Possible values are within the range of positive integers.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

There are 3 jobs in the input folder and all files needed for the jobs are also present. The Worker should start up immediately at EEC startup and the number of parallel jobs is set to 2.

Flow:

The Worker starts up automatically at EEC startup and the parallel processing of 2 jobs is started immediately. Then the third job is processed by itself.

### Note

Information about the supported version of Eplan Electric P8 and ProPanel is available under the[Software and hardware requirements](admin_r_it_reqs_software.htm).

The Automatic Job Server startup and Maximum number of parallel jobs preferences can also be specified in the initialization file, for example, ec.ini (see[de.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart](admin_r_vmargs_worker_autostart.htm)and[de.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.maxconcurrentjobs](admin_r_vmargs_worker_maxconcurrentjobs.htm)). The values specified in the initialization file overrule the values of the preferences. If the initialization file contains a value, this is displayed in the preferences.

parent_admin_r_prefs_logging.title: Logging preferences
parent_admin_r_prefs_logging.content:
# Logging preferences

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Logging preferences

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Logging

For the error search in a model, notes (debug output) are output in the console view during the execution of different actions.

The following output is provided for the structure generators:

     * All plug and socket values
     * All objects that do not fit because
     * the superordinate component is not permitted or
     * the subordinate component would become its own superordinate component.

The debug output is enabled and/or disabled via Preferences > Logging > User > Structure generator DEBUG or Preferences > Logging > User > Structure generator > <Discipline name> DEBUG (see the following figure). Below Structure generator, only those disciplines are offered for which the action Generate structure has already been started.

### [Tab Logfile](javascript:void\(0\);)

The Activated option switches the logging on/off.

You define the formatting of a log entry (deactivated) in the Layout input field.

Enter the name of the log file in the File input field (deactivated).

Enter the possible size of the log file in the input field Maximum file size.

### [Tab Rolling logfile](javascript:void\(0\);)

The Activated option switches rolling logging on/off.

You define the formatting of a log entry (deactivated) in the Layout input field.

Enter the directory, the file pattern and the file extension of the rolling log file in the File pattern input field.

You can run the rolling logging at intervals from Minute-based to Weekly. In rolling logging, a new log file is generated for each new time interval during which a log entry is made. The file name of the log file corresponds to the time stamp, for example 2022-11-24_14-45.log. For a user-defined interval you specify an interval pattern in the input field Derive from pattern, for example YYYY\MM\dd\hh-mm generates a directory structure for the year, the month and the day and in it a log file, for example 2022\11\24\14-45.log.

### [Tab Console (external:OS)](javascript:void\(0\);)

The Activated option switches on/off the output to the operating system console.

You define the formatting of a log entry in the Layout input field.

Statement | Meaning | Example | Result  
---|---|---|---  
%d{Format} | Time stamp | %d{ISO8601} | 2022-11-24 14:45:09.533  
%d{YYY-MM-dd hh:mm:ss} | 2022-11-24 14:45:09  
%c{1} | Component | <%c{1}> | <P8Generator>  
%m | Message text | %m | Cannot find parameter 'Parameter name'.  
  
### [Tab Console (internal)](javascript:void\(0\);)

The Activated option switches on/off the output to the internal console.

You define the formatting of a log entry in the Layout input field.

Enter a value in kilobytes (kB) for the buffer memory of the internal console in the Console buffer input field.

Statement | Meaning | Example | Result  
---|---|---|---  
%d{Format} | Time stamp | %d{ISO8601} | 2022-11-24 14:45:09.533  
%d{YYY-MM-dd hh:mm:ss} | 2022-11-24 14:45:09  
%c{1} | Component | <%c{1}> | <P8Generator>  
%m | Message text | %m | Cannot find parameter 'Parameter name'.

parent_admin_r_prefs_mediasources.title: Media sources
parent_admin_r_prefs_mediasources.content:
# Media sources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Media sources

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > Media sources

     * The Resources folder specifies which folder is initially searched for media source files.

Default value: <EEC installation folder>\resources\MediaSources

The absolute path is shown below the input field.

Both absolute local paths, absolute UNC paths as well as relative paths are valid for Resources folders:

Absolute paths are used as specified.

Relative paths consist of the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) and this specification. If no path is specified for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file, the path consists of the EEC installation folder and the relative path.

### [Example for a local path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2022_1\resources

Specification of Resource folder = media

Resulting path = C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2022_1\resources\media

### [Example for a UNC path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=\\\myShare\resources\

Specification of Resource folder = media

Resulting path = \\\myShare\resources\media

### Note

Only existing folders are valid. Non-existing folders are not created by EEC.

parent_admin_r_prefs_plc_s7.title: STEP 7
parent_admin_r_prefs_plc_s7.content:
# STEP 7

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## STEP 7

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > PLC > STEP 7

     * The Source folder specifies which folder is initially searched for STEP 7 source files.

Default value: <EEC installation folder>\resources\PLC

The absolute path is shown below the input field.

For Source folders absolute local paths, absolute UNC paths as well as relative paths are valid:

Absolute paths are used as specified.

Relative paths consist of the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) and this specification. If no path is specified for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath in the initialization file, the path consists of the EEC installation folder and the relative path.

### [Example for a local path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2022_1\resources

Specification for Source folder = plc\step7

Resulting path = C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2022_1\resources\plc\step7

### [Example for a UNC path](javascript:void\(0\);)

Specification in the initialization file: -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath=\\\myShare\resources\

Specification for Source folder = plc\step7

Resulting path = \\\myShare\resources\plc\step7

### Note

Only existing folders are valid. Non-existing folders are not created by EEC.

### Read more

     * [Preferences for disciplines](admin_r_prefs_disciplines.htm)

parent_admin_r_prefs_plc_s7simatic.content:
# SIMATIC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## SIMATIC

Path to the preferences:

Menu Window > Preferences

Dialog Preferences > Disciplines > PLC > STEP 7 > SIMATIC

     * The SIMATIC project folder specifies the folder, where the SIMATIC Manager manages its projects. The SIMATIC default folder is used, if no project folder is specified.
     * The option SIMATIC project in one source must be enabled, if all source files for the STEP 7 project are located in a packed file. Ordinarily this option must be disabled to create STEP 7 files out of distributed resources. This option is to be selected so that the Compile SIMATIC sources action can be executed free of faults. The Compile SIMATIC sources action can only be used with a 32-bit installation of the EEC that is older than Version 2.8.
     * The number of files which may result in compiler errors without interrupting the STEP 7 file generation is specified in the Maximum number of files with compiler errors field.
