     * [de.eplan.eec.datasource.defaultTable](admin_r_vmargs_datasource_defaulttable.htm)
     * [de.eplan.eec.datasource.user](admin_r_vmargs_datasource_user.htm)
     * [de.eplan.eec.datasource.password](admin_r_vmargs_datasource_password.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_hs.title: hsqldb
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_hs.content:
# hsqldb

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## hsqldb

User name and password must always be provided in uppercase letters so as to allow for access from the EEC.

Table and column names are always converted to uppercase letters for SQL requests. If this is not desired, they will have to be placed in quotes (").

Examples:

'select PartNumber from Parts' does not function: Error message: Table 'PARTS' does not exist.

'select PartNumber from "Parts'" does not function: Error message: Column 'PARTNUMBER' does not exist.

'select "PartNumber" from "Part"' works.

'select SupplierNumber from Suppliers' works.

'select SupplierNumber from Suppliers' works.

The column names in the query results are case-sensitive, i.e., they must be precisely as stated in the database:
    
        ...perform('select "Artikelnummer" from "Artikel"').collect(x|x.Artikelnummer)

    ...perform('select Lieferantennummer from Lieferanten').collect(x|x.LIEFERANTENNUMMER)

### Note

OpenOffice Base behaves exactly like hsqldb in the case of SQL requests.

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_ms.title: Microsoft SQL Server
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_ms.content:
# Microsoft SQL Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Microsoft SQL Server

When Microsoft SQL Server is used, all tables and column names are handled case-insensitively. The table and column names can be in uppercase or lowercase letters.

Contrary to the spelling used in the database, the column names are supplied in the result in the same way as in the SQL statement:
    
        ..perform('select ARTIKELNUMMER from ARTIKEL').collect(x | x.ARTIKELNUMMER)

    ..perform('select Artikelnummer from Artikel').collect(x | x.Artikelnummer)

As for requests with an asterisk, all column names are supplied as specified in the database:
    
        ..perform('select * from Artikel').collect( x | x.Artikelnummer)

### Note

Microsoft Access behaves with SQL requests identically to Microsoft SQL Server.

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_or.title: Oracle
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_or.content:
# Oracle

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Oracle

In an Oracle database, the column names are by default case-insensitive, i.e., uppercase / lowercase spelling does not matter. Internally, Oracle enters the column names in uppercase letters â and this is how they are also received in the EEC.

In this mode, it does not matter for SQL requests whether column and table names are spelled with uppercase or lowercase letters. When accessing the requested data, all column names must be entered in uppercase letters.
    
        ...perform('select * from Artikel ').collect( x | x.ARTIKELNUMMER)

Oracle is also capable of stating column names as case-sensitive by placing them in quotes in the Create Table statements or by selecting the Maintain Case-Sensitivity check box in the object browser when creating tables.

In this case, all SQL requests must also be placed between apostrophes:
    
        ..perform('select "Artikelnummer" from "Artikel"').collect(x | x.Artikelnummer)

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_po.title: PostgreSQL
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_database_po.content:
# PostgreSQL

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## PostgreSQL

All table and column names are converted to lowercase letters: i.e., all table and column names are then expected in lowercase letters by the database as well.

If uppercase letters are to be supported as well, all SQL requests must be placed in quotes.

The style of the column names in the results corresponds to the table definition.

âselect customernumber from customerâ works.

âselect PartNumber from Partsâ does not function.

âselect âPartNumberâ from âPartsââ works.

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_datasourceo.title: Data source objects
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_datasourceo.content:
# Data source objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Data source objects

Data sources are, like disciplines (ECAD, PLC, etc.), are integrated into the system by way of a separate system library:

To use data sources, the DataSource system library must be imported into the modular system.

The following figure shows the inheritance hierarchy:

The base object DataSource becomes specialized in CSV sources and relational sources. In the case of relational sources, one distinguishes between any relational databases (DatabaseDataSource) and specific data sources (OpenOffice).

The methods for access to external data sources are documented in the formula collection (see chapter [Data source objects]()).

The special characteristics of the individual data source objects are described in the following subsections

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_openofficeb.title: OpenOfficeBaseDatasource
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_openofficeb.content:
# OpenOfficeBaseDatasource

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## OpenOfficeBaseDatasource

OpenOfficeBaseDatasource is available for access to OpenOffice Base (odb files):

### Note

OpenOffice Base behaves exactly like hsqldb in the case of SQL requests.

When editing OpenOfficeBaseDatasource, it must be ensured that the OpenOffice Quickstarter is closed. Just to be sure, there will be a prompt prior to editing.

The display of the warning can be activated/deactivated in the settings.

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_recommendat.title: General recommendation for the choice of column and table names
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_recommendat.content:
# General recommendation for the choice of column and table names

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## General recommendation for the choice of column and table names

The following recommendations refer to cases where the database is to be optionally changeable.

If all table and column names are only in uppercase letters, the SQL requests sent to most databases (exception: PostgreSQL) are compatible, without having to place table and column names in quotes.

Alternative:

Table and column names are always placed in quotes in SQL requests. This is compatible also with most databases. In the case of Oracle, however, keep in mind that the table was set up with uppercase / lowercase letters.

If in future there is to be a switch only between Microsoft products (Access and Microsoft SQL Server), there is no special style to be observed when choosing table and column names, because their behavior is identical.

parent_eecbase_k_datasources_usage_of_da.title: Using data sources as resources
parent_eecbase_k_datasources_usage_of_da.content:
# Using data sources as resources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Using data sources as resources

External databases (Oracle, SQL server, etc.) with large data volumes continue to be maintained externally. Frequently, though, there are transparent models, for example, simple tables, where wire cross-sections are specified, certain part data are managed, etc. For such data formats (CSV files, MDL files, etc.), it often makes sense to transfer management to EEC.

All data sources, except DatabaseDataSource (with a Connection URL), therefore, are integrated into EEC in a manner similar to a new discipline. This has the following advantages:

     * Data sources can be edited with the respective editors and are managed like discipline resources.
     * The editor to be used can be set in Eclipse via the file extensions.
     * The data can be internalized in the model.
     * Editing via Edit Resource is possible. Any open database connections will be closed automatically in the process.
     * As with other disciplines, a default resource folder can be defined in the settings:

The settings for the connection pool are listed in detail in the following table.

Parameter name | Default value | Description  
---|---|---  
Initial connection pool size | 1 | Number of connections to be captured at the start. This number should be greater than the minimum number, and less than the maximum size of the connection pool.  
Minimum size of the connection pool | 0 | Minimum number of connections that are served simultaneously.  
Maximum size of the connection pool | 3 | Maximum number of connections that are served simultaneously.  
Increment for pool increase | 2 | Number of connections that are added when the connection pool is exhausted.  
Maximum idle time of a connection in the pool | 300 | Time in seconds during which an unused connection remains in the pool until it is deleted.  
Maximum age of a connection in the pool | 28800 | Absolute time in seconds during which a connection exists in the pool before it is disconnected and deleted. Connections with few unused times are also removed. The maximum age is set to 0 by default.  
Retry attempts | 5 | Number of connection attempts before it is abandoned. If the attempt to connect is never to be abandoned, 0 must be entered.  
Pause between connection attempts | 1000 | Time in milliseconds between two connection attempts.

parent_eecbase_k_delete_mechatronic_comp.title: Deleting mechatronic components in a library
parent_eecbase_k_delete_mechatronic_comp.content:
# Deleting mechatronic components in a library

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting mechatronic components in a library

They can be deleted via the context menu of the library component. In the process, the corresponding library object and all project components of this type are deleted.

If one or several objects are to be deleted from the library catalog, a prompt will be appear where the deletion of individual objects can be confirmed and / or canceled.

parent_eecbase_k_delete_mechatronic_comp.title: Deleting mechatronic components within a project
parent_eecbase_k_delete_mechatronic_comp.content:
# Deleting mechatronic components within a project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting mechatronic components within a project

The mechatronic component can be deleted using its context menu. It should be noted here that all sub-elements of the mechatronic object will also be deleted:

If one or several objects are to be deleted from the project catalog, a prompt will be appear where the deletion of individual objects can be confirmed and / or canceled.

parent_eecbase_k_editing_resources.title: Editing of resources directly from the EEC
parent_eecbase_k_editing_resources.content:
# Editing of resources directly from the EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Editing of resources directly from the EEC

Resources can be edited directly from within the EEC. For this purpose, in the popup menu of the discipline-specific component, select the Edit Resource action.

This mechanism can be applied to any resource. The editing is launched via the Edit Resource action. This is illustrated in the following example on the basis of the Sensors_Actuators_Body component:

If after editing the resource its interface has changed, the Synchronization editor will launch automatically, in which one must define the effects that the changes have on the model:

EEC finds the editor based on the type of the resource file of a component, for example, a specific editor for *.ema files. The assignment of editors to file types (file codings) is configured in the settings under General > Content types and General > Editors > File Assignments:

For association purposes, it is possible to distinguish between internal and external editors. If no internal editor is assigned, the file type is edited using the external editor that was previously configured in the operating system.

Internal editors: The internal editors are entered by default in the settings as template editors, recognizable by the extension (default), for example, for *.ema files, the internal editor Eplan project (default). We generally recommend the use of the internal template editors. If a resource is not edited by means of the internal template editor, but another internal editor, the synchronization process that keeps the data model consistent will not start. An error message will be displayed to point out this consequence.

External editors: In the file association dialog, external editors can be assigned explicitly within the EC, for example, Microsoft Word for *.txt editors. This mechanism (defined by Eclipse), however, is not used for editing external resources.

Reason: In order to be able to start the synchronization at the right time after editing external files, EEC must start the external editor as a subordinate process to find out the time when it was closed. Therefore, only the editors set up in the operating system are used for editing resources with external editors.

These editors are used automatically if the area Associated editors contains no other entries.

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange.title: Data exchange via EOX
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange.content:
# Data exchange via EOX

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Data exchange via EOX

EOX files (Engineering Object Exchange Format) offer the possibility to exchange any libraries/projects (assemblies).

For the development of models it is possible to exchange only the libraries by means of EOX files. The export of project modifications in PX files should be used primarily for the archiving of projects.

The upper figure shows how the assemblies are synchronized between the employees through data exchange via EOX.

The following sections illustrate the data exchange on the basis of different scenarios. In contrast to the export of assemblies, the import, offers various options depending on the approach.

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.title: Strategies for solving conflicts
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.content:
# Strategies for solving conflicts

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Strategies for solving conflicts

Manual and system strategies can be applied to the resolution of conflicts that may occur during synchronization.

A system strategy can be applied for all conflicts of the same type.

A manual strategy is applied only to the conflict displayed.

To reduce the number of conflicts to those that can be solved manually, one must first choose a system strategy.

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.title: Manual strategies
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.content:
# Manual strategies

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Manual strategy Relational conflict

## Manual strategies

Apart from the system strategy, the modular system developer itself offers also a manual strategy for solving the conflict. In the following example, it is not the value (Gripper to grip) from the EOX file that is accepted for merging; instead, the modular system developer first highlights the option Merge value manually, and then enters in the Internal model column a new value (Fast and small gripper. By saving the editor ([Ctrl] \+ [S]), the manually entered value is adopted.

Particularly relational conflicts cannot be solved by a system strategy, but require a manual strategy.

In this case (described in Scenario 4), the option is offered to navigate to the component in the internal model (Navigate to component in internal model) in order to solve the conflict there manually. For example, the relation with the deleted Component can be removed in the internal model.

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.title: System strategy
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_conflict_s.content:
# System strategy

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## System strategy

The system strategy Select value can be applied to conflicts that can be solved by overwriting the components of the internal model with the components of the EOX file or vice versa.

The following image shows the application of the system strategy Select value (see [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm) Scenario 2).

The conflict can be solved via the system strategy Select value. The user must select whether the target value is to be the value from the EOX file or the value of the internal model.

If this strategy can be applied to all other conflicts as well, it is possible to solve all conflicts of the same type without any further interaction by selecting the check box Use as global strategy.

This, then, leaves the conflicts (if any) that can be solved only through a manual strategy.

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_ex.title: Exporting libraries/projects as an EOX file
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_ex.content:
# Exporting libraries/projects as an EOX file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exporting libraries/projects as an EOX file

To export libraries or model data both an export wizard of the EEC user interface and a command can be used (see [Engineering.ExportEOXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_exporteoxcommand.htm)).

How to export model data as an EOX file by means of the export wizard of the EEC user interface:

    1. Click File > Export....

The Export wizard opens.

    1. Select Model > Wizard for exporting model data in the EOX format.
    2. Click [Next >].

The wizard for EOX export starts.

    1. In the EOX file field, enter a file name directly, or use [Browse...] to select a specific storage location.
    2. Highlight the libraries/projects to be exported.

Selected libraries/projects are identified with a check mark , while the dependent libraries/projects are identified by a square .

    1. Click [Finish].

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_im.title: Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_im.content:
# Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file

One of three options is used during the import of an EOX file:

### [Adding](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly to be imported is added to the existing model with the Add option, without existing assemblies being overwritten. No objects are deleted.

### [Overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

With the Overwrite option, existing assemblies of the same name are replaced with assemblies to be imported of the same name. Objects that are not part of the assemblies to be imported are deleted.

### [Adding/overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

With the Add/overwrite option the existing assemblies and the assemblies to be imported are compared with the assemblies of the common back-end. This option therefore only makes sense if both model data have the same origin.

On the basis of revision 1, two users (User1 and User2) work independently of each other on copies of this revision status. User 1 receives an EOX file from User 2, and imports it using the Add/overwrite option.

     * User2 adds new classes.
     * Classes deleted by User2 are deleted at User1âs end.
     * Classes deleted by User1 are not replaced by existing classes by User2.
     * For classes that were changed by both users, a decision has to be made which change is to be applied.

The following scenarios help to identify which option is to be applied in which case.

### [Working steps for importing an EOX file](javascript:void\(0\);)

The data of a model, i.e., the libraries and projects, are imported as an EOX file.

The following steps are necessary to import an EOX file:

    1. Select File > Import.

The Import wizard opens.

    1. Select Model > Model data (EOX).
    2. Confirm via [Next >].
    3. In the Import EOX section select the EOX file to be imported.
    4. Highlight the libraries and projects to be imported.

Selected libraries/projects are identified with a check mark , while the dependent libraries/projects are identified by a square .

    1. In the Import options area select the option Add or Overwrite or Add/overwrite.
       * Add adds all new assemblies to the current workspace.
       * Overwrite adds new assemblies to the current workspace and overwrites assemblies of the same name.
       * Add/overwrite merges assemblies of the same name. In the process a comparison between the previous revision (common back-end) and the change statuses of the EOX file to be imported and the current model takes place.
    2. If you have selected the Add/overwrite option, select a Common back-end in addition.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].

The wizard is terminated and the Synchronize view opened.

The following symbols can be displayed in the Synchronize view:

Symbol | Meaning  
---|---  
| The incoming changes shown with a plus sign (+) indicate that a component is missing in your assembly. This will be added.  
| The incoming changes shown with a minus sign (-) indicate that there is one more component in your assembly. This will be removed.  
| The incoming changes shown with the red double arrow indicate that interaction on the part of the user is required.  
  
If the synchronization does not show any changes for which an interaction of the user is required, the changes can be applied with .

During the synchronization, the following information is written to the console:

     * The absolute names of added components and parameters.
     * The absolute names of deleted components and parameters.
     * The absolute names of modified components and parameters.

The following scenarios illustrate the different merge results.

### [Scenario 1](javascript:void\(0\);)

The following scenario is based on a project (base), which is modified by two users (User1 and User2) independently of each other by deleting or adding.

User1 has deleted the instance GripperB, and added the instance GripperD.

User2 has deleted the instance GripperC, and added the instance GripperE.

### [Adding](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User1 is to be added to the assembly of User2 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The class Gripper appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change.

The instances GripperC and GripperD appear in the view Synchronize as incoming changes with a plus sign in the blue arrow. These will be added.

### Note

In this scenario, it is not clear whether GripperC and GripperD were created by User1 or deleted by User2. This requires coordination.

### [Overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User2 is to be overwritten with the assembly of User1 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The class Gripper appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change.

The instances GripperB and GripperE appear in the view Synchronize as incoming changes with a minus sign in the blue arrow. These will be deleted.

The instances GripperC and GripperD appear in the view Synchronize as incoming changes with a plus sign in the blue arrow. These will be added.

### Note

In this scenario, it is not clear whether GripperC and GripperD were created by User1 or deleted by User2. This requires coordination.

This scenario can be used to return to a project saved as an EOX file. All changes made up to this point will be lost.

### [Adding/overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User2 is to be merged with the assembly of User1:

The class Gripper appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change.

The instance GripperB appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a minus sign in the blue arrow. This will be deleted.

The instance GripperD appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a minus sign in the blue arrow. This will be added.

### Note

In this scenario, the reference to the base makes it clear that GripperB was deleted by User1, and GripperD created by User1.

### [Scenario 2](javascript:void\(0\);)

Scenario 2 is based on a project (base), which is modified by two users (User1 and User2) independently of each other by modifying the comment for GripperC.

User1 has changed the comment of GripperC to Gripper to grip.

User2 has changed the comment of GripperC to Fast gripper.

### [Adding](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User1 is to be added to the assembly of User2 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The instance GripperC appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a red double arrow. An interaction by User2 is required.

User2 must select a strategy:

The only strategy that can be chosen here is Select value. The value to be used as the target value must be chosen via the radio buttons EOX file (source) or Internal model or Merge value manually. The strategy chosen will also be applied to all other conflicts if the check box Use as global strategy has been selected.

### [Overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User2 is to be overwritten with the assembly of User1 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The instance GripperC is displayed in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. This will be added.

### [Adding/merging](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assembly of User2 is to be merged with the assembly of User1 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The instance GripperC appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a red double arrow. An interaction by User2 is required.

User2 must therefore select a strategy:

The only strategy that can be chosen here is Select value. The value to be used as the target value must be chosen via the radio buttons EOX file (source) or Internal model or Merge value manually. The strategy chosen will also be applied to all other conflicts if the check box Use as global strategy has been selected.

### Note

If in the imported EOX a class or instance has been deleted, the class will be deleted during the 3-Way Merge, and the instance will also be gone, but only after the project is re-created.

But if there is already another instance of the deleted class in the project, deleting the class should be thought over again.

The following solutions are possible:

    1. Not deleting the class.
    2. Deleting the class before synchronization, and creating a new class with the same properties. Subsequently the 3-ways-merge is restarted (see [Synchronize again](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_resynchronize.htm)).

### Note

Conflicts at the attributes level should always be solved before conflicts at the class level.

### [Scenario 3](javascript:void\(0\);)

Scenario 3 is based on an architecture and a modular system (base), which is modified by two users (User1 and User2) independently of each other.

User1 deletes the components Gripper and GripperA, and instead inserts the components Pusher and PusherA.

User2 deletes the component GripperA, and inserts the component GripperB.

### [Adding](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assemblies of User1 are to be added to the assemblies of User2 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The components Pusher and PusherA appear in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. These will be added, no interaction is required.

The component Transfer appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. This will be changed, no interaction is required.

### [Overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assemblies of User2 are to be overwritten with the assemblies of User1 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The components Gripper and GripperB appear in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow and minus sign. These are removed, no interaction is required.

The components Pusher and PusherA appear in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. These will be added, no interaction is required.

The component Move appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. This will be changed, no interaction is required.

### [Adding/overwriting](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assemblies of User2 are to be merged with the assemblies of User1 (User2 performs the import procedure):

The components Pusher and PusherA appear in the Synchronize view as an incoming change with a blue arrow. These will be added, no interaction is required.

The component Transfer appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a blue arrow. This will be changed, no interaction is required.

The components Gripper and GripperB appear in the view Synchronize as an incoming change with a red double arrow. There is a conflict. The component Gripper cannot be deleted, because GripperB is an instance of the component Gripper.

More information on the conflict of a component is provided by opening the conflict component:

In this case, an internal conflict resolution is not possible; there is no selection of system strategies to choose from.

Instead, a strategy at the workspace level is recommended: The relation of the component GripperB with the component Gripper is to be deleted.

The following approach is possible to solve the relational conflict:

    1. User1 re-creates the component Gripper, and exports the EOX file again with the same name and in the same folder. Using [Synchronize with last configuration], User2 repeats the import of the EOX file (see [Synchronize again](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_resynchronize.htm)).
    2. User2 removes the component GripperB, and using [Synchronize with last configuration], repeats the import of the EOX file (see [Synchronize again](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_resynchronize.htm)).

### [Scenario 4](javascript:void\(0\);)

Scenario 4 is based on an architecture and a modular system (base), which is modified by a user (User1).

User1 adds to the modular system the component GripperB, removes instance of GripperA mounted in the component Transfer, and instead mounts an instance of GripperB. Then, User1 renames the instance of GripperB to GripperA.

### [Adding](javascript:void\(0\);)

The assemblies of User1 are to be added to the assemblies of Base (any user performs the import procedure):

The component GripperB appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change both in the architecture and the modular system with a blue arrow and minus sign. This will be added.

The component Transfer appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change in the architecture with a blue arrow. This will be added.

The component Transfer appears in the view Synchronize as an incoming change in the modular system with a red double arrow. There is a conflict. The component Transfer cannot be added, because GripperA is an instance of the component GripperB.

More information on the conflict is provided by opening the conflict component:

The conflict description mentions the specific conflict (1): An object with the same name but of a different type is already in use.

The component GripperA mounted in the Base assembly had an internal reference to component GripperA, but the component GripperA mounted in the User1 assembly had an internal reference to component GripperB. The conflict must be solved at the workspace level, for example, by renaming the mounted component GripperB.

To implement this manual strategy, a link to the component is provided in the internal model (2).

parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_resynchron.title: Synchronize again
parent_eecbase_k_eox_exchange_resynchron.content:
# Synchronize again

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Synchronize again

If a conflict during the import of an EOX file can be solved only at the workspace level, the synchronization process must be aborted. After the conflict resolution, the EOX file must be imported again.

To avoid having to enter all information again in this case, the Synchronize with last configuration  button is used.

After solving the conflict at the workspace level, the following steps must be taken:

    1. Export the relevant assemblies again to an EOX file.

The EOX file must be saved under the same name and in the same folder as before.

    2. Click [Synchronize with last configuration] in the view Synchronization.

The EOX file of the same name is imported again, without launching the import wizard. Then, the new synchronization relations are displayed in the Synchronize view.

parent_eecbase_k_exchange_project_change.title: Exchanging project changes
parent_eecbase_k_exchange_project_change.content:
# Exchanging project changes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exchanging project changes

Project changes can be exported to a PX file (csv format separated with semicolon) or imported from such a file. During the import a project of the same name is created that contains the instances and parameter values of the PX file.

Since the exported file is in the csv format, it is possible to modify the data stored in it (e.g., in Excel) in order to configure with it a new machine or plant solely by importing the modified PX file.

The export can be started in two ways:

     * Exporting via the menu (see [Exporting project changes](eecbase_k_export_project_changes.htm)): File > Export.
     * Exporting via the projectâs context menu: Export project changes.

The import is started via the menu File > Import > Project changes ... (see [Importing project changes](eecbase_k_import_project_changes.htm)).

If after the export of project changes a change is made to the modular system, e.g., renaming library components, this will produce conflicts during the import of the original project changes. These conflicts can be solved manually by updating the PX file to be imported or automatically by means of a plug-in (see [PX update](eecbase_k_px_update.htm)).

parent_eecbase_k_export_project_changes.title: Exporting project changes
parent_eecbase_k_export_project_changes.content:
# Exporting project changes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Exporting project changes

The following sequence describes how to export project changes to a PX file.

    1. Highlight the project whose changes you wish to export.
    2. Select Export project changes from the popup menu.

The Export Project Changes dialog opens.

    1. Enter a file name for the PX file.
    2. If necessary, correct the selection of the project whose changes you want to export.
    3. If necessary, mark the check box Including extension points.

Option Including extension points marked: Components placed on extension points are also exported. The extension points are not updated during the import process. If no components are placed on the extension points, a PX file as small as possible is created. The update of the extension points then has to be executed explicitly.

Option Including extension points not marked: Components placed on extension points are not exported. The extension points are updated during the import process. With this option it is not possible to remove components which are already placed on extension points via PX-Import.

    1. Click [Finish].

Since the export file is in the csv format, it can be opened and modified with any editor, for example, Excel (see the following figure).

The parameter values are stored in the PX file without an annotation, because the annotations lose their validity in a new project anyway. In addition, the readability of the exported data is improved.

parent_eecbase_k_generation_of_project_d.title: Creating project documents
parent_eecbase_k_generation_of_project_d.content:
# Creating project documents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating project documents

Project documents are generated by means of system-specific generators (see [Generate structure](eecbase_k_linking_dcs_generate_structure.htm)). During the generation process, the discipline-specific components are assembled according to their configuration .

Thus, the components list is created and opened:

For created data, a folder with the name of the project is created in the workspace and, below, a folder with the name of the discipline.

The syntax is as follows:
    
        <Installation folder>\workspace\<Project name>\<Discipline name>\<created data>

For the Word tutorial, the created file is stored as follows:
    
        c:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2_4_20141203_2019\workspace\Feeder\Word\ListOfComponents.docx

### Note

It is recommended that you remove files that are no longer required or obsolete versions from the target directory before creating project documents again.

While creating the file with Web EEC it must be ensured, that the created files of different users are stored in accordingly named folders. Therefore during the generation process via Web EEC the folder structure is extended by a further folder, which is given the name of the session ID. The session ID is a sequence of numbers and letters based on the ID of the session on the web server and of an internal EEC session, e.g. 74647372_nf78sdf9s.

The syntax is as follows:
    
        <Installation folder>\workspace\<Project name>\<Session ID>\<Discipline name>\<created Daten>

For the Word tutorial, the created file would be stored as follows (example):
    
        ..\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\2_4_20141203_2019\workspace\Feeder\74647372_nf78sdf9s\Word\ListOfComponents.docx

The actions and commands called within are described in the specific chapters of the respective discipline:

     * [ECAD actions]()
     * [IO generator actions](), [Specific actions for AutomationWorX](), [Specific actions for CoDeSys]() and [Specific actions for STEP 7]()
     * [Text discipline actions]()

parent_eecbase_k_import_excel_csv.title: Importing CSV or Excel files with default schemes
parent_eecbase_k_import_excel_csv.content:
# Importing CSV or Excel files with default schemes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing CSV or Excel files with default schemes

The standard scheme files KeyValue.xsl and Parameter.xsl are provided for importing a CSV or Excel file.

The KeyValue.xsl scheme file is used to store the rows of a CSV or Excel file as a map in the ParameterMap parameter.

Through the Parameter.xsl scheme file the rows of a CSV or Excel file are interpreted as a Parameter name and Parameter value.

In both cases a parameter is not created. Rather a parameter of the same name has to exist in the project that has the value from the import file assigned to it!

Import with KeyValue.xsl scheme file:

### [Example CSV file for the import](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        GripperA;1

    GripperB;2

    GripperC;;

This file is first converted to:
    
        <?xml version="1.0"?>

    <xmlTable>

    	<row number="1">

    		<col number="1">GripperA</col>

    		<col number="2">1</col>

    	</row>

    	<row number="2">

    		<col number="1">GripperB</col>

    		<col number="2">2</col>

    	</row>

    	<row number="3">

    		<col number="1">GripperC</col>

    		<col number="2"></col>

    	</row>

    </xmlTable>

Result in the project:
    
        ParameterMap=Map{Pair{'GripperA','1'},Pair{'GripperB','2'},Pair{'GripperC',''}}

### [Example Excel file for the import](javascript:void\(0\);)

| A | B  
---|---|---  
1 | GripperA | 1  
2 | GripperB | 2  
3 | GripperC |   
  
This file is first converted to:
    
        <?xml version="1.0"?>

    <xmlTable>

    	<sheet name="Table1">

    		<row number="1">

    			<col number="1">GripperA</col>

    			<col number="2">1</col>

    		</row>

    		<row number="2">

    			<col number="1">GripperB</col>

    			<col number="2">2</col>

    		</row>

    		<row number="3">

    			<col number="1">GripperC</col>

    			<col number="2"></col>

    		</row>

    	</sheet>

    </xmlTable>

Result in the project:
    
        ParameterMap=Map{Pair{'GripperA','1'},Pair{'GripperB','2'},Pair{'GripperC',''}}

Import with Parameter.xsl scheme file:

### [Example CSV file for the import](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        GripperA;1

    GripperB;2

    GripperC;;

This file is first converted to:
    
        <?xml version="1.0"?>

    <xmlTable>

    	<row number="1">

    		<col number="1">GripperA</col>

    		<col number="2">1</col>

    	</row>

    	<row number="2">

    		<col number="1">GripperB</col>

    		<col number="2">2</col>

    	</row>

    	<row number="3">

    		<col number="1">GripperC</col>

    		<col number="2"></col>

    	</row>

    </xmlTable>

Result in the project:

Name | Result | Value | Type  
---|---|---|---  
GripperA | 1 | ='1' | String  
GripperB | 2 | ='2' | String  
GripperC |  | ='' | String  
  
### [Example Excel file for the import](javascript:void\(0\);)

| A | B  
---|---|---  
1 | GripperA | 1  
2 | GripperB | 2  
3 | GripperC |   
  
This file is first converted to:
    
        <?xml version="1.0"?>

    <xmlTable>

    	<sheet name="Table1">

    		<row number="1">

    			<col number="1">GripperA</col>

    			<col number="2">1</col>

    		</row>

    		<row number="2">

    			<col number="1">GripperB</col>

    			<col number="2">2</col>

    		</row>

    		<row number="3">

    			<col number="1">GripperC</col>

    			<col number="2"></col>

    		</row>

    	</sheet>

    </xmlTable>

Result in the project:

Name | Result | Value | Type  
---|---|---|---  
GripperA | 1 | ='1' | String  
GripperB | 2 | ='2' | String  
GripperC |  | ='' | String

parent_eecbase_k_import_project_changes.title: Importing project changes
parent_eecbase_k_import_project_changes.content:
# Importing project changes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing project changes

By importing project changes (*.px file), you can extend an existing project with the contents of the project changes.

The result is saved either in the project of the same name or in a new project.

### Note

If the project change (*.px file) contains several projects, the changes cannot be saved in a new project.

### [Importing project changes into an existing project](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Click File > Import.
    2. The Import dialog opens.
    3. Select Project > Configuration Result (PX).
    4. Click [Next >].
    5. The Import project changes dialog opens.
    6. Select the PX file to be imported.
    7. Highlight the project whose changes you wish to import.
    8. Click Finish.
    9. The project catalog then contains the existing project extended with the imported project changes.

### [Importing project changes into a new project](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Click File > Import.
    2. The Import dialog opens.
    3. Select Project > Configuration Result (PX).
    4. Click [Next >].
    5. The Import project changes dialog opens.
    6. Select the PX file to be imported.
    7. Select the option Create project.
    8. Enter a name for the new project.
    9. Click Finish.
    10. The project catalog then contains the new project.

parent_eecbase_k_import_projectdata.title: Importing project data via wizard
parent_eecbase_k_import_projectdata.content:
# Importing project data via wizard

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing project data via wizard

Files in different formats can be imported by using the import wizard.

The following formats are supported:

     * Excel (*.XLS, *.XLSX, *.XLSM)
     * CSV
     * IMX
     * XML

New libraries, components and projects can be added as well as parameter values set through the importing process.

The following figure shows the scheme of the file import and the instantiation of the resulting project:

Depending on the format of the source file, also a scheme file and optionally a preferences file (template) are needed. A file created by the customer, a standard file for key value pairs KeyValue.xsl or a standard file for importing parameter values Parameter.xsl can be selected as the scheme file.

A template file can, for example, contain all the components and parameter values that always exist in a project and the imported file contains all the further components and parameter values for a specific project.

This is how you call the Import wizard:

    1. Select the menu File > Import....

The Select dialog opens.

    2. In the tree structure, select the Project > Import entry.
    3. Click [Next >].

The Import file dialog opens.

    4. Enter the name of the file to be imported in the Source file name field.
    5. Select a standard scheme file from the drop-down list, if appropriate.
    6. If Customer scheme is selected in the drop-down list, specify the name of the scheme file to be used as the basis for importing the source file.
    7. Enter the name of the template file in the Template file name field.
    8. Mark the check box and enter the name of the new project in the Create a new project field, if a new project is to be created with the import of the Excel file. If the name already exists, an index is automatically added to the end of the name (e.g., Project, Project2, Project3, etc.).

The project is now listed in the Project Catalog. Existing projects of the same name are overwritten in the process.

    9. If the check box Create new project is not selected, mark a project into which importing is to be carried out.
    10. Confirm your selection with [Finish].

### Read more

     * [CSV Transformation](refcsv_r_csv_transformation.htm)
     * [Excel-Transformation](refexcel_r_excel_transformation.htm)
     * [XML Transformation](refxml_r_xml_transformation.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_import_projects.title: Importing projects and components
parent_eecbase_k_import_projects.content:
# Importing projects and components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Importing projects and components

It is possible to create new projects or to insert components into existing projects by using commands and an import wizard for different file formats.

In addition parameter values can be set in the imported projects and components.

The commands are available for importing:

     * [Engineering.ForcefulEOXImportCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_forcefuleoximportcommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportCSVCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importcsvcommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportExcelCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importexcelcommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportIMXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importimxcommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportIMXWithTemplateCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importimxwithtemplatecommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportPXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importpxcommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportLibrariesCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importlibrariescommand.htm)
     * [Engineering.ImportXMLCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importxmlcommand.htm)

More:

[Importing project data via wizard](eecbase_k_import_projectdata.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_drag_dro.title: Inserting via DragÂ \u0026Â Drop
parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_drag_dro.content:
# Inserting via DragÂ \u0026Â Drop

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting via Drag & Drop

Mechatronic components are inserted in the project via Drag & Drop from the library, depending on the initial situation, by placing the component either on the project, the root of the mechatronic components or a placeholder.

### Note

The selection formula for placeholders and extension points is calculated only for the selection dialog, but not during the generation and not during the instantiation via Drag & Drop. If the instantiated component contains further extension points with selection formulas, these will be evaluated.

parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_popup.title: Inserting via the context menu
parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_popup.content:
# Inserting via the context menu

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting via the context menu

Empty project:

Using the context menu of the empty project, the menu item Add > Mechatronic can be selected. Here, the possible levels are shown of the type with which a mechatronic component can be inserted. For example, in the Feeder Tutorial project, the Station level is defined. See figure:

The Finder for mechatronic components appears. This allows for the desired components to be found and inserted. Several components can be selected.

Inserting with given mechatronic structure:

If a mechatronic structure is already in place, the Mechatronic object represents the root of all mechatronic components. New mechatronic components are inserted via the context menu of the Mechatronic object.

The appropriate mechatronic component is selected in the context menu via the Add menu item:

The mechatronic components Finder opens; see the previous figure.

parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_view_com.title: Inserting via the Component Selector view
parent_eecbase_k_insert_mcs_via_view_com.content:
# Inserting via the Component Selector view

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting via the Component Selector view

In the Component Selector view, all components (2) are listed that are derived from an abstract library component (1). If the abstract component has parameters, their values (3) will be shown to the right of the names of the derived components. The selection of components can be limited by enabling the filter function (4). An input line appears (5) where one filter value per column can be entered. Using Drag & Drop, the listed components can be integrated into the project structure (6) in the Project Explorer as well as into a Form-UI (7).

parent_eecbase_k_libraries.title: Creating libraries
parent_eecbase_k_libraries.content:
# Creating libraries

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating libraries

The new library is created in the library catalog.

As a rule libraries contain specific objects, for example architecture components. Specific objects are derived from system objects. To this purpose the matching system library has to be imported into the newly created library.

To create a new library:

    1. Click  (New library).

The wizard for creating libraries opens.

    1. Enter a name for the new library.
    2. Optionally mark all system libraries.
    3. Optionally mark one or more favorite system libraries.
    4. Optionally mark one or more specific system libraries.
    5. Optionally mark all self-created libraries.
    6. Optionally mark one or more self-created libraries.

By selecting a self-created library all libraries depending on it are also marked.

    1. Click [OK].

The new library is subsequently listed in the library catalog.

### Read more

     * [Perspectives](gui_k_perspectives.htm)
     * [System libraries](eecbase_k_methodology_system_libraries.htm)
     * [Preferences for favorites](admin_r_prefs_favoriten.htm)
     * [Decorators for labelings](admin_r_prefs_label_decorators.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_libraries_creating_proj.title: Creating Projects
parent_eecbase_k_libraries_creating_proj.content:
# Creating Projects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating Projects

New assemblies, i.e., libraries or projects, are created in the library and / or project catalog.

Right-click to open the popup menu in the project catalog. Here, select New > Project. Alternatively, it is also possible to use the corresponding symbol: 

The project wizard appears for the creation of a new project. In the wizard, it is possible to select the name of the project and the libraries to be imported, etc. Alternatively, one can also define new projects using the project editor.

### Note

The name of a new object should not contain a special character (){}[].:,;-/\Â§$%&#~<>|= so that on the one hand it is file system-compliant and on the other hand no problems occur during the creation of formulas.

The project editor has three entries in its editor menu. By default, the Attributes editor page is open. Here, the name of the project must be entered. This can be chosen freely, but there must not be any duplicates. Each project must have a unique name. The name is applied by saving the editor.

parent_eecbase_k_libraries_import.title: Importing libraries
parent_eecbase_k_libraries_import.content:
# Importing libraries

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing libraries

Libraries can import further libraries. This way, the necessary libraries for a modular system can be selected according to the task at hand. To do this, open the library and, using the  button, open the Imported Libraries editor page.

The icon  (Add library) is used to start the Library finder dialog. The libraries to be integrated can now be selected. Once the selection is complete, the relations between the libraries are represented graphically.

In the following example, a new library with the name T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem is created, and the libraries T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Step7_Ui are imported:

The  (Hiding assemblies which are not directly imported) icon allows you to only show the directly imported assemblies.

parent_eecbase_k_libraries_importing_lib.title: Importing libraries into projects
parent_eecbase_k_libraries_importing_lib.content:
# Importing libraries into projects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Importing libraries into projects

The assignment of libraries to be imported is realized on the editor page Imported libraries.

The icon  (Add library) is used to start the Library finder dialog. The libraries to be integrated can now be selected. Once the selection is complete, the relationships of the Assemblies (projects and libraries) are displayed in the Imported libraries view.

The dependencies of Assemblies (projects and libraries) are represented clearly in the Imported libraries view. See figure:

In the process, all necessary system libraries are integrated.

The  (Hiding assemblies which are not directly imported) icon allows you to only show the directly imported assemblies.

parent_eecbase_k_libraries_open_projects.title: Opening projects in the project perspective
parent_eecbase_k_libraries_open_projects.content:
# Opening projects in the project perspective

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Opening projects in the project perspective

There are several ways of opening a project in the project perspective. This is described in detail in the section. One option is to open the popup menu of the project and select the menu item Open Project Perspective > In new window.

parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs.title: Link between mechatronic and discipline-specific components
parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs.content:
# Link between mechatronic and discipline-specific components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Link between mechatronic and discipline-specific components

Discipline-specific components can be linked to mechatronic components. The mechanism corresponds to the nesting of mechatronic components. For this purpose, a mechatronic component is assigned a discipline-specific component.

This is done on the library component:

The above figure shows how the discipline-specific component Sensors_Actuators_Body (2) is integrated into the mechatronic component Feeder (1) via Drag & Drop.

Thus, every Feeder station supplies its own components list in the case of a configuration:

parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs_configurati.title: Configurations
parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs_configurati.content:
# Configurations

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Configurations

The configuration of the discipline-specific components is similar to that of mechatronic components. The configuration of the components list is illustrated by the tutorial model.

In the Feeder station, the body (Sensors_Actuators_Body) and the chapters (Actuators_Chapter and Sensors_Chapter of the components list (ListOfComponents have already been integrated:

The Heading parameter is specified here accordingly as a fixed value and applied to the ListOfComponents document during the generation. This value could also be calculated using a formula.

parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs_generate_st.title: Generate structure
parent_eecbase_k_linking_dcs_generate_st.content:
# Generate structure

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Structure Generating Discipline structure Generating

## Generate structure

In a complete mechatronic project configuration, the discipline-specific components are found in different locations of the configuration. For example, in the case of the ListOfComponents discipline, the document body is integrated directly at the Feeder station; see the following figure.

Individual fragments, for example, Sensors_Actuators_Line, are found in the mechatronic configuration at the corresponding components â in this case, sensors and actuators.

By using the structure generator, it is possible to isolate the discipline-specific structure and to generate it according to the configuration of the project and the corresponding discipline. The created structure serves as a basis for creating the actual components list in the text format (project document). The structure generator of the text discipline is started via the Mechatronic context menu:

The result of the structure generator is the specific structure of the respective discipline. This is represented in the Project Catalog at the bottom end:

It is possible to navigate between the components in the project and the created structure. For navigating from the created structure to the Mechatronic, one selects, within the BOM structure, in the context menu of an object Go to... > Mechatronic (Go to... > Mechatronic):

This way, for example, the sensor-actuator line of the ListOfComponents is displayed in the Mechatronic:

The reverse navigation is also possible. By selecting a discipline-specific component in the project, one can navigate to the corresponding component in the created structure:

parent_eecbase_k_maintenance.title: Maintenance of model data
parent_eecbase_k_maintenance.content:
# Maintenance of model data

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Maintenance of model data

## [Set mechatronic root names to current locale](javascript:void\(0\);)

Replaces the names of all MechatronicRoot objects of a model with the language currently set.

The name of the mechatronic in the project can be changed individually. The action Model > Maintenance > Set mechatronic root names to current locale sets the names of all MechatronicRoot objects of a model to the translation of âMechatronicâ in the language currently set.

The following figure shows the name of a MechatronicRoot before (left) and after (right) executing this action.

## [Set Project Library Names To Current Locale](javascript:void\(0\);)

Sets the names of all project libraries of a model to the translation in the language currently set.

The following figure shows the name of a project library before (left) and after (right) executing this action.

## [Maintain copy sources](javascript:void\(0\);)

When copying a class, the original is noted in the copy. Similar to the absolute names in formulas, this is immune to move operations. The copy source remains valid even after deletion or creation with the same name, but different class.

This action updates the copy sources and deletes notes on invalid classes.

## [Maintain absolute type names in formulas](javascript:void\(0\);)

Using this action, all formulas are updated where absolute names of components are used.

Absolute names of components in formulas (e.g., when using rmos, mos, type, ref, etc.) are typically immune to move operations of the objects referenced.

But this mechanism may fail as a result of operations such as deletion or new creation with the same name. To prevent this, this action can be executed where indicated so as to update all formulas.

Prior to any model export to an EOX file, it is recommended that the absolute names of components in formulas be updated.

## [Convert object names file system compliant...](javascript:void\(0\);)

The names of objects allow characters which are not file system compliant. Due to this action the names of all objects of the model are changed in a way that they correspond to the conventions of the file system.

## [Check Native Method Contributions](javascript:void\(0\);)

If native methods were developed for the current model, you can check with this function whether the plugin is loaded and the implementation of the native method is available.

parent_eecbase_k_management_of_assemblie.title: Management of assemblies
parent_eecbase_k_management_of_assemblie.content:
# Management of assemblies

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Management of assemblies

The content of the modular system being created is stored in a library. The objects, components and parameters of a modular system are stored in such a library.

Depending on the functional scope and desired disciplines, further system libraries have to be added. Assembly is the term given to a configuration of system, modular system and project libraries.

parent_eecbase_k_mediasource.title: Media sources
parent_eecbase_k_mediasource.content:
# Media sources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Media sources

The system library MediaSource allows for the internalization of media data in a model, which makes references to externally stored media data unnecessary. Due to the internalization of media data, it is no longer necessary to pass them on additionally to an EOX file and / or to back them up.

Currently, these media formats (image formats) are supported: JPG, GIF (not animated), PNG, BMP, and ICO.

The resource folder for media sources can be specified in the settings (see the following image). If stated as a relative path, relative to the installation folder of EEC or to the value of the variable, the resource folder is de.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath (see [Java Virtual Machine Arguments (VMArgs)]()). As well, an absolute path to another resource folder can be used.

With the model variable Disciplines > Media sources > Store resources), you can specify globally whether new media source resources are to be internalized directly. By default newly added resources are not stored (see [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_mediasources_resources.htm)).

Images (Image), image registers (Image register), and image register extensions (Image register extension) can be created in a library.

In analogy to other source files, image objects can be created via mass import.

parent_eecbase_k_mediasource_image_objec.title: Image objects
parent_eecbase_k_mediasource_image_objec.content:
# Image objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Image objects

The MediaSource library allows the creation of image objects. A MediaSource image object manages an image and enables the internalization of the image in the model. This neutralizes the reference to objects in the file system, and the backing up, restoration and replacement of models are simplified. The MediaSource image object is upstream from the actual image and enables uniform usage in EEC in analogy to other model objects. To access the image thus stored, the image object has a preview page in the editor, as well as two methods for the formula language (see [Image object methods](refformulas_r_image_objects_methods.htm) and [Image register methodss](refformulas_r_image_register_methods.htm)).

The imported and/or referenced image is loaded from the image object using the image method. An inactive image can be represented using the method disabledImage. This way, the image is changed in color prior to loading. By converting the colors to grayscale, the graphical effect is one of an inactive button. The imported image itself is not modified by these changes in color and contrast.

To insert a new image in the library, select New > Media sources > Image in the context menu.

If the model variable MediaSources â Manage Resources has the value false:

On the Attributes page of the editor for the image (see figure above), use [Import], or in the Resource path field [..] to (1) create a reference to the external resource at first. Then use the Internalize button (2) to internalize the image in the library (also see [Resources and their management](eecbase_k_resources_and_their_management.htm)).

If the model variable MediaSources â Internalize Resources has the value true:

On the Attributes page of the editor for the image, use [Import], or in the Resource path field, [...] to internalize the image directly in the library.

An internalized image can be converted to a reference to an external file by way of [Externalize]. Use [Internalize] to convert a reference to an external image to an internalized image.

An internalized image can be viewed on the Preview page of the editor.

parent_eecbase_k_mediasource_image_regis.title: Image register and image-register extension
parent_eecbase_k_mediasource_image_regis.content:
# Image register and image-register extension

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Image register and image-register extension

The new MediaSource system library allows the flexible referencing of images through the image register.

The image register is the assignment of components to an image object each for the library component and the project component (see the following figure). This property is implemented in all places where objects are given a graphical representation. The assignment for an image object of the project components is optional. If no image object is specified, the register uses the same image objects for library and project components.

An example of the usage of image registers is the placing of objects in diagrams. By assigning the object class to a certain image (and the configuration of the diagram in order to use the image register), all elements of the same type can be assigned to a uniform image. This assignment can also be modified centrally in the image register, which then applies to all diagrams that use this image register.

In order to access the assignment table of an image register, several methods have been added to the formula language. Like image objects, the image register, too, offers the methods "image" and "disabledImage". But while a parameter is not needed for an image object, a key object must be specified for the image register. Then, the register searches the assignment table for an entry for the class of the key object and / or the key object itself, if it is already a class object. If the register does not find an assignment, it will look for one for the supertype of the class. This will be repeated until no further supertype is available or an image has been found. If no image is found for the top supertype either, the method will return <<null>>.

If an assignment is found, the register will distinguish between two cases: the key object originates from the class level, or it comes from the instance level. If the former is true, the register loads the image of the assigned image object for the class level. If the key object comes from the instance level, the register will check whether the assignment found provides an image for the project component. If this is true, the image of the image object assigned there will be loaded. Otherwise, the image object of the library component will be used.

Problem:

Since the image registers lie in libraries, it is possible with more complex models that a change to an image register is not possible, not desired or not useful. Possibly individual images cannot be assigned unambiguously, which would render a central image register impossible. If this affects only part of the images, the constant component of the images assigned would have to be entered and managed several times.

Solution:

Using image register extensions, it is possible to extend the assignment table of registers dynamically (see the following figure).

To extend a register, create a new image register extension. The extension allows for the same assignment of classes to image objects as the image register. The register to be extended is selected in the editor for the extension. This way, all entries in the assignment table of the register extension are assigned to those of the register. The extension becomes noticeable in the evaluation of an image request for a class.

Now, the evaluation does not merely search the image register, but checks the assignment tables of the register extensions first. If the search of extensions finds an image, that image will be used. If none of the extensions defines an image, the search is run on the image register itself. This order creates the possibility of overwriting the assignments of the image register by means of extensions.

This functionality lies fully within the image register. Even when register extensions are used, all requests are directed to the register. The image register searches for existing extensions automatically. A further distinction in the formulas is not necessary.

With more complex models, it may happen that a register is extended by several register extensions. These extensions may be located in different libraries. If a project is searched for an assignment, the register must not search each individual extension. The project may possibly not import every library that contains an extension for the register. From the point of view of the project, therefore, the request must be handled as if the non-visible extensions did not exist.

The key object serves as orientation for the register in this context. The register checks which libraries are visible from the key object. All extensions that are in libraries outside of this view will be ignored for the request.

Depending on the origin of the key object, the view becomes too limited. If the key object comes from a modular system library, register extensions will not be visible from the project library. In such cases, an extended image and / or disabledImage method can be used. This will adopt two parameters. The first parameter is a "context object"; the second parameter, the already known key object. If the key originates in an imported library, a context object can be transferred to determine the extensions in visible libraries.

Special case:

If there are several extensions eligible for assignment in the search of extensions, the import order in the context library / context project is used. If two extensions are located in the same library and define different images for the same object, the entry used will be selected randomly. The extensions in the library are stored in the data model in an unsorted order. Such an unclear assignment does not trigger an error in the model validation, but may cause behavior that is not immediately comprehensible in terms of the runtime.

Create a new image register:

To insert a new image register in the library, select New > Media sources > Image register in the popup menu.

The new image register is created without data at first. On the Image assignment page of the editor (see the following figure), the user selects for an object type (1) the images for the library component (2) and the project component (3):

Re-create the image register extension:

An image register can be extended with an image register extension. To implement a new image register extension, select New > Media sources > Image register extension in the popup menu.

On the Attributes page, select the image register to be extended:

On the Image assignment page, the user then selects for an object type (1) the images for the library component (2) and the project component (3).

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_action.title: Actions
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_action.content:
# Actions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Actions

Actions connect the user interface (UI) to the application logic

The Action system object is the superclass for all future options that will be available when calling application-logic commands from the user interface.

Thus far, the types provided by the system are [SelectionActions](eecbase_k_methodology_selectionaction.htm), which always require selecting the object or objects for which an action is to be carried out.

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_command.title: Commands
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_command.content:
# Commands

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Commands

Commands are objects that have exactly one method, called an execute method. This way, a sequence can be called from methods by compiling a list of commands. Such a list is used, for example, in connection with [SequenceSelectionActions](eecbase_k_methodology_sequenceselectionaction.htm).

The plug-ins of the system provide commands via system libraries:

Management tasks, for example, enabling and disabling the formula cache (Start/StopFormulaCacheCommand) or saving of changes (SaveObjectCommand, are provided as atomic command operations. These are used to compile a sequence to automate a workflow (also see [Commands](refcommands_r_commands.htm)).

This fine-granular division into commands makes sense so as to provide options for performance optimization (e.g., use of an active formula cache for several commands, etc.).

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_interface.title: Interface
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_interface.content:
# Interface

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Interface

In modern programming languages (Java, C#), there is a concept known as interfaces. Its purpose is to formally specify the interfaces of a component independently of its implementation. Components can implement several interfaces, which makes multiple interface inheritance possible.

The inheritance concept itself already allows for parameters of components to be standardized (the AbstractStation component of the Pfuderer model, for example, defines the parameters StationNumber, Location, Plant, and FunctionText, which are inherited by the components Inserting, Magazine, etc.) But it is both the interface (selection of parameters) and implementation (formulas, methods) that are inherited by child classes.

Thanks to the interface concept, it is now possible to define interfaces and / or interface hierarchies independently of their implementation by way of components.

the ILocation interface represented in the graphic at first defines, in an implementation-neutral manner, that a component that implements this interface must have the parameters Place and StationNumber. For example, this interface can then be assigned to the components AbstractStation and TurningPlate.

This example shows that interfaces can be used to define additional classifications: Using the ILocation interface, all components are selected that have the required parameters for sufficiently specifying the location, Place and StationNumber.

Using interfaces

     * Fieldbus linking:

So far, the algorithm for fieldbus linking has been able to detect control components of a configuration only indirectly on the basis of their containing control hardware components of a PLC discipline.

The current implementation of fieldbus linking now defines interfaces (IController, ICoupler, ITerminal) that can be used to highlight control components explicitly (Marker Interfaces).

     * Better performing formula requests:

rmos requests can generally be accelerated considerably through selective searches for a type and the definition of an rmos index.

But often it is not a specific type that is looked for, but, for example, all objects with the parameters A and B. Before these requests were formulated with a general rmos request, followed by a select Statement, which required a lot of computing capacity.

Here, interfaces allow for an additional classification and thus a better performing request: An interface is defined with parameters A and B, and assigned to all relevant component classes. The rmos request is now limited by means of the interface, instead of a type. An index can also be defined for interfaces.

Overall, interfaces allow for better, more expressive modeling. As the screenshot shows, interfaces can be extended (inherited), which means that interfaces hierarchies can be defined.

Note:

Similar to the Eclipse programming standard, we always precede interfaces with a capital âIâ.

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_methods.title: Methods
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_methods.content:
# Methods

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Methods

In analogy to the object-based programming languages, it is possible to define for any objects not only parameters, but also methods.

Methods have

     * a name (e.g., execute),
     * arguments (e.g., List selectedEOS), as well as
     * a return type (e.g., Engineering.Object or no return type).
