Generally, methods are used to navigate or modify model objects. These objects are known as EngineeringObject (EO). The EOs relevant to a method are typically selected by the user in a view. Frequently, these selected EOs are transferred as a list (selectedEOs, see screenshot).

A method can be implemented in Groovy (script), Native Java (plug-in) or through a sequence:

     * Groovy:

Due to the object-based integration of scripts into the application model, there are approved management options for scripts (see [SCRIPTING](refscripting_r_scripting.htm)).

     * Native Java:

Fully typed and with the option of remote debugging (see [SCRIPTING](refscripting_r_scripting.htm))

     * Sequence:

Table-format specification of a sequence (see [SequenceSelectionActions](eecbase_k_methodology_sequenceselectionaction.htm)).

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_new_system_.title: Overview of newer system objects
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_new_system_.content:
# Overview of newer system objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Overview of newer system objects

The following figure shows the revised basic structure of the application model:

The following table lists the new system objects, as well as the motivation for extensions.

System objects | Motivation  
---|---  
Interfaces | Better, higher-performance models by separating interface specification and implementation  
     * Action
     * SelectionAction
     * SequenceSelectionAction
     * Command
     * Root
     * DisciplineRoot
     * MechatronicRoot
| Flexible configuration of the user interface (context menus, buttons)  
  
The new system objects are explained in detail below.

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_roots.title: Roots
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_roots.content:
# Roots

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Roots

When creating a project configuration or creating a structure, roots are created automatically below which the objects of the model will be inserted.

These roots are defined as objects in the system libraries, so that they can be assigned actions:

     * DisciplineRoot
     * MechatronicRoot

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_selectionac.title: SelectionActions
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_selectionac.content:
# SelectionActions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## SelectionActions

A SelectionAction takes a set of selected objects (selectedEOs, usually a multi-selection) and transfers it to its internal run method

SelectionActions have the following attributes:

     * Image: The symbol here is displayed both in the context menu and as a button in the toolbar if the option Project explorer toolbar button is enabled.
     * Selected object: Object within the classification hierarchy of the application model for which the action can be executed. For example, if RTS24 of the Pfuderer model is selected, the action will be displayed only in the context menu for RTS24 objects (classes / instances).
     * Visible in library/Visible in projects: Specifies whether the action for classes (library) or instances (projects), or both, can be executed, for example, only for RTS24 instances in project configurations
     * Number of selected objects: Specifies the number of objects that can / must be selected so that the action can be executed in a meaningful manner. Examples include 1, 1..2, 3..6, + (=1..n)

Note: This specification is derived, for example, from boundary conditions of the implementation of the run method. This may be set up only for the processing of one object.

     * Popup path: Defines the path to the action within the context menu. The individual path entries are separated by a forward slash. For example, menu/sub menu/create.
     * Displayed Name: Action name displayed in the context menu.
     * Occurs In Project Toolbar: A button is added to the toolbar in the Project Explorer view, which is used to start the action.

Note: To make this change visible, the view must be closed and re-opened (or EEC must be restarted).

Finding SelectionActions:

When adjusting an pop-up structure that consists of many actions, it is important to ensure that it is possible to navigate from a pop-up entry to the corresponding action. This can be achieved by executing the action via the context menu, while keeping the [CTRL] key pressed.

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_sequencesel.title: SequenceSelectionActions
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_sequencesel.content:
# SequenceSelectionActions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## SequenceSelectionActions

SelectionActions can be adjusted in any manner via scripts (see [SCRIPTING]()). But customizing the UI does require scripting expertise in this case. Scripts also have the disadvantage that the script code specifies when to call which commands, for example. The connection between actions and commands is not modeled, and therefore cannot be navigated either.

For the definition of simple actions, the system object SequenceSelectionAction has been defined. It allows for the specification in table form of a sequence of [Commands](refcommands_r_commands.htm).

The connections between actions and commands are modeled for SequenceSelectionAction, i.e., it is possible, for example, to use standard mechanisms ([Alt] \+ mouse) to navigate from the table with commands (see the above figure) directly to the commands.

When a SequenceSelectionAction is executed, a script is created automatically from the modeled sequence of commands in the background, and then executed as a general SelectionAction.

The command [Engineering.AbortExecutionIfErrorCommand]() is inserted in order to, in the event of an error that occurs during the execution of a command, to end the execution of the following commands.

parent_eecbase_k_methodology_system_libr.title: System libraries
parent_eecbase_k_methodology_system_libr.content:
# System libraries

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## System libraries

System libraries contain specific objects from which objects are derived into a new library.

The following libraries are available:

System library | Description  
---|---  
AutomationWorX | Specific PLC objects for AutomationWorX  
CoDeSys | Specific PLC objects for CoDeSys  
DataSource | Objects for data sources  
Document | Objects for documents  
ECAD | Objects for schematics  
ECAD3D | Objects for 3D panel layouts  
Engineering | Base objects for mechatronics modular systems  
Graph2D | Objects for the graphical configuration  
JobServer | Objects for Job Server  
MediaSource | Objects for images and their management  
Office | Generally valid objects for Microsoft Office documents  
PLC | Generally valid PLC objects  
STEP 7 | Specific PLC objects for STEP 7  
TextDiscipline | Objects for text documents  
Word | Objects for Microsoft Word documents

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Navigation and search in the model
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Navigation and search in the model

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Navigation and search in the model

To navigate within an assembly, one clicks on the object while keeping the [Alt] key pressed. Similar to hyperlinks, the option for navigating can be recognized by the fact that the mouse pointer changes from an arrow to a hand symbol (see the following figure).

The following navigation options exist:

     * From project component to library component.
     * From parameter used to parameter definition.
     * From object in formula result to object definition.

If the formula result is a collection of objects, editors will be opened for all objects.

The following figure shows an example of navigation from a project component to a library component.

EEC comes with a search function. The search comprises the following search functions:

     * [Text search](eecbase_k_model_navigation_search_fulltextsearch.htm)
     * [Search for failed formula results](eecbase_k_model_navigation_search_errorformulasearch.htm)
     * [Formula search](eecbase_k_model_navigation_search_formulasearch.htm)
     * [Disabler Search Page](eecbase_k_model_navigation_search_disablersearch.htm)
     * File search
     * [Unused objects search](eecbase_k_model_navigation_search_unusedobjectssearch.htm)

The text search can take place globally via the  button in the toolbar or via the menu Search > Text search.

The text search can also be started using the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [H].

Alternatively to the global search, a targeted search in selected assemblies or objects is also possible via the popup menu in the Explorer view.

Depending on whether a search is started globally or based on a selection, the search takes place across the entire Workspace or is limited to Selected objects). In addition the search can be limited to parameters, interfaces, mechatronic components, discipline components or a combination of these.

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Disabler Search Page
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Disabler Search Page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Disabler Search Page

The Disabler search can be used to look for formulas in Disabler conditions.

The Disabler search delivers more specific results than the normal formula search. The representation of the results is identical.

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Search for failed formula results
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Search for failed formula results

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Search for failed formula results

The search for formulas that do not deliver an error-free result by default refers to the entire workspace or the previously selected objects in the Project catalog.

In addition the search can be restricted to Parameters and/or Disabler and/or Extension points.

The result is displayed in a table. All of the objects contained in it can be opened by double-clicking or using the Open popup menu entry. Marked objects are deleted from the result list and the model via the [Del] key or the popup menu entry Remove Selected Match. All of the matches are removed from the result list and the model with the Remove All Matches popup menu entry.

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Formula search
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Formula search

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Formula search

A frequent search query refers specifically to formulas. For example, the user looks for all formulas that contain the mc command.

Beyond the text search, this search supports the more specific search for formulas:

Unlike the text search, the result table of the formula search shows the parameter (Declared In) and the component that contain the formula:

The result is displayed in a table. All of the objects contained in it can be opened by double-clicking or using the Open popup menu entry. Marked objects are deleted from the result list and the model via the [Del] key or the popup menu entry Remove Selected Match. All of the matches are removed from the result list and the model with the Remove All Matches popup menu entry.

The Search/replace... function can be selected from the popup menu.

The Search/replace function encompasses the usual possibilities for carrying out changes in the currently determined formulas.

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Text search
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Text search

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Text search

The text search looks for objects in the entire model by default that contain the text. The search range can be limited by the object type in order to search, for example, only in parameters or ECAD components. In addition, the result can be reduced through categorization.

The following figure shows a search in all libraries of the tutorial model for the object type TextDisciplineComponent using the search text sensor:

For example, this search yields the following result:

The result is displayed in a table. All of the objects contained in it can be opened by double-clicking or using the Open popup menu entry. Marked objects are deleted from the result list and the model via the [Del] key or the popup menu entry Remove Selected Match. All of the matches are removed from the result list and the model with the Remove All Matches popup menu entry.

parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.title: Unused objects search
parent_eecbase_k_model_navigation_search.content:
# Unused objects search

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Unused objects search

It is a specific requirement for modular system developers to identify objects that cannot be installed in the model on the basis of the mechatronic level configuration and pre-instantiated components and extension points. Projects and instances contained in the model are not captured by the search. When a component is identified as installable, its supertypes, parameters and interfaces implemented by it will also be identified as installable.

### Note

Note that this is only about the possibility of installing a component, rather than whether or not it is actually installed.

The present model can be searched only if it is valid, as invalid models lack important connections, which causes objects to be recognized, falsely, as not being usable. This is why the model is validated first prior to a search. If validation finds an error, the search process is terminated. This does not include warnings about missing resources.

The search starts with the mechatronic components of the top level, as these can generally be installed. Then, the extension points and pre-instantiated components are parsed.

Objects that use each other, without one of them being determined to be mountable, are deemed unused objects.

Furthermore, the search excludes objects

     * that have class methods that were not defined in system libraries;
     * that are created as abstract components, but do not contain their own or inherited class methods, nor any specific and used subtypes, and that are not used for type limitation in connection with extension points.

The search for unused objects includes parameters, interfaces, mechatronic components and discipline components.

The search can be canceled at any time, however, no partial result will be displayed in this case.

The result is displayed in a table. All of the objects contained in it can be opened by double-clicking or using the Open popup menu entry. Marked objects are deleted from the result list and the model via the [Del] key or the popup menu entry Remove Selected Match. All of the matches are removed from the result list and the model with the Remove All Matches popup menu entry.

In the Installed column, the display shows whether an unused parameter is installed or not, with Yes and No.

Example table:

Name | Absolute name | Installed | Comment  
---|---|---|---  
separateParameter | T_ECAD_P8.ECAD.Parameter | No | non-installed and unused parameter  
installedParameter | T_ECAD_P8.ECAD.Parameter | Yes | installed but unused parameter  
  
Double-clicking the name of a parameter opens it in the editor.

parent_eecbase_k_nesting_mechatronic_com.title: Nesting of mechatronic components
parent_eecbase_k_nesting_mechatronic_com.content:
# Nesting of mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Nesting of mechatronic components

Generally, there are recurring configurations in a project. To minimize the configuration work, closed units can be formed. These consist of a component in which further components are installed (pre-initiated). By means of Disablers the installed components can be deactivated, depending on the type of the project.

Mechatronical components have the Components field below the editor menu.

With  (Add component) a component, a placeholder or an extension point is added.

With  (Duplicate component) a selected installed component is duplicated.

With  (Replace component) a selected installed component is replaced with another component.

The following figure shows that in the mechatronic component Axis the components Cylinder, Valve, Position_1, Position_2, Position_3, and Position_4 are installed (pre-initiated).

If you install this Axis component in accordance with the figure above, this results in the following structure:

parent_eecbase_k_open_user_administratio.title: Open user management
parent_eecbase_k_open_user_administratio.content:
# Open user management

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Open user management

User management is done in the User management (authorization) perspective:

The system has pre-defined the user roles Administrators, Developer, and Configurator, as well as the user Administrator. The Administrator always has all rights.

parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing.title: Parameterization
parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing.content:
# Parameterization

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterization

Mechatronic components have properties. Examples of this are Power, Revolutions, Variant xy available, DT or PLC address. Parameters are used to describe these properties of a component. Parameters are used to realize specific variants of components. Parameters are used in a large number of components with the same meaning.

So that parameter names, their type and other properties can be defined across components, a separate object is created for each parameter:

parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_discipli.title: Parameterization of discipline components
parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_discipli.content:
# Parameterization of discipline components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterization of discipline components

Using discipline-specific components, project-specific parameters can be created in the project documents. In the following figure, the $ProjectID parameter exists in the resource as a placeholder. During the generation, this will be replaced by the specific project parameter. The project parameter is set on the Parameters editor page.

Parameterization is carried out via the Parameters editor page. Apart from its type property, each parameter also has the fields Value, Default, Standardized, Type, Declared in and Comment:

The Value field describes the value of the parameter that the field is to assume when the component is integrated into a project. This value is assigned as a default value.

The Default field in this specific case is not filled. Its function is explained in Advanced Workflows.

The Standardized field is to tell the user in the project whether this parameter is to be configured by him or her or whether it should not be edited. The Standardized field can be filled with two values, true and false. If a parameter is standardized, the parameter is grayed out in the project. But it can still be overwritten.

The Type field identifies the type of the parameter.

The Declared in field shows the object in which the parameter is declared.

In the Comment field, a comment can be entered on the use or meaning of the parameter.

If a component, parameterized in the library, is integrated, the contents in the Value field provide the default values.

parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_library_.title: Parameterizing of library components
parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_library_.content:
# Parameterizing of library components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing of library components

Parameterization is carried out via the Parameters editor page. Apart from its type property, each parameter also has the fields Value, Default, Standardized, Type, Declared in, and Comment:

The Value field describes the value of the parameter that the field is to assume when the component is integrated into a project. This value is assigned as a default value.

The Default field in this specific case is not filled.

The Standardized field is to tell the user in the project whether this parameter is to be configured by him or her or whether it should not be edited. The Standardized field can be filled with two values, true and false. If a parameter is standardized, the parameter is grayed out in the project. But it can still be overwritten.

The Type field identifies the type of the parameter. The Declared in field shows the object in which the parameter is declared.

In the Comment field, a comment can be entered on the usage or meaning of the parameter.

If a component, parameterized in the library, is integrated, the contents in the Value field provide the default values.

parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_project_.title: Parameterizing of project components
parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_project_.content:
# Parameterizing of project components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing of project components

Project components can be parameterized to library components according to the definition, i.e., parameter values can be assigned. The 'Floor' component added for an IT_SolutionMap _Floor_ already has a number of parameter values. These are values already assigned in the library. They are imported into the Default field as default settings:

The Parameter editor page of a project component is structured in a way similar to the editor page of the library component. In additional, there is a further field Result. The result is calculated from the Value field. If it is empty, the value is taken from the Default field.

Project-specific parameter values can be assigned in the Value field. Any existing default values will be overwritten. The result will be adjusted accordingly. For example, if the floor number is given as A 0, but A 4 is desired, A 4 will be entered in the Value field of the corresponding parameter. This can be seen in the above figure.

Thus, the default value of the Number parameter is overwritten, and represented in the Result field.

Parameter values can also be calculated using formulas. This way, no manual entry would be required. In this case formulas are entered in the Value field, and the result is calculated automatically.

The precise usage of formulas and their syntax are described in the section [Formula language]().

parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_with_for.title: Parameterization with formulas
parent_eecbase_k_parameterizing_with_for.content:
# Parameterization with formulas

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterization with formulas

The use of formulas in library components allows for variants of components in the project to be calculated.

The formula is created directly in the parameter editor, in the Value column. When creating extensive formulas, it is recommended that the Formula editor view and / or modal formula editor be used. The modal formula editor is started by using the Popup menu > Open Formula editor. Both the Formula editor view as well as the modal formula editor support the user through syntax highlighting and Content Assist.

Related bracket pairs for the bracket types [ ], { } und ( ) are highlighted in the formula editor. To this purpose the cursor must be positioned in the formula editor after an opening or closing bracket in order to highlight the opposite bracket with a frame.

Only in the modal formula editor can you use the shortcut key Ctrl + F or use the Popup menu > Search/replace to search for a specific term and replace it with another term.

Using the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [Z], the last entry can be undone (UNDO), and restored (REDO) using the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [Y].

To develop a formula which delivers the expected result, you can proceed as follows:

    1. Open the project component, for which the formula is to be developed.
    2. Select the Value field of the corresponding parameter.
    3. Use the Formula editor for the development of the formula.

The result of the formula can be checked in the adjacent field Result.

If the calculated result corresponds to expectations, the formula is transferred to the library component using Popup menu > Move Formula to Library Component.

If it is a formula in a pre-instantiated library component, the formula is transferred into the superordinary library component using Popup menu > Move formula to Superordinary Library Component. In this case an interface formula (generally =mc.$<Parameter name>) is inserted in the parameter of the installed library component using Popup menu > Insert Interface Formula to Installed Library Component.

If it is a formula in a pre-instantiated project component, the formula is transfered into the installed library component using Popup menu > Move Formula to installed Library Component.

### Note

The Parameter editor in the project component must be saved to apply the changes and be able to see the effects.

With the  button you can stop and start the calculation of the formulas for mechatronic project components.

If the formula calculation is switched off, the renewed calculation of all parameters is not executed in the editor when a formula is being edited. The results already calculated are used for the calculation of the edited formula.

### Note

The  button is only displayed when the [de.eplan.eec.enableStartStopFormulaCalculation](admin_r_vmargs_enablestartstopformulacache.htm) runtime option is entered in the initialization file.

parent_eecbase_k_placeholder.title: Creating placeholders
parent_eecbase_k_placeholder.content:
# Creating placeholders

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating placeholders

With the level configuration you can predefine hierarchies of mechatronic components. With the modular system development you can predefine the positions where the project engineer may insert further components. To this purpose a placeholder instead of a component is installed.

To create a placeholder:

    1. In the Components area click .

    1. Select the Placeholder object.

    1. Optionally change the name of the placeholder.

### Note

The name of a new object should not contain a special character (){}[].:,;-/\Â§$%&#~<>|= so that on the one hand it is file system-compliant and on the other hand no problems occur during the creation of formulas.

    1. In the Type field optionally select the type of the components that may be installed on this placeholder.
    2. In the Multiplicity field enter the number of components which may be installed on this placeholder.
    3. In the Disabled field optionally enter a formula to deactivate the placeholder under specific conditions.
    4. Save with [Ctrl] \+ [S] or .

parent_eecbase_k_projectdatacheck.title: Checking Project Data
parent_eecbase_k_projectdatacheck.content:
# Checking Project Data

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Checking Project Data

In the project data you can check whether the set selection values correspond to the actually possible selection values.

For the section of a check you can select between Workspace and Selected object.

Within a section you can further restrict the check to Parameter, Disabler or Extension points, or any other combination.

The option Check for constraint violation can be used to find parameter values which violate the specified value limits.

The check is started with Project data > Check project data. The restrictions for the section and the checking of objects can then be selected in the dialog subsequently opened.

The result is displayed in a table. All of the objects contained in it can be opened by double-clicking or using the Open popup menu entry. Marked objects are deleted from the result list and the model via the [Del] key or the popup menu entry Remove Selected Match. All of the matches are removed from the result list and the model with the Remove All Matches popup menu entry.

### Note

During the project data check, disabled objects are not considered and the formula cache is enabled.

A formula cache that is enabled requires memory.

Depending on the size of the model, the value for the memory (-xmx) in the initialization file (*.ini) has to be modified.

See [-xmx](admin_r_vmargs_xmx.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_px_create_eclipse_plugi.title: Creating a plug-in in Eclipse
parent_eecbase_k_px_create_eclipse_plugi.content:
# Creating a plug-in in Eclipse

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Creating a plug-in in Eclipse

The PX updates are implemented in Java. To do this, the Eclipse development environment must be set up (see [Implementing native Java](eecscripting_k_implement_native_java.htm)).

A new plug-in project is created using the wizard of the same name:

    1. Open the menu File > New > Other.
    2. Select Plug-in Project.
    3. Confirm the selection with [Next >].

The plug-in project is given a name and settings for the source and output folder in the subsequent dialog.

    4. Enter a name for the plug-in in the Project Name field.
    5. Check the information for the Source folder and Output folder, as well the Eclipse version.
    6. Confirm with [Next >].

Information on the version and publisher of the plug-in is entered in the subsequent dialog.

    7. Enter the name of the plug-in in the Name field.
    8. Enter your own company name in the Provider field.
    9. Close the wizard with [Finish].

Eclipse then switches to the Plug-in Development perspective. Further settings can be configured there.

    10. Open the Dependencies tab.
    11. Add to the list of Required Plug-ins the plug-in com.mind8.project.exchange (1.0.1).
    12. Save the editor using [Ctrl] \+ [S].

The plug-in must be assigned an extension point. This is done in the Extensions tab.

    13. Open the Extensions tab.
    14. Add to the list of All Extensions the extension point com.mind8.project.exchange.IProjectExchangeModelUpdate.
    15. Enter in the toVersion field the version number to which the plug-in is updated (target version).
    16. Enter a self-explanatory name in the name field (optional).
    17. Enter a description of the plug-in in the description field (optional).
    18. Enter the EOX file name of a specific model in the modelID field if the plug-in is relevant only to this model. Do not enter an EOX file name if the PX updates are to be applied to all PX files.
    19. Save the editor using [Ctrl] \+ [S].

The Java class can be created on the same tab.

    20. Click the class link.

The New Java Class dialog opens.

    21. Without entering further information, confirm the dialog with [Finish].

Then, the new Java class is opened in an editor.

false = delete only the specified project component.  
Return value | Boolean | true = at least one project component has been deleted.  
false = no project component has been deleted.

parent_eecbase_k_px_plugin_export.title: Exporting plug-ins
parent_eecbase_k_px_plugin_export.content:
# Exporting plug-ins

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Exporting plug-ins

The newly created plug-in must at first be exported, and then copied to the folder \dropins.

    1. Highlight the plug-in (for example, de.eplan.px.update) in the Package Explorer.
    2. Right-click to open the context menu.
    3. Select Export in the context menu.

The export wizard opens.

    4. Open the Plug-in Development branch.
    5. Select Deployable plug-ins and fragments.
    6. Confirm with [Next >].

The next wizard dialog opens.

    7. The correct plug-in must be highlighted in the Available Plug-ins and Fragments list.
    8. On the Destination tab, choose as the output directory the dropins directory of the current EEC installation.
    9. Close the wizard with [Finish].

A message window shows the export of the plug-in by way of a progress bar.

parent_eecbase_k_px_update.title: PX update
parent_eecbase_k_px_update.content:
# PX update

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## PX update

PX files always need to be updated when projects are to be created by importing archived PX files whose modular systems have meanwhile been changed.

The necessary changes in a PX file can generally be done manually. Following the scheme above, the library component C in the archived project would have to be renamed to B. Then, the import of the PX file works without any conflict. With a large number of PX files and / or old projects, however, the effort of manually updating them can be too much. For automating PX updates there is a concept to create oneâs own plug-ins by means of methods supplied. Use of these plug-ins requires the versioning of the modular system.

parent_eecbase_k_px_updateparametervalue.title: updateParameterValue
parent_eecbase_k_px_updateparametervalue.content:
# updateParameterValue

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

updateParameterValue

## updateParameterValue

Updates the value of the parameter with the stated absolute path in all existing projects.

updateParameterValue(String parameterAbsPath, Object parameterValue)  
---  
Argument | String | instanceAbsPath | Absolute path to the project component.  
Object | parameterValue | New value of the parameter.  
Return value | Boolean | true = at least one parameter value has been updated.  
false = no parameter value has been updated.

parent_eecbase_k_px_versioning_modular_s.title: Versioning of the modular systems
parent_eecbase_k_px_versioning_modular_s.content:
# Versioning of the modular systems

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Versioning of the modular systems

Versioning is done by introducing a string parameter that is used as a holder for the version identifier.

The value of the parameter has the following definition:
    
        [Beliebiger String] "V" + Nummer + "." + Nummer

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
    

    Feeder3000 V 1.13

**V** , and the numbers separated by the period are relevant to the versioning; the text in front of that is optional.

The name of the version control parameter must be added to the VM arguments in the initialization file of EEC (see also [Java Virtual Machine Arguments (VMArgs)](admin_r_vmargs.htm)):
    
        com.mind8.project.exchange.model.ProjectExchangeModel.modelKitVersionHolderPath

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
    

    -Dcom.mind8.project.exchange.model.ProjectExchangeModel.modelKitVersionHolderPath=  
    T_Mechatronik_Baukasten.Komponenten.Basis.Modellversion

The update process also uses the model name (EOX filename) beside the version number.

parent_eecbase_k_px_write_method_updatep.title: Writing method updateProjectExchangeModel

parent_eecbase_k_resources_and_their_man.title: Resources and their management
parent_eecbase_k_resources_and_their_man.content:
# Resources and their management

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Resources and their management

Resources can be managed either externally in the file system or internally in the model.

The resource path validation displays the input of an invalid path in the editor as an error. Outside of editors (e.g., EOX Import), this is displayed as a warning.

To allow for an integrated versioning not only of the components, but also of the referenced resources, it is possible to manage (internalize) the resources also internally, i.e., in the model.

The state graph of the following graphic shows the states that a component can adopt with respect to the resource, and which changes are possible:

Assigning resources is not meaningful in the case of abstract components. Their state, therefore, is No resource.

A component can be assigned an external resource. In this state, the component is merely assigned a path to the external resource. Alternatively, it is also possible to save the resource in the model (Resource internal).

As illustrated in the graphic, it is possible to modify the state of a component in such a way that an external resource is converted to an internal resource (internalize) or an internal resource to an external resource (externalize).

Independently of this, a resource can be imported as new for an existing component (import). Both for internal and external resources, it is possible by analogy to export a resource to a file system.

Whether a resource must be managed internally or externally is defined separately for each discipline (except text discipline) using a model variable:

     * ECAD - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_ecad_ressourcesinternalize.htm).
     * Media sources - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_mediasources_resources.htm).
     * Office Word - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_office_word_resources.htm).
     * PLC AutomationWorX - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_plc_awx_resources.htm).
     * PLC CoDeSys - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_plc_cds_resources.htm).
     * PLC STEP 7 - [Manage Resources Internally](admin_r_modelvar_plc_s7_resources.htm).

The appropriate differentiation must be selected for text disciplines on the Attributes page of a TextDisciplineConfiguration. The Manage Resources Internally check box has to be selected.

Existing external resources are imported into the model via the popup menu in library views using the action Internalize External Resources.

It is possible to internalize entire libraries or only individual files.

Internal resources of individual discipline components or of all discipline components below a unit or of all discipline components of a library can be exported to the resources folder of the corresponding discipline via File > Externalize Internal Resources. The externalized resources are then linked to the discipline components.

The storage location of a resource is specified on the Attributes editor page.

### Note

The consistent versioning of models requires that all external resources be internalized in the model.

If the resource of a discipline component has been changed externally, for example by an additional parameter, the data of the discipline components can be updated with data of the resources by means of the [Synchronize...] button. To this purpose the Synchronization view opens and lists the changed discipline components.

parent_eecbase_k_searching_errors_by_mea.title: Debugging by means of the message log
parent_eecbase_k_searching_errors_by_mea.content:
# Debugging by means of the message log

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Debugging by means of the message log

All actions executed by the user and resulting messages are entered in a message log. The message log stores the number of actions that has been defined in the settings. This means that, for example, only the last 100 actions are logged. Since actions produce a varying number of subactions and messages, the list may not only comprise the 100 actions, as configured, but also a large number of subactions and messages.

    1. To enhance the overview all actions are structured hierarchical and branches can be expanded and collapsed. New, unread actions and messages are displayed in bold, already read ones in regular.
    2. Decorators identify the severity level of a message.
    3. Errors are described in the Message / Description column.
    4. The status line contains the error-causing object.
    5. The error-causing object is listed in a table.
    6. The popup menu is used to navigate to the object causing the error.
    7. The error-causing object.
    8. The icons of the tool bar offer actions to expand and collapse branches and indicate all messages as read resp. as unread. and to filter messages with several criteria. Actions can be filtered with the criteria Error, Warning, Info and All other (including actions without sub actions).

parent_eecbase_k_searching_errors_by_mea.title: Message log settings
parent_eecbase_k_searching_errors_by_mea.content:
# Message log settings

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Message log settings

The message log lists all user-relevant activities, and thus assists in the search for errors in the model. Under Settings > Message Log the maximum number of activities to be logged can be limited.

    1. The maximum number of retained activities is entered in the Number of retained activities field. If the maximum number is reached, then the oldest message is deleted from the message log as soon as a new message occurs (FIFO - First In First Out principle).
    2. The logging period , for how many hours messages are to be kept in memory so that for example, only the messages of the last 2 hours are stored, is entered in the Protocol timescale for activities (h) field. The oldest message is deleted from the message log as soon as a new message occurs (FIFO - First In First Out principle). If a null or nothing is entered, then activities are stored without limit.

### Note

The protocol timescale for activities only takes place when new adding new messages, so that messages can be displayed in the log, which are older than the protocol timescale allows in principle.

parent_eecbase_k_set_adminstrator_passwo.title: Assign administrator password
parent_eecbase_k_set_adminstrator_passwo.content:
# Assign administrator password

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Assign administrator password

The administrator of EEC installation should define a password. To do this, he must first log on as Administrator:

The authorization is enabled if the Administrator is not the only user. Then, the login panel appears at the start.

The authorization data are also all contained in an EOX. They are ignored for synchronization purposes. During the initial import to a relational DB, they are also imported.

parent_eecbase_k_switch_eox_backend.title: Changing the EOX back-end
parent_eecbase_k_switch_eox_backend.content:
# Changing the EOX back-end

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Changing the EOX back-end

After the first start with selection of a EOX file for data management, EEC always starts with this EOX file.

The menu File > Switch back-end can be used both to open the dialog for selecting an EOX file as well as a different EOX file that has already been used.

    1. Click File > Switch back-end.
    2. Mark a previously selected EOX file.

The data model is immediately changed to the selected EOX file.

    3. Click Select.

The dialog Choose new backend opens.

    4. Select a EOX file
    5. Confirm your selection with [OK].

The selected file appears in the list of previously selected EOX files at the next time the data basis should be switched.

parent_eecbase_k_synchronize_disciplinec.title: Synchronization of discipline components
parent_eecbase_k_synchronize_disciplinec.content:
# Synchronization of discipline components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Synchronization of discipline components

If the resource of a discipline component has been changed, for example extended with an additional parameter, the discipline component should be synchronized with the changes.

If the resource is stored externally, the synchronization can be started through the following actions:

     * Select a library, unit or discipline component Popup menu > Synchronize external resources....
     * Open the discipline component, click [Synchronization...] in the Attributes editor.

If the resource is stored internally, the synchronization is started through the following action:

     * Open the discipline component, click [Update & Synchronize] in the Attributes editor. In this action, the internal resource is first replaced by the external resource and then the data in the model is synchronized.

The action opens the Synchronization view in which all discipline components are listed that were changed. Double-clicking the changed discipline component opens the comparison editor. In it the parameters of the discipline component are listed before and after the synchronization.

     * Parameters unchanged in the resource are grayed out.
     * Parameters removed in the resource are crossed out.
     * Parameters added in the resource are represented as normal.
     * For P8 macros the macro description before and after the synchronization.

By saving the editor the changes represented in the Parameters after synchronization: list are applied.

### Note

If an external resource is synchronized for an ECAD discipline component, the synchronization view opens. The name field displays the absolute name of the discipline component to be synchronized. To navigate to the discipline component, the absolute name must be copied to the clipboard and inserted into the library catalog. In the library catalog, the tree for the discipline component is opened and the searched discipline component is selected.

parent_eecbase_k_units.title: Working with units
parent_eecbase_k_units.content:
# Working with units

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Working with units

To structure objects in assemblies, the EEC has a unit structure that is analogous to the folder structure of the file system. Similar to Windows Explorer, units can be used for structuring purposes like folders.

More:

[Using units in the library](eecbase_k_units_in_library.htm)

[Using units within projects](eecbase_k_units_in_project.htm)

# Using units in the library

You are here: 

## Using units in the library

For example, mechatronic components are stored as shown in the following figure:

parent_eecbase_k_units_in_project.title: Using units within projects
parent_eecbase_k_units_in_project.content:
# Using units within projects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Using units within projects

In order to structure large projects into subprojects, projects have units as already familiar from libraries.

Any number of subprojects can be defined within a project by means of units. Within the units, there are the familiar Mechatronics root and the Discipline roots.

parent_eecbase_k_use_of_parameters_in_me.title: Inserting parameters into components
parent_eecbase_k_use_of_parameters_in_me.content:
# Inserting parameters into components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting parameters into components

Use the Parameters editor page of a component to insert a parameter.

You can add one or more parameters to a component using the Parameter Finder  or the drag & drop method.

### Read more

     * [Finder dialog](gui_k_finder_dialog.htm)
     * [Parameterization](eecbase_k_parameterizing.htm)

parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_disab.title: Disabler
parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_disab.content:
# Disabler

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Disabler

A very common form of variant management is the working with maximum specifications of machines and plants. A project with maximum configuration serves as the template here. Components not needed are deleted and / or disabled in the specific project.

EEC supports this approach through the Disabler concept. A disabler allows mechatronic and discipline-specific components to be disabled (not deleted). Individual components are disabled on the basis of parameter values, wit a disabler being either true or false.

### Note

By disabling via disablers, the components are preserved in the model, but they are left out from the calculation of formulas, for example, by way of mos, rmos, etc. This filtering in the calculation of formulas requires increased computation capacity, which can cause noticeable deceleration in the case of very extensive models. In such cases, one must check whether variant management should be realized via extension points rather than disablers.

Each mounted component has a disabler. In the following figure of the tutorial model, one can see that the feeder can be equipped with an inspection unit (Inspect). The inspection unit exists by default, but it can be disabled in the project via the evaluation of the formula of the disabler.

In this example, it is specifically the $Option_Inspect_available parameter that is evaluated.

If the parameter value is false, the result is a machine type that does not contain the inspection unit; see the following figure:

The evaluation of all disabler formulas in a model can be converted from negative to positive logic via the model variables Use positive logic in component activation formula. The following figure shows the conversion of the model variables:

parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.title: Extensions
parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.content:
# Extensions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Extensions

In contrast to projects with disablers, which must contain all variants in the base library according to the maximum principle, each variant is stored in a separate library for projects with extension points. Using the extension points (interfaces), the components from the variant libraries are linked to the base library at the correct positions.

The extension points can be defined both for mechatronic components and discipline components. The disabler function can also be applied to extension points. In the Update variants explicitly mode (static disabler mode), the instances concerned can be deleted or re-instantiated, like class-defined components.

The properties of an extension point are defined at the class level.

Extension points are created as [Creating placeholders](eecbase_k_placeholder.htm) and assigned the Extension point attribute, as seen in the following image:

Extension points behave like normal placeholders in the project. Multiple mounting (multiplicity) is not checked at the time of the instantiation, but regularly at the time of validation. Any existing recursions are not checked.

The actions Update extensions and Remove extensions enable type conversions during the library development with existing projects. These actions must be enabled manually. There is no automatic updating of extension points. The associated commands allow for an automatic use in user-defined actions (see [BASE actions](eecbase_k_base_actions.htm)).

Method like the reconstruction of libraries to extension points:

    1. Start with all existing libraries, but without projects.
    2. Define the interfaces for the extension points: One interface in the modular system for each different variant position.
    3. Delete all fixed mounted variants (note down classes!) and reconstruct the newly added placeholders as extension points.
    4. Remove from the base library the imported variant libraries, and import the base library from the variant libraries.
    5. Enter the correct interfaces in the classes, as noted down under point 3.

Display of the implemented and extended interfaces in the editor

In the interface view of the editor, the relations to the interfaces are displayed in the Declared in column. For the model represented below, this produces for the class Station the following interface declaration:

The table lists the interfaces implemented by the component itself ahead of the inherited interfaces.

The following figure shows a model where the superclass Abstract_Station implements the interfaces I1 and I3. The Station class implements the I2 interface, which extends the I3 interface.

parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.title: Multiplicity
parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.content:
# Multiplicity

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Multiplicity

The amount of instances of a library component to place on an extension point is specified by the Multiplicity value.

The value is specified as a range of values with the syntax <start value>..<end value> or as any value with the syntax *.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

It should not be possible to install up to four instances. | 
    
        0..4  
  
---|---  
Exactly two instances can be placed. | 
    
        2..2  
  
Any number of instances can be placed. | 
    
        *

parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.title: Selection formulas
parent_eecbase_k_variantmanagement_exten.content:
# Selection formulas

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## Selection formulas

For placeholders and extension points a selection formula can be specified (see the following figure), which determines during instantiation and / or updates which of the visible library components is to be instantiated. With manual configuration, the selection can be limited to a subset of the library components of the extension point.

The following rules apply to the calculation of the selection formula:

     * The selection formula yields a Boolean value or an integer:
       * true means that each possible component is instantiated exactly once and / or that the instance is retained.
       * false means that no component is instantiated and / or that the instance is deleted.
       * An integer specifies the number of components to be instantiated that correspond to the specified type. If a type is specified from which multiple types can be derived, the number of components is instantiated from each derived type.

### Note

Manual updating of the extension points:

If the number of components to be instantiated is larger than the specification for multiplicity allows, the instantiation is not performed. A corresponding error message is output.

Updating the extension points during the creation of a project:

If the number of components to be instantiated is larger than the specification for multiplicity allows, the instantiation is performed. It is recommended to check the model for validity by using the menu item Model > Validate model

     * The formula is evaluated in the context of the component to be instantiated, meaning as if the instance were installed at the extension point. All parameters of the instance can be accessed, as well as, of course, the enclosing component (mc). But it is not possible to access components contained in the instance (e.g., mos or rmos on the instance itself).
     * When the formula cache is enabled ([Engineering.StartFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_startformulacachecommand.htm)), the result of the expressions used in the selection formula, rmos and mos, must not contain any objects inserted on extension points. If this cannot be avoided, the [Engineering.UpdateExtensionsCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_updateextensionscommand.htm) may have to be executed several times, or the formula cache has to be disabled ([Engineering.StopFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_stopformulacachecommand.htm)).
     * Debugging:

If the selection formula does not yield a Boolean value, or if a formula error occurs, this will be displayed after updating the extension points: The affected library components are instantiated, and â in analogy to the Disabler â the result of the selection formula for this project component is displayed on the parameter page. The result can be analyzed with the formula inspector.

### Note

If the selection formula cannot determine a value, the placeholder and / or extension point will behave as if there is no selection formula.

The model variable Maximum Number Of Classes To Check For Placeholder Selection Formula defines a limit from which a warning will be issued. The warning alerts the user that the evaluation of the selection formula will take up a longer period of time. This happens when many library components correspond to the type of the extension point (e.g., if Engineering.Component is selected as the type). The selection formula must then be calculated for every possible library component.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

Selection formula:
    
        =mc.$SelectionConditionAndType.name.startsWith('A')

Here, instantiation is made dependent on a condition in the enclosing mechatronic component, combined with a class nomenclature.

### Note

When instantiating via Drag & Drop, the selection formula for the placeholder or extension point is not calculated during the drag process, so that the instantiation cannot be excluded. If the instantiated component contains further extension points with selection formulas, these will be evaluated.

parent_eececad_k_assignment_of_window_ma.title: Assigning window macros to ECAD objects
parent_eececad_k_assignment_of_window_ma.content:
# Assigning window macros to ECAD objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Assigning window macros to ECAD objects

The window macro and ECAD object are linked in EEC. On the Attributes editor page of the ECAD object (e.g., Fragment), the link to the corresponding window macro is established by importing the macro file (resource storage location can be set via the settings). The storage location of the window macro must be stated.

The macro file (resource) is at first stored externally â in a folder that can be chosen freely. If necessary, the entire content of the macro file â after saving it first â can be internalized in the model via [Internalize]. This saves the entire content of the file to the database. The externally stored file can then be deleted. Only externally stored macros can be overwritten directly from within Eplan Electric P8 by overwriting a macro.

However, in this case there is no automatic synchronization of the external macro with the ECAD object in EEC. This synchronization must be triggered manually via the popup menu (Synchronize External Resources... .

By selecting a page or window macro, the variables defined in the placeholder object, as well as the sockets and plugs will be imported into EEC automatically as parameters of the ECAD object. These will be displayed on the Parameters editor page.

The variables from the placeholder object are created as a parameter of the String type; the Plug and Socket texts define their data type using the keywords Plug and / or Socket.

Assigning resources is not meaningful in the case of abstract components. Their state, therefore, is **No resource**.

If the resource of a component is stored externally, the component is merely assigned a path to the **external resource**. Alternatively, it is also possible to save the resource in the model, i.e., in the database, resp. in the EOX file (**Resource internal**).

As illustrated in the graphic, it is possible to modify the state of a component in such a way that an external resource is converted to an internal resource (Internalize) or an internal resource to an external resource (Externalize).

Independently of this, a resource can be imported as new for an existing component (import). Both for internal and external resources, it is possible by analogy to export a resource from the EEC to a file system.

Existing **external resources** are internalized via the popup menu in library views using the action Internalize External Resources:

It is possible to internalize entire libraries or only individual files.

The Attributes page of the components is represented in such a way that the three possible states of resources, and the respective actions that are possible, become transparent:

**Note:** In the event of versioning the **resources must be managed internally** so that EEC can ensure the consistency of the model.

parent_eececad_k_avoid_manual_changes_on.title: Preventing manual changes to macros
parent_eececad_k_avoid_manual_changes_on.content:
# Preventing manual changes to macros

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Preventing manual changes to macros

The Edit Macros Only Externally model variable prevents manual changes to ECAD macros from within EEC.

If the value of the model variables is set to true, the menu entry remains visible, but is grayed out (see the figure below). This serves as a note to the user that the function has not been removed generally, but has simply been disabled.

parent_eececad_k_chapter.title: Creating a chapter
parent_eececad_k_chapter.content:
# Creating a chapter

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating a chapter

Objects of the **Chapter** type are used to create a structure within a schematic into which the pages will be placed. This way, the page macros can be arranged according to the Eplan Electric P8 tree structure, and page numbers assigned according to specific criteria, so that, for example, the pages for sensors start at the page number 200.

Unlike all other ECAD objects, Chapter macros have neither internal nor external resources.

An object of the Chapter type is generated via the popup menu New > ECAD > Chapter.

parent_eececad_k_create_ecad_discipline.title: Creating an ECAD discipline
parent_eececad_k_create_ecad_discipline.content:
# Creating an ECAD discipline

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating an ECAD discipline

Requirement:

     * The ECAD license module is part of the current installation.
     * A valid version of Eplan Electric P8 is installed.

The ECAD system library that is to be imported into the current modular system library is required so that the ECAD discipline can be processed. This system library contains the discipline-specific basic classes and methods for Eplan Electric P8.

In addition the T_Ecad_Ui library can be imported. This contains preassembled commands to execute the generation of the ECAD structure and of the schematics. In principle you can create these commands yourself (see [Creating project documents](eececad_k_generate_project_docu.htm)). The T_Ecad_Ui library is provided by the <EEC installation folder>\install\Tutorial\P8\Ecad_Ui.eox file as a part of the tutorial. The library is added to the library catalog by importing the EOX file.

For the discipline Eplan Electric P8 settings are to be set (see [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_prefs_ecad_p8.htm)) and model variables to be agreed (see [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_modelvar_eplanp8.htm)).

parent_eececad_k_create_ecad_object.title: Creating ECAD objects
parent_eececad_k_create_ecad_object.content:
# Creating ECAD objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating ECAD objects

Requirements:

     * The ECAD system library is imported into the current library.

The following sections describe the creation of the discipline component for the parent classes WiringDiagram, Chapter, Page and Fragment.

New discipline components are created via the context menu.

As good practice it is recommended to not use those discipline components directly but rather to enclose them in a mechatronic component.

Advantages of this procedure:

     * The parameter values are largely calculated on the mechatronical level, even before the discipline structure exists.
     * If the resource of a discipline component is changed or synchronized, the formulas for the calculation are then not lost.

The calculated parameter values of the mechatronics components are taken from the discipline component via a formula of the syntax =mc.$<parameter name>. To enter these so called interface parameters in a easier way, you can mark all parameters and insert them at the same time via the popup menu > Add interface parameters

parent_eececad_k_create_parameter_in_mac.title: Creating parameters in ECAD macro
parent_eececad_k_create_parameter_in_mac.content:
# Creating parameters in ECAD macro

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating parameters in ECAD macro

Parameters can be defined only on symbol properties that are captured by a placeholder object. For example, it is not possible to define the variant of the symbol or the property Main function as a parameter. The following figure shows what a parameter may look like for the technical characteristic of a device:

The parameter name can generally be chosen freely. When choosing a name, it should be kept in mind that the same parameter name should always be used for the same task, where possible, including in various macros, in order to avoid an excessive number of parameters.

If a parameter name is preceded by "hidden_" (for example "hidden_Order"), the parameter value is not visible in a created schematic. The invisible parameter value is still available in the Eplan Electric P8, for example for calculations.

When it comes to the placing of the placeholder object, a suitable position near the affected graphic should be selected. The following figure shows what the parameters for a device in a placeholder object look like:

When generating the window macro keep in mind that the placeholder object is part of the macro content. Otherwise, no variables will be defined in the macro. If text objects for Plug and / or Socket are also defined in the same macro, note that these texts must **not** be assigned to the placeholder object! But they do have to be included in the macro graphic.

During the generation of the schematic with EEC, the parameter is replaced by the value defined or calculated in EEC.

parent_eececad_k_create_window_macro.title: Window macros
parent_eececad_k_create_window_macro.content:
# Window macros

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Window macros

All partial circuits are defined as window macros in Eplan Electric P8 and exported as data. Only in EEC is a decision taken as to whether the partial circuit is a page or a fragment. The macro type page macro can be used for EEC macros, but does not provide any support of variants.

parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_object_in_l.title: Deleting ECAD objects from the library
parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_object_in_l.content:
# Deleting ECAD objects from the library

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting ECAD objects from the library

The library object can be deleted using its context menu. In the process, the corresponding library object and all project instances of the object are deleted.

A prompt must be confirmed to go ahead with the deletion.

parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_object_in_p.title: Deleting ECAD objects from within a project
parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_object_in_p.content:
# Deleting ECAD objects from within a project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting ECAD objects from within a project

The ECAD object can be deleted using its context menu. It should be noted here that all structurally subordinate objects of the ECAD object will also be deleted:

A prompt must be confirmed to go ahead with the deletion.

parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_objects.title: Deleting ECAD objects from the project
parent_eececad_k_delete_ecad_objects.content:
# Deleting ECAD objects from the project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting ECAD objects from the project

ECAD objects can be deleted from a library or also from a project.

parent_eececad_k_ecad.title: ECAD
parent_eececad_k_ecad.content:
# ECAD

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## ECAD

The ECAD discipline serves to create schematic fragments consisting of page and window macros. The schematic fragments are allocated to the mechatronical components of the modular system. A discipline-specific concept is defined by means of the Plug-Socket concept.

An ECAD discipline structure is created from the configuration by means of a command. From the resulting ECAD structure the schematics are created, also by means of a command.

parent_eececad_k_ecad_actions.title: ECAD P8 actions
parent_eececad_k_ecad_actions.content:
# ECAD P8 actions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## ECAD P8 actions

The following commands are available in Actions for the generation of the ECAD structure and of wiring diagrams:

    1. [ECAD.GenerateECADCodeCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadcodecommand.htm): Generates the schematic.
    2. [ECAD.GenerateEcadStructureCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadstructurecommand.htm): Generates the ECAD structure.
    3. [ECAD.OpenECADComponentCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadcomponentcommand.htm): Generates and opens the schematic for the passed ECAD component. The created schematic only includes the passed components and all superordinate components.
    4. [ECAD.OpenECADPagesCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadpagescommand.htm): Generates and opens the passed wiring diagrams of the project.
       * If the project is not existing yet, it is created, as by OpenECADComponentCommand.
       * If the project already exists, so the pages are created in it. If the pages already exist, the command replaces the existing pages by new ones.
    5. [ECAD.OpenProjectDiagramsCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openprojectdiagramscommand.htm): Generates and opens all the wiring diagrams of a project.
    6. [ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm): Generates the ECAD project
    7. [ECAD.OpenEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadprojectcommand.htm): Opens the relevant ECAD project
    8. [ECAD.UpdateEcadPagesCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_updateecadpagescommand.htm): Updates pages in the ECAD project
    9. [ECAD.GenerateEdzForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateedzforecadprojectcommand.htm): Generates an EDZ file of the specified project components of the WiringDiagram type.
    10. [ECAD.GenerateXmlForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generatexmlforecadprojectcommand.htm): Creates a XML file ec-instructions.xml, as well as the macro files of the project which should be created in the specified folder.

parent_eececad_k_ecad_objects.title: ECAD objects
parent_eececad_k_ecad_objects.content:
# ECAD objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## ECAD objects

The structure of an ECAD project is characterized by four ECAD objects (super classes):

     * Schematic (Super class WiringDiagram)
     * Chapter (Super class Chapter)
     * Page (Super class Page)
     * Fragment (Super class Fragment)

The hierarchy of the super classes is shown in the following figure:

Discipline components of the super classes WiringDiagram, Page and Fragment enclose files of the ECAD systems, so called Resources.

For Eplan Electric P8 these are:

Basic project template for WiringDiagram,

Page macro for Page,

Symbol macro, or window macro for Fragment.

The resource Basic project template contains all project properties, as well as the project master data, such as system libraries, plot frame, forms, etc., but no graphical content.

Discipline components of super class Chapter do not enclose any resources, they are only used for the structuring of the ECAD project. Since the structuring is largely achieved by the discipline components of super classes WiringDiagram and Page, the use of the super class Chapter is usually unnecessary.

To create the structure of an ECAD project structure identifiers are necessary, which are specified in Eplan Electric P8 by using the structure identifier management.

In the EEC in the Model variables view the names of the parameters are assigned to every possible structure identifier (see [Model variables](admin_r_modelvariables.htm) > [Disciplines](admin_r_modelvar_disciplines.htm) > [ECAD](admin_r_modelvar_ecad.htm) > [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_modelvar_eplanp8.htm)). These parameters have to be available in the ECAD objects WiringDiagram and Page. The parameter values then determine the classification of the ECAD objects in the project structure.

### Read more

     * [Structure of the device tag](../../../../plattform/2025/content/htm/devicetaggui_k_aufbauvonbm.htm)

parent_eececad_k_ecad_objects_as_compone.title: ECAD objects as components of mechatronics objects
parent_eececad_k_ecad_objects_as_compone.content:
# ECAD objects as components of mechatronics objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## ECAD objects as components of mechatronics objects

ECAD objects can be integrated directly into mechatronics objects. This way, mechatronics objects bring with them the corresponding electro-technical objects at the instantiation.

To utilize this mechanism, the electro-technical objects are added to the mechatronics (library) objects as components.

The following figure shows the mechatronics object Lights and its components. One can see that the mechatronics object brings with it the components Lights, as well as the mechatronics objects Lamp1 to Lamp4.

Parameter values that are calculated in the discipline structure result in formula errors during the configuration because the discipline structure does not yet exist at this point.

In order to avoid these formula errors, it is good practice to insert each discipline component into an "own" mechatronics component. This allows every parameter value required to already be calculated and transferred to the installed discipline component in the mechatronics structure.

The following figure illustrates this using the pressure sensor from the tutorial as an example:

    1. Originally all the parameters for the installed discipline component Sensor_Pressure were calculated in the discipline component.
    2. These parameters are now calculated in the mechatronics component. In this case the formulas are less complicated because it is not necessary to reference from the discipline structure into the mechatronical structure (with origin).
    3. The discipline component now obtains the parameter values from the mechatronics component in which it is installed (with mc.$Parametername).

parent_eececad_k_editing_macro_in_eec.title: Editing macros directly from within EEC
parent_eececad_k_editing_macro_in_eec.content:
# Editing macros directly from within EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Editing macros directly from within EEC

Regardless of whether the content of a macro is stored externally or internally, the macro can be opened directly from within EEC for editing. To do this, use the menu item Edit Macro in the popup menu of the respective page or fragment respectively.

The macro is loaded directly in Eplan Electric P8 and can also be edited there. After editing, the edited temporary page in the graphical editor must not be closed. To save the changes in the macro, Eplan Electric P8 **must** be closed. This will save the changes directly to the resource.

If parameters were added, changed or deleted during editing, the synchronization dialog appears after closing Eplan Electric P8. In the following example, two new parameters PageName and PageDescription have been added within the Lamp macro. In the dialog, the parameters before and after the change are contrasted in two columns.

Saving the editor applies the changed parameter interface of the macro. To manually add any parameters that may have been omitted, use [>>>] between the two windows. This way, even a parameter not used in the parameter interface of the macro can be integrated into the EEC object.

parent_eececad_k_edzfilegeneration.title: Eplan Data Zip (EDZ file) generation
parent_eececad_k_edzfilegeneration.content:
# Eplan Data Zip (EDZ file) generation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Eplan Data Zip (EDZ file) generation

An EDZ file contains all the specifications that are required to create one or more macros from them in P8.

An EDZ file can be created without an installation of Eplan Electric P8 on the same computer. A valid installation of Eplan Electric P8 is required to create the P8 macros from the EDZ file.

The generation is carried out by means of the command ECAD.GenerateEdzForEcadProjectCommand.

Prerequisites for the generation of the EDZ file are:

     * A part.xml file has to exist. This can be created by the text discipline if is not yet provided by other subsystems. The file is to be specified as an argument partXmlFilePath.
     * An image file for the part has to exist. The file is to be specified as an argument pictureFilepath.
     * A C# script has to exist whose absolute file name is specified by the model variable Name of the parameter for a C# script and whose arguments are specified by the model variable Name of the parameter for C# script arguments.
     * The model variables under Eplan Data Zip (.edz) generation are to be taken into consideration.

parent_eececad_k_eplanp8preferences.title: Eplan Electric P8 configuration
parent_eececad_k_eplanp8preferences.content:
# Eplan Electric P8 configuration

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Eplan Electric P8 configuration
