The configuration file <EEC Installation Folder>\workspace\\.metadata\\.plugins\org.eclipse.core.runtime\\.settings\com.mind8.ecad.eplanp8.prefs is additionally responsible for the configuration of Eplan Electric P8.

In order to ensure that Eplan Electric P8 does not hang up unnoticed you can set in the configuration file the number of calls for creating P8 data after which Eplan Electric P8 is terminated.

Insert a line with the following syntax:
    
        com.mind8.ecad.eplanp8.countdown=<count>

The value for <count> specifies the number of calls after which a restart of Eplan Electric P8 is carried out.

Eplan Electric P8 is restarted by the next call to create P8 data.

parent_eececad_k_example_basic_schematic.title: Basic wiring diagram with fuse and contact
parent_eececad_k_example_basic_schematic.content:
# Basic wiring diagram with fuse and contact

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Basic wiring diagram with fuse and contact

The basic wiring diagram is created by removing the individual lamps. A placeholder object is added to define the required parameters, as well as the sockets of the possible number of lamps.

This basic wiring diagram is saved as a window macro in the folder defined for the resources.

parent_eececad_k_example_creating_page_w.title: Example: Creation of a page for light
parent_eececad_k_example_creating_page_w.content:
# Example: Creation of a page for light

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Example: Creation of a page for light

Initial situation: Schematic page with a maximum of 5 lamps.

The variance in this example is the number of lamps. Thus, the page is broken down into the following essential elements:

     * Basic wiring diagram with fuse and contact
     * A variant (A) of a single lamp with its terminals and connections via T-pieces.
     * A variant (B) of a single lamp with its terminals and connections via angles for the last connection.

In the placement of sockets and plugs, the relative position to the graphic is very important. It is therefore recommended that all sockets be placed in the entire circuit, and to derive the individual macros from this by copying. Generally, the alignment of the plug and socket texts is irrelevant. Only the location of their extension points matters.

parent_eececad_k_example_single_lamp_wit.title: A single lamp and its terminals
parent_eececad_k_example_single_lamp_wit.content:
# A single lamp and its terminals

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## A single lamp and its terminals

Since the lamps are displayed in two variants you require two macros with the same name for the ECAD object but different specifications of the variant. The multiple use is realized through multiple instantiation in EEC. The lamp needs a plug in order to connect the macro graphic with a socket in the basic wiring diagram. In addition, a placeholder object (anchor symbol) is needed for the definition of the parameters â here: Terminal1, Terminal2 and DT \- required. The window macros for the lamp in the variants A (right) and B (left) look as follows:

Via the EP plug, the lamp is connected with a socket from the basic wiring diagram. The parameters DT, Terminal1 and Terminal2, as well as the page number and index of the lamp, are filled with project-specific values in EEC.

The rule for the use of variant A or B is specified as a formula in the Variant parameter so that the lamps from 1 to 4 are placed as variant A and the lamp 5 as variant B.

parent_eececad_k_execute_c_script_in_p8.title: Executing C# scripts in Eplan Electric P8

parent_eececad_k_fragment.title: Creating fragments
parent_eececad_k_fragment.content:
# Creating fragments

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating fragments

An object of the **Fragment** type is created in a way similar to that of the Page object. Use the popup menu to select New > ECAD > Fragment.

The above selection opens the Fragment object with the Attributes editor page:

The name field is highlighted in red as long as no valid name has been entered. The name must be unique within a unit. During the assignment of a resource, the file name of the resource is entered automatically as the object name. This can be modified if necessary. Saving the editor creates the new fragment.

A file of an Eplan Electric P8 window macro is assigned to the object as a resource via [Import]. In this macro, it would not make sense to define page properties as parameters, because page properties cannot be defined via a fragment. Only one Plug may be defined in an Eplan Electric P8 window macro. Further Sockets can be defined to place further fragments on the fragment.

An object of the Fragment type can be saved only if a resource has been assigned, or if the object is highlighted as abstract.

parent_eececad_k_fragment_inheritance.title: Inheritance of fragments
parent_eececad_k_fragment_inheritance.content:
# Inheritance of fragments

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inheritance of fragments

If several fragments have the same basic properties, and if there are rules (formulas) that are common to all fragments, it is often useful to combine the common elements in an abstract fragment.

An abstract fragment thus defines a classification level from which specific fragments of the modular system are derived. The fragments thus derived are assigned the basic properties defined for the abstract fragment automatically.

A Fragment object that is set to Abstract on the Attributes page can also be saved without a resource.

parent_eececad_k_generate_project_docu.title: Creating project documents
parent_eececad_k_generate_project_docu.content:
# Creating project documents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating project documents

You open the configured ECAD structure with an action for generating the schematic in the ECAD system.

To this purpose you use a self-created SequenceSelectionAction that, for example, opens the schematic in EPLAN Electric P8 via an entry in the popup menu of the schematic object (see [SequenceSelectionActions](eecbase_k_methodology_sequenceselectionaction.htm)).

Alternatively, you can also execute the action Open schematic for **one** page or fragment.

In addition it is possible to execute the action Open pages to open **several** selected pages.

EEC automatically enters information in each created page stating that it was created by EEC, provided that this feature is available in the Eplan Electric P8 version used. The property name is called Automatically created <11006> and contains the value EEC.

By entering this property, EEC can differentiate between its own created pages and those created externally. Start the Generate Schematic action again. Only the automatically created pages of the schematic will be modified and/or overwritten.

Externally created pages are overwritten only on request.

The commands come with different signatures, which allows them to be combined with each other easily.

Application 1: Action "Generate structure, generate schematic and open"

The object selected for the action is MechatronicRoot.

     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadStructureCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadstructurecommand.htm): Generates the ECAD structure.
     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm): Generates the ECAD project.
     * [ECAD.OpenEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadprojectcommand.htm): Opens the relevant ECAD project.

All three commands have a signature that fits the object MechatronicRoot.

The associated action SequenceSelectionAction now has the following methods:

     * [Engineering.StartFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_startformulacachecommand.htm): Enable cache
     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadStructureCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadstructurecommand.htm): Generate an ECAD structure
     * [Engineering.StopFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_stopformulacachecommand.htm): Disable cache
     * [Engineering.AbortExecutionIfErrorCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_abortexecutionIferrorcommand.htm): Termination if error has occurred
     * [Engineering.SaveObjectCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_saveobjectcommand.htm): Save
     * [Engineering.StartFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_startformulacachecommand.htm): Enable cache
     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm): Generate an ECAD project
     * [Engineering.StopFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_stopformulacachecommand.htm): Disable cache
     * [Engineering.AbortExecutionIfErrorCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_abortexecutionIferrorcommand.htm): Termination if error has occurred
     * [Engineering.SaveObjectCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_saveobjectcommand.htm): Save
     * [ECAD.OpenEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadprojectcommand.htm): Open associated ECAD project

Application 2: Action "Generate structure and generate schematic"

The object selected for the action is MechatronicRoot.

     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadStructureCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadstructurecommand.htm): Generates the ECAD structure.
     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm): Generates the ECAD project.

Both commands have a signature that fits the object MechatronicRoot.

If the project is already loaded in an instance of Eplan Electric P8, the project only needs to be updated (select project, press the [F5] key) to represent the changes in the instance. To do this, the Eplan Electric P8 version must be configured in such a way that projects are not opened exclusively.

Application 3: Action "Generate schematic"

Depending on the action for the selected object, the command exhibits varying behavior:

If the object selected for the action is MechatronicRoot or EcadRoot, the command will create the entire wiring diagram.

If the object selected for the action is a list, the command will expect pages and or fragments as elements of the list. The command deletes all pages from the schematic, and then creates the selected pages as new again.

If the project is already loaded in an instance of Eplan Electric P8, the project only needs to be updated (select project, press the [F5] key) to represent the changes in the instance. To do this, the Eplan Electric P8 version must be configured in such a way that projects are not opened exclusively.

     * [ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm): Generates the ECAD project.

Application 4: Action "Update schematic"

The object selected for the action is a list. The command expects pages and / or fragments as elements of the list. It creates the pages and/or the pages with the selected fragments as completely new. Even if only a fragment of a page has been modified and selected the complete page is always regenerated. All other pages remain unchanged.

If the project is already loaded in an instance of Eplan Electric P8, the project only needs to be updated (select project, press the [F5] key) to represent the changes in the instance. To do this, the Eplan Electric P8 version must be configured in such a way that projects are not opened exclusively.

     * [ECAD.UpdateEcadPagesCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_updateecadpagescommand.htm): Updates all the pages of the ECAD project.

Application 5: Action "Open schematic"

The command opens an already created schematic using the Eplan Electric P8 version stated in EEC.

It does not matter which object (page, fragment, schematic, EcadRoot, etc.) has been selected.

The command reports an error if the project has not been created yet.

     * [ECAD.OpenEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadprojectcommand.htm): Opens the relevant ECAD project.

Application 6: Creating an EDZ file for ECAD project

An EDZ file is created, which can be imported by Eplan Electric P8 by means of an add-on (see Description of EDZ format in the Eplan Electric P8 help). Since Eplan Electric P8 is not required to generate the EDZ file, specifying the ECAD settings is not necessary (see [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_prefs_ecad_p8.htm)).

A root object must be selected for this action. All project components of the WiringDiagram type are affected. An EDZ file is created and its name is returned as the result.

     * [ECAD.GenerateEdzForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateedzforecadprojectcommand.htm): Creates an EDZ file and returns the name of the created file.

parent_eececad_k_insert_ecad_object_in_p.title: Inserting ECAD objects into a project
parent_eececad_k_insert_ecad_object_in_p.content:
# Inserting ECAD objects into a project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting ECAD objects into a project

The ECAD structure can be built directly in a project by adding (instantiating) ECAD objects to a project directly from libraries (using Drag & Drop or via the popup menu).

This direct instantiation of ECAD objects, which is explained in the following subsections, is useful for tests and / or the understanding of the context in EEC during the initial training period.

The indirect route via mechatronic components is recommended: In the library, ECAD objects are assigned to mechatronic components (see [Link between mechatronic and discipline-specific components](eecbase_k_linking_dcs.htm)), a macro that represents a limit switch, for example, of the mechatronic component Limit switch. Only mechatronic components can be integrated into a project (see [Inserting mechatronic components in a project](eecbase_k_insert_mcs.htm)). The ECAD structure is then derived automatically from the mechatronic configuration (see [Generate structure](eecbase_k_linking_dcs_generate_structure.htm)).

parent_eececad_k_insert_page_and_fragmen.title: Inserting pages and fragments
parent_eececad_k_insert_page_and_fragmen.content:
# Inserting pages and fragments

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting pages and fragments

Pages and fragments are added via the popup menu of the corresponding object in the project or via Drag & Drop directly from the library. The following graphic shows how to add a page via the popup menu:

The Page classes Finder dialog opens.

In this example, we choose the Lights page. This inserts the Lights page into the schematic.

Lamps can also be added on this page.

Lamps are added, like pages, via the popup menu or via Drag & Drop.

parent_eececad_k_insert_schematic_object.title: Inserting schematic objects via Finder
parent_eececad_k_insert_schematic_object.content:
# Inserting schematic objects via Finder

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting schematic objects via Finder

Requirement: An empty project has already been created, and the library with ECAD objects has been imported.

Using the popup menu of the empty project, select the menu item Add > ECAD > Schematic:

The Finder dialog for wiring diagram classes appears. The desired objects can be located in the Finder and inserted:

Use the [Search] button to display the available wiring diagrams. Confirm your selection with [OK] to create a root of the "ECAD" discipline as well as an instance of the selected schematic in the project.

parent_eececad_k_management_of_pagestruc.title: Management of page structure properties
parent_eececad_k_management_of_pagestruc.content:
# Management of page structure properties

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Management of page structure properties

Engineering providers, for example, due to different customer requirements, have to create wiring diagrams with different **Page structures** (e.g., numbering by higher-level function number/ location designation) from within a modular system. The objective behind the modular system draft is to design schematic pages in such a way that they can be deployed in schematics with different numbering concepts. This requires that a schematic page must be able to use formulas to determine the page structure in which it will be used.

Page structure properties can be modeled using the **Schematic object**. This way, alternative structural concepts can be realized in a model.

Since the page structure properties are defined in the **P8 wiring diagram templates** , it is also important to note that these definitions can be imported into the EEC model.

Thus, it is possible to manage the page structure properties in the schematic template and to import it into EEC model by way of synchronization.

All page structure properties supported by P8 are known to EEC as model variables (see [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_modelvar_eplanp8.htm)).

The model variables specify the names of the parameters that represent the corresponding properties in EEC, e.g., the parameter for the page number of the eponymous property.

Whether this property is actually used to generate a schematic will be defined only by the schematic template. If the page structure with higher-level function number and location designation is defined there, EEC, too, will only use these properties.

The settings from the schematic template are adopted in the EEC model by synchronizing a schematic object with the external resource, the schematic template (basic project template) (see [Synchronization of discipline components](eecbase_k_synchronize_disciplinecomponents.htm)).

The properties of the basic project template are set up in the schematic object as parameters according to the settings in the model variables.

The modeling of the properties depends on the existence of parameters. Schematic pages, for example, query the schematic object whether there is a parameter for the location designation in order to take a decision regarding the numbering (the values of these parameters have no effect).

The following formula is used to check the existence of parameters and adjust the page numbering accordingly:
    
        =if dc.parameter('Location').isNull() then

    ...

    else

    ...

    endif

The previous option of setting the page structure globally in the settings does not exist anymore.

### Note

Note the DT syntax check setting in the project template. It also applies to the creation of pages. For example, if only numerals and uppercase letters are permitted, and if you want to create a page with the property $Location=St01, this will result in unexpected errors when creating a schematic. The same applies to editing the macros.

### [Detached representation of substructures](javascript:void\(0\);)

By default, the page navigator of P8 displays both function designation and location designation in a single level in the tree view.

Example for the default representation of the tree for a schematic page with a location designation of the notation main identifier.Sub-identifier.Sub-sub-identifier:

In order to achieve a broken-up representation of the substructures, the File > Settings > Projects > "Project name" > Display > Tree structure (pages) check box Detached representation of substructures in P8 is to be selected.

With this setting the tree for the same schematic of the example is displayed broken down:

In order for a schematic to be generated with this setting, the basic project template (*.zw1) has to be created again with this setting and assigned to the schematic component in EEC. If the name of the basic project template is unchanged, the resource for the schematic component only has to be synchronized in EEC.

Depending on the selected separator in the P8 project, the value of the parameter for the location designation has to be built up.

For the example shown above the value of the parameter for the location designation ='HK1.UK1.UUK1' for page 1 PLC_Sensors_1_8 and ='HK1.UK2.UUK2â for page 2 PLC_Sensors_9_16.

### Note

The name of the parameter for the location designation must be entered in the model variables!

parent_eececad_k_mapping_partsdata.title: Collecting parts data in a map
parent_eececad_k_mapping_partsdata.content:
# Collecting parts data in a map

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Collecting parts data in a map

The parts data of the parts database can be read out and saved in a parameter called partsData. You limit the scope of the parts data to be read in by means of specifications in a further parameter called partsDataFilter.

The corresponding commands and actions are provided in the PartsData_Customizing library.

When the Get Parts Data action is executed, EEC generates a call for a C# script with arguments that correspond to the specifications in the partsDataFilter parameter in the <Installation folder>\workspace\PartsData\ExportParts.cs file and then executes the call. The parts data read out are temporarily stored in the <Installation folder>\workspace\PartsData\parts.xml file and transferred as a map into the partsData parameter.

The parts data can be evaluated further with the known methods of the formula language.

To implement reading out the parts database into an existing model:

    1. Import the <Installation folder>\install\partsdata\PartsData_Customizing.eox file.
    2. The PartsData_Customizing library is now located in the library catalog.
    3. Import the library PartsData_Customizing into the library of the mechatronics of your model.
    4. The commands and action can now be used in the mechatronic model.
    5. Create the partsData and partsDataFilter parameters with the Map type and add them to any mechatronic component.

To read out the parts data:

    1. Click Get Parts Data in the popup menu of the project component with the partsData parameter.
    2. The parts data are read out and stored in the partsData parameter.

To restrict the parts data to be read in:

    1. Enter a map with the following syntax for the partsDataFilter parameter.
        
                =Map{Pair{'PROPERTY_1','Value_1'},Pair{'PROPERTY_2','Value_2'},Pair{'PROPERTY_n','Value_n'}}

Example:
        
                =Map{Pair{'ARTICLE_MANUFACTURER','PXC'},Pair{'ARTICLE_PRODUCTGROUP','3'},Pair{'ARTICLE_DESCR1','Feed-through terminal with spring-cage connection'}}

### Note

Repeated reading-out of the parts database is not efficient. The action should be executed on a component of the highest level. All other components can use the formula language to extract their required values from the map in this component.

### Read more

     * [Map](refformulas_r_map_methods.htm)

parent_eececad_k_page.title: Creating Pages
parent_eececad_k_page.content:
# Creating Pages

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating Pages

An object of the **Page** type is created via the popup menu New > ECAD > Page.

The selection of the above action will open the following editor:

The editor of a new page is opened via the Attributes editor page.

A resource (called a window macro in the terminology of P8) is assigned to the object via [Import]. During the assignment of the resource, the file name of the resource is entered automatically as the object name. This can be modified if necessary.

If page properties are to be calculated in EEC, the page properties (except for structure identifiers and page names) must be captured via a placeholder object in the same macro.

The following figure shows the properties dialog of a placeholder object for a page in P8.

The page properties can be inserted into the table via the  (New) symbol. Then the corresponding variables can be assigned to the page properties.

An object of the Page type can be saved only if a resource has been assigned, or if the object is highlighted as abstract.

In general ECAD disciplines can be completely installed from a schematic (a component of the WiringDiagram type) and any number of pages (components of the Page type) without having to further divide the pages into fragments. Necessary variants are specified via different pages and activated/deactivated via Disabler (see [Disabler](eecbase_k_variantmanagement_disabler.htm)).

parent_eececad_k_page_inheritance.title: Inheritance of pages
parent_eececad_k_page_inheritance.content:
# Inheritance of pages

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inheritance of pages

If several pages have the same basic properties, and if there are rules (formulas) that are common to all pages, it is often useful to combine the common elements in an abstract page.

An abstract page thus defines a classification level from which specific pages of the modular system are derived. The derived pages are assigned the basic properties defined for the abstract page automatically.

A Page object that is set to Abstract on the Attributes page can be saved also without a resource.

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_connection.title: Parameterizing connection point designations
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_connection.content:
# Parameterizing connection point designations

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing connection point designations

The connection point designations of a macro are controlled by means of a variable in the placeholder object.

To this purpose the user has to agree a variable for the <20038> Connection point designations (all) property in P8.

The value for the connection point designation is specified by using the syntax ='Connection point1;Connection point2;Connection pointX'.

The following table shows how a variable is agreed for the <20038> Connection point designations (all) property in P8 using a relay coil as an example:

Row | Property | Current value | Variable  
---|---|---|---  
19 | <20038> Connection point designations (all) | A1;A2 | <ConnectionPointDesignation>  
  
As a result the macro with this placeholder object has the parameter ConnectionPointDesignation.

In the schematic the value ='A3;A4' results in a symbol with the connection points A3 and A4:

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_ecad_objec.title: Parameterizing ECAD objects
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_ecad_objec.content:
# Parameterizing ECAD objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing ECAD objects

The basic principles of the parameterization of objects are explained in the section [Parameterizing library objects](eececad_k_parameterize_library_object.htm).

The following figure shows a wiring diagram object in which two constants have been created.

The parameters of the wiring diagram object can be accessed within the ECAD structure via the formula droot.<Parameter>.

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_library_ob.title: Parameterizing library objects
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_library_ob.content:
# Parameterizing library objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing library objects

The following figure shows the Lights library object.

Fixed values (such as Lamp1) or formulas (such as =dc.dos.indexOf(this)+1) can be entered in the Value column.

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_plug_socke.title: Parameterizing plugs and sockets
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_plug_socke.content:
# Parameterizing plugs and sockets

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing plugs and sockets

The lights in the example can contain up to 5 lamps, which is why the page defines 5 sockets. At each socket, a lamp can be docked via its plug. Each socket must have a name that is unique within the component (Lamp1 to Lamp6).

To place a lamp on the lighting, its plug called EP (=placeholder) must be set to the value of a socket, for example, Lamp1. The variant (A or B) is calculated with a if-then-else loop for the parameter of the same name.

The following image shows the mechatronic project components on top in which the calculation of the parameter values take place and the discipline-specific project component below that adopts the calculated parameter values.

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_project_ob.title: Parameterizing project objects
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_project_ob.content:
# Parameterizing project objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Parameterizing project objects

When using an ECAD object in a project, the constant value is applied or the formula is calculated:

Parameters whose values do not derive from library specifications must be parameterized in a project-specific manner (e.g., Lamp1, Lamp2, etc.).

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_properties.title: Setting properties for projects or pages
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_properties.content:
# Setting properties for projects or pages

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

# Setting properties for projects or pages

There are several possibilities for setting properties for projects or pages:

    1. You use a parameter that uses the prefix of the [Prefix For Eplan Electric P8 Properties](admin_r_modelvar_p8propertiesprefix.htm) model variables in the name. The syntax is: PropId_<property number>.

### Example for property without index

The parameter name used to set the value for the <1002> Structure description property is PropId_1002.

A parameter of the String type is sufficient for properties without index.

### Example for property with index

The parameter name used to set the value for the <11901 1> Supplementary field[1] property is PropId_11901.

A parameter of the Map type is to be used for properties with index.

The key-value pairs specify the index and its value.

Example for the properties of <11901 1> Supplementary field[1] and <11901 2> Supplementary field[2]:

=Map{Pair{'1', 'SupplementaryFieldText_1'}, Pair{'2', 'SupplementaryFieldText_2'}}.

    1. You are using a parameter of the PropertyMap type. The PropertyMap contains either a list of values or a map with pairs of indexes and values of the properties. In the case of a PropertyMap with a list of values, the position of the value in the list corresponds to the index of the property.

### Example for PropertyMap with list

Example for the properties of <11901 1> Supplementary field[1], <11901 2> Supplementary field[2], <11901 4> Supplementary field[4], as well as <11057 1> Macro description[1]:

=Map{Pair{'11901', List{'SupplementaryFieldText_1','SupplementaryFieldText_2','','SupplementaryFieldText_4'}, Pair{'11057', 'MacroDescriptionText'}}.

### Example for PropertyMap with map

Example for the properties of <11901 1> Supplementary field[1], <11901 2> Supplementary field[2], <11901 4> Supplementary field[4], as well as <11057 1> Macro description[1]:

=Map{Pair{'11901', Map{Pair{'1','SupplementaryFieldText_1'},Pair{'2','SupplementaryFieldText_2'},Pair{'4','SupplementaryFieldText_4'}}}, Pair{'11057', 'MacroDescriptionText_1'}}.

For path 1, key-value pairs are transferred as the value, whereby the key corresponds to the name of the user supplementary field.

Example for User supplementary field[2]:

ProjectSupplementaryFields=Map{Pair{'Eplan.Project.UserSupplementaryField2','230/400VAC'}}

A string is specified as the value for path 2.

Example for User supplementary field[2]:

PropId_40002='230/400VAC'

### Note

If both ways are used to set the value of the same user supplementary field, the value of the parameter specified via the model variable is always used.

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_supplement.title: Supplementary fields
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_supplement.content:
# Supplementary fields

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Supplementary fields

The values of supplementary fields can be transferred by means of parameters of the type Map. The name of the parameter has the following syntax:

PropId_<Property ID>

The values are transferred as key-value pairs, whereby the key corresponds to the index of the user supplementary field and the value corresponds to the value of the supplementary field.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Create a parameter of the type Map with the name PropId_10901.
    2. Add the parameter to the discipline component of the WiringDiagram type.
    3. Enter the value =Map{Pair{'1','110 VAC'},Pair{'2','230 VAC'},Pair{'3','400 VAC'}} for the parameter.
    4. Generate the schematic.

In Eplan Electric P8 the result for the project properties looks as follows:

Property name | Value  
---|---  
Supplementary field [1] | 110 VAC  
Supplementary field [2] | 230 VAC  
Supplementary field [3] | 400 VAC

parent_eececad_k_parameterize_usersupple.title: User supplementary fields
parent_eececad_k_parameterize_usersupple.content:
# User supplementary fields

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)  
  
You are here: 

## User supplementary fields

The values of user supplementary fields can be set in two ways:

    1. By means of a Map type parameter. The name of the parameter has to be specified in the model variable Name of the parameter for user supplementary field.
    2. By means of a String type parameter. The name of the parameter follows the syntax PropId_<ID of the user supplementary field>.

For path 1, key-value pairs are transferred as the value, whereby the key corresponds to the name of the user supplementary field.

Example for User supplementary field[2]:

ProjectSupplementaryFields=Map{Pair{'Eplan.Project.UserSupplementaryField2','230/400VAC'}}

A string is specified as the value for path 2.

Example for User supplementary field[2]:

PropId_40002='230/400VAC'

### Note

If both ways are used to set the value of the same user supplementary field, the value of the parameter specified via the model variable is always used.

parent_eececad_k_plug_socket_concept_for.title: Plug-socket concept in the schematic
parent_eececad_k_plug_socket_concept_for.content:
# Plug-socket concept in the schematic

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Plug-socket concept in the schematic

The conceptual approach of the plug-socket concept is explained in the section [Plug-Socket concept](concept_k_plug_socket_concept.htm). The following section explains the use of the concept with Eplan Electric P8.

The plug-socket concept fulfills two functions in the context of the ECAD discipline. On the one hand, it can be used to describe a discipline-specific structure (logical placement), and on the other hand, it represents the correctly positioned placement of the macros / fragments to each other (graphical placement).

Logical placement:

The logical link between the electro-technical objects is established by way of the **plug-socket concept**. For example, if a page has a socket, different plugs within a project can reference it. This results in a logical, discipline-specific project structure. When the ECAD structure is created, the ECAD objects are placed logically.

Graphical placement:

The plug-socket concept is also used to arrange fragments with partial circuits in the correct position to each other on a schematic page. **Sockets** represent the placeholder for fragments in this case. Fragments can be docked to these placeholders. On a fragment, a plug represents the handle. In the placement, the extension points of the free texts of the socket and the plug are aligned with each other. This is done while generating and opening the Eplan Electric P8 project from within the ECAD structure.

### Notes

     * No plug may be placed on one page.
     * Only one plug may be placed on one fragment.
     * Any number of sockets can be placed on pages and fragments.

Plugs and sockets are entered in the schematics as free text, and included only in the macro content, but not in a placeholder object. By importing the macro as a resource, they become the parameter of the respective ECAD object. The data type of the parameter is, then, Socket or Plug.

Plug:

Syntax:
    
        #<Plug:Parametername>

Example:

Socket:

Syntax:
    
        #<Socket:Parametername>

Example:

When opening a schematic, plugs and sockets are connected to each other. Here, the graphical elements of the resources concerned are arranged in such a way that the extension points of the text elements Socket and Plug lie perfectly on top of each other. Socket and Plug are not created from the parameter name, but from the values set in the parameters. In Eplan Electric P8, connections are created automatically via the integrated "Autoconnecting" function.

Please note that the calculated values of plugs and sockets in the mechatronic structure may sometimes differ from those in the discipline structure. For example, the **this** method yields different values. If **this** is used as an argument in the method **indexOf()** , it is possible that the result delivers a value of -1 (not found) in the discipline if the collection to which the method is applied does not contain the ECAD structure object.

parent_eececad_k_preparation_of_ecad_pag.title: Modularization of ECAD pages
parent_eececad_k_preparation_of_ecad_pag.content:
# Modularization of ECAD pages

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Modularization of ECAD pages

To modularize ECAD pages, it is essential that two tasks be carried out. First, the entire pages are broken down into meaningful partial circuits in the form of window macros. Second, the generated values are set up as parameters. Eplan Electric P8 uses the placeholder object to define variables. The variables thus defined become the parameters of the ECAD object when the resource is imported. During the generation of the schematic, they can then be calculated from within EEC and filled with concrete values.

Parameters can be assigned for many elements of the schematic, for example, for terminal designations, structure identifiers/device tags, technical characteristics or page numbers.

In practice defining multiple placeholder objects if many objects are to be parameterized has to proved to be successful. The parameters are read out jointly in EEC. Thus, nothing changes in EEC if several or only one placeholder object is used.

Rule:

The syntax #<Parametername> may be used only for the definition of the plugs and sockets, because they are not assigned to any placeholder object. All other parameters in the placeholder objects use the following syntax by default in Eplan Electric P8:

<Parameter name>

Text objects:

Further properties of a parameter can be entered in text objects, but they will be evaluated only if the parameter does not yet exist up to the time the document is read in. **Type** , **Default value** , and **Comment** can be entered in the sequence.

The syntax then follows this pattern:
    
        <Type:Parameter name=Default value//Comment>

Example:
    
        <String:Location=St01Insert//MountingLocation>

This means that Type, Default value, and Comment are independent of each other, and each is optional.

When a resource is assigned or edited and then saved, the content is read in and the parameter sequences are evaluated. Then, the ECAD discipline determines the parameters that already exist and which parameters have yet to be created. In the latter case, the appropriate parameters are created, while taking all of the entries into account.

In the process, missing entries for optional information, i.e. "Type", "Default value", and "Comment", are replaced by the following values:

Statement | Value  
---|---  
Type | String  
Default value | <empty>  
Comment | <empty>  
  
If a new parameter has been created, or if a parameter already exists with the stated name, it will be assigned to the text object whose resource has just been read in.

parent_eececad_k_schematics.title: Creating schematics
parent_eececad_k_schematics.content:
# Creating schematics

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating schematics

A discipline component of the super class WiringDiagram is created via the popup menu New > ECAD > Schematic.

The effective discipline components are defined in the following editor:

The editor starts with the editor page Attributes.

A resource is assigned via [Import]. Here, as a rule a project template created in Eplan Electric P8 in the *.zw9 format is linked. Alternatively you can also link saved projects in the *.zw1 format. This linked file is used at the opening of the entire schematic or a schematic page from within EEC and contains all relevant master data and settings for the target project.

The name given automatically to the discipline component is the file name of the resource, but this can be changed manually (Note: The name must be unique within a **unit**!). The new discipline component is created by saving in the editor.

For the project structure parameters must be added to the discipline component. The parameter names are to be assigned to the respective model variables (see [Model variables](admin_r_modelvariables.htm) > [Disciplines](admin_r_modelvar_disciplines.htm) > [ECAD](admin_r_modelvar_ecad.htm) > [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_modelvar_eplanp8.htm)).

parent_eececad_k_structure_identifier_ma.title: Structure identifier management in EplanÂ ElectricÂ P8
parent_eececad_k_structure_identifier_ma.content:
# Structure identifier management in EplanÂ ElectricÂ P8

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Structure identifier management in Eplan Electric P8

The management of structure identifiers in Eplan Electric P8 (for example, "Function designation" [preceding sign "="] and "Location designation" [preceding sign "+"]) affects two important areas of the electrical engineering documentation, which may also operate independently of each other.

    1. The order of schematic pages in a project.
       * The order of the schematic pages becomes visible in the page navigator, and is also the output order for printing and export (PDF, DXF etc.).
    2. The order of the schematic objects (devices, terminal strips, plugs, PLCs, cables, etc.) in a project.
       * The order of schematic objects influences the sequence in the navigators for devices, terminal strips, plugs, PLCs and cables.
       * Another, and partially more important, effect of the order of the above schematic objects becomes visible in the logic evaluations of the schematics, e.g., the source and target of a cable in the cable layout.

Both the schematic pages and schematic objects can contain structure identifier independently of each other. Thus, it is possible to find structure identifiers in a schematic object for which there is no schematic page.

If a new schematic page or new schematic object is created in Eplan Electric P8 (regardless of whether directly or via EEC), all new structure identifiers will be entered in the central structure identifier management. In the process, only new structure identifiers are created, and always, by default, added to the end of the list.

The management of the structure identifiers is opened in Eplan Electric P8 via the tab Tools > Command group Management > Structure identifier.

The following table shows an example of structure identifier management (tab List):

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
2 | B |  |  | No |   
3 | A |  |  | No |   
4 | CA |  |  | No |   
5 | CA1 |  |  | No |   
  
The order of the entries in structure identifier management is the determining order both for the schematic pages and also for all other schematic objects where structure identifiers play a role.

Previously, structure identifiers could be moved from within EEC only via the schematic pages in Eplan Electric P8. In the process the structure identifiers in accordance with the order of creation of the schematic pages in EEC (the order of the schematic pages in the ECAD structure of EEC project) were entered into the Eplan Electric P8 structure identifier management.

image/svg+xml ECAD Schematic Page2 Page1 Mechatronic EEC ECAD Schematic Page1 Page2 Mechatronic EEC Plant1 Location1 1 Page 1 P8 =Plant2+Location2/1 Plant2 Location2 1 Page 2 Schematic Plant2 Location2 1 Page 2 P8 Plant1 Location1 1 Page 1 Schematic =Plant1+Location1/1 =Plant2+Location2/1 =Plant1+Location1/1

In many cases is necessary for the identifier structure to have a different order in Eplan Electric P8 than that created by default via the creation of schematic pages from EEC. Therefore the structure identifiers need to be manipulated independently of the creation of schematic pages and / or schematic objects.

Generally, there are two ways of manipulating structure identifiers:

    1. Before the schematic pages and / or schematic objects in a schematic are created.

The structure identifiers (their order and any descriptions) are created first in an empty project. All schematic pages / schematic objects that are created in the schematic afterwards (and that contain existing structure identifiers) are sorted according to this order.

    1. After the schematic pages and / or schematic objects in a schematic are created.

Here, the order and, if appropriate, the description of the structure identifiers in a schematic in which pages and objects already exist is changed.

### Note

In general all structure identifiers can be assigned to the devices in the EEC. Depending on the number of pages and objects in the schematic this can take longer.

### [Example for change in the structure identifier](javascript:void\(0\);)

Structure identifier management before:

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
2 | B |  |  | No |   
3 | A |  |  | No |   
  
Structure identifier management afterwards:

Row | Complete structure identifier | Structure description | Original description | Usage | Status  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1 | <Empty identifier> |  | <Empty identifier> | ? |   
2 | A | Higher-level function 1 |  | No |   
3 | B | Higher-level function 2 |  | No |   
  
Changing the order of the structure identifiers before the schematic pages / schematic objects are created:

To change the order of the structure identifiers from within EEC before the schematic pages / schematic objects are generated, a corresponding model variable is available for each structure identifier:

Parameter | Default name of the parameter  
---|---  
Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Function Designations | SKFunktionskennzeichen  
Parameter Name For Equipment Number List | SKAnlagennummer  
Parameter Name For Installation List | SKAufstellungsort  
Parameter Name For User-defined List | SKBenutzerdefiniert  
Parameter Name For Document Kind | SKDokumentenart  
Parameter Name For The Sequence Of Location Designations | SKOrtskennzeichen  
Parameter Name For Functional Assignment List | SKFunktionaleZuordnung  
  
The output value is a list that consists either only of designations or pairs that are composed of a designation and a description.

### [Example for List](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        =List{'Anlage1', 'Anlage2', 'Anlage3'}

### [Example for Map](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        =List{Pair{'A', 'de_DE@Beschreibung A;en_US@Description A;'}, 

    	Pair{'B', 'de_DE@Beschreibung B;en_US@Description B;'}, 

    	Pair{'C', 'de_DE@Beschreibung C;en_US@Description C;'}}

If the generation process adds further structure identifiers, they will be added to the existing ones in Eplan Electric P8. If an order already exists for a structure identifier in structure identifier management, EEC will try to merge the two lists.

For example, if the order is already defined in the project template as âBâ, âCâ, and EEC requests âAâ, âCâ, these elements do not contradict each other in terms of the order, and after the generation process, this produces either the list with âAâ, âBâ, âCâ or âBâ, âAâ, âCâ, because the order between âAâ and âBâ has not been defined.

### Read more

     * [Structure of the device tag](../../../../Plattform/2025/Content/htm/devicetaggui_k_aufbauvonbm.htm)

parent_eececad_k_support_of_placeholder_.title: Support for placeholder objects with value sets
parent_eececad_k_support_of_placeholder_.content:
# Support for placeholder objects with value sets

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Support for placeholder objects with value sets

Requirements:

     * A parameter specification with the notation #<Parameter name> is inserted in the Name field in the Placeholder object tab.

### Note

No more than one parameter definition may be entered in the Name field of the placeholder object.

To define a value set, the name of the parameter has to be entered in the variant table and corresponding values have to be specified. When reading in the resource a parameter by the same name is generated, whose selection values correspond to the entries in the variant table.

If the assigned value set is missing, or EEC does not find the value set due to differing uppercase / lowercase spelling, an error message appears and the generation process is aborted.

During the generation of the schematics, the portion #<> of the parameter definition is removed from the placeholder name. For example, the name #<ValueSet> becomes the new name ValueSet.

If the model variable Write macro parameter structure in parameter is set to the value true, a parameter whose name is specified in the model variable Name of the parameter for the macro parameter structure is inserted in the discipline component when the macro is read in. The parameter stores a list of maps containing, among other things, the names of the existing value sets.

In the settings, it is possible to define that the macro boxes are retained so that the user has the macro-specific popup menu items (Update macro... etc.) available at the macro box. This option can be set separately for window macros and symbol macros. Both options are disabled by default.

Similarly you can specify the removal of placeholder objects created in the schematics in the settings.

### Read more

     * [Eplan Electric P8](admin_r_prefs_ecad_p8.htm)
     * [Parameter Name For The Macro Parameter Structure](admin_r_modelvar_p8macrodata.htm)
     * [Write Macro Parameter Structure In Parameter](admin_r_modelvar_p8writemacrostructure.htm)
     * [Creating Placeholder Objects](../../../Plattform/2025/Content/htm/macrosgui_h_platzhalterobjekteerzeugen.htm)

parent_eececad_k_support_of_window_macro.title: Support for variants of window macros
parent_eececad_k_support_of_window_macro.content:
# Support for variants of window macros

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Support for variants of window macros

Window macros with all representation types and variants are supported. Window macros are identified by the representation type (Neutral, Single-Line, Multi-Line, Graphic, etc.) and the variant (A, B, C, etc.). Corresponding model variables exist for these parameters. When importing a window macro, the parameters Representation type and Variant are generated, provided they exist in the window macro. A Selection value is created for the parameter of the variants for each available variant.

The use of window macros has the advantage that only one discipline component with one resource has to be created. In the parameter view of the discipline component, the parameters Representation type and Variant (if available) must be added, and, for example, filled with a permissible value via a formula.

### Note

The same parameters should be created in all variants of a window macro, because formulas that reference missing parameters of a variant would otherwise cause aborting during generation of the schematic.

### Note

During the synchronization of the external resource, EEC collects all the parameters from all variants. It is not an error if a parameter of the same type appears in different variants. If it is missing from a variant, it is not an error either.

If during the generation process EEC finds a window macro with several variants, it will use the model variables Name Of The Parameter For The Representation Type of Window Macros and Name Of The Parameter For The Variant of Window Macros in order to select the correct variant. If these model variables are not specified, the generation process is terminated, and a corresponding error message is shown.

### Read more

     * [Parameter Name For The Window Macro Variant](admin_r_modelvar_p8variant.htm)
     * [Parameter Name For The Window Macro Representation Type](admin_r_modelvar_p8representationtype.htm)

parent_eececad_k_switch_from_p8_to_eec.title: Navigating from a macro in P8 to a discipline component in EEC
parent_eececad_k_switch_from_p8_to_eec.content:
# Navigating from a macro in P8 to a discipline component in EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Navigating from a macro in P8 to a discipline component in EEC

During the development phase the possibility of navigating from a macro in the generated schematic to the discipline component in the mechatronic modular system is helpful.

### Note

If multiple instances of EEC are open, only one component in the first instance of EEC is jumped to.

Requirements:

     * An action for P8 is set up.
     * The optional P8 Integration Service Add-On function is installed (see [EEC P8 Integration Service Add-On](admin_h_installation_optionalfeature_p8_integration.htm)).
     * The option Delete placeholders after generation is deactivated in the settings.

### [Setting up Action for P8](javascript:void\(0\);)

    1. Open Eplan Electric P8.
    2. Open the popup menu of the toolbar.  

    3. Click Customize ribbon.
    4. Select Further commands (tools) in the drop-down of the left selection window.
    5. Click Open the component in EEC.
    6. In the right selection window select the tools subgroup after which the new EEC group is to be inserted, e.g. Synchronize.
    7. Click New command group, to create the EEC group.
    8. Assign a name to the new command group, for example EEC.
    9. Mark the new command group.
    10. Click the blue arrow  pointing towards the right to insert the command Open component in EEC in the new command group.
    11. Confirm with OK to finish the creation of the group.
    12. The new EEC group is now displayed in the ribbon on the right next to the Synchronize group.

If a schematic has been generated successfully, you can navigate from each macro of the schematic to the corresponding discipline component in the configuration.

    1. Select a macro in the schematic.
    2. Click Open component in EEC in the EEC toolbar.
    3. The corresponding discipline component opens in EEC.

parent_eececad_k_synchronize_project_pro.title: Synchronization of project properties
parent_eececad_k_synchronize_project_pro.content:
# Synchronization of project properties

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Synchronization of project properties

Eplan Electric P8 project properties can be calculated in EEC and transferred to the Eplan project to be created.

Transfer of project data from EEC to Eplan Electric P8:

The properties of an Eplan Electric P8 project can be configured in EEC and transferred at the time of generation. Eplan-specific project properties have unique identifying numbers (ID) (for example 10230) as well as a name. To allow the EEC user access to all possible properties, while remaining independent of the Eplan Electric P8 version used, EEC establishes the reference to the project properties via the ID.

### Note

Project properties have been introduced in Eplan Electric P8 as of Version 2.5 that dispose of an identifying name instead of an identifying number, for example [ESS] Report control <ESS.Project.Reports>. These cannot be influenced by EEC by means of model variables!

Setting project properties from EEC:

     * Display project property IDs in Eplan Electric P8:

To display the IDs of the project properties in Eplan Electric P8, the following setting has to be carried out in Eplan Electric P8: Options > Settings... activate the switch User > Representation > Interface: Display identifying numbers.

     * Determining project property IDs:

Select the project and then select in the menu Project > Properties .... All properties whose value column is not grayed out can be written from EEC, for example 20919 ("order number").

Note: Some properties that cannot be edited in Eplan Electric P8 can be set via Eplan Electric P8 API.

     * Generate a parameter object for project property in EEC:

The project properties are mirrored in EEC by parameter objects whose names consists of two parts: The common prefix and a number, for example, PropId_20919.   
The prefix applies to the entire model and can be modified in EEC in the Model > Model variables > ECAD > Eplan Electric P8 > Prefix for Eplan Electric P8 properties menu.

     * Assigning parameter objects to schematic diagram objects:

Project properties are defined in EEC by assigning the parameter objects with the name scheme mentioned above to the wiring diagram objects. During the generation the values are transferred to Eplan Electric P8 automatically.

     * Setting parameter values:

There are two types of properties: normal and indexed properties. Normal properties contain the value in EEC as a string. Indexed properties are assigned data of the data type List resp. Map. The index always starts at 1. Prior to the access, the API checks whether the permissible range has been exceeded.

Examples of access:
    
        PropId_10150=List{Pair{1,'Initiale Version'},Pair{2,'Testdaten geÃ¤ndert'}}
    
        PropId_10230='KL'

An object of the Schematic type can be saved only if a resource has been assigned or if the object is marked as "abstract". An abstract object is used to define a classification level in order to generate objects that are derived from an abstract object with all the properties of the latter automatically.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges.title: Edges
parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges.content:
# Edges

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Edges

Edges are used to represent connections between project components in a diagram, e.g. a Gantt chart representing the connections between implementations.

The appearance of edges is highly customizable by means of attributes and router schemes (see [connection](refgraph2d_r_connection.htm)).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges_delete_referen.title: Deleting references to nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges_delete_referen.content:
# Deleting references to nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting references to nodes

If a project component that has references to parent and/or child components is deleted from a diagram or model, references to the deleted components should also be deleted from the parent and/or child components.

The following example code defines a command with the type deleteNodeCommand which deletes the node of the type Execution (1) and additionally with the action ClearParameterReferenceAction (2) the value of the parameter with the reference to the successor (4) in the ancestor component (3).

After deletion of the node of the type Execution (1) the parameter Successor (4) in the ancestor component (3) is deleted.

If further parameter values have to be deleted, further actions with the same command must be configured.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges_simple_connect.title: Simple connections
parent_eecgraph2d_k_edges_simple_connect.content:
# Simple connections

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Simple connections

Simple connections serve to depict 1:1 references between two nodes, e.g. parent-child references.

This is illustrated using the example of two roller conveyors in the âBodyshell workâ demo model. In the planning diagram, a reference from parent SK1RLB1 (1) to child SK1RLB2 (2) is represented by a green arrow.

The parent node stores a reference to the child node in the Child parameter, and the child node stores a reference to the parent node in the Parent parameter (see [connection](refgraph2d_r_connection.htm)).

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <reference id="ReihenfolgeReferenz" type="connectable" src="=isInstanceEO()

    	and isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Funktion')"

    	target="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Funktion')">

    	<forward multiplicity="*" connector="=getParameter('Nachfolger')"/>

    	<backward multiplicity="*" connector="=getParameter('VorgÃ¤nger')"/>

    </reference>

    <connection id="Reihenfolge" reference="ReihenfolgeReferenz"

    	width="3" style="1" color="17,240,70"

    	sourceDeco="empty"

    	targetDeco="filled-arrow"/>

Connections between nodes can be created both manually and automatically.

Creating connections manually:

    1. Select a palette entry for âConnectionsâ (Order in the above screenshot)
    2. Click the first node.
    3. Click the second node.

Connections can be created continuously by simultaneously holding down [SHIFT].

Creating connections automatically:

If the ordering of the nodes derives, for example, from the ordering of the corresponding project components in the model structure, the connections are automatically inserted into the diagram.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor.title: Graphical editor
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor.content:
# Graphical editor

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Graphical editor

Diagrams of the type specified in the configuration (planning diagram or Gantt chart) are created using the graphical editor. Once a diagram has been inserted into a project, the graphical editor appears with the editing area empty.

The graphical editor for planning diagrams consists of the following elements (see the following diagram):

1\. Toolbar,

2\. Palette, and

3\. Diagram area.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_commands.title: Inserting and moving project components via commands
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_commands.content:
# Inserting and moving project components via commands

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting and moving project components via commands

In the diagram configuration, the <command> tag can be used in connection with the following name-space expansions to define which components from the project tree may be inserted via drag and drop, which components from the palette may be instantiated, and which components may be moved from one superordinate component to another:

     * Command for inserting from the project:
    
        <command xsi:type="createNodeForInstance">

     * Command for inserting from the modular system:
    
        <command xsi:type="createNodeForClass">

     * Command for instantiating from the palette:
    
        <command xsi:type="instantiateAndCreateNode">

     * Command for moving a component from one superordinate component to another:
    
        <command xsi:type="changeInstanceParent">

     * which graphical objects may be dragged from one diagram into another (request="Change_parent" attribute).

### [Example code for the definition of an element that may be dragged into the diagram, creating a new instance in the process](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <command source="=isClassEO() and (absoluteName='Rohbau_Baukasten.Mechatronik.Schutzkreis')"

    	target="Diagram"

    	request="Drop_and_instantiate" />

### [Example code for the definition of an element that may be dragged into the diagram](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <command source="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	target="Diagram"

    	request="Drop_at_target" />

### [Example code for the definition of an element that may be dragged from one diagram into another](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <command source="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Baukasten.Mechatronik.Roboter')"

    	target="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	request="Change_parent" />

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_create_contin.title: Generate connections successively
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_create_contin.content:
# Generate connections successively

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting graphical objects and components from the diagram

Deleting a graphical object from the diagram with the [Del] key or via Context menu > Delete from the diagram does not affect the model; it only deletes the graphical object. However, if a graphical object in a diagram is deleted by pressing [Shift]+[Del] or via Context menu > Delete from the model, the project component will also be deleted from the model.This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting graphical objects and components from the diagram

Deleting a graphical object from the diagram with the [Del] key or via Context menu > Delete from the diagram does not affect the model; it only deletes the graphical object. However, if a graphical object in a diagram is deleted by pressing [Shift]+[Del] or via Context menu > Delete from the model, the project component will also be deleted from the model.

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Generate connections successively

With [Shift] pressed, it is possible to insert connections successively. When [Shift] is released, this function ends once the last connection has been completed.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_delete_object.title: Deleting graphical objects and components from the diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_delete_object.content:
# Deleting graphical objects and components from the diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting graphical objects and components from the diagram

Deleting a graphical object from the diagram with the [Del] key or via Context menu > Delete from the diagram does not affect the model; it only deletes the graphical object. However, if a graphical object in a diagram is deleted by pressing [Shift]+[Del] or via Context menu > Delete from the model, the project component will also be deleted from the model.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_dialgram_zoom.title: Zoom for the diagram editing area
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_dialgram_zoom.content:
# Zoom for the diagram editing area

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Zoom

## Zoom for the diagram editing area

A zoom factor of between 5% and 3200%, along with width, height, and page can be set for viewing a diagram (see the following image).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_ellipses.title: Ellipses
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_ellipses.content:
# Ellipses

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Ellipses

In the configuration file, it is now possible to define ellipses for the figure class.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Schutzkreis"

    	super="Komponente"

    	valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	acceptedChildren="Funktion LabelAsSlave">

    	<figureEllipse figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.container.Ellipse">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="showLabel">

    				<read value="true"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boColor">

    				<read value="62,12,144"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="transparency">

    				<read value="80"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="bColor">

    				<read value="255,255,255"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boWidth">

    				<read value="4"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefWidth">

    				<read value="250"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefHeight">

    				<read value="100"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureEllipse>

    	<property id="text">

    		<read value="=$Bezeichnung"/>

    		<write key="value" receiver="=getParameter('Bezeichnung')"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

With the definition in the example code, an instance of the safety circuit is represented in the diagram as an ellipse. The size and shape of the ellipse can be changed by dragging the bendpoints (see the following figure).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_filtering_gra.title: Filtering graphical objects
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_filtering_gra.content:
# Filtering graphical objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Filtering

## Filtering graphical objects

The use of filters in the project catalog affects how project components are represented in the project catalog and how graphical objects are depicted in the project diagram.

The following screenshots show the Planning_Diagram of the âBodyshell workâ demo model, first without and then with an activated filter. So that the filter can hide the robots from the project and hence from the diagram as well, the âRobotâ component in the modular system is assigned the category Visibility > private.

Without the filter activated, the diagram displays all of the robots:

With the filter activated, the diagram does not display the robots:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_insert_diagra.title: Insert diagram in project
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_insert_diagra.content:
# Insert diagram in project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Insert diagram in project

In the diagram configuration, the user can define which position in the project a diagram can be inserted at. A diagram can be inserted in two ways at the defined position.

     * By dragging and dropping (instantiating) the diagram definition into the project (see the following screenshot).

     * By selecting Insert > Diagram name from the context menu of the component.

For the Bodyshell Work project, two diagram types (Planning_Diagram and Procedure_Diagram) have been configured, so that both diagrams can be inserted for a single Plant.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_insert_projec.title: Inserting project components
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_insert_projec.content:
# Inserting project components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting project components

To insert a project component that is already available in the model into the diagram, select the component and drag it into the editing area (see the screenshot below).

Selecting a superordinate component and inserting it into a diagram, e.g. a protective circuit in the âbodyshell workâ Planning_Diagram, does not add the subordinate components to the diagram; each component has to be inserted explicitly into the diagram.

In the flowchart of the âbodyshell workâ model, inserting a component (1) for each activity (forwards and backwards) creates a so-called Swimlane (2), as illustrated in the following diagram of a roller conveyor:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_instantiation.title: Instantiation of components
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_instantiation.content:
# Instantiation of components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Instantiation of components

To insert a new instance of a component into the project, select the component in the palette and instantiate it in the editing area with a single mouse click (see the following screenshot).

After the diagram is saved, the new project component (in this example, Robot3) is instantiated in the project (see the screenshot below).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_master_slave.title: Master-slave
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_master_slave.content:
# Master-slave

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Master-slave

Those of a diagramâs nodes that are not connected via edges but are functionally or declaratively related, for example a graphical object and a label, are configured as master and slave in such a way that the slave appears together with the master in the diagram as a unit.

The slave is typically positioned on the outer contour of the master (outside-edged).

The following screenshot illustrates as master a roller conveyor (1.) from the Bodyshell work planning diagram, and as slave the label (2.), which displays a small icon, the designation and the parameter for processing time.

Another example is a damper (1.) from a thermodynamic diagram, whose slaves (2.) indicate the direction of the current.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_nodes_edges_d.title: Nodes and edges diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_nodes_edges_d.content:
# Nodes and edges diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Nodes and edges diagram

EEC diagrams are node-edge diagrams, meaning that the graphical objects of the components are treated as nodes and the connections (routes) between the graphical objects are treated as links (see the following figure).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_palette.title: Palette
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_palette.content:
# Palette

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Palette

The palette appears at the right edge of the diagram editor, but can be docked at any position.

It primarily contains selection tools (1.). When planning diagrams are being edited, the palette also contains notational tools (2.).

In addition, groups (3.), components for instantiation in the diagram (4.), and connections (5.) can be listed in the diagram definition (see the following illustration).

The context menu (see the following illustration) can be used to select how palette elements should be displayed.

Possible settings are:

     * Layout > Columns displays one icon per column with text (see the above diagram).
     * Layout > List standard view, displays icons and texts.
     * Layout > Icons Only displays only icons.
     * Layout > Details displays icons, texts and tooltips.
     * Use large icons Both a small icon (smallIcon attribute) and a large icon (largeIconattribute) can be defined for each palette entry in the diagram configuration. Which icon is displayed depends on the setting.
     * Settings Opens the âsettingsâ dialog for the palette. The font for the texts can be set here. Furthermore, this dialog can be used to set whether the palette should close automatically when another palette is opened or when there isnât enough space.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_polygone.title: Polygons
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_polygone.content:
# Polygons

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Polygons

In the configuration file, it is possible to define polygons for the figure class that are specified in terms of the number of node points (bendpointsCount attribute). Furthermore, the bendpointsModifiable attribute can be used to specify whether the project engineer can insert additional bendpoints.

### [Example code for the definition of the safety circuit as a hexagon](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Schutzkreis"

    	super="Komponente"

    	valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	acceptedChildren="Funktion LabelAsSlave">

    	<figurePolygon figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.container.Polygon">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="showLabel">

    				<read value="true"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boColor">

    				<read value="62,12,144"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="transparency">

    				<read value="80"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="bColor">

    				<read value="255,255,255"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boWidth">

    				<read value="4"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefWidth">

    				<read value="250"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefHeight">

    				<read value="250"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="bendpointsCount">

    				<read value="6"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="bendpointsModifiable">

    				<read value="true"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figurePolygon>

    	<property id="text">

    		<read value="=$Bezeichnung"/>

    		<write key="value" receiver="=getParameter('Bezeichnung')"/>

    	</property>

    /node>

The size and shape of the polygon can be changed by dragging the corners (see the following figure).

To interactively change the number of bendpoints and hence the shape of a polygon, select the polygon and then press [P]. Between each pair of node points at the corners (bendpoints), a small bendpoint appears in the middle of the straight line.

Inserting a bendpoint:

Pressing [P] and dragging a bendpoint from the middle of a straight line causes a new bendpoint to be created.

Deleting a bendpoint:

Pressing [P] and dragging a bendpoint from one corner onto another corner causes two straight lines to become one straight line and the overlapping bendpoints to become a single bendpoint.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_print.title: Print function for diagrams
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_print.content:
# Print function for diagrams

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Printing
