## Print function for diagrams

It is now possible to print graph-UIs. The printout can be optimized using the âprint previewâ function. For example, the diagram can be scaled to fit a single page.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_select_change.title: Selecting and changing edges
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_select_change.content:
# Selecting and changing edges

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Selecting and changing edges

Existing edges may be selected with a left click. A selected edge indicates its start point (1.) and endpoint (2.) (see the screenshot below).

With these points, the edges can be connected (routed) to other nodes. To do so, select a point and drag it onto another object. In the following screenshot, the endpoint of the edge is dragged from roller conveyor SK1RLB2 to SK1RLB3. Because we are dealing here with a type of connection that always takes the shortest path, the new connection is routed without a bendpoint.

If the edge is a polyline configured to allow changes to be made to bendpoints, each bendpoint can be moved as desired. The following screenshot illustrates a connection between roller conveyor SK1RLB2 and SK2RLB1 via a polyline whose bendpoints have been moved arbitrarily.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_select_move_r.title: Selecting, moving and rotating nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_select_move_r.content:
# Selecting, moving and rotating nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Selecting, moving and rotating nodes

Select an individual node or edge by left-clicking the object. To select multiple nodes or edges, either click them one after another while holding down the [Shift] key or drag a frame around the objects as shown in the following screenshot. To accomplish this, click and hold the left mouse button in the first corner of the frame and then move the mouse diagonally to the opposite corner. When the mouse button is released, all of the objects contained within the frame are selected.

Another option is to select the first object with the mouse and then add other objects to the selection by pressing the [Shift] and [Arrow] key.

If an object is selected, it can be moved with the mouse or with the following key combination: decimal point + arrows. The same can be done with multiple objects selected, without changing their positions in relation to each other.

If an object is configured to allow rotation, it can be rotated via popup menu > Rotate > 90Â° to the right or popup menu > Rotate > 180Â° clockwise or with the following key combinations: [Ctrl] \+ [R] for clockwise and [Ctrl] \+ [L] for counterclockwise. When an object is rotated, the new upper-left corner occupies the same position as the old upper-left corner, as illustrated in the following screenshot of a robot being rotated 90Â° to the right. The arrow points to the upper-left corner.

For the diagram in the screenshot above, the node for the robot is configured as master and its label is configured as slave. To ensure that the slave remains legible, it is not rotated, but instead retains its position on the lower side (SOUTH) of the master. For more information on masters and slaves see [Nodes as master with slave](eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with_slave.htm).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_timeline_zoom.title: Zoom for the timescale of a Gantt chart
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_timeline_zoom.content:
# Zoom for the timescale of a Gantt chart

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Timescale

## Zoom for the timescale of a Gantt chart

The timescale of a Gantt chart can be changed by zooming in and out (see the following screenshot). By selecting Time Zoom > Zoom In or Zoom Out in the context menu, the timescale can be stretched or compressed. Time Zoom > No zoom can be used to reset the zoom factor of the timescale back to value defined in the configuration file.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_toolbar.title: Toolbar
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_toolbar.content:
# Toolbar

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Toolbar

The toolbar offers the following functions:

     * Undo: Undoes the last change.
     * Repeat: Repeats the last change.
     * Zoom factor: Sets the editing area to the selected zoom factor.
     * Background image: Opens a dialog for selecting a background image for the editing area.
     * Alignment: Aligns the selected nodes. A selection can be opened via the arrow to the right of the icon.

The selection icons (see the following illustration) can be used to align the selected nodes with one another or with the diagram-editing area.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_user_defined.title: User-defined graphic editors
parent_eecgraph2d_k_editor_user_defined.content:
# User-defined graphic editors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## User-defined graphic editors

A diagram configuration is created as an XML file in the modular-system library. It determines the appearance of the diagram and the behavior of nodes and links. A guide to the individual XML elements used in configuring diagrams may be found in the [GRAPH2D](refgraph2d_r_graph2d.htm) reference section.

Multiple diagram configurations can be created, e.g. to make it possible to create both a planning diagram and a Gantt chart.

The following diagram presents a schematic of an information model whose library contains a diagram configuration. An excerpt from the diagram configuration reveals its structure. In the project, a diagram used in the graphical configuration can be inserted at a predefined position in the model. The diagram can represent all or part of the project.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_graph2d.title: GRAPH2D
parent_eecgraph2d_k_graph2d.content:
# GRAPH2D

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

##  GRAPH2D

The GRAPH2D discipline enables developers to configure a diagram editor for node-edge diagrams. With the aid of a node-edge diagram, a project engineer can configure a plant or machine graphically.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_as_inst.title: Instantiating nodes from the tool palette and inserting them into the diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_as_inst.content:
# Instantiating nodes from the tool palette and inserting them into the diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Instantiating nodes from the tool palette and inserting them into the diagram

In addition to standard elements, the tool palette can also contain nodes and edges. When a palette entry is selected and the mouse cursor is moved over the diagram, a command is simultaneously run to determine which placeholder or extension point can receive the new project component. The appearance of the mouse cursor changes accordingly to indicate where the new node may be inserted.

The new node is positioned in the diagram via a left click, and the project component is simultaneously inserted at the specified placeholder.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)

A palette entry and a command for instantiating from the tool palette:
    
        <paletteEntry id="RoboterCreation" group="Komponenten" text="Roboter"

    	smallIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.

    	Planungs_Diagramm.Werkzeugpalette_Roboter_Objekterstellung').getImage"

    	largeIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.

    	Planungs_Diagramm.Werkzeugpalette_Roboter_Objekterstellung_24').getImage">

    	<eoCreation command="command3"/>

    </paletteEntry>

    <command xsi:type="instantiateAndCreateNode" src="=isClassEO() and  
    	(absoluteName='Rohbau_Baukasten.Mechatronik.Roboter')"  
    	eoClassPath="Rohbau_Baukasten.Mechatronik.Roboter" id="command3"/>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_automat.title: Inserting nodes automatically
parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_automat.content:
# Inserting nodes automatically

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting nodes automatically

Slave nodes can be automatically inserted into the diagram in two ways.

    1. Master nodes and corresponding slave nodes are instantiated in the project structure. Next, the master nodes are manually inserted into the diagram, causing the corresponding slave nodes to be automatically inserted as well.
    2. Slave nodes whose master nodes have already been inserted into the diagram are then instantiated in the project structure. When the diagram is opened, they are specified in accordance with the configuration and then placed at the appropriate master node.

In order to distinguish automatically inserted slave nodes after the diagram has been opened, a special color can be set for their borders. The slave nodes retain this one-color designation until the diagram has been saved (CTRL+S).

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)

When master nodes are inserted manually, the salve nodes are inserted automatically. These slave nodes are displayed with a green border:
    
        <node id="Station" super="Komponente"  
     valid="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Mechatronik.RoboterStation')"  
     slaveNodes="=mos('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Mechatronik.Roboter')">

    	<figureImage figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.Image">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="img">

    				<read value="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Diagramm_Konfiguration.  
     				Planungsdiagramm.Register').image(this)"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="highlightBoColor">

    				<read value="0,255,0"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureImage>

    	<slaveNode node="Roboter" autoUpdated="false" layout="outsideEdged"  
     	direction="=$diagramDirection" parent="=mc">

    		<movement rotateWithMaster="NO" rotate="NO"/>

    	</slaveNode>

    </node>

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)

Slave nodes are inserted automatically into the diagram subsequently. These slave nodes are displayed with a green border:
    
        <node id="Station" super="Komponente"  
     valid="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Mechatronik.RoboterStation')"  
     slaveNodes="=mos('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Mechatronik.Roboter')">

    	<figureImage figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.Image">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="img">

    				<read value="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten_V2.Diagramm_Konfiguration.  
     				Planungsdiagramm.Register').image(this)"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="highlightBoColor">

    				<read value="0,255,0"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureImage>

    	<slaveNode node="Roboter" autoUpdated="true" layout="outsideEdged"  
     	direction="=$diagramDirection" parent="=mc">

    		<movement rotateWithMaster="NO" rotate="NO"/>

    	</slaveNode>

    </node>

parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_from_dy.title: Inserting nodes from dynamic tool palette
parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_from_dy.content:
# Inserting nodes from dynamic tool palette

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting nodes from dynamic tool palette

If the project structure contains instances that still have to be inserted in the diagram, they can be collected in the palette by means of an appropriate configuration and can then be inserted from the palette into the diagram. The <nodeCreation> element creates a palette entry for each project component that can be specified by means of a formula for the entries attribute. Selecting the palette entry attaches the node to the mouse cursor, allowing it to be placed in the diagram via a left click.

If a node, which is determined by a formula of the attribute entries is deleted from the diagram, it is put back to the palette.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)

All the nodes of the project components of the BodyshellWork_Architecture.LevelComponents.Safety_circuit type are determined and those inserted into the palette that have not yet been placed in the âBodyshellâ work diagram.
    
        <paletteEntry id="SchutzkreisCreation"

    	group="Komponenten" text="=$Bezeichnung"  
    	

    	smallIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Planungs_Diagramm.  
    	Werkzeugpalette_Schutzkreis_Objekterstellung').getImage"  
    	

    	largeIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Planungs_Diagramm.  
    	Werkzeugpalette_Schutzkreis_Objekterstellung_24').getImage">  
    	

    	<nodeCreation entries="=rmos('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.  
    	Schutzkreis')"/>  
    </paletteEntry>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_from_pr.title: Inserting nodes from the project structure into the diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_from_pr.content:
# Inserting nodes from the project structure into the diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting nodes from the project structure into the diagram

If there are already components in the project structure, they can be inserted into the diagram via drag and drop. When dragging and dropping components, a check is run to determine where in the diagram they can be inserted.

The following illustration shows a robot being positioned in the Planning_Diagram of the âBodyshell workâ demo model via drag and drop.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_in_diag.title: Inserting nodes into a diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_insert_nodes_in_diag.content:
# Inserting nodes into a diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Inserting nodes into a diagram

Nodes can be placed in a diagram both manually and automatically. Automatic positioning is limited here to nodes from components that are already available in the project structure.

Nodes from existing project components can be positioned manually. However, by means of an action, project components can also be instantiated immediately before the node is positioned.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_node_decorators.title: Decorating nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_node_decorators.content:
# Decorating nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Decorating nodes

Node decoration can be configured at a superordinate level and overwritten by each specific node definition.

Decorators are as follows:

     * Border color when unselected (boColor),
     * Border color when selected (selectedBoColor),
     * Border width (boWidth),
     * Distance from content to border (boMargin),
     * Border line style (boLineStyle),
     * Background color when unselected (bColor),
     * Background color when selected (selectedBColor),
     * Border color of an automatically inserted slave node (autoSlaveBoColor),
     * Border color of slave nodes when the corresponding master node is selected (highlightBoColor),
     * Font color when unselected (fColor),
     * Font color when selected (selectedBColor),
     * Predefined width (prefWidth),
     * Predefined height (prefHeight),
     * Transparency (transparency)

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes.title: Node
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes.content:
# Node

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Node

In general, the nodes represent the components of a project.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_editable_te.title: Node as editable text element
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_editable_te.content:
# Node as editable text element

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Node as editable text element

The attributes of a component can also be represented as nodes This enables important attribute values to be displayed in diagrams, e.g. as slave nodes directly on a master node.

The following example illustrates how the name of a component can be displayed and modified with the help of a slave node.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Funktion" super="Komponente" slaveNodes="=this">

    	<figureImage figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.Image"/>

    	<slaveNode id="LabelAsSlave" node="LabelAsSlave" super="Komponente"

    		valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    		isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Funktion')"

    		layout="outsideEdged" direction="SOUTH" parent="=this">

    		<figureLabel figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.Label">

    			<properties>

    				<property id="text">

    					<read value="=name"/>

    					<write key="name" receiver="this"/>

    				</property>

    				<property id="prefWidth">

    					<read value="100"/>

    				</property>

    				<property id="prefHeight">

    					<read value="30"/>

    				</property>

    			</properties>

    		</figureLabel>

    		<property id="showLabel">

    			<read value="true"/>

    		</property>

    		<movement rotateWithMaster="NO" rotate="NO"/>

    	</slaveNode>

    </node>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_ellipse.title: Node as ellipse
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_ellipse.content:
# Node as ellipse

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Node as ellipse

In order to depict a node as an ellipse, the element <figureEllipse> must be set as a sub-element of <node>.

In addition to the basic properties for nodes, the width and height of the ellipse may be set using the following specific properties: <property id="prefWidth"> and <property id="prefHeight">.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Schutzkreis"

    	super="Komponente"

    	valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	acceptedChildren="Funktion LabelAsSlave">

    	<figureEllipse figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.container.Ellipse">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="showLabel">

    				<read value="true"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boColor">

    				<read value="62,12,144"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="transparency">

    				<read value="80"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="bColor">

    				<read value="255,255,255"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="boWidth">

    				<read value="4"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefWidth">

    				<read value="250"/>

    			</property>

    			<property id="prefHeight">

    				<read value="100"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureEllipse>

    	<property id="text">

    		<read value="=$Bezeichnung"/>

    		<write key="value" receiver="=getParameter('Bezeichnung')"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

Result:

With the definition in the example code, an instance of the safety circuit is represented in the diagram as an ellipse. The size and shape of the ellipse can be changed by dragging the handles. If the [Shift] key is held down while a corner handle is dragged, the size is modified without changing the original aspect ratio. The [Ctrl] \+ [Shift] key combination changes the size of the node from the midpoint of the node (coaxial).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.title: Nodes as master with slave
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.content:
# Nodes as master with slave

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Nodes as master with slave

If a direct dependency exists between components in the mechatronic structure such that the components should always be displayed together, they can be represented as master and slave nodes. If a master node is inserted into the diagram, its slave node is always inserted with it, nor can the latter be deleted without also deleting the former.

The slave node is arranged at a predetermined position on the border of the master node (see [Orientation of master and slave](eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with_slave_orientation.htm)). This position can be changed later on.

If master is moved, the slave node remains in the same position relative to the master. If the master node is rotated, the orientation of the slave node relative to the master node may change (see [Rotation of master and slave](eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with_slave_rotation.htm))

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.title: Slave nodes as docking points for master nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.content:
# Slave nodes as docking points for master nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Slave nodes as docking points for master nodes

Master nodes can be docked together with the help of slave nodes, provided the slave nodes are configured as docking points. This requires that the slave node be designated a docking point via the snapPoint="true" attribute, that it be aligned on the outer contour of the master via the layout="onEdge" attribute, and that it be positioned on the outer contour of the master via the position attribute. The mechatronic component must have a parameter for storing the object reference.

### [Sample code for a master node with slave nodes as docking points](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="abstractBelt" slaveNodes="=mos">

    	<figureBase>

    		<properties>

    			<property id="resizable">

    				<read value="true" />

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureBase>

    	<slaveNode id="startSlave" node="snapPointStart"

    	parent="=mc" layout="onEdge" direction="=$Direction"

    	creation="byMaster" snapPoint="true" position="50%" />

    	<slaveNode id="endSlave" node="snapPointEnd"

    	parent="=mc" layout="onEdge" direction="=$Direction"

    	creation="byMaster" snapPoint="true" position="50%" />

    	<slaveNode id="stopperSlave" node="stopperApp"

    	parent="=mc" layout="outsideEdged" creation="byMaster"/>

    </node>

    <reference id="ReihenfolgeReferenz" type="connectable"

    	src="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('SnapPoint.Mechatronic.ConnectionPoints.BeltConnectionPointStart')"

    	target="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('SnapPoint.Mechatronic.ConnectionPoints.BeltConnectionPointEnd')">

    	<forward multiplicity="1" connector="=getParameter('Reference')" />

    	<backward multiplicity="1" connector="=getParameter('Reference')" />

    </reference>

Result:

To begin with, the nodes to be connected are stored in the diagram editor. Then a node is moved over the nodes to be connected until red cross-hairs appear (see the below screenshot). The object reference for this connection is stored in the âreferenceâ parameter.

To delete this connection, hold down the [Alt] key and move the node away from the connected nodes.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.title: Orientation of master and slave
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.content:
# Orientation of master and slave

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Orientation of master and slave

The positioning of the slave node on the border of the master node may be specified in the configuration of the master node. The principle for positioning on the outside edge of the master node is called outside-edged, while positioning on the inside edge is called inside-edged (not currently supported).

A slave node is initially positioned at one of the cardinal directions relative to the master node as specified by the direction attribute of the <slaveNode> element. Which cardinal direction a slave node may be moved to manually in relation to the master node is specified by the direction attribute of the <movement> sub-element.

Sample code for aligning slave nodes to a master node:

The example of the thermodynamic model demonstrates how to align inputs and outputs as slave nodes that are always aligned in relation to the master node so as to ensure that the airflow direction is maintained.

In the configuration of the master node (id=âAbstractThermodynamicComponent"), the slaveNodes=â=rmos(âThermodynamic_Architecture.ConnectionPoint')" attribute indicates that all subordinate components of the ConnectionPoint type are to be aligned as slave nodes.

Nodes with node=âAirInputâ and nodes with nodes=âAirOutputâ are all defined as slave nodes. Their orientation in relation to the master node is specified by the direction="WEST" and direction="EAST" attribute.

### [Sample code for a master node with slave nodes as docking points](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="AbstrakteThermodynamikKomponente"

    	super="Komponente"

    	slaveNodes="=rmos('Thermodynamik_Architektur.Anschluss')"

    	valid="=isInstanceOf('Thermodynamik_Architektur.IThermodynamikFunktion')">

    	<figureImage>

    		<configuration>

    			<editRequestHandler xsi:type="formui:formui-handler"/>

    			<editRequestHandlerInfo xsi:type="formui:formui-handler-info"

    			refId="TDD"/>

    		</configuration>

    		<properties>

    			<property id="img">

    				<read value="=type('Thermodynamik_Baukasten.

    					Diagramm_Konfiguration.Bildregister').image(this)"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figureImage>

    	<slaveNode node="LuftEingang" layout="outsideEdged" direction="WEST"

    		parent="=mc">

    		<movement direction="EAST SOUTH WEST NORTH" rotate="YES"/>

    	</slaveNode>

    	<slaveNode node="LuftAusgang" layout="outsideEdged"

    		direction="EAST" parent="=mc">

    		<movement direction="EAST SOUTH WEST NORTH" rotate="YES"/>

    	</slaveNode>

    </node>

    <node id="ThermodynamischerAnschluss" super="Komponente"

    	valid="=isInstanceOf('Thermodynamik_Architektur.Anschluss')">

    	<property id="prefWidth">

    		<read value="20"/>

    	</property>

    	<property id="prefHeight">

    		<read value="20"/>

    	</property>

    	<property id="boColor">

    		<read value="0,0,0"/>

    	</property>

    	<property id="boWidth">

    		<read value="0"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

    <node id="LuftEingang" super="ThermodynamischerAnschluss"

    	valid="=isInstanceOf('Thermodynamik_Baukasten.Basis.LuftEingang')">

    	<property id="img">

    		<read value="=type('Thermodynamik_Baukasten.

    		Diagramm_Konfiguration.Bildregister').image(this)"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

    <node id="LuftAusgang" super="ThermodynamischerAnschluss"

    	valid="=isInstanceOf('Thermodynamik_Baukasten.Basis.LuftAusgang')">

    	<property id="img">

    		<read value="=type('Thermodynamik_Baukasten.

    		Diagramm_Konfiguration.Bildregister').image(this)"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.title: Positioning slave nodes on the edge of master nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.content:
# Positioning slave nodes on the edge of master nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Positioning slave nodes on the edge of master nodes

If no position attribute has been assigned for slave nodes, all slave nodes that have the same value for the direction attribute are distributed evenly along the length of the edge of the master node. The position of a slave node on the edge of a master node can be specified via the position attribute either as a percentage or in pixels. As the value increases, the position of the slave node changes in a clockwise direction along the edge of the master node (see the below diagram).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.title: Rotation of master and slave
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_master_with.content:
# Rotation of master and slave

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Rotation of master and slave

Both master nodes and slave nodes can be rotated in the diagram, provided the configuration allows this. If and how the slave node can be rotated is defined by a combination of two specifications, as the following diagram illustrates.

The rotation is selected in the diagram via the context menu of the node. Nodes can be rotated 90Â° to the right or 180Â° (see the following diagram).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_polygon.title: Node as polygon
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_as_polygon.content:
# Node as polygon

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Node as polygon

In general, all that is needed to represent a polygon is the number of corner points (bendpointsCount). The number of bendpoints determines the basic form of the polygon, from a triangle to a polygon with any number of sides.

### [Example code for a rectangle](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Schutzkreis" valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')">

    	<figurePolygon figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.container.Polygon">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="bendpointsCount">

    				<read value="4"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figurePolygon>

    </node>

Result:

The number of bendpoints can be edited in the diagram, if the property bendpointsModifiable is inserted and set to true.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_nested.title: Nested nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_nested.content:
# Nested nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Nested nodes

Nodes are also arranged in nested order within a diagram in accordance with the hierarchy of the mechatronic structure. For example, in the planning diagram for the âBodyshell workâ model, roller conveyors and robots can only be positioned within a safety circuit. To this end, a list of all subordinate nodes is defined in the configuration of the superordinate node by means of the acceptedChildren attribute.

Sample code for a nested structure:

This example illustrates the nesting of a safety circuit and robots in the planning diagram for the âBodyshell workâ demo model. The relevant parts of the code are in boldface. Every node that corresponds to the configuration of Function and LabelAsSlave is permitted as a subordinate node of SafetyCircuit. In this example, the node for robots is configured as a Function and is therefore a permitted sub-element of SafetyCircuit.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <node id="Schutzkreis" super="Komponente" valid="=isInstanceEO() and

    	isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Schutzkreis')"

    	acceptedChildren="Funktion LabelAsSlave">

    	<figurePolygon figure="com.mind8.graph2d.figure.container.Polygon">

    		<properties>

    			<property id="bColor">

    				<read value="255,255,255"/>

                </property>

    			<property id="boLineStyle">

    				<read value="1"/>

                </property>

                <property id="showLabel">

    				<read value="true"/>

                </property>

                <property id="boColor">

    				<read value="62,12,144"/>

                </property>

                <property id="boWidth">

    				<read value="4"/>

                </property>

                <property id="bendpointsCount">

    				<read value="4"/>

                </property>

                <property id="bendpointsModifiable">

    				<read value="true"/>

    			</property>

    		</properties>

    	</figurePolygon>

    	<property id="text">

    		<read value="=$Bezeichnung"/>

    		<write key="value" receiver="=getParameter('Bezeichnung')"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

    <node id="Roboter"

    	super="Funktion"

    	valid="=isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Baukasten.

    	Mechatronik.Roboter')">

    	<property id="img">

    		<read value="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.

    		Planungs_Diagramm.Register').image(this)"/>

    	</property>

    </node>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_simple.title: Simple nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_nodes_simple.content:
# Simple nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Simple nodes

The simplest display format for a node is a polygon, an ellipse or an individual graphical object. A simple node represents an individual component of a project.

parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_comp.title: Palette entries for components
parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_comp.content:
# Palette entries for components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Palette entries for components

Components that are to be inserted into the diagram via a palette entry, thus creating an instance in the project, must have the following attributes and sub-elements:

     * id attribute: a unique name for the palette entry.
     * group attribute: Name of the group in which the palette element is incorporated.
     * text attribute: Text displayed in the palette.
     * smallIcon attribute: Icon that is displayed when the Use large icons setting is deactivated (size 16 x 16 pixels).
     * largeIcon attribute: Icon that is displayed when the Use large icons setting is activated (size 24 x 24 pixels).
     * eoCreation sub-element, command attribute: unique name of the command for instantiating the component.

The following sample code presents a complete definition for an individual palette entry. The definition of the command is also included, to illustrate the connection between the two definitions.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <paletteEntry

    	id="Abschluss"

    	group="Bauteile"

    	text="HausabschlÃ¼sse"

    	smallIcon="=type('Wasserversorgung_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Bilder.abschlussSmallIcon').getImage"

    	largeIcon="=type('Wasserversorgung_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Bilder.abschlussLargeIcon').getImage">

    	<eoCreation command="command9"/>

    </paletteEntry>

    <command xsi:type="instantiateAndCreateNode"

    	src="=isClassEO() and (absoluteName='Wasserversorgung_Baukasten.Bauteile.Abschluss')"

    	eoClassPath="Wasserversorgung_Baukasten.Bauteile.Abschluss"

    	id="command9"/>

Result:

The following screenshot shows the palette entry with a large icon (right) and a small icon (left).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_conn.title: Palette entries for connections
parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_conn.content:
# Palette entries for connections

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Palette entries for connections

Components that are to be connected together manually in a diagram require a palette entry that creates both a connection line in the diagram and a reference between the components to be connected.

The following code snippet begins by describing which project components (instances of Function) can be connected by means of parameters (Child and Parent).

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <reference id="ReihenfolgeReferenz" type="connectable"

    	src="=isInstanceEO() and

    		isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Funktion')"

    	target="=isInstanceEO() and	

    		isInstanceOf('Rohbau_Architektur.Ebenenkomponenten.Funktion')">

    	<forward multiplicity="*" connector="=getParameter('Nachfolger')"/>

    	<backward multiplicity="*" connector="=getParameter('VorgÃ¤nger')"/>

    </reference>

This code snippet, which is also referenced, specifies how the connections are to be displayed:

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <connection id="Reihenfolge"

    	reference="ReihenfolgeReferenz"

    	width="3"

    	style="1"

    	color="17,240,70"

    	sourceDeco="empty"

    	targetDeco="filled-arrow"/>

The palette entry references the code fragment for displaying the connections. The Order text is displayed as a palette entry, along with either a small icon (smallIcon) or a large one (largeIcon), depending on the setting.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <paletteEntry id="ReihenfolgeVerbindung"

    	group="Verbindungen"

    	text="Reihenfolge"

    	smallIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Planungs_Diagramm.

    		Werkzeugpalette_Reihenfolge_Verbindung').getImage"

    	largeIcon="=type('Rohbau_Baukasten.Diagramm_Konfiguration.Planungs_Diagramm.

    		Werkzeugpalette_Reihenfolge_Verbindung_24').getImage">

    	<connectionCreation connection="Reihenfolge"/>

    </paletteEntry>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_grou.title: Grouping palette entries
parent_eecgraph2d_k_palette_entries_grou.content:
# Grouping palette entries

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Grouping palette entries

In addition to selection tools (Select and Marquee) and note-taking tools (Sticky Note and Sticky note connection) for planning diagrams, the palette can also contain any number of entries for instantiating components and creating connections between them.

To distinguish these entries visually, they are assigned to groups, as illustrated in the following sample code. The symbol definition has been left out of the code to make the example clearer.

### [Example code](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <paletteEntry id="Rohrverbindung" group="Verbindungen" text="Rohrverbindung" ...>

    	<connectionCreation connection="Wasserstrom"/>

    </paletteEntry>

    <paletteEntry id="Abschluss" group="Bauteile" text="HausabschlÃ¼sse" ...>

    	<eoCreation command="command9"/>

    </paletteEntry>

    <paletteEntry id="Behaelter" group="Bauteile" text="BehÃ¤lter" ...>

    	<eoCreation command="command8"/>

    </paletteEntry>

    <paletteEntry id="Rohre" group="Bauteile" text="Rohr" ...>

    	<eoCreation command="command10"/>

    </paletteEntry>

Result:

parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes.title: Deleting nodes
parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes.content:
# Deleting nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting nodes

Nodes can be deleted in a way that either affects only the diagram and the project components represented in it or that also deletes the project components themselves.

When deleting project components that have connections to parents and/or children, care should also be taken to delete the references to these components in the corresponding parameters (see [Deleting references to nodes](eecgraph2d_k_edges_delete_references_to_node.htm)).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes_from_di.title: Deleting nodes from the diagram
parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes_from_di.content:
# Deleting nodes from the diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting nodes from the diagram

To delete a node from the diagram without simultaneously deleting the project component represented in the diagram, either select Remove from diagram (see the screenshot below) from the context menu or press the [Del] key.

This only causes references to parent and/or child components in the corresponding parameters to be deleted when permitted by the diagram definition (see [Deleting references to nodes](eecgraph2d_k_edges_delete_references_to_node.htm)).

parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes_from_pr.title: Deleting nodes from the project structure
parent_eecgraph2d_k_remove_nodes_from_pr.content:
# Deleting nodes from the project structure

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Deleting nodes from the project structure

Project components represented in the diagram may be deleted from the project structure together with nodes represented in the diagram by selecting Delete from model from the context menu.

This only causes references to parent and/or child components in the corresponding parameters to be deleted when permitted by the diagram definition (see [Deleting references to nodes](eecgraph2d_k_edges_delete_references_to_node.htm)).

parent_eecjobserver_k_job_results.title: Storing created files
parent_eecjobserver_k_job_results.content:
# Storing created files

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Storing created files

The created files of a Job are stored in a folder that is specified in the job definition (see [Job definitions](refjobserver_r_jobdefinitions.htm)). A distinction is made as to whether the job was completed successfully or whether an error has occurred.

### Note

The Job Server generally works on the basis of a model that is specified as an EOX file.

The Job Server only has read rights for the EOX file.

To save the changed model data, execute the command [Engineering.SaveObjectCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_saveobjectcommand.htm) and then export by using the command [Engineering.ExportEOXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_exporteoxcommand.htm) as an EOX file. The exported EOX file can be offered for download with the command [JobServer.MarkFileForDownloadCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_markfilefordownload.htm).

The commands have to be declared as [action](refjobserver_r_action.htm) in the job definition.

Below the folders specified, the Worker creates for each job a subfolder with the name of the job, followed by the timestamp.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        \\myShare\jobserver\Out\Job1234_1_140827_1523_55119\1234_Project.jmx

If the job instantiates a project, a result folder with subfolders for each instantiated project will be created below this folder. The name of the subfolder corresponds to the name of the project.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        \\myShare\jobserver\Out\Job1234_1_140827_1523_55119\results\1234_Project_1

    \\myShare\jobserver\Out\Job1234_1_140827_1523_55119\results\1234_Project_2

If the job creates target data for a discipline, they will be stored in subfolders named after the disciplines.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        \\myShare\jobserver\Out\Job1234_1_140827_1523_55119\results\Project_1\BOM\BOM1.txt

    \\myShare\jobserver\Out\Job1234_1_140827_1523_55119\results\Project_1\P8\Feeder.elk

Automatic deleting of files

The Job Server disposes of a time-controlled service for removing files. The service continuously deletes the following files and data whose moment of editing is older than 30 days:

     * Generated files of a Job.
     * All the log entries belonging to a Job.

parent_eecjobserver_k_joblogs_ui.title: User interface of the Job-specific Logs
parent_eecjobserver_k_joblogs_ui.content:
# User interface of the Job-specific Logs

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

User interface of the Job-specific Logs

## User interface of the Job-specific Logs

The user interface for the Job-specific Logs shows the logged events of the Job Server.

The Job-specific Logs are displayed in a separate browser window.

The log shows the messages in chronological order with the most recent one being at the top.

The messages are colored in accordance with their severity. Information is in black, the Job Server starts and stops in green, errors in red, warning orange and details in light gray.

The message can be displayed and hidden according to their severity by marking the filter of the same name.

The browser search function can be used to search through all messages.

parent_eecjobserver_k_jobs_ui.title: User interface of the Jobs
parent_eecjobserver_k_jobs_ui.content:
# User interface of the Jobs

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## User interface of the Jobs

The user interface for the Jobs shows all the Jobs with their current status, the Job name, the associated Job definition, the Worker, who is carrying out or has carried out the Job and the actions currently possible. The number of days after which Jobs are removed is displayed below the list. Jobs that are older than the displayed number of days are removed. The number of days can be set through an entry in the initialization file (ec.ini) (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.cleanup.days](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_cleanupdays.htm)).

By default, the list shows all the Jobs sorted chronologically, with the most recent ones at the top.

The entries in the list can be filtered according to Status, Priority, Job name, Job definition and Worker. The number of entries per page can be limited by using the buttons [10], [25], [50] and [100].

The list of jobs can be manipulated by means of actions. The arrangement of the jobs in the list changes through the actions:

Action/priority | Symbol | Meaning  
---|---|---  
Normal priority |  |  Every job initially has a normal priority. All jobs with the priority are processed according to the FIFO principle.  
Delete Job |  | Removes a Job from the list.  
Processed with highest priority |  |  No job is being processed currently: Moves the job to the highest position. This action moves other jobs with this priority one position downwards. A job is currently being processed: Moves the job to the position below the running job. This does not cause a running job to be aborted. This action moves other jobs with this priority one position downwards.  
Process with next highest priority |  |  No job with highest priority is available currently: Moves the job to the highest position. If this priority is assigned to further jobs, they are positioned below. A job with the highest priority is available: Moves the job to the position below a job with the highest priority. If this priority is assigned to further jobs, they are positioned below.  
Process with next lowest priority |  |  Moves the job to the lowest position. This action moves other jobs with this priority one position upwards.  
Open Job progress window |  | Opens a popup window with the progress information for the Job.  
Open Job log window |  | Opens the Job-specific log.  
  
The following statuses can be displayed for the Jobs:

Status | Symbol | Meaning  
---|---|---  
Queued |  | The Job Server has been started, the Job is located in the queue. As soon as a slot in the Job Server becomes free, the next Job in the queue is started.  
Running |  | The Job Server is executing a pending Job.  
FinishedSuccessfully |  | The Job has been completed successfully by the Job Server. The files of the Job have been copied into the agreed directory.  
FinishedInError |  | The Job Server reported an error while processing the Job. Processing has been aborted and the files of the Job have been copied into the agreed directory (see [failedFolder](refjobserver_r_failedfolder.htm)). An e-mail has been sent to predefined addresses. The contents of the error log can be inserted as a text into the e-mail (see [jobdefinition](refjobserver_r_jobdefinition.htm)).  
Cancelled |  | A Job was cancelled by the user during the execution.  
  
Additionally to these states, the job can

     * display a unique Job id,
     * display a Job name. There are two case differentiations:
       * fileTrigger: If the Job is located in the queue, the name of the file that triggered the FileTrigger is displayed. As soon as the Job leaves the queue, the Job name that is specified in the Job definition is displayed, extended by the time stamp.
       * webserviceTrigger: If the Job is located in the queue, a file name cannot be displayed. The post parameter js.queuedJobName can be used to transfer a Job name that is displayed during this state. For example, the URL http://localhost:8787/request/jobdef_web?js.queuedJobName=Job with SAP-ID: 2029338&file=C:\noscan\models\import-export-model\sample\MyProj.px is used to display the Job name Job with SAP-ID: 2029338. As soon as the Job leaves the queue, the Job name that is specified in the Job definition is displayed, extended by the time stamp.
     * List created files for the download (see [JobServer.MarkFileForDownloadCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_markfilefordownload.htm)).
     * Own a detailed status text. This can be modified during the execution by means of a command (see [JobServer.SetProgressMessageCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_setprogressmessagecommand.htm)).

In addition to these statuses a free status text can be output through a native command.

In addition, further information can be displayed that is created by a script during the execution of the Job and transferred to the browser.

For example, various results of the generation can be listed:

The data for the display are transferred as a Map. The API IJobAPI implements a method called getResultData() that initially returns a Map. The following Information to be displayed are to be attached to this Map.

### [Example code in Groovy](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        import com.mind8.mechatronic.skill.api.IJobAPI;

    resultData = IJobAPI.DEFAULT.getResultData();

    data = [

    ["Station 1 Ã¼berprÃ¼ft": "Material vorhanden"],

    ["Station 2 Ã¼berprÃ¼ft": "Material nicht vorhanden"],

    ["Station 3 Ã¼berprÃ¼ft": "Material vorhanden"]

    ];

    resultData.put("MaterialÃ¼berprÃ¼fung der Stationen", data);

### Tip

In principle the method resultData.put() can process any objects. Since the transferred objects are converted into the JSON format, only simple objects such as List, Map and String should be used.

By means of a HTML file the look of the status indication is adjusted to customer-specific requirements (see [jobStatusHtml](refjobserver_r_jobstatushtml.htm)).

parent_eecjobserver_k_jobserver.title: Job Server and Job Server Worker
parent_eecjobserver_k_jobserver.content:
# Job Server and Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server and Job Server Worker

The Job Server is used to control one or more Job Server Workers. Job Server Workers can generate files or execute long-running processes asynchronously in the configuration phase, on the basis of a model with corresponding actions and commands.

The Job Server simultaneously serves as a server for a user interface that can be accessed within the EEC through the Perspective Job Server or via a browser.

By default a Job Server Worker is installed and registered with its address at the Job Server. Further Job Server Workers can be installed and registered with their address at the Job Server. If more than one Job Server Worker is registered, the pending Jobs are executed in a distributed fashion.

The status of a Job Server Worker is always queried by the Job Server and is displayed in the user interface.

The created files of a Job can either be displayed for downloading via the user interface or be stored in an agreed folder (see [action](refjobserver_r_action.htm) and Mark file for downloading).

Each Job Server Worker delivers status information:

     * Existing Job definitions,
     * Pending Jobs,
     * Completed Jobs,
     * Aborted Jobs.

parent_eecjobserver_k_jobserver_ui.title: User interface of Job Server
parent_eecjobserver_k_jobserver_ui.content:
# User interface of Job Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

User interface of Job Server

##  User interface of Job Server

A user interface is available for Job control and administration of the Job Server. This can be displayed both in EEC and in a browser. When the Job Server is started, the Job Server perspective is opened automatically. For information on opening the Job Server perspective manually see [Perspectives](eecbase_k_perspectives.htm).

The URL specified in the address line is used for display in a browser.

The user interface provides the categories Workers, Logs and Jobs in the navigation bar.

parent_eecjobserver_k_logfile.title: Log file
parent_eecjobserver_k_logfile.content:
# Log file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Log file

During the processing of a Job definition, logging is carried out in the console and a log file.

If the Job cannot be processed successfully, a sub-folder with the name of the Job is created below the Folder for Failed Jobs (see User preferences for the Job Server). The log file is then written to this sub-folder.

If the Job is successful, a sub-folder with the name of the Job is created below the Folder for Output Data preferences for the Job Server. The log file is then written to this sub-folder.

If an e-mail is sent in the event of success or an error, it will contain a link with the address of the subfolder that contains the log file in order to allow for quick access to it.

parent_eecjobserver_k_logs_ui.title: User interface of the Logs
parent_eecjobserver_k_logs_ui.content:
# User interface of the Logs

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

User interface of the Logs

## User interface of the Logs

The user interface for the Logs shows the logged events of the Job Server.

The log shows the messages in chronological order with the most recent one being at the top. In addition the messages are divided into groups Today), This week) and Older than a week.

The messages are colored in accordance with their severity. Information is in black, the Job Server starts and stops in green, errors in red, warning orange and details in light gray.

The message can be displayed and hidden according to their severity by marking the filter of the same name.

If the time filter Today's jobs is used, only those messages are listed that occurred on the current day. This list is updated at regular intervals. The time filter All logs displays all the messages again.

The number of days after which log entries are removed is displayed below the list. Log entries that are older than the displayed number of days are removed. The number of days can be set through an entry in the initialization file (ec.ini) (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.cleanup.days](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_cleanupdays.htm)).

The browser search function can be used to search through all messages.

parent_eecjobserver_k_perspective_jobser.title: Perspective Job Server
parent_eecjobserver_k_perspective_jobser.content:
# Perspective Job Server

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

##  Perspective Job Server

Depending on whether it is being used by the Job Server or by the Worker the Perspective Job Server consists of the views Message Log, Console, Progress and the user interface of the Job Server (also see [Perspectives](eecbase_k_perspectives.htm)).

If the Perspective is used by the Worker, two buttons for controlling the Worker are located in the toolbar:

     * Start Worker : Activates the Worker, i.e. as soon as a Job exists that fulfils the file trigger or Web Service trigger of the Worker, processing is begun.
     * Stop Worker : Ends the Worker, which means that the Job currently being processed is aborted at an appropriate point. After the Job is restarted, it is not continued at the last position, but starts from the beginning.

The Worker is started automatically during the start and opens the Perspective Job Server through the Java VM argument in the initialization file (for example ec.ini) (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart](admin_r_vmargs_worker_autostart.htm)).
    
        -Dde.eplan.eec.jobserver.worker.autostart=true

You can also specify in the initialization file the language in which the user interface of the Job Server is to be started, for example German (see [-osgi.nl](admin_r_eclipse_args_nl.htm)):
    
        -Dosgi.nl=de_DE

parent_eecjobserver_k_security.title: Security precautions
parent_eecjobserver_k_security.content:
# Security precautions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Security precautions

If the Job Server is used in productive operation, certain security precautions have to be taken to ensure that the Job Server cannot be manipulated. The Job Server itself does not provide any protection strategies. In practice a reverse proxy is usually inserted between the Job Server and the Internet / Intranet (for example nginx).

The Job Server communicates with the browser via URLs. The basic idea is to enable or disable these URLs in order to protect the Job Server and ensure its functionality.

A URL is divided into a scheme, host and components (Scheme: http, Host: www.JobServer.de:8080). The components form the path of the URL. This has to be either enabled or disabled. A component can either by a text (for example jobs, bower_components) or a variable (for example {id} a text that changes constantly) or a variable amount of components (for example /bower_components/* means the components bower_components and all components lying behind it).

As a rule the following areas exist:

Job Status

     * Display of the standard Job Status page. The following URLs must be enabled for the Job Status page to function:
        
                /jobs/{id}
        
                /bower_components/*
        
                /api/jobs/{id}
        
                /api/jobs/{id}/downloads/{filename}

     * Display of a self-created Job Status page. The URLs for the standard Job Status page and the following ones have to be enabled:
        
                /custom/html/*

     * Display of the version number in the Job Status page:
        
                /api/version

Create Job

     * Posting of a Job to the Job Server:
        
                /jobs/request/{triggerName}
        
                /api/jobs/request/{triggerName}

Administration page

All the URLs must be enabled for the Administration page. In practice only allowing specific computers or users access to the complete Job Server to administer it has proved to be successful.

parent_eecjobserver_k_worker.title: Job Server Worker
parent_eecjobserver_k_worker.content:
# Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Job Server Worker

An EEC with activated Worker plugin on the same computer on which the Job Server is also installed is called a Local Worker. If the installation with activated Job Server Worker plugin is on a different computer, it is called a Remote Worker.

The following figure shows the Job Server Worker variants.

Job Server Workers can generate files or execute long-running processes asynchronously in the configuration phase, on the basis of a model with corresponding actions and commands. These processes are called Jobs below.

The Job Server assigns the Jobs to be processed to registered Job Server Workers. Job Server Workers are registered automatically at a Job Server. For automatic registration a corresponding entry has to be carried out in the initialization file (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_aliasurl.htm)).

The initial bases for the Jobs to be processed are so-called Job definitions (see [Job definitions](refjobserver_r_jobdefinitions.htm)). Job definitions are XML files with the extension .JMX.

A folder in which the Job definitions are stored has to be specified in the settings for each Job Server Worker (see [Preferences for the Job Server Worker](admin_r_prefs_js_worker.htm)).

The Job Server Worker reads these Job definitions in once at the start. Reading in of the Job definitions can be triggered manually through the user interface of the Job Server and of the Job Server Worker.

A Job definition always contains the following specifications:

     * Name of the Job definition.
     * Name of the model.
     * Type of the trigger that triggers a Job (for example the file extension .px).
     * Directory specification for storing the created files in case of success and failure.
     * Names of actions or commands that are to be executed for a Job.

Depending on the contents of the Job definition, the Job Server Worker expects either a file with a defined file extension in an incoming folder (File Trigger) or a specification within the calling URL (Web Service Trigger) to start a Job (see [action](refjobserver_r_action.htm)).

If several files are contained in an incoming folder in the case of a File Trigger, the files are processed in their chronological order (FIFO principle).

The data created are stored in a folder, which is also specified in the job definition (see [Storing created files](eecjobserver_k_job_results.htm) and [Job definitions](refjobserver_r_jobdefinitions.htm)).

parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui.title: User interface of the Job Server Worker
parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui.content:
# User interface of the Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## User interface of the Job Server Worker

In the Workers category of the Job Server perspective a list of all registered Job Server Workers is displayed.

The identically view is displayed in a browser. Therefore the URL shown in the address field of the Job Server perspective is copied to the browsers address field.

Each worker automatically registers in the initialization file via specification of de.eplan.jobserver.private.aliasURL at the Job Server (see [de.eplan.eec.jobserver.aliasurl](admin_r_vmargs_jobserver_aliasurl.htm)).

Each registered Job Server Worker is displayed in a tab with its status, name, its address and its Job definitions (see also [Status indication for Job Server Worker](eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_health.htm)).

The list of job definitions of all the registered Job Server Workers is updated by using [Update job definitions]. If a Job Server Worker can not be reached at this time (for example offline), this is indicated by a message for a limited time. The list of Job definitions is also updated when the Job Server or the Job Server Worker is restarted.

Details of a Job definition are displayed when expanding the Job definition (see [Information on Job definitions](eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_jobdef.htm)).

An existing job definition can be deleted from the Job Server Worker by using [Remove].

### [Uploading a job definition to a Job Server Worker](javascript:void\(0\);)

Use the [Upload] button to upload a job definition to a connected Job Server Worker. In the process an existing job definition of the same name is updated.

The job definition is then available immediately.

### [Uploading a job definition to several Job Server Workers](javascript:void\(0\);)

A job definition can be updated on the Job Server by using the [Replace JMX] button and can be distributed from the Job Server to all Job Server Workers on which the job definition of the same name exists.

### Note

A job definition must be created in the UTF-8 without BOM format.

parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_health.title: Status indication for Job Server Worker
parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_health.content:
# Status indication for Job Server Worker

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

User interface of the Job Server Worker

## Status indication for Job Server Worker

The current state of each Job Server Worker is indicated by means of a symbol in the list of Job Server Workers. The state is queried and the display is updated by the Job Server in regular time intervals.

Symbol | Meaning  
---|---  
| The Job Server Worker is not available, for example the connection is lost or the Job Server Worker stopped. If the connection ended due to a timeout, the Job Server tries to contact the Job Server Worker again for three times with a one-second pause. After the third failed connection attempt, the Job Server Worker is indicated as not available.  
| The Job Server Worker is started and is waiting for Jobs.  
| The Job Server Worker is working on one or more Jobs.  
| The Job Server Worker is working on one or more Jobs and has no other free slots. This means that the Job Server Worker cannot accept any further Jobs, but the operation is not disrupted.  
| The Job Server Worker is working on one or more Jobs, but the connection to the Job Server is lost. The Job Server Worker is waiting for a connection after processing the Job to transmit the created data.  
  
If a Job Server Worker is unable to connect to the Job Server, then it waits until the Job Server can be reached again.

The progress display indicates this state by a plain text message:

The processing is completed after a successful connection. If the Job Server Worker is stopped, it tries to transmit the states of the completed jobs to the Job Server after the next start.

parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_jobdef.title: Information on Job definitions
parent_eecjobserver_k_worker_ui_jobdef.content:
# Information on Job definitions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Information on Job definitions

The Workers tab contains a list of job definition names. Multiple Workers may have job definitions with identical names, which then must have the same content. This ensures, that each Worker creates the same result for a specific job.

Colored marker beside the name of the job definitions have the following meaning:

     * Yellow marker indicate, that two ore more job definitions with the same name exist, which differ in content.
     * Red maker indicate errors in the job definition, which prevent job execution.

By means of the > symbol, the detailed information on a job definition are unfolded.

If the input folder, base model, output folder, failed folder and the job status html are specified as UNC paths, then these entries are used as links. This feature is supported by the following browsers:

     * Firefox (only with plugins)
     * Chrome (only with plugins)

If two or more Worker have job definitions with identical names, but different content, it is indicated by a yellow marker beside the job definition name. The difference between the first job definition, which operates as reference and the following job definition is indicated by a yellow exclamation sign. The tooltip displays the expected specification.

An error in the job definition is indicated by a red exclamation sign. By clicking the exclamation sign, detailed information on the error is displayed.

The information on a job definition are logged and displayed in the user interface of the logs (see [User interface of the Logs](eecjobserver_k_logs_ui.htm)).

Job definitions, which contain errors, are logged as errors, error-free job definitions as details.

### Note

If an error occurs during the initialization of Eplan Electric P8, the associated job definition is disabled. The failed job is marked accordingly.

parent_eecoffice_k_create_new_word_compo.title: Creating Word components
parent_eecoffice_k_create_new_word_compo.content:
# Creating Word components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Creating Word components

New Word components are created in the library preferably by calling the corresponding unit via the popup menu as follows: New > Word > Main document to create a new main-document component, or New > Word > Fragment document to create a fragment-document component.

For the new Word component, enter a unique name and select the DOCX file to be imported. The path to the (external) DOCX file is subsequently displayed in the Resource path field.

When a resource is first selected, EEC enters the filename without the extension as the suggested value for the name of the component.

### Note

At present, the Office discipline only supports files in Office Open XML format after ECM-376, without macros. These files have a DOCX extension and are the default file format for Microsoft Word 2007 and newer versions of Microsoft Word.

When a new Word file is created in Windows Explorer via the popup menu, the file is 0 KB large and therefore empty. When it is opened for the first time and a zero is entered as the sole content and the file is saved, it increases in size to >10 KB. This is conventional Word behavior; an empty DOCX file is not initialized with the default content of the template (Normal.dot) until it is processed for the first time. However, because the 0 KB files contain no evaluable contents, errors occur when these files are assigned to the Office discipline. It is therefore advisable to create the Word file first, before the associated EEC object is created and the Word file is assigned to it as a resource.

parent_eecoffice_k_edit_word_resources.title: Edit resources of Word components
parent_eecoffice_k_edit_word_resources.content:
# Edit resources of Word components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Edit resources of Word components

If a Word file is assigned to a Word component as a resource, the editing of the Word file should always be started from EEC.

To edit the resource of a Word component, simply open the popup menu for this component and select Edit main document or Edit fragment document.

Word then opens and the file can be edited.

If Word is closed without saving, all of the changes are lost. If the file is saved and Word is then closed, EEC reads in the modified Word file. If the EEC detects that no changes were made to the required parameters, the new resource is implemented immediately. If changes to the parameters are detected, the Compare Editor opens. Only when this editor has been saved does EEC adopt the changes to the resource. If the Compare Editor is closed without saving, all of the changes to the Word file are lost.

### Note

After changing a Word resource, in some cases Word is not closed completely in background. Therefore the EEC process cannot be closed and the synchronize view cannot be opened, if parameter entries have changed or added. It is also not possible to edit another Word resource.

In these cases the Windows task of Word must be closed in task manager manually.

parent_eecoffice_k_field_functions.title: Field functions
parent_eecoffice_k_field_functions.content:
# Field functions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

Field function

## Field functions

The use of fields is supported in main and fragment documents.

Fields whose contents are collections of other fields, for example TOC and Index, have to be updated after the generation of the document (see [Word.UpdateWordFieldsCommand](refcommands_r_word_updatefieldscommand.htm)). The updating always refers to the total of all fields that are contained in the created document.

parent_eecoffice_k_formatting.title: Formatting
parent_eecoffice_k_formatting.content:
# Formatting

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Formatting

The most important thing for all formattings is that the format names and their format settings are identical in all DOCX files used! As a rule WORD documents are based on the document template Normal.dot and obtain the format templates from this file. All formatting changes should therefore always be created with the underlying document template.

The underlying document template can be changed under Developer tools > Word-Add-Ins, Templates tab. Format changes are automatically transferred into the document template by means of the option Update document format templates automatically. Additional document templates can be added under Global document templates and add-ins. Their formats are then additionally made available in all documents. To transfer the formats already created from any DOCX file into the document template, click [Organize...]. Individual formats can be exchanged in the following dialog between two WORD files.

If the fragment whose content is collected via a loop control structure consists of only a single paragraph, then the content adopts the paragraph format in which the content portion of the control structure is formatted; only character formatting is carried over. This means that the paragraph formatting of the collected fragment document is ignored, but not the character formatting.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

The control structure of the main or fragment document collecting the content:

(*{LOOP:dos}*)Text before the paragraph to be inserted

(*{Content}*)

Text after the paragraph to be inserted (*{END_LOOP}*)

The paragraph to be collected:

Item _Type_ Designation

Result:

Text before the paragraph to be inserted

Item _Type_ Designation

Text after the paragraph to be inserted

If the fragment whose content is collected via a LOOP control structure consists of more than one paragraph, then the content of the first paragraph adopts the paragraph format in which the content portion of the structure is formatted, while all other paragraphs retain their own paragraph formatting. This means that only the paragraph formatting of the first paragraph of the collected fragment document is ignored.

### [Example](javascript:void\(0\);)

The control structure of the main or fragment document collecting the content:

(*{LOOP:dos}*)Text before the paragraph to be inserted

(*{Content}*)

Text after the paragraph to be inserted (*{END_LOOP}*)

The paragraphs to be collected:

List of sensors/actuators

Item Type Designation

Result:

Text before the paragraph to be inserted

List of sensors/actuators

Item Type Designation

Text after the paragraph to be inserted

If one of the subsequent paragraphs being collected has a format that is not available in the collecting main or fragment document, this formatting is imported into the collecting document.

parent_eecoffice_k_graphics.title: Images
parent_eecoffice_k_graphics.content:
# Images

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Images

In general images of the following formats are supported:

     * DIB
     * EMF
     * GIF
     * JPEG
     * PICT
     * PNG
     * WMF

Images can be directly content of a main document or of a fragment. When generating the project document directly, inserted images are applied 1:1 (see [Internal images](eecoffice_k_graphics_internal.htm)).

Images can be stored on the file system (called external images). External images can be inserted into the document by means of a parameter sequence (see [External images](eecoffice_k_graphics_external.htm)).

Images can be inserted into a document via image objects (see [Image objects](eecbase_k_mediasource_image_object.htm)).

parent_eecoffice_k_graphics_external.title: External images
parent_eecoffice_k_graphics_external.content:
# External images

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## External images

All images are designated as external images when they are stored on the file system and are inserted in the main document or fragment per reference. At the moment of generation of the project document, the referenced image must be available on the file system.

Two different parameter sequences are available to reference an image:

Parameter sequence 1: #{ImagePath:Parameter name}

This parameter sequence should not be used anymore for new projects because it requires that an image with a resolution of 100 DPI is available. If the image is available in a different resolution, the expansion of the image in the Word document does not match the expectations. If the resolution of the image corresponds to 100 DPI, it is inserted with a scaling of 100% into the document. The parameter of the type ImagePath must contain an absolute path to an image file.

Parameter sequence 2: #{ImageInfo:Parametername}

The parameter of the type ImageInfo must include a map with special key-value pairs:

Key | Value | Description  
---|---|---  
SourceImagePath | <absolute path> | Absolute path to the image file  
SourceImageVerticalResolutionInDPI | Integer | Vertical resolution of the image in DPI. Without this parameter EEC tries to read this value from the meta information of the image file.  
WordDocumentPageWidthInCM | Double | Width of the Word document in cm. For example, a value of 21.0 has to be specified for an A4 document.  
WordDocumentPageHeightInCM | Double | Height of the Word document in cm. For example, a value of 29.7 has to be specified for an A4 document.  
WordDocumentMarginLeftInCM | Double | Left margin in cm.  
WordDocumentMarginRightInCM | Double | Right margin in cm.  
FitToDocumentWidth | Boolean |  Adjust the image to the width of the document. true = Adjust the image size to the document width false = Do not adjust the image size to the document width  
WordDocumentDestinationScaleHorizontalInPercent | Integer | Horizontal scaling of the image in per cent.  
WordDocumentDestinationScaleVerticalInPercent | Integer | Vertical scaling of the image in percent.  
WordDocumentDestinationRotation |  Right Left |  Rotation of the image by 90 degrees. Right = Rotation clockwise Left = Rotation counterclockwise  
  
### Note

The Word_Customizing library must be imported in order to use the ImagePath and ImageInfo types. The library is provided with the file <EEC installation folder>\install\other\model\Word_Customizing.eox.

The following figure shows a fragment with a parameter sequenced for an external image:

parent_eecoffice_k_graphics_internal.title: Internal images
parent_eecoffice_k_graphics_internal.content:
# Internal images

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Internal images

All images are designated as internal images that are inserted directly into the Word document via Insert > Images. It is not a reference to an externally available image. After insertion the image on the file system can be deleted.

The following figure shows an image which is inserted directly into a fragment:

parent_eecoffice_k_hierarchy_of_word_com.title: Hierarchy of Word components
parent_eecoffice_k_hierarchy_of_word_com.content:
# Hierarchy of Word components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## Hierarchy of Word components

The hierarchy of the two Word components, Main document and Fragment document, is permanently stored in the system library.

parent_eecoffice_k_office.title: OFFICE
parent_eecoffice_k_office.content:
# OFFICE

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm)

You are here: 

## OFFICE

Word support makes it possible to create documents based on DOCX files. The Office discipline offers its own objects and methods. Their implementation will be described in greater detail in what follows.
