     * **HTMLGenerateAndOpenStructure:** Executes GenerateHTMLStructureCommand, GenerateHTMLCodeCommand, and OpenHTMLWithEclipseCommand in a single step.
The actions should be understood as useful suggestions for deploying a text discipline. They can, of course, be modified as needed. Text discipline: In the \install\icon-collection folder we have started collecting icons that can be used in configuring text disciplines (additional contributions to the collection are very welcome!). Plug-socket variant adjustable: The text discipline supports the two plug-socket variants Plug Socket (Names are sockets) and Plug Socket (Names do not define sockets) of the structure generator. They can be set in the text discipline editor.

parent_eectext_k_level_configuration.title: Level configuration of text disciplines
parent_eectext_k_level_configuration.content:
# Level configuration of text disciplines

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Fragment Folder File Template file Fragment Level configuration of text disciplines Discipline-specific structures are constructed hierarchically. Specific levels can be given for each discipline, e.g. HTML body, tables, rows and cells for an HTML discipline. Therefore, analogous to mechatronical level configuration, a level configuration also has to be created for a text discipline. To do this, open the New library object wizard via the context menu and select the top-level component of the text discipline (in the example, HTMLComponent). After [Finish] is clicked, the editor for the level component appears. Analogous to mechatronic level configuration, permissible sub-levels can be defined on the editorâs sub-level page. In previous models, care had to be taken to ensure that each level component of a text discipline had exactly one modular-system component, e.g. in the parts-list discipline of the demo model. In the current version of the text discipline, it is therefore also possible for level components to already be assigned resources, parameters, etc. As a result, level components can also be used directly as modular system components. If this is prevented by the previous approach, then the Abstract attribute must be set, in order to prevent the component from being used directly, i.e. to make it instantiable. For components of the text discipline, the following output types may be declared: Folder, File, Template File or Fragment. The following figure shows the output types of the text-discipline components and their possible configuration in the discipline-specific configuration: Previously, it was specified in the discipline configuration whether one document for every discipline component or one document for the discipline configuration should be created. This option has been deprecated, because the structure of the folders and files to be created can now be constructed more flexibly by means of the output types. Files and template files are created in the first top-level folders. The names of the created folders and template files correspond to the discipline names, meaning that they can be calculated via the Name of the parameter for the names in discipline structures model variable. Objects of the File output type are only passed by EEC. In the process the file name can be defined through a formula, while the file extension is retained.

parent_eectext_k_parameter_sequences.title: Parameter sequences
parent_eectext_k_parameter_sequences.content:
# Parameter sequences

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Parameter sequences A parameter sequence is a text block defined by a special basic structure that is rarely encountered in documents. The sequence begins with the opening token and ends with the closing token. The default values for the tokens are #{ for the opening token and } for the closing token. These values can be adjusted via the parser configuration of each individual text discipline. The parameter to be applied is defined between the opening and closing token. The name of the parameter is required for this. Other properties of the parameter can also be specified, but these will be evaluated only if the parameter does not yet exist when the document is read in. Type, Default value, and Comment can be entered in the sequence. The syntax then follows this pattern:
    
        #{Typ:ParameterName=DefaultValue//Comment}

[Example](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        #{String:Location='St01Insert'//InstallationSite}

This means that Type, Default value, and Comment are independent of each other, and each is optional. When a resource is assigned or edited and then saved, the content is read in and the parameter sequences are evaluated. The text discipline then determines which parameters already exist and which have yet to be created. In the latter case, the appropriate parameters are created, while taking all of the entries into account. In the process, missing entries for optional information, i.e. Type, Default value, and Comment, are replaced by the following values: Statement | Value  
---|---  
Type | String  
Default value | <empty>  
Comment | <empty>  
If a new parameter has been created, or if a parameter already exists with the stated name, it is assigned to the main document or fragment whose resource has just been read in.

parent_eectext_k_parserconfiguration.title: Parser configuration of text disciplines
parent_eectext_k_parserconfiguration.content:
# Parser configuration of text disciplines

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Parser configuration of text disciplines For each text discipline you can specify settings for the parsing of the resource files independently. The call takes place via the text discipline component <Text discipline name>Parser Configuration. The following figure shows a typical parser configuration with the default settings: The name of the parser configuration automatically conforms to the name of the text discipline. A separate icon can be specified in the Image field for each text discipline. Tokens can be specified for the following parser elements in the Parameter syntax area: Parameter | Default token | Meaning  
---|---|---  
Start | #{ | With this token the parser recognizes the beginning of a parameter sequence  
Type Name Separator | : | With this token the parser detects that all characters between the start token and the separator token are to be interpreted as the type and all subsequent characters up to a further token as the name of the parameter  
Token assignment | = | If an assignment token follows the name of a parameter, all the subsequent characters up to a further token are interpreted as the parameter value  
Comment identifier | // | After the token for the comment start all further characters until the token end are interpreted as a comment  
End | } | With this token the parser recognizes the end of a parameter sequence

parent_eectext_k_text.title: TEXT
parent_eectext_k_text.content:
# TEXT

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  TEXT The TEXT discipline serves for the creation of any text file, consisting of folders, template files, files and fragments. The text files can contain parameter sequences that can be replaced during the generation of the documents through current/calculated parameter values.

parent_eectext_k_textdiscipline_actions.title: Text discipline actions
parent_eectext_k_textdiscipline_actions.content:
# Text discipline actions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Text discipline actions In order to create text discipline documents in the workspace of EEC and to open them with an editor, actions are executed via the context menu to initiate the following commands:  
    1. [TextDiscipline.GenerateTextDisciplineCodeCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_generatetextdisciplinecodecommand.htm): Generates the text discipline files. They can be viewed in the navigator.
    2. [TextDiscipline.OpenTextDisciplineComponentsWithEclipseCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_opentextdisciplinecomponentswitheclipsecommand.htm): Opens the text discipline component with EEC-internal editor (see also Editing resources directly from EC).
    3. [TextDiscipline.OpenTextDisciplineComponentsWithOSCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_opentextdisciplinecomponentswithsocommand.htm): Opens the text discipline component in the editor which is assigned in the operating system to open text files.

parent_gui_k_contentassist.title: ContentÂ Assist
parent_gui_k_contentassist.content:
# ContentÂ Assist

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Content Assist The Content Assist helps with the formulation of a formula in the formula editor. In line with the current cursor position in the formula, the Content Assist shows a list of possible parameters and methods. The Content Assist is called in the formula editor using the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [Space].

parent_gui_k_editors_history.title: Editor history
parent_gui_k_editors_history.content:
# Editor history

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Editor history The editor history allows the user to open previously edited editors again. Using the two icons , , the user can navigate to the previous and subsequent editors. The icons are located on the global toolbar. To switch to a specific editor in the history, a list of editors can be opened using the small triangles next to the icons.

parent_gui_k_editors_internal_structure.title: Internal structure of editors
parent_gui_k_editors_internal_structure.content:
# Internal structure of editors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Internal structure of editors The following figure shows the internal structure of an editor. It consists of the areas Editor menu (A) and Editor page (B):
    1. The editor menu contains different menu items that open an editor page.
    2. If a further editor is selected, it is displayed directly below the existing editor.
    3. By default only one editor page is visible. However, several editors can be opened by pinning individual editors by using the icon .

parent_gui_k_editors_notes.title: Notes
parent_gui_k_editors_notes.content:
# Notes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Notes The editor for notes can be used to assign notes that are available in the text (*.txt) or Word (*.docx) format to objects. In the notes editor, the icon Add opens a dialog for selecting a note resource. The search path for notes can be configured in the settings under Disciplines.
    1. The selected resource is listed in the table of the editor.
    2. The content is displayed below the table in an editor window for the respective format, where it can also be edited.
Note If files are to be used for notes that the system will not recognize as being in the text format, you can add these to the content types in the text format under Window > Settings > General > Content types > Text.

parent_gui_k_editors_working_on_editor_a.title: Working in the editor area
parent_gui_k_editors_working_on_editor_a.content:
# Working in the editor area

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Working in the editor area Objects can be edited by means of editors. These are opened from within a view by double-clicking the object to be edited. Alternatively, they can also be opened via the Popup menu > Open. In addition to the views already explained, the editor represents a further view of the model. It is a specific view of a specific object. While modifications to views, for example, renaming of an object, are applied to the model immediately, they must first be saved in the editor. Values, properties and parameters of objects can be edited in editors. Editors follow the principle Open \- Change \- Save \- Close \- Life Cycle:
     * Open: The editor is opened for an object to be edited. The object can be edited.
     * Change: If a modification has been made but not saved, the editor is in the Change state.
     * Save: After editing, the editor can be saved to apply the data to the model. The editor is saved either via the Menu > File > Save, the icon of the global toolbar , or via the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S].
     * Close: The editor is closed via the Menu > File > Close or the small x in the title bar of the editor.
    1. The opened editor is in the Change state. This is indicated by the asterisk to the left of the editor name.
    2. In the editor area, it is also possible to open several editors of various objects. In the case of stacked editors, it is always the active editor that is visible. Switching to an editor will make it visible, and it will then take up the full editor area. In analogy to the views, there is also the possibility to open several editors in the editor area in a visible manner. For this purpose, the editors are arranged on top of each other or side by side. This must be executed manually. To do this, the editor must be grabbed in the title bar with a left mouse click and placed in the lower half of the editor area.
Note If an editor is closed without saving, any modifications made will be lost.

parent_gui_k_editors_xml_editor_for_form.title: XML editor for Form-UIs and diagram definitions
parent_gui_k_editors_xml_editor_for_form.content:
# XML editor for Form-UIs and diagram definitions

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  XML editor for Form-UIs and diagram definitions An internal XML editor is available for creating Form-UIs and diagram definitions. The editor offers two views Source and Design of the XML document. Source: The source view (see the following figure) corresponds to an XML editor with code highlighting and content assist. Code highlighting: Code highlighting is used to represent text passages of the diagram definition in different colors, for example, command names (tags) green, attribute names purple, attribute values blue, and content black (see above figure). The colors can be chosen freely in the settings under XML > XML files > Editor > Syntax Coloring (see the following figure). Content assist: The content assist function substantially speeds up the input of formulas, tag and attribute names, as well as attribute values, while minimizing the number of typos at the same time. For example, the input of < followed by the key combination [CTRL] + [Space] brings up a selection of possible tag names that are permitted in the current position. If the proposal is adopted via the [Enter] key, the Content Assistant will insert the selected tag and also the closing tag. When inputting formulas, the Content Assistant lists the available methods and parameters after inputting a period using the key combination [CTRL] + [Space]. The names of the parameters available in the superordinate component are listed for integrated components. To list the parameter names of the superordinate component in the Content Assistant for pre-instantiated (integrated) components, .mc$ (1) must be entered before the Content Assistant is called (Ctrl + Space). All available parameters of the superordinate component are listed in here (2). The Content Assistant function behaves the same way when entering attribute values. As soon as the attribute name is entered, the Content Assistant will show a list of permissible attribute values. But this works only if it involves a pre-defined selection of attribute values, for example, for the attribute align a list with center, left and right is displayed (see the following figure). Design: The XML document for the Form-UI (see the following figure) or a diagram definition is displayed in a table in a structured form. The left column shows the elements, attributes, etc. in the hierarchical order, and the right column contains the relevant values and / or contents. Each entry in the left table column can be distinguished easily by the symbol. The following table lists the different symbols: Symbol | Meaning  
---|---  
| Element  
| Attribute  
| Comment  
| Processing instruction  
| PCDATA  
| CDATA  
This editor view is controlled entirely via the popup menu. In line with the respective element highlighted in the left column of the table, the popup menu offers a number of possibilities.

parent_gui_k_filter.title: Filter
parent_gui_k_filter.content:
# Filter

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Filter To improve clarity in case of larger models, it helps to filter certain objects from a variety of objects and hide the ones that are not needed. For this purpose, filters can be set up for views. The Filter dialog is opened via the  symbol of the view-specific toolbar. Filters are always subtractive. The filter is switched on or switched off via the symbol  of the view-specific toolbar. The view is updated after the settings have been confirmed.

parent_gui_k_finder_dialog.title: Finder dialog
parent_gui_k_finder_dialog.content:
# Finder dialog

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Finder dialog When creating a modular system or configuration of a machine, existing objects need to be found, for example, when a project engineer wants to use suitable base machines or a function modular system from the library. A Finder function including dialog is available to speed up this process. The Finder dialog is started via . The Finder dialog has a specific window title, depending on the object type being searched, but its essential functional principle is identical for all object types. The following figure shows a Finder for parameters: Name: Is used to enter a search term or parts of it to restrict the list of searched objects. The input field is designed as a combo box, which means previously input expressions can be selected in case of repeated queries. Case sensitive: Restricts the search for objects to matches which correspond exactly to the case sensitivity in the Name field. Regular expression: Regular expressions are used in the Name field to restrict the list of searched objects. [Expanded search options]: Opens a dialog to restrict the search for objects in a more differentiated way by entering name, unit and comment. [Find]: Starts the search with the specified search criteria and restrictions. All search results that correspond to the specifications are listed in the list. Without a specification the results list contains all the objects of the current library. The sorting of the list is alphabetical and can be customized by clicking a column heading. The list of search results consists of the following elements: Name column: Contains the object name. Unit column: Contains the absolute paths to the objects. Comment column: Contains the contents of the comment field of the objects. Type column: Contains the types of objects. Meta type column: Contains the specifications for the type of the objects and their forms of content. For example, String Type: Socket describes a parameter of the Socket type with the form of content String. [Export...]: Opens a dialog to export the parameters to a CSV file. Optionally you can export the column headings and open the CSV file after a successful export. [OK]: Inserts one or all marked objects into the editor of the current object. [Cancel]: Closes the dialog without inserting an object into the editor of the current object.

parent_gui_k_hyperlink_navigation.title: Hyperlink navigation
parent_gui_k_hyperlink_navigation.content:
# Hyperlink navigation

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Hyperlink navigation In EEC, it is common for information to be displayed in views and editors that is defined elsewhere. The following screenshot shows, for example, a formula that is displayed in the editor of a project component, but was defined in a library component. Using the hyperlink navigation, it is possible to switch from project objects to referenced objects. To do this, press the ALT key on the orange-color field of the editor, so that a hand appears. While keeping the [Alt] key pressed, left-click the button, and the view switches to the editor of the library object. The following figures show how the mouse pointer becomes a hand when the [Alt] key is pressed on the orange-color field. A left click establishes the switch to the library object. See the next figure:
     *      *      * 1
     * 2

parent_gui_k_infoportal.title: Eplan Information Portal
parent_gui_k_infoportal.content:
# Eplan Information Portal

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Eplan Information Portal Starts an Eplan website with information about the Eplan products (help systems, tutorials, etc.). Here you have the possibility to generally search for terms in all stored technical publications via a full text search.

parent_gui_k_perspectives.title: Perspectives
parent_gui_k_perspectives.content:
# Perspectives

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Perspectives In order to manage certain combinations from within views, the concept of Perspectives has been created. A perspective is a compilation of views and an editor area. The perspectives Engineering, Project, Synchronization and Job Server. They can be extended and adjusted.
    1. Open perspective
    2. Pre-defined perspectives: Engineering, synchronization, project and Job Server
The Engineering perspective shows all libraries and projects. The Project perspective allows views of only a specific project that has been selected previously. Depending on the project displayed in the Project Explorer view, only the libraries visible to the project are represented in the Project libraries view: One can switch between these two perspectives either by left-clicking the corresponding icon or via the menu Window > Open Perspective > Engineering / Project. Another way of switching from the project catalog of the Engineering perspective to the Project perspective is via the popup menu of the corresponding project. To do this, select the menu item Open Project Perspective > In current window. Within the Project perspective, it is possible to switch between projects without having to leave the perspective. To do this, select the desired project from the drop-down menu via the  icon. [Defining perspectives](javascript:void\(0\);) EEC allows the user to define and save his or her own perspectives. For this, one configures the windows and editors as needed. Then, select the menu Window > Save Perspective As... . In the following dialog, a designation must be assigned to the perspective. Even the pre-defined perspectives Project and Engineering can be re-defined by saving an existing configuration from Views as Perspectives using the name Project or Engineering. [Perspective Job Server](javascript:void\(0\);) The perspective Job Server can be accessed through the menu Window > Open Perspective > Other... > Job Server (Window > Open Perspective > Other... Job Server: The perspective Job Server consists of the views Message Log, Console and Progress. The Message Log logs among others the messages of the Job Server. The logging is limited to a specific period of time or to a specific number of messages by settings (see [Message log settings](eecbase_k_searching_errors_by_means_of_messagelog_user_preferences.htm)). The Console shows further messages, even those which are not relevant for the message log. The Progress view shows the progress of the running Job Server operation as a bar graph. Additionally the toolbar provides two buttons:
     * Job Server startup : Activates the Job Server, which means that the Job Server searches for JMX files in the folder which is set in the settings. If a JMX file is available, the Job Server starts executing the directives contained in the file. Together with the Job Server a new console is started.
     * End Job Server : Ends the Job Server, which means that the currently running job is aborted at an appropriate point. After the Job is restarted, it is not continued at the last position, but starts from the beginning.
Note Before closing the EEC, the last perspective displayed will be saved and restored when the EC is launched again. It may happen sometimes that the rebuilding of an editor page after data changes may cause the EEC to crash. When the EEC is started again, an attempt is made to rebuild this editor page, which causes a crash, or â if the EC has not yet saved the information about the opened editor in a permanent manner â the last opened page is rebuilt upon opening the editor again, which also causes a crash. To prevent this, it is now possible to prevent the setup of the editor by pressing the keys [Ctrl] \+ [Shift] at the same time. This will also reset the page to be displayed, i.e., next time the editor is opened, it will open with the attribute page. In the first case, when starting the EEC, the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [Shift] must be pressed, and kept pressed, until the EEC appears. In the second case, the editor of the object must be opened via the Popup menu > Open, while keeping the key combination [Ctrl] \+ [Shift] pressed. If several editors are open at the start of the EEC, the cancelation of the setup applies only to the visible editor. If this one is closed, the other editors will be set up, if [Ctrl] \+ [Shift] is again pressed.

parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback.title: Create Eplan support request
parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback.content:
# Create Eplan support request

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create Eplan support request Even with careful testing prior to shipping, programming errors can never be ruled out entirely. Fixing such an exception error requires your assistance. Depending on the respective license, the data recorded with the Eplan support request wizard and a description which is required for reproduction and rectification of the error is sent to the e-mail address [support@eplan.de](mailto:support@eplan.de) or to an e-mail address that is stored in the model variables under Eplan support request > E-mail address deviating from the Eplan support for the Eplan support request.
     * Model variable without specification or a developer license is active: Eplan support request is sent to the e-mail address [support@eplan.de](mailto:support@eplan.de).
     * License Config: Eplan support request is sent to the e-mail address that is stored in the model variable E-mail address deviating from the Eplan support.
The following data can be attached to the Eplan support request:
     * The complete model. Please take into account the maximum file size that is possible as an attachment of an e-mail. This option is enabled by default.
     * Discipline resources. This option is required if resources are not managed internally in the model (see [Resources and their management](eecbase_k_resources_and_their_management.htm)). The resource files are only attached if the global path to the resources is specified (see [de.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath](admin_r_vmargs_global_resourcePath_new.htm)). This option is enabled by default.
     * Content of the \dropins folder. With this option plugins located in the /dropins folder are attached. This option is enabled by default.
     * Database queries. In order to reproduce errors better, there is an option to record database queries. It is recorded which query was sent to which database and which reply the database provided. This way, it is possible to trace the communication with databases, without having the content of all databases available. The recording of database queries has to be activated in the settings under Disciplines > Data sources. The evaluation of such records is basically only possible by Support.

parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_call.title: Call
parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_call.content:
# Call

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Call The Quality Feedback wizard appears automatically with an exception error if the overall system identifies an error in a program component for which no error handling exists yet. Before the program is terminated automatically after an exception error, in order to prevent loss of data, the Quality Feedback wizard appears. For program errors that do not cause the program to be terminated, call the Quality Feedback wizard manually:
    1. Select the menu item File > Export....
    2. In the Export dialog, select in the folder Other the entry Quality Feedback.
    3. Click [Next >].

parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_create_and_.title: Create and send
parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_create_and_.content:
# Create and send

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create and send In the upper text field, enter the steps required to reproduce the error. For example, describe which check boxes, menu items and buttons must be activated, and in which order, in order to arrive at the error. If you have called the Quality Feedback wizard manually, please explain how to recognize the error and what should be the correct way. The Quality Feedback Report can be prioritized using three levels of priority. The three levels are Low, Medium, and High. Without a selection, the default priority is Low. The priority is also displayed in the file name of the Quality Feedback Report. Example: Report_low_2008-08-14_18-23.zip In addition, the priority is also stored in the file report-details.txt in the ZIP file. To modify the preferences for file name and folder in which the Quality Feedback Report is to be created, click [Browse...] and select a new target file. If the error can be reproduced only with your data, activate the check box Add model. Please note that this could increase the file size substantially, and delay the creation. When the Quality Feedback wizard is called manually, create the file by clicking [Finish]. Please send the file as an e-mail attachment to [support@eplan.de](mailto:support@eplan.de). If the Quality Feedback wizard has opened automatically, you can create the file by clicking [Save]. By clicking [Send by e-mail], the default e-mail program of your operating system will open as well. Add the file created to the e-mail as an attachment manually, and click [Send].

parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_data_securi.title: Data protection
parent_gui_k_qualityfeedback_data_securi.content:
# Data protection

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Data protection The Quality Feedback Report is a ZIP file and essentially contains the configuration details, settings and descriptions of current or previous errors and events. Except for the model, all files contained in the ZIP file are text files. Before sending the Quality Feedback Report, you can unzip the ZIP file to delete in the text files whatever you do not want to send.

parent_gui_k_start_eec.title: Starting EEC
parent_gui_k_start_eec.content:
# Starting EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Starting EEC EEC is started via the Start menu using the Start EEC icon and / or by executing the ec.exe file. Depending on the configuration the program start is carried out in different sequences. No explicit model specification is configured:
    1. Query of the data management (EOX file).
    2. Display of the EEC user interface with the Engineering perspective.
A model specification is configured and EEC is started for the first time:
     * Display of the EEC user interface with the Engineering perspective.
A model specification is configured and EEC is started again:
     * Display of the EEC user interface with the perspective last used.
The following information can be found in the title bar of the EEC user interface:
     * EEC version.
     * If required, a restriction of the access rights to the data management.
     * The data management used.
Read more Data basis selection for EOX data management [Perspectives](gui_k_perspectives.htm) [Data management title](admin_r_prefs_show_backend.htm)

parent_gui_k_synchronization_of_views_an.title: Synchronization of views and editors
parent_gui_k_synchronization_of_views_an.content:
# Synchronization of views and editors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Synchronization of views and editors Each editor is logically linked to an object within a view. When switching between two editors, it often helps to also select the relevant object in opened views. If the object being edited in the editor is to be selected in the view, synchronization between the editor and the view is possible. To do this, use the  button in the view. This turns on synchronization. When switching between editors, the corresponding element for each editor is displayed in the synchronized view. Synchronization can be enabled and disabled via the  button.

parent_gui_k_terminology.title: Terminology
parent_gui_k_terminology.content:
# Terminology

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Terminology All modeled contexts and stored data are mapped in the information model (in short: model) and stored in a database management system or an EOX file. The model is organized by means of assemblies. Assemblies are organizational units. Assemblies can be projects and libraries. To a project, only objects from imported libraries are visible. The sum of libraries necessary to create a project is referred to as the modular system. The objective is to cover the project requirements in as flexible a manner as possible. This is given by the modular system organization. The systems to be integrated, for example, Eplan Electric P8 or Simatic S7, require system libraries. These are supplied with EEC and cannot be modified. They contain the necessary basic components.

parent_gui_k_toolbars.title: Toolbars
parent_gui_k_toolbars.content:
# Toolbars

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Toolbars The EEC Workbench contains a global toolbar**(A)** which is used to execute global actions, for example, full-text search, and a view-specific toolbar (B), for example, to generate the schematic for the project displayed in a view.

parent_gui_k_views.title: Views
parent_gui_k_views.content:
# Views

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Views The user interface consists of different views of the model. The following figure illustrates the understanding of views: Views generally show a section of the overall model: Views serve to represent objects of the model in a suitable structure based on a selection that is meaningful in terms of the task at hand. As a result of the modular combination of different views, a number of workflows can be supported. An object of the model can be displayed in several views at the same time. Modifications to a view are imported into the model, and other views are updated. Views can be selected via the menu Window > Show view. In the following figure different views are identified through a yellow framing.
    1. Views can be moved in any manner as well as arranged side-by-side or on top of each other within the window by grabbing them with a left mouse click. Alternatively, views can also be stacked.
    2. Views are closed by using the small x in the title bar.
    3. The familiar Windows mechanisms can be used to change the size of the respective view: Touching the border of the window allows for its size to be modified. Minimizing and maximizing are possible via the two icons at the top right-hand border of each view window. Alternatively, double-clicking the title bar will maximize and minimize a view.

parent_main_k_chapters.title: Chapter overview
parent_main_k_chapters.content:
# Chapter overview

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Chapter overview The whole EEC documentation consists of the following chapters:
     * Introduction
     * Concept
     * EPLAN Engineering Configuration
     * Tutorial
     * Reference
     * Administration
     * Release Notes
The chapter [Concepts](concept_k_concepts.htm) presents the methodological principles of EEC. In addition to the functional configuration methodology, it also describes the IT philosophy of EEC, concepts regarding team support, as well as other topics. The Concepts chapter does not require previous knowledge of EEC and is aimed at readers that do not themselves use EEC. The chapter [BASIC](eecbase_k_base.htm) uses workflows that are to be supported by the system to explain how the software works. The EEC implements the principles outlined in the Concepts chapter. As a result, the sections in the two chapters are closely correlated. Hyperlinks are used to create logical links between these sections. The [Tutorial](tutorial_k_tutorial.htm) chapter is designed to provide a brief introduction to the topic of functional configuration and makes no claim to be complete. The tutorial, however, uses hyperlinks to direct you to sections in the previous two chapters, where you can read more on the topics. The key tool to automate calculations and formally model rule sets within the system is the formula language. Graphical user interfaces can be created for project engineers with the aid of XML files. The [Reference](reference_r_reference.htm) chapter serves as a reference guide for the following:
     * Formula language.
     * Regular expressions.
     * User interfaces (Form-UI).
     * Scripting methods.
     * Text generator functions.
     * Commands.
     * 2D graphical editors (GRAPH2D).
     * Tags of Job Server job definitions.
     * Tags of IMX data format.
     * Tags for transformation of XML import files.
     * Tags for transformation of Excel import files.
The [Administration](admin_r_administration.htm) chapter examines the various installation options for the EEC, system requirements, etc., and, like the EEC chapter implements the concepts from chapter 2. Which new features are implemented in the current release is listed in the [Releases Notes](releasenotes_k_feature_releases.htm) chapter.

parent_main_k_docu_use_notes.title: Notes on using the document
parent_main_k_docu_use_notes.content:
# Notes on using the document

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Notes on using the document Before you start reading in the help system, please pay attention to the following notes: Examples are introduced by this graphic and are expandable. Click the arrow to show the sample text. [Example:](javascript:void\(0\);) Here the sample text is displayed. Click the arrow, to hide the sample text again. Note: You will find additional notes behind this graphic that will make using the program easier. Tip: Behind this graphic you will find useful tips, for example operation alternatives.
     * Elements of the user interface are tagged in blue, thus making them easy to find in the text.
     * Texts in bold point to information that is of special importance and that you must pay attention to.
     * Code examples, directory names, direct input, etc. are displayed in monospaced font.
     * To make it easier to find certain program options, some help topics have a preceding menu path. Select the menus and menu options tagged in blue to access the respective option from the main menu.
     * In the instructions which lead you step-by-step to a certain target, you can find the reaction of the program to your action at many locations behind the arrow symbol pointing to the right .
     * Below Read more note you can find hyper links to related pages.

parent_main_k_home.title: Eplan Engineering Configuration
parent_main_k_home.content:
# Eplan Engineering Configuration

Eplan Engineering Configuration Concepts Installation variants Modules Tutorial Formula Language Release Notes Process Consulting  Engineering-Software  Implementation  Global Support

parent_main_k_how_eec_supports_functiona.title: How does the EEC support functional configuration?
parent_main_k_how_eec_supports_functiona.content:
# How does the EEC support functional configuration?

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  How does the EEC support functional configuration? EEC incorporates all tools that are used in machine and plant engineering processes (CAx systems, Office products, programming systems, etc.) to create a cross-discipline, integrated end-to-end system: The advantage is that existing know-how and data can also be reused with the existing systems. Building on existing data, it is possible to quickly achieve success with EEC using module-based approaches.

parent_main_k_introduction.title: EPLAN Engineering Configuration
parent_main_k_introduction.content:
# EPLAN Engineering Configuration

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  EPLAN Engineering Configuration EPLAN Engineering Configuration is an object-oriented development system for configurations of machines and plants. The following figure is interactive. Click on the items in it to find out more about the possibilities of EEC. #### [Start](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Create a decomposition
    2. Identify properties
    3. [Identify common parts/variants](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Identify common parts
      2. Identify variants
    4. Specify component structure
    5. Specify the top-level component
    6. Create Modular system
    7. Create configuration
    8. Automate processes (optional)
#### [Create Modular system](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. [Create architecture library](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create library
      2. Import library 'Engineering'
      3. Create level components
      4. Specify level configuration
      5. Specify top-level component
    2. [Create mechatronic library](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create library
      2. Import architecture library
      3. Create units for mechatronic components
      4. Create unit for parameters
      5. Create parameters
      6. [Create components](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Create variants by disabler
        2. Add parameters to components
        3. Assign values and formulas to parameters
      7. Install components
      8. [Optional](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Create extension points
        2. Create placeholders
    3. [Create disciplin library](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create library
      2. Import discipline library
      3. Create architecture library
      4. Import interfaces library
      5. Create units
      6. Create parameters
      7. [Create discipline components](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Import resource file
        2. Add parameters to components
        3. Assign values and formulas to parameters
      8. [Enclosing discipline components](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Extend component with interface
        2. Install discipline component
        3. Create interface parameters
    4. [Create interface library](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create library
      2. Import 'Engineering' library
      3. Create interfaces
#### [Create configuration](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. [Interactive via drag & drop](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create project
      2. Select libraries
      3. Select top-level component
      4. Drag component from modular system into project
      5. Execute actions
    2. [Interactive via Form-UI](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create project
      2. Select library
      3. Select top-level component
      4. [Start Form-UI](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Enter/select values
        2. Instantiate components
        3. Import discipline library
        4. Update extension points
        5. Generate documents
    3. [Interactive via Graph2D](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create project
      2. Select library
      3. Select top-level component
      4. [Start Graph2D diagram](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Drag graphic items into diagram
        2. Enter values via Form-UI
        3. Import discipline libraries
        4. Update extension points
        5. Generate documents
    4. [Partially automated via import](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Create project
      2. Select library
      3. Select top-level component
      4. [Start Form-UI](javascript:void\(0\);)
        1. Enter/select values
        2. Execute actions
        3. Instantiate components
        4. Import discipline libraries
        5. Import partial configurations
        6. Update extension points
        7. Generate documents
      5. Execute actions
#### [Automation](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Install Job Server
    2. Install Job Server Worker
    3. Create basic project
    4. Create import files
    5. Create job definitions
    6. [Deliver job definitions to Job Server](javascript:void\(0\);)
      1. Generate documents by Worker
      2. Provide download links
    7. Create methods to trigger jobs
All relevant components of a machine or plant are therefore modeled as mechatronic and discipline-specific components and organized in libraries. The discipline-specific components save references to contents from third-party systems or the contents themselves, for example macros from EPLAN Electric P8. With the help of parameters and methods, rules can be created to influence the type and compilation of the components during the configuration. For this configuration different user interfaces can be created. Depending on the requirements the interface is designed as a Gantt diagram, as a graphic diagram or similar to a website. The configuration of a machine or plant is subsequently compiled by using the user interface. Then discipline-specific documents are created from this, for example schematics.

parent_main_k_modules.title: Modules: Overview
parent_main_k_modules.content:
# Modules: Overview

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Modules: Overview The modular structure makes it possible to determine the scope of service yourself. Module | Submodule | Description  
---|---|---  
BASIC |  |  The BASE module is equivalent to a fully operational EEC that allows you to create object-oriented models. Because the models are object-oriented, it is possible to formally describe complex systems and their versions and configuration rules. Key features of the BASE module:
     * Development.
     * Create libraries.
     * Define architectures (e.g., hierarchical levels of plants).
     * Create library components.
     * Define formulas (rules).
     * Create Form-UIs, i.e., specific user interfaces for different user groups (project engineers, sales employees, etc.).
     * Configuration (for test purposes).
     * Manage projects.
     * Configuration and parameterization of projects using standard editors.  
Configuration with Form-UIs.
See [Terminology](eecbase_k_terminology.htm)  
Note:  
Configuration licenses are required to put the EEC into productive service.  
TEXT |  The TEXT module makes it possible to create any text-based documents on the base of a model. Possible uses include the generation of:
     * Text files (parts lists, ini files, error text files, etc.)
     * HTML files (documentation, etc.)
     * XML files (Word, Excel, or PowerPoint files).
The TEXT module does not have interfaces to existing systems. Rather, it is restricted to generating text documents as well as entire folder structures based on freely defined rules. The TEXT module cannot be deeply integrated into other system; however, it does allow data to be exchanged, something which is fully adequate in many cases, on the basis of exported text documents that can then be imported by a downstream system. See [TEXT](eectext_k_text.htm)  
FORM-UI |  The FORM-UI module is an editor that allows the configuration and the execution of actions on the basis of interactive pages, similar to HTML pages. See [Form-UI](refformui_r_formui.htm)  
GRAPH2D |  The GRAPH2D module is a generic graphic editor used to define form-specific diagrams, e.g., layout diagrams, Gantt diagrams, block diagrams, CFC editors, state graphs, etc. See [ GRAPH2D](eecgraph2d_k_graph2d.htm)  
PLC | STEP 7 |  The PLC.STEP 7 module makes it possible to create PLC software for SIMATIC STEP 7 from Siemens. See [PLC](eecplc_k_plc.htm)  
CODESYS |  The PLC.CODESYS module makes it possible to create PLC software for 3Sâs CoDeSys PLC programming system. See [PLC](eecplc_k_plc.htm)  
AUTOMATIONWORX |  The PLC.AUTOMATIONWORX module makes it possible to create PLC software for Phoenixâs AutomationWorX. See [PLC](eecplc_k_plc.htm)  
ECAD | P8 / Fluid |  The ECAD.P8 module makes it possible to create schematics for Eplan Electric P8. See [ECAD](eececad_k_ecad.htm)  
ECAD3D | Pro Panel |  The ECAD3D.Pro Panel module makes it possible to create three-dimensional mounting layouts. See [Eplan Pro Panel](eecpropanel_k_propanel_requirements.htm)  
OFFICE | WORD |  With WORD support, the OFFICE module makes it possible to create docx files in the Office Open XML format based on ECMA-376 without macros for Word 2007 and Word 2010. See [OFFICE](eecoffice_k_office.htm)  
SCRIPTING |  The SCRIPTING module allows the modular system developer to add scripts to the existing EEC functionality. Scripts are programmed in Groovy on the basis of Java. Typical uses include, among others, data import and special calculations. See [SCRIPTING](eecscripting_k_scripting.htm)  
SAP |  By means of the generic SAP interface data between SAP and EEC are transferred bidirectional. On one hand, the function modules contained in the SAP standard can be activated. On the other hand, however, individually programmed modules can also be used. See [SAP interface](eecsap_k_sap.htm)

parent_main_k_what_is_functional_configu.title: What is functional configuration?
parent_main_k_what_is_functional_configu.content:
# What is functional configuration?

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  What is functional configuration? Machines and plants are complex mechatronic systems that require systematic coordination between the disciplines of mechatronic, electric, and software technology. One approach to structuring machines and plants that is well suited for cross-discipline cooperation and coordination is a functional classification of production equipment and the creation of functional units and/or mechatronic components that can be reused wherever possible either within a project or in multiple projects. The goal here is to configure the machines or plants for a specific order to the greatest extent possible based on pre-defined mechatronic components and to create all production-specific documentation (parts list, engineering drawings, wiring diagrams, software, etc.). The systematic reuse achieved in this way creates enormous potential in terms of cost/time saving and regarding the quality of machines and plants. A cross-discipline approach is required to map the high degree of variation in plant and special machinery manufacturing using the minimum number of modular components possible. Unlike traditional engineering, as many tasks as possible should be carried out independent of the discipline: The focus is on the functions and not the tools used to implement them! Functional configuration consistently uses the functional classification of production equipment, also taking into account that different perspectives regarding a single machine or plant are required with the individual engineering disciplines (e.g., mechanical/electrical engineering).

parent_refcommands_o_overview.title: Commands: Overview
parent_refcommands_o_overview.content:
# Commands: Overview

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Commands: Overview Commands are objects, that provide algorithms for certain tasks (e.g. creating of a schematic diagram). The algorithms are implemented in execute methods. Each command has at least one execute method. Several execute methods with different arguments can also be specified. Commands are connected to the user interface within actions (calls by the right mouse key or Form-UI (see also [Commands and actions](concept_k_commands_and_actions.htm)). The plug-ins of the system provide commands via system libraries. Command | Area | Description  
---|---|---  
[AutomationWorX.GenerateAutomationWorXCodeCommand](refcommands_r_automationworx_generateautomationworxcodecommand.htm) | AutomationWorX | Generates the program code for AutomationWorX. The code can be viewed in the navigator.  
[AutomationWorX.GenerateAutomationWorXStructureCommand](refcommands_r_automationworx_generateautomationworxstructurecommand.htm) | AutomationWorX | Generates the AutomationWorX structure.  
[CoDeSys.GenerateCoDeSysCodeCommand](refcommands_r_codesys_generatecodesyscodecommand.htm) | CoDeSys | Generates the program code for CoDeSys. The code can be viewed in the navigator.  
[CoDeSys.GenerateCoDeSysStructureCommand](refcommands_r_codesys_generatecodesysstructurecommand.htm) | CoDeSys | Generates the CoDeSys structure.  
[CoDeSys.GenerateENIProjectCommand](refcommands_r_codesys_generateeniprojectcommand.htm) | CoDeSys | Passes the created CoDeSys program code to the ENI server.  
[ECAD.GenerateECADCodeCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadcodecommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates the schematic.  
[ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectcommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates the Eplan Electric P8 project.  
[ECAD.GenerateEcadProjectFromTypicalFileCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadprojectfromtypicalfilecommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates the Eplan Electric P8 project on the basis of a typical.xml in the format of EEC One.  
[ECAD.GenerateEcadStructureCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateecadstructurecommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates the ECAD structure.  
[ECAD.GenerateEdzForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generateedzforecadprojectcommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates an EDZ file of the specified project components of the WiringDiagram type.  
[ECAD.GenerateMacroAndEdzForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generatemacroandedzforecadprojectcommand.htm) | ECAD | By using the command data are packed into an EDZ file whose contents represent a part for the P8 parts database.  
[ECAD.GenerateXmlForEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_generatexmlforecadprojectcommand.htm) | ECAD | Creates an XML file ec-instructions.xml, as well as the macro files of the project to be created in the specified folder.  
[ECAD.OpenECADComponentCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadcomponentcommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates and opens the schematic for the passed ECAD component.  
[ECAD.OpenECADPagesCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadpagescommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates and opens the passed wiring diagrams of the project.  
[ECAD.OpenEcadProjectCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openecadprojectcommand.htm) | ECAD | Opens the Eplan Electric P8 project.  
[ECAD.OpenProjectDiagramsCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_openprojectdiagramscommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates and opens all schematic diagrams of a project.  
[ECAD.UpdateEcadPagesCommand](refcommands_r_ecad_updateecadpagescommand.htm) | ECAD | Generates a project with specific pages of an Eplan Electric P8 project depending on the preference Reuse existing project for schematic generation.  
[Engineering.AbortExecutionIfErrorCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_abortexecutionIferrorcommand.htm) | Engineering | Aborts die execution of all following commands, if an error occurred in one of the preceding commands.  
[Engineering.ClearAllParameterValuesCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_clearallparametervaluescommand.htm) | Engineering | Deletes all parameter values of the mechatronic structure.  
[Engineering.CopyFileCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_copyfilecommand.htm) | Engineering | Copies a file or a folder to the specified target.  
[Engineering.CreateInstanceCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_createinstancecommand.htm) | Engineering | Creates a new instance.  
[Engineering.CreateNewProjectBasedOnPXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_createnewprojectbasedonpxcommand.htm) | Engineering | Creates a new project based on a PX file.  
[Engineering.ExportEOXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_exporteoxcommand.htm) | Engineering | Exports the selected assemblies to an EOX file.  
[Engineering.ExportProblemReport](refcommands_r_engineering_exportproblemreport.htm) | Engineering | Exports the current model as a ZIP archive with the name Report_<priority>_<YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM>.zip, creates a Quality Feedback Report and starts the local e-mail client in order to send the Quality Feedback Report.  
[Engineering.ExportPXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_exportpxcommand.htm) | Engineering | Exports changes to the project to a PX file.  
[Engineering.ForcefulEOXImportCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_forcefuleoximportcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports an EOX file.  
[Engineering.ImportCSVCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importcsvcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports a project according to the assigned CSV file.  
[Engineering.ImportExcelCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importexcelcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports an Excel file according to the specified schema and template file into a project or a project component.  
[Engineering.ImportLibrariesCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importlibrariescommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports the libraries into the project, which are passed by the list.  
[Engineering.ImportIMXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importimxcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports a project according to the assigned IMX file.  
[Engineering.ImportIMXWithTemplateCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importimxwithtemplatecommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports an IMX file according to the specified template file into a project or a project component.  
[Engineering.ImportPXCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importpxcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports all projects occurring in a PX file.  
[Engineering.ImportXMLCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_importxmlcommand.htm) | Engineering | Imports a project according to the assigned XML file.  
[Engineering.NewProjectCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_newprojectcommand.htm) | Engineering | Creates a new project.  
[Engineering.OpenCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_opencommand.htm) | Engineering |  Opens the object that is specified by absoluteName. In the Web EEC environment: Returns an URL of the created file.  
[Engineering.OpenResourceCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_openresourcecommand.htm) | Engineering | Opens the created file with the specified path of the specified project.  
[Engineering.RemoveExtensionsCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_removeextensionscommand.htm) | Engineering | The execute method of this command removes the components, which are inserted on extension points.  
[Engineering.RemoveLibrariesCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_removelibrariescommand.htm) | Engineering | Removes the libraries from the project, which are passed by the list.  
[Engineering.RemoveImportedLibrariesAndExtensionsCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_removeimportedlibrariesandextensionscommand.htm) | Engineering | Deletes from the project all instances of the specified library that were inserted into the project via an extension point.  
[Engineering.SaveObjectCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_saveobjectcommand.htm) | Engineering | Saves the modifications on the model.  
[Engineering.SendMailCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_sendmailcommand.htm) | Engineering | Sends an e-mail with the specified content from the specified sender to the specified addresses with the specified attachments.  
[Engineering.SetParameterValueFromJsonCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_setparametervaluefromjsoncommand.htm) | Engineering | Saves the contents of a JSON structure as a map in a parameter. The JSON structure is transferred to the Job Server as POST data.  
[Engineering.SetParameterValueFromJsonFileCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_setparametervaluefromjsonfilecommand.htm) | Engineering | Saves the contents of a JSON file as map in a parameter.  
[Engineering.StartFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_startformulacachecommand.htm) | Engineering | Activates the formula cache.  
[Engineering.StopFormulaCacheCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_stopformulacachecommand.htm) | Engineering | Disables the formula cache.  
[Engineering.UpdateExtensionsCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_updateextensionscommand.htm) | Engineering | The execute methods of this command remove the invalid components, which are inserted on extension points and inserts new valid components.  
[Engineering.UpdateStaticDisablerCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_updatestaticdisablercommand.htm) | Engineering | Sets the explicit version update of outdated projects.  
[Engineering.ZipFileCommand](refcommands_r_engineering_zipfilecommand.htm) | Engineering | Packs single files or a folder into a ZIP file.  
[JobServer.MarkFileForDownloadCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_markfilefordownload.htm) | JobServer | Marks the specified file for downloading.  
[JobServer.PutResultCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_putresultcommand.htm) | JobServer | Sets the value of a key-value pair in the ResultData object.  
[JobServer.SetProgressMessageCommand](refcommands_r_jobserver_setprogressmessagecommand.htm) | JobServer | Sets the value that is returned in the result of a status query for the "message" key.  
[Plc.GenerateFieldbusCommand](refcommands_r_plc_generatefieldbuscommand.htm) | PLC | Generates all start addresses and connections.  
[Plc.OverwriteAddressesCommand](refcommands_r_plc_overwriteaddressescommand.htm) | PLC | Creates new start addresses, any existing formulas for start addresses are not taken into account, sensors and actuators remain unaffected.  
[Plc.OverwriteConnectionsCommand](refcommands_r_plc_overwriteconnectionscommand.htm) | PLC | Generates all connections new.  
[Plc.OverwriteFieldbusCommand](refcommands_r_plc_overwritefieldbuscommand.htm) | PLC | Generates all start addresses and connections new.  
[Plc.RecreateAddressesCommand](refcommands_r_plc_regenerateaddressescommand.htm) | PLC | Generates all start addresses new.  
[Plc.RegenerateConnectionsCommand](refcommands_r_plc_regenerateconnectionscommand.htm) | PLC | Generates all connections new.  
[Plc.ResetAddressesCommand](refcommands_r_plc_resetaddressescommand.htm) | PLC | Deletes previously calculated start addresses.  
[Plc.ResetConnectionsCommand](refcommands_r_plc_resetconnectionscommand.htm) | PLC | Deletes previously calculated connections.  
[Plc.ResetFieldbusCommand](refcommands_r_plc_resetfieldbuscommand.htm) | PLC | Deletes all start addresses and connections.  
[Plc.UpdateAddressesCommand](refcommands_r_plc_updateaddressescommand.htm) | PLC | Creates new start addresses, any existing formulas for start addresses are not taken into account, sensors and actuators remain unaffected.  
[Plc.UpdateConnectionsCommand](refcommands_r_plc_updateconnectionscommand.htm) | PLC | Creates the non-existing connections.  
[Plc.UpdateFieldbusCommand](refcommands_r_plc_updatefieldbuscommand.htm) | PLC | Generates start addresses and connections of control components, sensors and actuators, which have no connection or start address yet and of sensors and actuators, whose connections do not exist anymore (e.g. because a control component is deleted).  
[Step7.GenerateSimaticProjectCommand](refcommands_r_step7_generatesimaticprojectcommand.htm) | STEP 7 | Passes the created Simatic program code to the Simatic-Manager.  
[Step7.GenerateStep7CodeCommand](refcommands_r_step7_generatestep7codecommand.htm) | STEP 7 | Generates the STEP 7 program code.  
[Step7.GenerateStep7NumbersCommand](refcommands_r_step7_generatestep7numberscommand.htm) | STEP 7 | Generates the block numbers for STEP 7 blocks.  
[Step7.GenerateStep7StructureCommand](refcommands_r_step7_generatestep7structurecommand.htm) | STEP 7 | Generates the STEP 7 structure.  
[TextDiscipline.GenerateTextDisciplineCodeCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_generatetextdisciplinecodecommand.htm) | TextDiscipline | Generates the text discipline files.  
[TextDiscipline.GenerateTextDisciplineStructureCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_generatetextdisciplinestructurecommand.htm) | TextDiscipline | Generates the structure of the text discipline components.  
[TextDiscipline.OpenTextDisciplineComponentsWithEclipseCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_opentextdisciplinecomponentswitheclipsecommand.htm) | TextDiscipline | Opens the text discipline component with the EEC-internal editor.  
[TextDiscipline.OpenTextDisciplineComponentsWithOSCommand](refcommands_r_textdiscipline_opentextdisciplinecomponentswithsocommand.htm) | TextDiscipline | Opens the text discipline component in the editor which is assigned in the operating system to open text files.  
[Word.GenerateWordCodeCommand](refcommands_r_word_generatewordcodecommand.htm) | Word | Generates a Word document from the active discipline components.  
[Word.GenerateWordPDFCommand](refcommands_r_word_generatewordpdfcommand.htm) | Word | Opens the PDF file which was created by Word in the editor which is assigned in the operating system to open PDF files.  
[Word.GenerateWordStructureCommand](refcommands_r_word_generatewordstructurecommand.htm) | Word | Generates the discipline structure for a Word document from the active discipline components.  
[Word.OpenWordDisciplineCommand](refcommands_r_word_disciplinecommand.htm) | Word | Opens the Word discipline component in the editor which is assigned in the operating system to open Word files.  
[Word.OpenWordPDFCommand](refcommands_r_word_openwordpdfcommand.htm) | Word | Opens the PDF file which was created by Word in the editor which is assigned in the operating system to open PDF files.  
[Word.UpdateWordFieldsCommand](refcommands_r_word_updatefieldscommand.htm) | Word | Updates the field contents of all fields within a created Word document.
