The formula in the attribute tooltip="=if not src.$Name.isNull then src.$Name else if not src.$Number.isNull then src.$Nummer else 'Enter data' endif endif + ' | ' + if not (target.$Name.isNull) then target.$Name else if not (target.$Number.isNull) then target.$Number else 'Enter data' endif endif" determines the value of the parameter Name or Number of the source and target component of a connection, to display this data as a tooltip. If both the parameters are <null> then as a replacement, the text Enter data is displayed as a tooltip. The second connection distinguishes itself, in principle, from the first only because of the router, the line color and the formula for determining the parameter values for the tooltip, because here, it is necessary to first navigate from the in-built component to the parent component, to evaluate the parameter Name or Number there.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_connections_configur.title: Configuring a router
parent_tutgraph2d_h_connections_configur.content:
# Configuring a router

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Configuring a router The persons (workers) are to be connected to the devices (PC, Telephone) by means of polylines, which can have any number of bend points, and the LAN jacks (LAN_Jack) of the devices (PC, Telephone, Printer, 2way_LANJack, 5waySwitch) between one another through direct lines.
    1. Replace the configuration of the router <router id="router"> with the following lines.
    
        <!-- Router -->

    <!-- Router for connecting worker and device -->

    <router id="Bendpoint" class="com.mind8.graph2d.router.Bendpoint" />

    <!-- Router for connecting slave and device -->

    <router id="ShortestPath" class="com.mind8.graph2d.router.ShortestPath" />

    1. In the tag <diagramEditor>, change the attribute router=ârouterâ to router="ShortestPath".
[Explanation](javascript:void\(0\);) The ID of the first router is Bendpoint, that of the second is ShortestPath. The types of router are specified by the same respective class names. For more types, see the section [router](refgraph2d_r_router.htm) in the Reference chapter. Since the ID router has been replaced, the reference to the ID of the router in the tag <diagramEditor> has to be changed. The default router should be the one with the ID ShortestPath.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_create_diagram_confi.title: Create new diagram configuration
parent_tutgraph2d_h_create_diagram_confi.content:
# Create new diagram configuration

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create new diagram configuration The unit Diagramconfigurations is already present in the library IT_ModularSystem. The new diagram configuration is to be created in this unit:
    1. Select the unit Diagram configurations.
    2. In the context menu, select New > Diagram definition.
    3. Input the name IT_SolutionMap.
    4. The modifications can be adopted by clicking the disk icon at the top left (or using the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S]).
The new diagram configuration is then added in the unit Diagramconfigurations. In the following, it is assumed that you save your modifications by the time you open the next object (disk icon top left or using the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S]), and if not, by the time you see the prompt and click Yes. The diagram configuration is edited in the Editor Configuration:
    1. Click Configuration to open the Editor.
    2. Click the Source tab to display the XML source text.
The diagram definition is filled with the following XML tags immediately after creation: Tag name | Description  
---|---  
<?xml> | The XML version used and the character coding  
</diagramEditor> | The stored schema, ID of the diagram and standard router. Encloses all the other tags of the definition  
<router> | Encloses the specific description of the router in <routerClass>  
<routerClass> | Specific description of the router that is given in <diagramEditor> (see also [Configuring connections](tutgraph2d_h_connections_configuration.htm)).  
<node> | Encloses the specific description of a node

parent_tutgraph2d_h_extend_configuration.title: Expanding the configuration with nodes for room components
parent_tutgraph2d_h_extend_configuration.content:
# Expanding the configuration with nodes for room components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Expanding the configuration with nodes for room components The nodes for room components must be configured in such a way that the corresponding image, suitable for the type of room component, is inserted in the diagram. To that end, there are images in an image register assigned to the types of room components.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_graph2d.title: GRAPH2D discipline
parent_tutgraph2d_h_graph2d.content:
# GRAPH2D discipline

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  GRAPH2D discipline A knowledge of the fundamentals is a precondition to be able to work through this part of the tutorial. These fundamentals are treated in the Tutorial section [Mechatronic](tutmechatronic_h_mechatronic.htm) and in the reference chapter[Form-UI](refformui_r_formui.htm). After the discipline model has been created, the result can be compared with the sample solution (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)).

parent_tutgraph2d_h_preparation_steps_ee.title: Preparatory steps in EEC
parent_tutgraph2d_h_preparation_steps_ee.content:
# Preparatory steps in EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps in EEC To concentrate in this tutorial on the configuration of the diagram, the entire data model is already present in the EOX file Graph2d_Ui.eox and is imported. The Graph2d_Ui.eox file is in the folder ..\Installationsordner\install\Tutorial\Graph2D. Read more  
     * [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm)

parent_tutgraph2d_h_task_configuration_d.title: Task: Creating a configuration diagram for office infrastructure
parent_tutgraph2d_h_task_configuration_d.content:
# Task: Creating a configuration diagram for office infrastructure

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Task: Creating a configuration diagram for office infrastructure A configuration diagram is to be created for the configuration of an office infrastructure: To achieve the objective of this task, this chapter first describes how the Tutorial project, with the architecture and modular library, should be imported by means of an EOX file. Then, the diagram configuration will be created and tested in steps. The configuration starts with the node for building components and room components (devices and persons). In the latter case, the master-slave relationship of nodes is studied. The nodes can first be dragged from the project tree into the diagram. For inputting parameter data, calls are created to pre-finished UIs. Next the definition is expanded with edges (connections), with which persons are connected with the devices, as also, the devices are connected with one another. Thereafter, the palette is extended, so that the nodes can then also be instanced and connected when working from the palette. Finally, the configuration is expanded in such a way that the components of the diagram can be moved at will in the framework of a permitted floor configuration. The following figure shows the data model for this tutorial:

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test1_insert_diagram.title: Insert diagram in project
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test1_insert_diagram.content:
# Insert diagram in project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Insert diagram in project A check should be carried out whether a diagram can be inserted in the project under the component Building.
    1. Select the project component Building.
    2. From the popup menu, select Add > Graph2D > IT_SolutionMap.
The diagram component is added under the project component Building (3). The editor of the diagram is open (4). The Editor area (5) is still empty and next to it, a palette (6) with the basic tools is displayed. At the top, in the toolbar (7) are the tools for aligning nodes, zooming and adding a wallpaper. Note The tool for aligning objects only becomes active when at least one object is selected in the Editor area. In the menu View, there are functions for aligning objects to a grid and/or to other objects, as well as for showing grid lines (see [Graphical editor](eecgraph2d_k_editor.htm)). The editor can be closed again for now.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test2_insert_floor_r.title: Test 2 - Inserting Floor and Room in the diagram.
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test2_insert_floor_r.content:
# Test 2 - Inserting Floor and Room in the diagram.

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 2 - Inserting Floor and Room in the diagram. A check should be made whether the component Floor can be dragged from the project tree into the diagram.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
    1. Drag the project component Floor with the mouse button held down from the project tree to the diagram editor area.
Till the mouse button is released, a rectangle of the size of the node to be added is displayed.
    1. Release the mouse button over the diagram editor.
Upon releasing the mouse button, a rectangle with a dash-dotted line and the name Floor A 4 is added.
    1. Repeat steps 2 and 3 with the project components Room, Room2, Room3 and Room4.
    2. Save the diagram ([Ctrl] \+ [S]).
To enlarge the visible editor area, the palette can be closed with a click on the triangle next to Palette and a corresponding zoom factor can be selected in the toolbar.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test3_insert_roomcom.title: Test 3 - Inserting the room components in the diagram
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test3_insert_roomcom.content:
# Test 3 - Inserting the room components in the diagram

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 3 - Inserting the room components in the diagram A check should be carried out whether the project components Worker, Telephone, PC, Printer, 2wayLAN_Jack and 5waySwitch can be dragged from the project tree into the rooms of the diagram.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
    1. Add the project component Worker by Drag & Drop from the project tree in the Room A 4.1 Office.
Upon releasing the mouse button, the image for a worker is inserted.
    1. Repeat steps 2 and 3 with all the project components that are arranged below Room, Room2, Room3 and Room4 in the project tree.
    2. Save the diagram ([Ctrl] \+ [S]).

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test4_call_formui_as.title: Test 4 - Calling Form-UIs as tooltips of the node.
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test4_call_formui_as.content:
# Test 4 - Calling Form-UIs as tooltips of the node.

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 4 - Calling Form-UIs as tooltips of the node. A check should be carried out whether, for the diagram node of Floor, Room, Worker, Telephone, PC, Printer, 2wayLANJack and 5waySwitch, a Tooltip-Overlay can be opened.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
    1. Double-click on a free area of the node Floor.
    2. Test the moving and changing the size of the window.
You can change the input fields in the right-hand column at will for testing. [Enter building data] takes you to a Form-UI that is assigned to the component Building.
    1. Now close the tooltip with a click on the red X.
    2. Test the opening of the tooltips with a double-click on all the other nodes of the diagram.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test5_check_slave_no.title: Test 5 - Checking the arrangement of the slave nodes
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test5_check_slave_no.content:
# Test 5 - Checking the arrangement of the slave nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 5 - Checking the arrangement of the slave nodes A check should be carried out whether the devices and persons in the rooms are labeled, and whether on the devices, in addition, LAN jacks are arranged as slaves.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
In the diagram, the names or numbers are shown as labels on the upper edge of the nodes for persons and devices (see Point 1 in the above figure). The LAN jacks of the devices are also shown as labels (see Point 2 in the above figure).
    1. Move the LAN-jack of PC Macroeconomix from the left-hand side to the right.
    2. When aligning, follow the displayed blue help line.
A help line is always displayed when the object to be moved is centrally situated, or with an outside edge in alignment with another object.
    1. Close the diagram without saving the changes.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test6_check_represen.title: Checking the representation of the connections
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test6_check_represen.content:
# Checking the representation of the connections

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Checking the representation of the connections A check should be carried out whether the connections between persons and devices, as well as between devices among one another, are all depicted correctly. At the same time, the correct display of the tooltips can be determined.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
All the connections are shown in the correct colors and along the shortest distance between the nodes of the diagram.
    1. Position the mouse pointer over the connection of Telephone 101 to 2wayLANJack 10404001.
The tooltip displays the following text: 1 of 101 | 1 of 10404001. The number 1 is the name of the in-built component of type LAN_Jack. The number 101 is the direct dial extension of the telephone and the number 10404001 is the number of the 2-X LAN-socket. According to the configuration, the connections between persons and devices are polylines and the following test will show that the straight connections can be changed into inflected connections (see the following figure, point 1).
    1. Select a connection, for example, the one between the person Jack Johnson and the telephone 101.
A bend point (2) is shown in the middle of the selected connection. If the mouse pointer is positioned over the bend point, the appearance of the mouse pointer (3) changes. This symbolizes that this bend point can now be moved. Every new straight line that is created gets a bend point in the middle, so that polylines that can be bent as many times as desired are obtained. Tip To simplify the alignment of the nodes and edges (connections), a grid can be displayed with the menu option View > Merge into grid.
    1. Close the diagram without saving the changes.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test7_insert_nodes_c.title: Test 7 - Insertion of nodes and connections from the palette
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test7_insert_nodes_c.content:
# Test 7 - Insertion of nodes and connections from the palette

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 7 - Insertion of nodes and connections from the palette A check should be made whether the palette entries for inserting nodes and connections in the diagram work.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
    1. Select the palette entry Floor.
    2. Navigate the mouse pointer without holding down any mouse button to a free area of the diagram.
    3. Save the node Floor with a left click.
    4. Repeat the insertion with the palette entries for Room, PC and 2way LAN Jack and Worker
    1. Select the palette entry Worker connection.
    2. In the newly inserted room click first on Worker and then on PC.
    3. Select the palette entry LAN connection.
    4. Click in succession on the slave node of the PC and then on a slave node of 2wayLANJack.
    5. Close the diagram IT_SolutionMap without saving the changes.

parent_tutgraph2d_h_test8_check_moving_n.title: Checking moving the nodes
parent_tutgraph2d_h_test8_check_moving_n.content:
# Checking moving the nodes

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Checking moving the nodes A check should be carried out whether the nodes for Room, and all the devices and workers can be moved from one parent node to another.
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the diagram IT_SolutionMap with a double-click.
    2. Create an additional floor (Floor2).
    3. Open the Form-UI for the new floor and enter the value A 5 into the Floor number field.
    4. Save the diagram ([Ctrl] \+ [S].
    1. Drag Room3 with the left mouse button held down from Floor to Floor2.
Note If moving a node is not possible, the mouse pointer symbol changes into a prohibitory notice.
    1. In Floor2, create an additional room (Room5).
    2. Open the Form-UI for the new room and select the value Office in the Room type field.
    3. Save the diagram ([Ctrl] \+ [S].
    4. Hold down the [shift] key and select all of the nodes in Room3.
    5. Drag the marked nodes with the left mouse button held down from Room3 to Room5.

parent_tutimp_h_import.title: Project generation per import
parent_tutimp_h_import.content:
# Project generation per import

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Project generation per import A basic knowledge of MS-Excel, CSV and XML files is necessary to complete this section of the tutorial. Several source files are required for this tutorial. Information which files these are and how they are made available is provided in the [Preparatory steps on a file level](tutimp_h_preparation_steps_files.htm) section.

parent_tutimp_h_preparation_steps_files.title: Preparatory steps on a file level
parent_tutimp_h_preparation_steps_files.content:
# Preparatory steps on a file level

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps on a file level An adapted mechatronics modular system and various import files are made available via a ZIP file for this tutorial. The files first have to be unzipped and stored into specific directories. Requirements:
     * EEC as a stand-alone installation Version 2.5.1 or higher.
Carry out the following steps to prepare the tutorial:
    1. Create the following folder on a file level (using Windows Explorer):
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources

    1. Unpack the contents of the following ZIP file into the folder of the preceding step:
    
        <EEC installation path>\install\Tutorial\Import\Tutorial_Import.zip

Subsequently the following directory structure exists under \resources:
    
        \Import

    \Import\CSV

    \Import\EOX

    \Import\IMX

    \Import\XML

    \Import\XSL

    1. Copy the file Tutorial_Import.eox from the directory
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\EOX\

into the directory
    
        <EEC installation path>\eox

The EOX file is used for all the following examples of this tutorial as data back end. A setting in the initialization file (ec.ini) has to be changed to this purpose. To ensure that the data back end file can be used for all steps of the tutorial, the access is restricted to Read-only (mode=r). To change the initialization file:
    1. Open the file <EEC installation path>\ec.ini in an any editor (e.g.++, Notepad).
    2. Replace the row
    
        -Dorg.foederal.sn.connectionURL=eox:///./eox/model.eox?mode=rw

by the row
    
        -Dorg.foederal.sn.connectionURL=eox:///./eox/Tutorial_Import.eox?mode=r

    1. Save the initialization file.
    2. Close the editor.

parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project.title: Part 1 - Creating a configuration by importing a file
parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project.content:
# Part 1 - Creating a configuration by importing a file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating configuration Creating configuration Part 1 - Creating a configuration by importing a file A new configuration is created by using the import wizard. Depending on the file type of the specified import file, the import wizard varies the input boxes for additionally required files, for example scheme and template file. Possible import files are CSV, Excel, IMX and XML files. The import scenarios are described using IMX and XML files as an example for this part.

parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project_imx.title: Creating a configuration by importing an IMX file
parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project_imx.content:
# Creating a configuration by importing an IMX file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a configuration by importing an IMX file Requirements:
     * The preparatory steps on the file level have been carried out.
     * EEC is started.
     * In addition to the system libraries, the Library catalog contains the architecture T_Mechatronic_Architecture and the modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
     * The **Feeder** project contains the Project catalog.
A new configuration with the name Feeder is to be generated which already exists, however. Therefore the existing configuration has to be removed first:
    1. In the Project catalog select the Feeder project.
    2. Click [Del].
The Delete dialog opens. The Feeder project is listed in it.
    1. Click [Finish].
The IMX file T1_Project.imx is imported to create a new configuration on the basis of the modular system. [Contents of the T1_Project.imx](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

    <imx xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude" version="1.0">

    	<project name="Feeder" save="true" >

    		<libraries>

    			<add type="String" value="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem"/>

    		</libraries>

    		<mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" />

    	</project>

    </imx>

Description of the IMX file: After the obligatory header data in Rows 1 and 2, the name of the new configuration is specified in Row 3 with the attribute name of the <project> tag on Feeder. Since the import wizard requires the specification of a project name, this specification is overridden by the import wizard. The save attribute with the value true determines that this configuration is saved immediately after creation. This tag is only effective if importing is carried out via action / command. The <libraries> tag encompasses the specification on which mechatronical modular system the configuration is based. With the <mo name="Feeder"> tag a component of the Feeder type is added to the configuration. This creates a new configuration.
    1. Select the menu item File > Import....
The import wizard opens with the Select page.
    1. Navigate to Project > Import.
    2. Confirm via [Next >].
The import wizard opens the page Import file.
    1. Specify the file T1_Project.imx in the Source file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\IMX

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file. If applicable, select the file filter IMX files (*.imx).
    1. Leave the Template file name field empty.
    2. If applicable, mark the Create new project option and enter the name Feeder in field to the right of it.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].
Result: The new configuration called Feeder is displayed in the project catalog: The Feeder configuration contains the Feeder with the built-in components Insert, Move and Store. The new aspect of this configuration is that a placeholder for components of the type Inspect is installed in Feeder. The Discard component is disabled and does not become active until a component of the type Inspect is installed on the placeholder.

parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project_xml.title: Creating a configuration by importing an XML file
parent_tutimp_h_t1_create_project_xml.content:
# Creating a configuration by importing an XML file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a configuration by importing an XML file Requirements:
     * The preparatory steps on the file level have been carried out.
     * EEC is started.
     * In addition to the system libraries, the Library catalog contains the architecture T_Mechatronic_Architecture and the modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
     * The **Feeder** project contains the Project catalog.
A new configuration with the name Feeder is to be generated which already exists, however. Therefore the existing configuration has to be removed first:
    1. In the Project catalog select the Feeder project.
    2. Click [Del].
The Delete dialog opens. The Feeder project is listed in it.
    1. Click [Finish].
The XML file T1_Project.xml is imported to create a new configuration on the basis of the modular system. To this purpose the scheme file XML_without_template.xsl is additionally required. [Contents of the T1_Project.xml](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

    <mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" >

    </mo>

Description of the XML file: After the obligatory header data in Row 1 a component of the Feeder type is added to the configuration in Row 2 with the tag <mo name="Feeder">. These specifications are converted internally to the IMX format that has the following contents:
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>  
    <imx xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude" version="1.0">  
    	<project name="Feeder" save="true" >

    		<libraries>

    			<add type="String" value="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem"/>

    		</libraries>

    		<mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" />

    	</project>

    </imx>

This creates a new configuration.
    1. Select the menu item File > Import....
The import wizard opens with the Select page.
    1. Navigate to Project > Import.
    2. Confirm via [Next >].
The import wizard opens the page Import file.
    1. Specify the file T1_Project.xml in the Source file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\XML

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file. If applicable, select the file filter XML files (*.xml).
    1. Specify the file XML_without_template.xsl in the Scheme file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\XSL

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file.
    1. Leave the Template file name field empty.
    2. If applicable, mark the Create new project option and enter the name Feeder in field to the right of it.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].
Result: The new configuration called Feeder is displayed in the project catalog: The Feeder configuration contains the Feeder with the built-in components Insert, Move and Store. The Placeholder_Inspect placeholder remains empty.

parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder.title: Part 2 â Placing components on placeholders and extension points
parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder.content:
# Part 2 â Placing components on placeholders and extension points

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Updating extension points Updating extension points Part 2 â Placing components on placeholders and extension points The import wizard cannot only be used to create a new configuration. In an existing configuration individual components can also be placed on placeholders and extension points.

parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_imx_ext.title: Placing components on an extension point by importing an IMX file
parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_imx_ext.content:
# Placing components on an extension point by importing an IMX file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Placing components on an extension point by importing an IMX file Requirements:
     * The preparatory steps on the file level have been carried out.
     * EEC is started.
     * In addition to the system libraries, the Library catalog contains the architecture T_Mechatronic_Architecture and the modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
     * The **Feeder** project contains the Project catalog.
     * Section [Creating a configuration by importing an IMX file](tutimp_h_t1_create_project_imx.htm) is carried out.
In order to place one or more components on an extension point in the existing configuration the IMX files T2_Extension_OneLevel.imx and T2_Extension_AllLevels.imx are imported. [Contents of the T2_Extension_OneLevel.imx](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

    <imx xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude" version="1.0">

    	<project name="Feeder" save="true" >

    		<libraries>

    			<add type="String" value="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem"/>

    		</libraries>

    		<mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" >

    			<mo name="Inspect" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Functiongroups.Inspect" calcExtensions="OneLevel"/>

    		</mo>

    	</project>

    </imx>

Description of the IMX file: After the obligatory header data in Rows 1 and 2, the name of the new configuration is specified in Row 3 with the attribute name of the <project> tag on Feeder. Since the import wizard requires the specification of a project name, this specification is overridden by the import wizard. The save attribute with the value true determines that this configuration is saved immediately after creation. This tag is only effective if importing is carried out via action / command. The <libraries> tag encompasses the specification on which mechatronical modular system the configuration is based. With the <mo name="Feeder"> tag a component of the Feeder type of the configuration is addressed. In the addressed component the <mo name="Inspect"> tag is used to place a component of the Inspect type on a suitable placeholder. The calcExtensions="OneLevel" attribute has the effect that the extension point that is installed in the Inspect component is updated. This means that a suitable component is placed on this extension point. [Contents of the T2_Extension_AllLevel.imx](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

    <imx xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude" version="1.0">

    	<project name="Feeder" save="true" >

    		<libraries>

    			<add type="String" value="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem"/>

    		</libraries>

    		<mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" >

    			<mo name="Inspect" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Functiongroups.Inspect" calcExtensions="AllLevels"/>

    		</mo>

    	</project>

    </imx>

Description of the IMX file: After the obligatory header data in Rows 1 and 2, the name of the new configuration is specified in Row 3 with the attribute name of the <project> tag on Feeder. Since the import wizard requires the specification of a project name, this specification is overridden by the import wizard. The save attribute with the value true determines that this configuration is saved immediately after creation. This tag is only effective if importing is carried out via action / command. The <libraries> tag encompasses the specification on which mechatronical modular system the configuration is based. With the <mo name="Feeder"> tag a component of the Feeder type of the configuration is addressed. In the addressed component the <mo name="Inspect"> tag is used to place a component of the Inspect type on a suitable placeholder. The calcExtensions="AllLevel" attribute has the effect that the extension point that is installed in the Inspect component is updated and all those located under it. This means that a suitable component is placed on this extension point and all suitable components located under it. Proceed as follows to place a component on an extension point of the subordinate level:
    1. Select the menu item File > Import....
The import wizard opens with the Select page.
    1. Navigate to Project > Import.
    2. Confirm via [Next >].
The import wizard opens the page Import file.
    1. Specify the file T2_Extension_OneLevel.imx in the Source file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
    
        <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\IMX

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file. If applicable, select the file filter IMX files (*.imx).
    1. Leave the Template file name field empty.
    2. Remove the Create new project option and mark the Feeder project in the list below it.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].
Result: The new configuration called Feeder is displayed in the project catalog: The Feeder configuration contains the Feeder with the built-in components Insert, Move and Store. The new aspect of this configuration is that the Inspect component is placed on the placeholder Placeholder_Inspect in Feeder. In the Inspect component the previously fixed installed component Orientationinspector is now placed on the ExtensionPoint_Orientationinspector extension point. The Orientationinspector component also has an extension point installed. However, no component has been placed on it. The Discard component is thus active and is also displayed. Note The display of placeholders, extension points and of components that are deactivated is influenced by the filter setting of the project catalog (see [Filter](eecbase_k_filter.htm)). Proceed as follows to install components on extension points of all the subordinate levels: In order to use the existing Feeder project the installed Inspect component has to be initially removed first:
    1. Mark the Inspect component in the Project catalog.
    2. Delete the component using the [Del] key.
    3. Confirm the selection with [Finish] in the Delete dialog.
After the Inspect component has been removed from the project the import can be started:
    1. Select the menu item File > Import....
The import wizard opens with the Select page.
    2. Navigate to Project > Import.
    3. Confirm via [Next >].
The import wizard opens the page Import file.
    4. Specify the file T2_Extension_AllLevel.imx in the Source file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
        
                C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringConfiguration\<Build-ID>\resources\Import\IMX

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file. If applicable, select the file filter IMX files (*.imx).
    5. Leave the Template file name field empty.
    6. Remove the Create new project option and mark the Feeder project in the list below it.
    7. Confirm with [Finish].
Result: The new configuration called Feeder is displayed in the project catalog: The Feeder configuration contains the Feeder with the built-in components Insert, Move and Store. The new aspect of this configuration is that the Inspect component is installed on the placeholder Placeholder_Inspect in Feeder. In the Inspect component the previously fixed installed component Orientationinspector is now placed on the ExtensionPoint_Orientationinspector extension point. The component Positionsensor_optical is placed on the extension point ExtentionPoint_Positionsensor_optical of the Orientationinspector component. The Discard component is thus active and is also displayed.

parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_imx_inst.title: Placing a component on a placeholder by importing an IMX file
parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_imx_inst.content:
# Placing a component on a placeholder by importing an IMX file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Placing a component on a placeholder by importing an IMX file Requirements:
     * The preparatory steps on the file level have been carried out.
     * EEC is started.
     * In addition to the system libraries, the Library catalog contains the architecture T_Mechatronic_Architecture and the modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
     * The **Feeder** project contains the Project catalog.
     * Section [Creating a configuration by importing an IMX file](tutimp_h_t1_create_project_imx.htm) is carried out.
The IMX file T2_Component.imx is imported in order to place a component in the existing configuration. [Contents of the T2_Component.imx](javascript:void\(0\);)
    
        <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

    <imx xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude" version="1.0">

    	<project name="Feeder" save="true" >

    		<libraries>

    			<add type="String" value="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem"/>

    		</libraries>

    		<mo name="Feeder" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder" >

    			<mo name="Inspect" typeClass="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Functiongroups.Inspect" />

    		</mo>

    	</project>

    </imx>

Description of the IMX file: After the obligatory header data in Rows 1 and 2, the name of the new configuration is specified in Row 3 with the attribute name of the <project> tag on Feeder. Since the import wizard requires the specification of a project name, this specification is overridden by the import wizard. The save attribute with the value true determines that this configuration is saved immediately after creation. This tag is only effective if importing is carried out via action / command. The <libraries> tag encompasses the specification on which mechatronical modular system the configuration is based. With the <mo name="Feeder"> tag a component of the Feeder type of the configuration is addressed. In the addressed component the <mo name="Inspect"> tag is used to place a component of the Inspect type on a suitable placeholder. Proceed as follows to install a component on a placeholder:
    1. Select the menu item File > Import....
The import wizard opens with the Select page.
    2. Navigate to Project > Import.
    3. Confirm via [Next >].
The import wizard opens the page Import file.
    4. Specify the file T2_Component.imx in the Source file name field.
The file is located in the following directory:
        
                <EEC installation path>\resources\Import\IMX

Use [Browse...] to navigate to the file. If applicable, select the file filter IMX files (*.imx).
    5. Leave the Template file name field empty.
    6. Remove the Create new project option and mark the Feeder project in the list below it.
    7. Confirm with [Finish].
Result: The new configuration called Feeder is displayed in the project catalog: The Feeder configuration contains the Feeder with the built-in components Insert, Move and Store. The new aspect of this configuration is that the Inspect component is placed on the placeholder Placeholder_Inspect in Feeder. In the Inspect component the previously fixed installed component Orientationinspector has been replaced by the ExtensionPoint_Orientationinspector extension point. The Discard component is thus active and is also displayed. Note The display of placeholders, extension points and of components that are deactivated is influenced by the filter setting of the project catalog (see [Filter](eecbase_k_filter.htm)).

parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_xml_inst.title: Placing a component on a placeholder by importing an XML file
parent_tutimp_h_t2_placeholder_xml_inst.content:
