See [Creating units](eecbase_k_create_unit.htm). Result:

parent_tutmechatronic_h_create_variant_p.title: Generation of variants via parameterization
parent_tutmechatronic_h_create_variant_p.content:
# Generation of variants via parameterization

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generation of variants via parameterization The structure of the Feeder component is not yet correct:
     * The function groups Inspect and Discard are permanently missing, and should be able to be enabled or disabled by means of a parameter.
     * The Axis component is used in two variants: On the one hand as X_Axis with four sensors and on the other hand as Y_Axis with a single end position sensor. In the following, the Axis component in the modular system is supplemented by three parameters to configure that an Axis component is used with one, two or four sensors. X_Axis should have four position sensors only if the function groups Inspect and Discard are available (see [Functional assembly](tutorial_k_example_feeder_functional_structure.htm)).

parent_tutmechatronic_h_create_variants_.title: Generation of variants via disabler
parent_tutmechatronic_h_create_variants_.content:
# Generation of variants via disabler

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generation of variants via disabler First, using the maximum principle, all possible components are created in the library. To create variants, the components that are not to be included will be excluded (disabled) by the composition. In this tutorial, we do not want to use in the Feeder component the function groups Inspect and Discard. For this purpose, we disable the installed components Inspect and Discard.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Click the installed component Inspect.
    3. In the Disabled field, enter = true.
    4. Repeat the steps 2 to 3 for the Discard component.
The effect is visible in the project without updating via [F5]: In the Feeder project component, only the components Store, Insert, and Move remain. Note As long as the filter is not enabled, the disabled components will be displayed as well. The filter is enabled in the toolbar of the Project Catalog.
    1. Enable the filter by clicking .
    2. Open the filter dialog by clicking .
You set the options in the filter dialog.
    1. Highlight the Disabled Components option.

parent_tutmechatronic_h_disable_componen.title: Disable components via disabler
parent_tutmechatronic_h_disable_componen.content:
# Disable components via disabler

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Disable components via disabler The presence of a component can be controlled via the so-called Disabler. If the condition entered here is true, the component will be disabled. For the example project, this means that disablers are set up for the function Functiongroups Inspect and Discard, for the sensors Position_2, Position_3, and Position_4 of the Axis function unit, and for the actuator Valve_2.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Click the Inspect component.
    3. In the Disabled field, enter the formula =not(mc.$Option_Inspect_available).
    4. Repeat the steps 1 to 3 for the Discard component.
With not, you negate the return value of the subsequent method mc.$Option_Inspect_available. Use this trick in order to apply, with the Option_Inspect_available parameter, positive logic (Option_Inspect_available = true: Inspect is enabled; Option_Inspect_available = false: Inspect is disabled). In the formula, mc (mechatronic component) refers to the (superordinate) mechatronic component â in this case, Feeder. From this, $Option_Inspect_available takes the value of the parameter of the same name. The point is used as the path separator, similar to the file path whose folders and subfolders are separated by slashes. Tip The input of formulas is simplified by a Content Assistant. For example, if you have written =not(mc., followed by [Ctrl] \+ [Space bar], possible additions are shown in a dialog. In many cases, values can be adopted from this dialog by selecting them, which simplifies manual input. For the axes, this has to be done for the position sensors Position_2, Position_3, and Position_4.
    1. Open the component Axis.
    2. In the editor, click the Position_2 component.
    3. In the Disabled field, enter the formula =not(mc.$Sensor_2_available).
    4. Repeat the previous steps for Position_3 with the formula =not(mc.$Sensor_3_available) and for Position_4 with =not(mc.$Sensor_4_available).
In the formula, mc (mechatronic component) references the superordinate component of the sensor, i.e., Axis. If after the point there were another mc, it would take you to a next higher level in the machine model for the Move Functiongroup, which uses Axis as the only component. In the subformula $Sensor_2_available, the dollar sign represents the parameter, followed by the parameter name, that is, the axis parameter Sensor_2_available. This subformula must be negated with the not function not() in order to obtain the correct value. The formula language is described in the section [Formulas](refformulas_r_formulas.htm). A disabler is also set up for the installed component Valve_2 of the component Cylinder.
    1. Open the component Cylinder.
    2. In the editor, click the Valve_2 component.
    3. In the Disabled field, enter the following formula =not(mc.$Sensor_Two_Valves).

parent_tutmechatronic_h_instantiate_comp.title: Instantiate components
parent_tutmechatronic_h_instantiate_comp.content:
# Instantiate components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Instantiate components In the previous step, you already selected the Feeder component, which was installed in the project as an instance in the project wizard. Without doing so first, you would have to instantiate the Feeder component manually. A component is instantiated by dragging it from the Library Catalog view to a project in the Project Catalog view. Instantiate means that an instance is created from the component specification. If the Feeder component has not been instantiated yet, proceed as follows:
     * Drag the Feeder component onto the Mechatronic object in the Feeder project.
The Mechatronic object is the root object of a mechatronic configuration (later in the tutorial, you will also encounter non-mechatronic configurations, meaning discipline-specific configurations). Remarks:
     * Try also to instantiate other components from the library by dragging them onto the Mechatronic object. This is not permitted for any objects except Feeder (the mouse pointer becomes a No Parking sign). This has to do with the rule defined in the architecture model that says that only Station can be the top element of a configuration (see [Specify the top-level component](tutmechatronic_h_assign_top_level_component.htm)). However, it is possible to integrate the Feeder component multiple times.
     * In the last screenshot, the term Feeder can be seen three times: As the name of a library component (class), as the project name, and as the name of the instance of the Feeder component in the project (instance). The identical names of classes and instances occur very often in practice, particularly when the class is used in a project only once.
In order to still give objects unique names, the absolute name should be used when in doubt, which is derived from the paths displayed in the tree structures:
     * T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Mechatronic.Stations.Feeder (Class)
     * Feeder (Project)
     * Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder (Instance)

parent_tutmechatronic_h_mechatronic.title: Mechatronic
parent_tutmechatronic_h_mechatronic.content:
# Mechatronic

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Mechatronic The mechatronic model is developed according to the maximum principle, so that non-desired components have to be switched off with an option (see also [Generation of variants via disabler](tutmechatronic_h_create_variants_per_disabler.htm)). The following disciplines are based on this mechatronic model:
     * Text
     * Word
     * Eplan Electric P8
     * Eplan Pro Panel
     * SIMATIC STEP 7
     * Project generation per import
     * Job Server
The disciplines are set up in such a way that their components are first built-in fixed into the components of the mechatronic. For experienced users the model in the connection point is rebuilt so that the discipline components can be relocated into their own libraries. Only on request are the discipline components inserted on the extension points of the mechatronic components. The following image schematically shows the conversion to extension points, using the example of the Feeder station. After the mechatronic model been created, the result can be compared with the sample solution (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)).

parent_tutmechatronic_h_select_variant.title: Select variant
parent_tutmechatronic_h_select_variant.content:
# Select variant

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Select variant Which variant of the component is used is determined at the time the component is used. Enable or disable the Inspect component by alternately setting the Option_Inspect_available parameter to true and false.
    1. Select the Parameters editor.
    2. For the Option_Inspect_available parameter, enter alternately true or false in the Value column.
You can see the result in the Project Catalog immediately after saving. Disabled components are not displayed in the Project Catalog if the filter is set up and enabled accordingly. For this purpose, click  and select the Disabled Components check box. After closing the filter dialog with [OK], the filter is enabled with the  icon. The Axis component is only used for Move, as X_Axis and Y_Axis. Y_Axis is the variant not specified; here, the parameters Sensor_2_available, Sensor_3_available, and Sensor_4_available must be given the value false:
    1. Open the Move function group.
    2. Select the component Y_Axis.
    3. In the table on the right, specify the value false in the rows Sensor_2_available, Sensor_3_available, and Sensor_4_available in the Value column.
The modifications are visible in the project without updating (via [F5]). X_Axis continues to have three sensors, while Y_Axis has only one Position_1 position sensor. X_Axis of the Move Functiongroup, depending on the Inspect Functiongroup, is to be equipped with or without the position sensors Position_2 and Position_3. For this purpose, the value of the Option_Inspect_available parameter is evaluated. Disabled components are not displayed in the Project Catalog if the filter is set up and enabled accordingly. For this purpose, click  and select the Disabled Components check box. After closing the filter dialog with [OK], the filter is enabled with the  icon.
    1. Open the Move function group.
    2. Click the X_Axis component.
    3. In the Value column for the parameters Sensor_2_available and Sensor_3_available, enter the formula: =mroot.$Option_Inspect_available.
In the formula, mroot references the root element of the mechatronic structure; in this case, Feeder, and $Option_Inspect_available queries the value of the parameter of the same name. A change to the Option_Inspect_available parameter now does not only affect the presence of the function groups Inspect and Discard, but also at the same time the presence of the position sensors Position_2 and Position_3. Test this behavior again, as already done before: If you set the value of the Option_Inspect_available parameter to true, the function groups Inspect and Discard, as well as the position sensors Position_2 and Position_3 will be installed. If you set the value of the Option_Inspect_available parameter to false, the function groups Inspect and Discard, as well as the position sensors Position_2 and Position_3 will be removed (see figure above). Disabled components are not displayed in the Project Catalog if the filter is set up and enabled accordingly. For this purpose, click  and select the Disabled Components check box. After closing the filter dialog with [OK], the filter is enabled with the  icon.

parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.title: Specify subcomponents
parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.content:
# Specify subcomponents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Specify subcomponents The goal of a standardization process is often to standardize certain substructures, for example, so as to ensure that a Feeder component always contains an Insert function group. In this case, it is very complicated, and prone to errors, to integrate the Insert function group into each project manually every single time. Relevant dependencies, therefore, should be defined already in the library. For each mechatronic component in the library, therefore, predefined contained components can be specified. In the following, the function groups Insert, Inspect, Store, Discard, and Move are always to be included in the Feeder component:
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Select the subcomponents Discard, Insert, Inspect, Move and Store.
    3. Using Drag & Drop, drag these subcomponents from the Library catalog view to the Components editor area.

parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.title: Specify subcomponents of the Cylinder component
parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.content:
# Specify subcomponents of the Cylinder component

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Specify subcomponents of the Cylinder component The Cylinder component must be supplemented with two valves.
    1. Assign the Valve component twice to the Cylinder.
    2. Name the installed components Valve_1 and Valve_2.

parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.title: Specify subcomponents of function groups
parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.content:
# Specify subcomponents of function groups

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Specify subcomponents of function groups In the case of the Feeder component, there are variances â for example, the function groups Inspect and Discard are installed only on an optional basis. If these variances are ignored at first (this will be addressed later), the entire functional assembly structure (see [Functional assembly](tutorial_k_example_feeder_functional_structure.htm)) can be predefined already in the modular system. Like with the Functiongroups for the Feeder station, the Functionunits must be added to the Functiongroups as components:
    1. Open Insert, and drag the Functionunits Separator and Stack to the components editor area (Components).
    2. Add the Orientationinspector Functionunit forInspect.
    3. For the Move Functiongroup, specify the Axis twice and the Gripper once as a component. The second Axis component will be inserted as Axis2 because components must be given unique names:
    4. Rename Axis to X_Axis and Axis2 to Y_Axis: Select the components to be renamed and with a further click switch to the editor mode (identifiable by the fact that only the text without a symbol is highlighted). Alternatively â as in the Library Catalog or Project Catalog view â you can switch to the editor mode using the [F2] function key.
Result:

parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.title: Specify subcomponents of function units
parent_tutmechatronic_h_specify_subcompo.content:
# Specify subcomponents of function units

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Specify subcomponents of function units Like the Functiongroups, the Functionunits, too, must be supplemented to include Actuators and Sensors.
    1. Assign to Axis the following components:
       * Cylinder
       * Valve
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_1
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_2
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_3
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_4
    2. Assign to Gripper the following components:
       * Valve
       * Pressuresensor
    3. Assign to Orientationinspector and Stack each the Positionsensor_optical component.
    4. Assign to Separator the following components:
       * Cylinder
       * Valve
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_1
       * Rename Positionsensor_inductive to Position_2

The section [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm) describes how an EOX file is imported. As an option for the import select Add. The following symbols can be displayed in the synchronization view: Symbol | Meaning  
---|---  
| The incoming changes shown with a plus sign (+) indicate that a component is missing in your solution.  
| The incoming changes shown with a minus sign (-) indicate that there is one more component in your solution than in the sample solution.  
| The incoming changes shown with the red double arrow indicate that action is required on the part of the user.  
Incoming changes generally indicate that your solution differs from the sample solution. Double-click on the incoming change to view further information on the component.

parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder.title: Example Feeder
parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder.content:
# Example Feeder

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Example Feeder The example machine is a feeder that removes parts from a magazine and moves them for further processing. After the parts are removed from the magazine, an optional check is performed to determine whether the workpiece is correctly positioned. After this has been performed, the part can optionally be rejected as a defective part.

parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_archite.title: Architecture model
parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_archite.content:
# Architecture model

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Architecture model In order to be able to combine and reuse library components in any project, a company must define general structures based on which components are developed and used. This is done by means of an architecture model. Terms such as function group and function have already been used in the feeder description. They correspond to a definition of hierarchical levels in a machine or plant specified by the Esslingen University of Applied Sciences for the feeder components. Other companies may, for example, alternatively use units, modules, or segments. Rules that are used as the basis for the modular system of feeders:
     * Stations contain function groups.
     * Function groups are comprised of function units.
     * Function units contain actuators and sensors.
Under these rules, a function group or station is, for example, not permitted to contain a sensor. The formal mapping of these rules in a library that defines the architecture model is shown in the following diagram: The rule for permitted sublevels is formalized using the is a permitted sublevel of relationship (shown using a dashed line and a filled rhombus), for example between Functiongroup and Functionunit. This property is inherited by all derived components (e.g., insert and separator). Because all library components inherit from an architecture model component, the rules apply to all modular system developers; in other words, a modular system developer cannot, for example, create a function group that contains a sensor. The following sections show step-by-step how to model the architecture model introduced in this section, the modular system, and the feeder project in EEC. The individual steps are structured as follows:
     * **Motivation:** This will explain why the step is necessary.
     * **Screenshot:** The screenshot shows EEC interface that should correspond to the current process step. Screenshots are also provided at the relevant locations and job steps.
     * **Step-by-step instructions:** A detailed description of the job steps are then provided in the step-by-step instructions.

parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_functio.title: Functional assembly
parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_functio.content:
# Functional assembly

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Functional assembly The functional assembly describes the functions to be performed by the mechatronic components. The feeder is divided into the Insert, Inspect, Discard, Store, and Move function groups. Every function group in turn consists of several function units, e.g., Separator, Stack, or Axis. If you break down the function units even further into sensors and actuators, the functional assembly structure would look as follows: In the figure above, the composition structure is displayed by means of is contained in relationships and a line with a filled rhombus as the arrow tip. As you can see, smaller components in particular, for example, Cylinder, Valve, or position sensor, are installed multiple times.

parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_modular.title: Modular system
parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_modular.content:
# Modular system

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Modular system A simple approach to reducing the complexity of machines and plants and to increase the level of standardization is to formally model recurrent solutions in a modular system. Components of the same design that are used multiple times in the feeder include cylinders, valves, and inductive/optical position sensors. Furthermore, the only difference between the X-axis and the Y-axis is the number of sensors; they can therefore be displayed in the modular system with several variants as a component that is used multiple times. Abstract modeling of the specific assembly structure in the modular system makes it possible to map key relationships and thus avoid redundancy. Information regarding an inductive sensor, e.g., the price or how it is rendered in the schematic diagram, only has to be saved once in the modular system as a result; this is crucial for keeping the amount of time required to maintain complex models to a minimum. All components of the feeder are specified as library components. During projection, the library components are added to a project (=instantiated, shown as a dotted arrow). The project components therefore do not themselves define the components; rather, they only reference to the library components. In the example Feeder, this results in a reduction in the number of components from 29 project components to 17 library components. The aim of modular system development is to reduce the number of manual operations required to create a project-specific configuration to a minimum. The modular system should ensure that the project engineer is not required to instantiate all 29 components of the example project. If he, for example, incorporates an axis into a project, it should be clear that a cylinder and valve must also be instantiated. These predefined settings are made by means of the is contained in relation in the modular system. To avoid redundancies, common features of the library components are further consolidated in a parent component. In the figure, the derived components are linked to their parent components from which they inherit properties by means of the is contained in relationship (an arrow with an empty triangle as its tip).

parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_overvie.title: Overview
parent_tutorial_k_example_feeder_overvie.content:

parent_tutorial_k_tutorial.title: Tutorial
parent_tutorial_k_tutorial.content:
# Tutorial

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Tutorial The step-by-step development of a modular system will be used to demonstrate functional engineering. The resulting modular system is used to create a Feeder station, a demo assembly developed at the Esslingen University of Applied Sciences: This [film](../Pictures/TZ_ablauf.wmv) shows a cycle of the demo assembly in operation. The tutorial is structured so that each chapter can be completed on its own (see figure below). Once the example machine has been visualized, the next step is to create a mechatronic modular system (feeder). The modular system for the feeder can be expanded to include disciplines. These can be created separately from each other. The purpose of every discipline is to create files such as a list of components or a schematic diagram. Presentation of the example machine ([Example Feeder](tutorial_k_example_feeder.htm)):
     * The diagram of the example machine serves as the basis for the [Functional assembly](tutorial_k_example_feeder_functional_structure.htm).
     * The functional assembly is used to create the abstract [Modular system](tutorial_k_example_feeder_modular_system.htm).
     * Next, the resulting architecture model is visualized ([Architecture model](tutorial_k_example_feeder_architecture.htm)).
Once the example machine has been visualized, the development of the [Mechatronic](tutmechatronic_h_mechatronic.htm) is described as follows:
     * In the first step, the basic architecture is defined ([Create mechatronic architecture model](tutmechatronic_h_create_mechatronic_architecture.htm)).
     * Building on this, the components required for the feeder are specified ([Design mechatronic modular system](tutmechatronic_h_develop_mechatronic_modular_system.htm)).
     * This then serves as the basis for configuring the feeder mechatronically ([Configuration](tutmechatronic_h_projects.htm)).
     * The [Generation of variants via disabler](tutmechatronic_h_create_variants_per_disabler.htm) and the [Generation of variants via parameterization](tutmechatronic_h_create_variant_per_parameterization.htm) are explained in separate sections.
A list of actuator and sensor components is used as an example to demonstrate how it is possible to create a text file from the mechatronic model using the [Text discipline](tuttext_h_text.htm):
     * The ListOfComponents is to be seen as the created results of a new discipline (see [Task: Generate a ListOfComponents](tuttext_h_task_generate_loc.htm)).
     * The ListOfComponents discipline is then added to the existing mechatronic model for the feeder ([Adding the ListsofComponents discipline to the mechatronic model](tuttext_h_extend_mechatronic_with_loc_discipline.htm)).
     * How it possible to use the plug-socket concept to generate a ListOfComponents that differs in structure from the functional layer is demonstrated in the section [Mechatronic model: Generating non-functional structures](tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_structure.htm) shown.
The ECAD discipline shows how a schematic for [Eplan Electric P8](tutp8_h_eplan_electric_p8.htm) can be created on the basis of the mechatronic model.
     * The first step is to [Create EPLAN Electric P8 components](tutp8_h_create_p8_components.htm) for this discipline.
     * The Eplan Electric P8 discipline is then added to the existing mechatronic model for the feeder ([Adding the schematic discipline to the mechatronic model](tutp8_h_extent_mechatronic_with_schematics_discipline.htm)).
     * The test at the end of the chapter [Use formulas for plugs, sockets, and PLC inputs](tutp8_h_assign_formulas_to_plugs_sockets_plc_inputs.htm) shows how to create a schematic that is aligned to the installed components on the basis of the plug-socket concept.
The Pro Panel discipline shows how a schematic for [Eplan Pro Panel](tutpropanel_h_propanel.htm) can be created on the basis of the mechatronic model.
     * The first step is to create discipline components for this discipline ([Creating EPLAN Pro Panel discipline components](tutpropanel_h_create_propanel_discipline_components.htm)).
     * The Eplan Pro Panel discipline is then added to the existing mechatronic model for the feeder ([Expanding the mechatronic model with Eplan Pro Panel components](tutpropanel_h_extend_mechatronc_with_propanel_components.htm)).
     * [Generating the finished state of the enclosure](tutpropanel_h_test2_generate_current_enclosure.htm) shows how an enclosure that is adapted to the installed components can be created with the aid of the disabler concept.
The PLC discipline is used to show how to create a program for [SIMATIC STEP 7](tutstep7_h_step7.htm) on the mechatronic model. This discipline is divided into four sections:
     * The first section shows how a STEP 7 program is to be created for the Feeder example machine ([Creating a STEP 7 program for Feeder](tutstep7_h_task1_generate_code.htm)).
     * In the second section addresses for the inputs and outputs are assigned ([Task 2 - Allocating addresses for the inputs and outputs](tutstep7_h_task2_assign_ioaddresses.htm)).
     * The third section shows how a symbol table is created ([Task 3 - Creating the symbol table](tutstep7_h_task3_generate_symboltable.htm)).
     * The last section shows how interrupt messages are inserted into the STEP 7 program ([Task 4 - Inserting fault messages into the STEP 7 code](tutstep7_h_task4_insert_error_messages.htm)).
The Office discipline shows how to create a list of actuators and sensors in table form as a Word file on the basis of the mechatronic model:
     * Word components are created first for this discipline ([Creating Word components](tutword_h_create_word_components.htm)).
     * The Word ListOfComponents is then added to the existing mechatronic model for the feeder ([Adding ListofComponents to the mechatronic model](tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_loc.htm)).
     * How it possible to use the plug-socket concept to generate a ListOfComponents that differs in structure from the functional layer is demonstrated in the section [Generating non-functional structures](tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_structure.htm) shown.
The GRAPH2D discipline does not reference the model of the example machine; for this reason, it has its own separate mechatronic model. The discipline shows to configure the office infrastructure with the help of the graphical diagram.
     * A diagram configuration is created from the ground up ([Create new diagram configuration](tutgraph2d_h_create_diagram_configuration.htm)).
     * Container objects (floor, room) are defined as polygons and linked with Form-UIs to enable data to be entered ([Expanding the configuration with nodes for floor and room](tutgraph2d_h_extend_configuration_by_floor_and_room_nodes.htm) and [Configuring tooltips for displaying Form-UIs](tutgraph2d_h_configuration_of_tooltips_for_formuis.htm)).
     * Devices and persons can be added as images to rooms. These can be linked using connections. Data can be added to these objects by way of Form-UI calls ([Configuring connections](tutgraph2d_h_connections_configuration.htm)).
     * The devices receive slave objects with which they can be tagged and through which they can be linked in order to map an IT landscape ([Configuring slave nodes as labels](tutgraph2d_h_configuration_of_slave_nodes_as_label.htm)).
     * Container objects, devices, persons, and connections are assembled in a tool palette ([Configuring palette entries](tutgraph2d_h_configuration_of_palette_entries.htm)).
     * Commands allow the creation of nodes from the project tree and the instantiation of new nodes and edges from the tool palette ([Commands for inserting room components (RoomComponent) from the project tree](tutgraph2d_h_commands_to_insert_roomcomponents_from_projecttree.htm) and [Test 7 - Insertion of nodes and connections from the palette](tutgraph2d_h_test7_insert_nodes_connections_from_palette.htm)).
The project generation via import shows how a complete configuration of the Feeder station is created on the basis of the mechatronics modular system via import wizard.
     * The import of IMX and XML files is used to show how a configuration is created.
     * it is shown how individual components are installed in the configuration on placeholders through the import of IMX and XML files.
     * The import of an IMX file is used to show how components are placed on placeholders and extension points.
     * In the completed configuration parameter values are et through the import of CSV files. This is shown with individual parameters of different types, but also with a parameter of the Map type that is filled with key-value pairs.
     * A further scenario shows how the imports are triggered by means of actions in a configuration UI, instead of via the import wizard.
The application of the Job Server shows how a configuration of the Feeder station is created on the basis of the mechatronical modular system by the Job Server or, respectively, by the Worker registered at it.
     * A Job definition is created in order to create a configuration via fileTrigger.
     * It is shown how the Job definition is extended in order to store the created configuration in a specific local folder.
     * The next step shows how the IMX file to be imported is changed in order to place components on placeholders and extension points.
     * A further scenario shows how a Job is started from the browser in order to create a configuration of the Feeder station.
     * The last section shows how key-value pairs are transferred to the URL by means of POST Job parameters in order to store these in a parameter in the configuration.

parent_tutp8_h_assign_fixed_values_to_pl.title: Assign fixed values for plugs and sockets to Eplan Electric P8 components
parent_tutp8_h_assign_fixed_values_to_pl.content:
# Assign fixed values for plugs and sockets to Eplan Electric P8 components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Assign fixed values for plugs and sockets to Eplan Electric P8 components For the structure of the ECAD discipline to be created without errors, values have to be assigned to the parameters for Plug and Socket. In the following steps the parameters of the plugs for all sensors occurring in the mechatronics structure are numbered beginning at I0.0 (notation for STEP 7).  
    1. In the Project Catalog, open the Mechatronics tree.
Tip If the tree structure does not indicate the sensors as in the below illustration, please check the settings for the filter. Uncheck the box for the ECAD option, if necessary.
    1. Open Sensor_optical.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. For the IP parameter, enter the value I0.0.
    4. Repeat Steps 2 to 4 for all further sensors and enter I0.1 to I0.7, I1.0 and I1.1 in the process.
    5. Then enter the values I0.0 to I0.7 and I1.0 to I1.7 for the socket parameters of the pages.
    6. Open the PLC_Sensors_1_8 page.
    7. Click Parameters in the editor.
    1. For the parameters Input1 to Input8, enter the values I0.0 to I0.7.
    2. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for the PLC_Sensors_9_16 page, and enter the values I1.0 to I1.7.

parent_tutp8_h_assign_formula_to_disable.title: Assign formula to Disabler for the second page
parent_tutp8_h_assign_formula_to_disable.content:
# Assign formula to Disabler for the second page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Assign formula to Disabler for the second page To prevent an empty page in the schematic when the Inspect function group is disabled, a formula for the Disabler must be entered. See [Disabler](eecbase_k_variantmanagement_disabler.htm).
    1. For the installed discipline component PLC_Sensors_9_16 in the Feeder component enter the following formula into the Disabled field:
        
                =not(mroot.rmos('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.ECAD.PLC_Inputs.abstract_Sensor').size > 8)

    2. This formula determines whether the total number of (abstract) sensors is greater than 8 (.size > 8). If the number is less than 8, the page is disabled. If the number is greater than 8, the page is active.

parent_tutp8_h_assign_formulas_to_plugs_.title: Use formulas for plugs, sockets, and PLC inputs
parent_tutp8_h_assign_formulas_to_plugs_.content:
# Use formulas for plugs, sockets, and PLC inputs

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Use formulas for plugs, sockets, and PLC inputs Before using the formula for calculating the plug parameter values, the fixed values have to be deleted first.
    1. In the Project Catalog open the Feeder component.
    2. Set the Option_Inspect_available parameter to true.
    3. Open the Mechatronic structure.
    4. For each sensor, delete the value of the IP parameter.
An abstract sensor is required so that the formula for the plug parameter has to be defined only in one location. The concrete sensors are then derived from the abstract sensor and inherit amongst other its parameters. In the ECAD.PLC_Inputs unit create the abstract sensor with the name abstract_Sensor with the Fragment superclass. See [Creating discipline components](eecbase_k_create_discipline_component.htm). Assign the abstract_Sensor class as the super class to the three sensors:
    1. Open Sensor_Pressure.
    2. In the Super class field, select abstract_Sensor.
    3. Repeat Steps 1 to 2 for the sensors Sensor_optical and Sensor_Inductive.
The abstract sensor requires a parameter that stores a list of sensors as well as a parameter for the function text. In the ECAD.Parameter unit create a parameter with the name Sensors and the type Collection, as well as a parameter with the name Functiontext and the type String. See [Create parameter](eecbase_k_create_parameter.htm). Assign the parameters IP, DT, Sensors and Functiontext to the abstract sensor:
    1. Open the abstract_Sensor class.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Add the parameters IP, DT and Sensors.
    4. In the Value field enter the following formula for the IP parameter:
        
                ='I' + ($Sensors.indexOf(origin.ifNull(this)) /8) + '.' + $Sensors.indexOf(origin.ifNull(this)).mod(8)

    5. This formula combines the various parts of a plug parameter. As such, the letter âIâ is assembled with two values that are separated by â.â. The first value is the position of the sensor in the list divided by 8. The second value is the position of the sensor in the list modulo 8.
    6. Enter the following formula for the Sensors parameter:
        
                =mroot.rmos('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.ECAD.PLC_Inputs.abstract_Sensor')

    7. This formula is used to determine a list of all subordinate components of the abstract_Sensor type.
At a later point you enter a formula for the DT parameter. The calculation of the socket parameters on the pages requires a parameter that contains the page number. Create the Page parameter of the String type in the ECAD.Parameter unit, and assign this parameter to the abstract_Page page. The parameter is inherited to the Page subclass. Formulas have to be entered in the abstract page for the values of the Page and PageDescription parameters. The formulas for the socket parameters Input1 to Input8 are entered directly in the Page page:
    1. Open the abstract page abstractPage.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Enter the following formula in the Value field of the Page parameter:
        
                =mroot.rmos('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.ECAD.Page.Page').indexOf(origin.ifNull(this))

    4. This formula is used to determine a list of all subordinate components of the Page type, and in it the pageâs own position.
    5. Enter the following formula for the PageDescription parameter:
        
                =name

    6. This formula determines the name of the page.
    7. On the Page page enter the following formulas for the parameters Input1 to Input8:
Name | Value  
---|---  
Input1 | ='I' + $Page + '.0'  
Input2 | ='I' + $Page + '.1'  
Input3 | ='I' + $Page + '.2'  
Input4 | ='I' + $Page + '.3'  
Input5 | ='I' + $Page + '.4'  
Input6 | ='I' + $Page + '.5'  
Input7 | ='I' + $Page + '.6'  
Input8 | ='I' + $Page + '.7'  
    8. These formulas are used to assemble the socket names on which the plugs of the sensors will fit later on. The socket name consists of the letter "I", the content of the Page parameter (here, 0 or 1), and the extension ".0" to ".7". This results in the socket names I0.0 to I0.7 and I1.0 to I1.7.

parent_tutp8_h_assing_formulas_to_parame.title: Assign formulas to parameters for DT and function texts
parent_tutp8_h_assing_formulas_to_parame.content:
# Assign formulas to parameters for DT and function texts

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Assign formulas to parameters for DT and function texts The Function text parameter was already created for the function texts and inserted into the abstract sensor abstractSensor. The DT parameter in the abstract sensor is given as a value a formula that assembles a device tag.
    1. Open the abstract_Sensor sensor.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Enter the following formula for the DT parameter:
        
                ='S' + ($Sensors.indexOf(origin.ifNull(this))+1)

    4. This formula assembles the texts S1 to S10 from the letter âSâ and the position of the sensor.
    5. Enter the following formula for the Functiontext formula:
        
                =mc.absoluteName.substring(mroot.absoluteName.size+1, mc.absoluteName.size - 1).replaceAll('\\.',' ')

    6. The Functiontext parameter determines only part of the required function text, but does shorten the formulas that still have to be entered in the next step for the function texts of the page.
Enter the formulas for the function texts of the Page discipline component.
    1. In the ECAD.Page unit, open the Page discipline component.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Enter the following formulas for the parameters Functiontext1 to Functiontext8:  

Name | Value  
---|---  
Functiontext1 | =if dos.size>0 then dos.at(0).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext2 | =if dos.size>1 then dos.at(1).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext3 | =if dos.size>2 then dos.at(2).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext4 | =if dos.size>3 then dos.at(3).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext5 | =if dos.size>4 then dos.at(4).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext6 | =if dos.size>5 then dos.at(5).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext7 | =if dos.size>6 then dos.at(6).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext8 | =if dos.size>7 then dos.at(7).$Functiontext else '' endif  
    4. These formulas determine the value (text) of the Functiontext parameter of the respective discipline-specific component.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Divide EplanÂ ElectricÂ P8 schematic into macros
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Divide EplanÂ ElectricÂ P8 schematic into macros

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Divide Eplan Electric P8 schematic into macros The loaded macro project is to be used to create macros. First, copy the 1 Sensors page twice. Using one copy, create a window macro of a page in which all sensors have been deleted (see the following figure). The other page is not required for the window macro. Using the second copy, a fragment is created of each unique sensor. First, though, all duplicate sensors as well as the PLC inputs and power supply lines must be deleted. Information: The copies ensure that the macros can be assigned to the corresponding original page. This assignment must be maintained, so that subsequent changes to the macro project can be synchronized with EEC.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Set device tag
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Set device tag

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Set device tag Insert a device tag to identify the sensors:
    1. Mark all the sensors on the 1b Sensors page.
    2. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Edit > Properties.
    3. The Properties (components): General devices opens.
    4. If required, open the Function tab.
    5. In the Displayed DT field, enter the text <DT>.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. The message window Settings: DT syntax check opens.
    8. Click [Ignore].
    9. The message window is displayed for each sensor and has to be closed correspondingly often by using [Ignore].

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Create copies of pages
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Create copies of pages

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create copies of pages Create the first copy of the 1 Sensors page for the window macro:
    1. If necessary, open the Macro_Project project again.
    2. Highlight the 1 Sensors page.
    3. Select the following commands: Start > Copy.
    4. Select the following commands: Start > Insert.
    5. The Adapt structure dialog opens.
    6. Enter 1a in the Page name field.
    7. Confirm with [OK].
    8. The Insertion mode dialog opens:
    9. Select the Do not modify option.
    10. Confirm with [OK].
    11. The Insert mode dialog is closed and the page is listed in the page navigator.
Change the description of the 1a Sensors page in the page properties:
    1. Highlight the 1a Sensors page.
    2. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Edit > Properties.
    3. The Page properties dialog opens.
    4. Enter Page in the Page description field.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. The Page properties dialog is closed and the page with the changed name is listed in the page navigator.
You must also create a copy of the 1 Sensors page for the fragments of the sensors:
    1. Copy the 1 Sensors page.
    2. Enter 1b in the Page name field.
    3. In the Insertion mode dialog select the Do not modify option.
    4. Confirm with [OK].
Now the Macro_Project project should contain the following pages:
     * 1 Sensors
     * 1a Page
     * 1b Sensors
     * 2 Sensors

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Create macro box
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Create macro box

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create macro box Create a macro box that combines all symbols of this page into a macro. Note Make sure that the anchor of the placeholder object is inside the macro box; otherwise, the macro cannot be created correctly.
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Insert > Command group Navigator > Insert macro box.
    2. Drag a frame around the power supply lines and PLC inputs.
    1. End the Insert macro box function using the [ESC] key.
Define the properties of the macro box.
    1. Highlight the macro box.
    2. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Edit > Properties.
    3. The Properties (components): Macro box opens.
    4. In the Name field, enter the file name Page\Page.
Note For the input of the name, German umlauts (Ã, Ã, Ã, Ã¤, Ã¶, Ã¼) are not permitted, because the default Eplan settings are used.
    1. Open the Display tab.
    2. Click (New).
    3. The Property selection dialog opens.
    4. Select from the list Macro: File name.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. You return to the dialog Properties (component): Macro box.
    7. Confirm with [OK].
    8. This completes the preparations for the window macro of the page. Export to a macro occurs at a later time.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Delete duplicate sensors, PLC inputs, and power supply lines
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Delete duplicate sensors, PLC inputs, and power supply lines

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Delete duplicate sensors, PLC inputs, and power supply lines Since only one macro can be created from each sensor type, all duplicate sensors must be deleted. This also applies for PLC inputs and power supply lines. The 1b Sensors page should then look like this:

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Delete sensors from page
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Delete sensors from page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Delete sensors from page The window macro of the page should not contain any sensors. They must be removed from the page:
    1. Double-click the 1a Page page to open it.
    2. Highlight all sensors.
    3. Delete the sensors using the [Delete] key.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Insert function texts in page
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Insert function texts in page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Insert function texts in page The function texts of the PLC inputs are to be inserted by the EEC while creating. Therefore, the existing texts are replaced by function texts:
    1. Double-click the first PLC input I0.0.
    2. The Properties (component) PLC and bus ports window opens.
    3. In the Function text field, exchange the text with the following:
    
        <Functiontext1>

    1. Confirm with [OK].
    2. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for each further PLC input, and use ascending numbers for the function texts (<Functiontext2>, <Functiontext3>, etc.).

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Creating a placeholder object macro for a page
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Creating a placeholder object macro for a page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a placeholder object macro for a page Insert a placeholder object in order to define the function texts as placeholders. Each function text is assigned a variable name. When the macros are imported into EEC, a parameter of the same name is created for each variable. These parameters store meaningful texts in EEC that are used instead of placeholders when generating the schematics.
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Insert > Command group Navigator > Insert placeholder object.
    2. Drag a frame around the power supply lines and PLC inputs.
    3. The Properties (components): Placeholder object opens.
    4. Open the popup menu of the table.
    5. Select Adopt variables.
    6. The Variable column is then automatically filled with the name of the variables.
Note The variables for the socket and plug parameters must not be entered in the Variable column, so that no duplicate objects can be created during the automatic application of the parameters.
    1. Select the Page property.
    2. Click  (New).
    3. The Property selection dialog opens.
    4. In the list select the entry Page description.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. The Property selection dialog is closed and you are returned to the dialog Properties (components): Placeholder object.
    7. In the Variable column, enter <PageDescription>.
    8. From the Variable column remove all variable entries for socket parameters (#<Socket:Input1> to #<Socket:Input8>).
    9. Open the Values tab.
    10. From the Variable column remove all variable entries for socket parameters (#<Socket:Input1> to #<Socket:Input8>).
    11. Confirm with [OK].
    12. The Properties (components): Placeholder object is closed.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Insert plugs for sensors
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Insert plugs for sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Insert plugs for sensors Only one plug is defined for each sensor. To ensure that the plugs are positioned correctly on the sockets of the 1a Page page, copy the sockets of this page to the clipboard, and insert them in the 1b Sensors page at the same position.
    1. Mark the sockets #<Socket:Input1>, #<Socket:Input3> and #<Socket:Input4> of the page 1a Page.
    2. Copy the sockets.
    3. Open the 1b Sensors page.
    4. Insert the sockets.
Note Press the keys [X] and [Y] one by one in order to position the inserted socket texts at the same location. Now create plug texts from the socket texts.
    1. Market the socket texts.
    2. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Edit > Properties.
    3. The dialog Properties (Text) opens.
    4. Replace the text <<...>> by #<Plug:IP>.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. All selected texts were changed simultaneously.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.title: Insert sockets in page
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_into.content:
# Insert sockets in page

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Insert sockets in page You must insert Sockets to ensure that the sensors can be placed by EEC when generating the page. Each socket defines a place on which a sensor (with plug) can be positioned. A macro can contain several sockets, but no more than one plug.
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Text > Insert.
    2. Enter the following text for the socket:
    
        #<Socket:Input1>

    1. Confirm with [OK].
    2. Position the text on the right above the PLC input, as shown in the following figure:
    1. Repeat the steps 1 o 5 for each further PLC input, and use ascending numbers for the socket (<Socket:Input2>, <Socket:Input3>, etc.). Alternatively you can copy the existing text via the commands Edit > Copy and Edit > Insert or paste it multiple times and adapt the texts.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_macr.title: Generating all macros
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_macr.content:
# Generating all macros

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating all macros All preparations for the macros are completed. Now all macros will be generated together:
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Master data > Command group Macros > Generate automatically.
    2. The dialog Generate macros automatically opens.
    3. Mark the Overwrite existing macros option.
    4. Mark the Apply to entire project option.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. The dialog Generate macros automatically is closed.
    7. Exit Eplan Electric P8.
The macros will be stored in the following directories:
    
        <EEC installation folder>\resources\ECAD\Tutorial\P8\Page

    <EEC installation folder>\resources\ECAD\Tutorial\P8\PLC_Inputs

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_sens.title: Create macro boxes for sensors
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_sens.content:
# Create macro boxes for sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create macro boxes for sensors Now, a fragment is prepared as a macro from each single sensor together with the associated plug. Note Make sure that the anchors of the placeholder objects are inside the macro boxes; otherwise, the macros cannot be created correctly.
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Insert > Command group Macros > Navigator > Insert macro box.
    2. Drag a macro box around each sensor together with the plug. The elements framed by the macro box may have to be moved first to a free position on the page. Make sure that the sensor and plug maintain their positions to each other.
    1. Highlight the macro box.
    2. Select the following commands: Tab Home > Command group Edit > Properties.
    3. The dialog Properties (components): Macro box opens.
    4. In the Name field, enter the text PLC_Inputs\Sensor_Inductive.
Note Do not use German umlauts (Ã, Ã, Ã, Ã¤, Ã¶, Ã¼) when entering the name. Note Specifying a folder (e.g. PLC_Inputs\\) when conducting a mass import of macros into EEC automatically creates a unit of the same name that facilitates structured arrangement of the macros.
    1. Open the Display tab.
    2. Click [New].
    3. The Property selection dialog opens:
    4. Select Macro: File name.
    5. Confirm with [OK].
    6. The Property selection dialog is closed and you are returned to the Properties (components) macro box dialog.
    7. Confirm with [OK].
    8. Repeat steps 1 to 10 for the other two sensors. Enter the texts PLC_Inputs\Sensor_optical and PLC_Inputs\Sensor_Pressure as the name of the other sensors.

parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_sens.title: Create placeholder objects for sensors
parent_tutp8_h_detach_wiringdiagram_sens.content:
# Create placeholder objects for sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Create placeholder objects for sensors Analog to the schematic page a placeholder object has to be added for each sensor to equip the macros with variables.
    1. Select the following commands: Tab Insert > Command group Macros > Navigator > Insert placeholder object.
    2. Open a frame for each sensor that includes all the elements of the sensor.
    3. The dialog Properties (components): Placeholder object opens.
    4. Open the popup menu of the table.
    5. Select Adopt variables.
    6. The Variable column is then filled with the name of the variables.
Note The variables for the socket and plug parameters must not be entered in the Variable column, so that no duplicate objects can be created during the automatic application of the parameters.
    1. From the Variable column remove the variable entry for the plug parameter (#<Plug:IP>).
    2. Open the Values tab.
    3. From the Variable column remove the variable entry for the plug parameter (#<Plug:IP>).
    4. Confirm with [OK].

parent_tutp8_h_eplan_electric_p8.title: Eplan Electric P8
parent_tutp8_h_eplan_electric_p8.content:
# Eplan Electric P8

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Eplan Electric P8 Basic knowledge of Eplan Electric P8 is necessary to complete this section of the tutorial. Several source files are required for this discipline. Information on the name of these files and how they are provided will be described in the [Example Feeder](tutorial_k_example_feeder.htm) section. After the discipline model has been created, the result can be compared with the sample solution (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)).

parent_tutp8_h_expert.title: Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points
parent_tutp8_h_expert.content:
# Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points The upgrade of the model to extension points is targeted towards experienced users who are already familiar with the basic functions. The currently structured model serves as the starting point. If the model has not been elaborated, the P8.eox sample file has to be imported as well as the EEC preferences, model variables and the P8 settings have to be adapted. The concept of extension points is based on the idea that only such components are installed on an extension point that import an interface by the same name and that are visible for the project. Those discipline components are visible whose libraries have been imported into the project. The number of components to be inserted is specified separately for each extension point or is controlled by formulas. The insertion of components is triggered by an update of the extension points. The model is upgraded to extension points by means of the following steps: [Deleting the project](tutp8_h_expert_delete_project.htm) [Expanding the architecture](tutp8_h_expert_extend_architecture.htm) [Creating the library for interfaces](tutp8_h_expert_create_interface_lib.htm) [Importing the library for interfaces into the modular system](tutp8_h_expert_import_interface_lib_to_mech.htm) [Installing the extension points in mechatronic components](tutp8_h_expert_extent_mech_by_extensionpoints.htm) [Creating the library for the discipline ECAD P8](tutp8_h_expert_create_discipline_lib.htm) [Encapsulating the discipline components in mechatronic components](tutp8_h_expert_encapsulate_discipline_comps.htm) [Modifying the formulas](tutp8_h_expert_correct_formulas.htm) [Creating the actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system](tutp8_h_expert_create_action_for_import_of_discipline.htm) [Generating the finished schematic](tutp8_h_expert_test.htm)

parent_tutp8_h_expert_correct_formulas.title: Modifying the formulas
parent_tutp8_h_expert_correct_formulas.content:
# Modifying the formulas

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Modifying the formulas New formulas are necessary due to the encapsulation of the discipline components and the usage of interface parameters. [The formulas for component M_Schematic](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_Schematic.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the following value for the FunctionDesignation parameter:
    
        =mc.$FunctionDesignation

    1. Save the component.
The formula =mc.$FunctionDesignation requires that a FunctionDesignation parameter exists in the higher-level component, meaning the Feeder component and that it supplies a suitable value. This parameter must still be inserted in the Feeder and have a value assigned to it. [Creating the FunctionDesignation parameter and inserting it into Feeder](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Mark the Mechatronic.Parameter unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select New > Engineering > Parameter.
    3. Specify the FunctionDesignation name.
    4. Specify the String type.
    5. Save the parameter.
Insert the FunctionDesignation parameter into the Feeder component.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Drag the Plant parameter from the Parameter unit into the editor page.
    4. Enter the ='Feederâ formula as the value.
    5. Save the component.
[Modifying the formulas for the component M_SchematicPage](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_SchematicPage.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the following values for the parameters:
Name | Value  
---|---  
Functiontext1 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 0) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 0).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext2 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 1) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 1).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext3 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 2) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 2).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext4 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 3) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 3).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext5 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 4) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 4).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext6 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 5) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 5).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext7 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 6) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 6).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Functiontext8 | =if (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).size > $PageNo * 8 + 7) then mroot.mos('ISensor',4).at($PageNo * 8 + 7).$Functiontext else '' endif  
Input1 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.0'  
Input2 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.1'  
Input3 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.2'  
Input4 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.3'  
Input5 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.4'  
Input6 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.5'  
Input7 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.6'  
Input8 | ='I' + $PageNo + '.7'  
PageDescription | ='PLC_Sensors_' + ((($PageNo + 1) * 8) - 7) + '_' + (($PageNo + 1) * 8)  
PageNo | =$PageNumber.asInteger -1  
PageNumber | =mc.mos('ISchematicPage').indexOf(this) +1  
FunctionDesignation | =mc.$FunctionDesignation  
    1. Save the component.
[Modifying the formulas for the components M_Sensor_Inductive, M_Sensor_optical and M_Sensor_Pressure](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_Sensor_Inductive.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the following values for the parameters:
Name | Value  
---|---  
DT | ='S' + ($Sensors.indexOf(origin.ifNull(this))+1)  
Functiontext | =mc.absoluteName.substring(mroot.absoluteName.size+1, mc.absoluteName.size - 1).replaceAll('\\\\.',' ')  
IP | ='I' + (mroot.mos('ISensor',4).indexOf(this) / 8) + '.' + mroot.mos('ISensor',4).indexOf(this).mod(8)  
Sensors | =mroot.rmos('ISensor')  
    1. Save the component.
    2. Repeat Steps 1 to 4 for the components M_Sensor_optical and M_Sensor_Pressure.

parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_action_for_.title: Creating the actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system
parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_action_for_.content:
# Creating the actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system The upgrade to extension points has the advantage that during the configuration of a machine or plant only mechatronic components are involved and the data model therefore remains smaller. Only when the documents of a discipline have to be created for the completely configured machine or plant, are the discipline components installed per import of the discipline library and update of the extension points. Both the import as well as the update are to be triggered through actions by means of buttons of a user interface. The actions are based on a command and a SequenceSelectionAction that are created in the following steps. [Creating the command for the import of a library](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select New > Engineering > Unit in the popup menu.
    3. Specify the Actions name.
    4. Select the new Actions unit.
    5. Select New > Object in the popup menu.
    6. Click Object > Command.
    7. Confirm with [Finish].
    8. Specify the ImportLibCommand name.
    9. Switch to the Methods editor page.
    10. Click .
    11. The New Name has to be entered in the table and the Name field.
    12. Specify execute as the name.
    13. In the drop-down list select LanguageSequence.
    14. In the Arguments area click on .
    15. The New Argument with the Engineering.Object type is entered in the table of the arguments.
    16. Enter root as the name.
    17. Enter Engineering.Root as the type.
    18. Repeat Steps 13 to 15 with the name libNames and the type Engineering.List.
The new arguments are entered as values for the signature of the method. It is now an execute method that requires the root node of the mechatronic and the list of the libraries to be imported as arguments. A table is shown below with the specification of type names, method names and method arguments.
    1. Click  above the table.
    2. In the Select method dialog, click Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > ImportLibrariesCommand.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].
    4. Repeat Steps 18 to 20 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > SaveObjectCommand.
    5. Save the command.
    6. With this command the framework commands for the import of libraries and the subsequent saving are performed.
    7. This concludes the creation of the command for the import of a library.
[Creating the SequenceSelectionAction for the update of extension points](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the Actions unit.
    2. Select New > Object in the popup menu.
    3. Click Object > Action > SelectionAction > SequenceSelectionAction.
    4. Confirm with [Finish].
    5. Specify the UpdateExtensionPointsAction name.
    6. Specify Update Extension Points in the Displayed Name field.
    7. Switch to the Methods editor page.
    8. Click .
    9. Click Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > StartFormulaCacheCommand.
    10. Confirm with [Finish].
    11. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > UpdateExtensionsCommand.
    12. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > StopFormulaCacheCommand.
    13. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > SaveObjectCommand.
    14. Switch to the Attributes editor page.
    15. Specify Component in the Selected object field.
    16. Save the SequenceSelectionAction.
    17. With this SequenceSelectionAction the framework commands for starting the formula cache, update of extension points, stopping the formula cache and the subsequent saving are performed consecutively.
    18. This completes the creation of the SequenceSelectionAction for updating the extension points.
The created actions are then linked with interfaces in a small user interface. The user interface (Form-UI) is created for the component Feeder and therefore is available directly after the creation of a project. [Creating the Form-UI for Feeder](javascript:void\(0\);) With the user interface you can not only set up the interfaces for the import of the library but you can also make the option for checking workpiece available for selection.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Change to the UI configuration editor page.
    3. Open the Source tab.
    1. In the <form> tag specify the Feeder title and the ID feeder.
    2. Insert the following lines after the opening tag <form>:
    
        <line>

    	<label font="Arial-20-bold">Feeder configuration</label>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<gap hSizePx="10"/>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<input receiver="=parameter('Option_Inspect_available')" type="check" ></input>

    	<label font="Arial-12">Add workpiece inspection</label>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<gap hSizePx="10"/>

    </line>

    <action name="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Actions.ImportLibCommand" 

    	arguments="=List{this.mroot.parent, List{'T_ECAD_P8'}}" 

    	type="button" 

    	text="Import ECAD P8 Discipline">

    </action>

    <action name="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Actions.UpdateExtensionPointsAction" 

    	arguments="=mroot" 

    	type="button" 

    	text="Update extensions">

    </action>

Through the first three rows the user interface receives the Feeder configuration header. The header is followed by three rows to create space. Below that is a row with a check box, followed by a text. The check box influences the Option_Inspect_available parameter that amongst others is used for the Disabler of the function groups Inspect and Discard. Three further lines follow to create space to the buttons. This is followed by two buttons that can each perform actions. The first button triggers the action to import the library T_ECAD_P8. With the second button the action for updating the extension points is performed. [Setting the default value for the Option_Inspect_available parameter](javascript:void\(0\);) The check box for the Option_Inspect_available parameter in the user interface should always have a defined state at the beginning of the configuration. In this case the check box should not be selected, so that the value is false.
    1. Open the Option_Inspect_available parameter.
    2. Switch to the Formula editor page.
    3. Enter false in the Value field.
    4. Save the parameter.
This value is now specified as the default value. As a result the Add workpiece inspection option will not be selected when opening the user interface for the first time. Read more
     * [Form-UI](refformui_r_formui.htm)
     * [action](refformui_r_action.htm)

parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_discipline_.title: Creating the library for the discipline ECAD P8
parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_discipline_.content:
# Creating the library for the discipline ECAD P8

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the library for the discipline ECAD P8 Previously, the discipline components were inserted in the mechatronic modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem below the ECAD unit. The discipline components is now to be collected in their own library. Create a library with the name T_ECAD_P8, which imports the system library ECAD and the self-created libraries already created T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Interfaces. See [Creating libraries](eecbase_k_libraries.htm). [Moving the discipline components into the library T_ECAD_P8](javascript:void\(0\);) Making the T_ECAD_P8 library visible for T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Click the symbol [Add library].
    4. In the Library finder dialog click [Search].
    5. Select the T_ECAD_P8 library.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. Save the library.
    8. The visibility is established.
Moving the discipline components
    1. Mark the ECAD unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Drag the unit into the T_ECAD_P8 library.
Undoing the visibility
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Select the T_ECAD_P8 library.
    4. Press the key [Del].
    5. Save the library.

parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_interface_l.title: Creating the library for interfaces
parent_tutp8_h_expert_create_interface_l.content:
# Creating the library for interfaces

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the library for interfaces The interfaces can be created directly in the mechatronic modular system. However, it is more practical to create these in a separate library and import them into all the libraries that require these interfaces. The names of interfaces can be selected freely. But it has become common to let the names begin with a capital âIâ. Create a library for the interfaces with the name T_Interfaces that imports the system library Engineering. Note If another discipline of the tutorial has already been edited, the T_Interfaces already exists. In this case the existing interfaces only have to be added. See [Creating libraries](eecbase_k_libraries.htm). How to create the interfaces:
    1. Call up the popup menu of the T_Interfaces library and select New > Engineering > Interface.
    2. Add ISchematic in the Name field.
    3. Save the interface.
    4. Repeat Steps 1 to 3 with the following names:
       * ISchematicPage
       * ISensor
       * IMechSensor
       * ISensorPressure
       * ISensorInductive
       * ISensorOptical
All interfaces that are required for the extension points are now defined for the ECAD P8 discipline. The IMechSensor interface is later implemented at all mechatronic sensors. This way you can easily calculate how many schematic pages have to be installed.

parent_tutp8_h_expert_delete_project.title: Deleting the project
parent_tutp8_h_expert_delete_project.content:
# Deleting the project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Deleting the project When modifying the mechatronic modular system, dependencies to the instances in the project can become invalid and lead to severe model errors. Before changes are performed to a modular system, the project should be removed. [Removing the Feeder project](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the Feeder project in the project catalog.
    2. Click [Del].
    3. Click [Finish].
    4. The project catalog is empty.

parent_tutp8_h_expert_encapsulate_discip.title: Encapsulating the discipline components in mechatronic components
parent_tutp8_h_expert_encapsulate_discip.content:
# Encapsulating the discipline components in mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Encapsulating the discipline components in mechatronic components In the course of the tutorial discipline components were created once through a mass import. Then formulas (for example to calculate a plug) were entered for the parameter values. If a macro was changed, for example through another parameter and the discipline component synchronized with the changed macro, the parameter values were lost. For this reason each discipline component is enclosed (encapsulated) in a mechatronic component. In the mechatronic component the same parameters are created and the formulas inserted as values. The parameters of the discipline components then always reference the parameters (interface parameters) of the same name of the encapsulating mechatronic components. After a synchronization you then only have to insert the references on the interface parameter again. [Creating the unit structure for mechatronic components](javascript:void\(0\);) The mechatronic components are to be created in a unit structure, analog to ECAD.
    1. Select the T_ECAD_P8 library.
    2. Select New > Engineering > Unit in the popup menu.
    3. Enter the Mechatronic name.
    4. Save the unit.
    5. Select the Mechatronic unit.
    6. Repeat Steps 2 to 4 with the names Page, Schematic, PLC_Inputs.
[Creating the mechatronic components](javascript:void\(0\);) For each mechatronic component an encapsulating mechatronic component now has to be created. Creating the capsule for the Schematic discipline component
    1. Select the new Mechatronic > Schematic unit.
    2. From the popup menu select New > Mechatronic > Function.
    3. Specify the M_Schematic name.
    4. Switch to the Interfaces editor page.
    5. Click  to open the Finder dialog.
    6. Click [Search].
    7. Select ISchematic.
    8. Confirm with [OK].
    9. Drag the Schematic discipline component into the component editor.
    10. Select the installed discipline component Schematic.
    11. Mark all parameters.
    12. Select Add interface temperature from the popup menu.
    13. Save the component.
    14. A reference to the parameter of the superior component (capsule) by the same name is now entered as the value. These parameters were inserted into the capsule during the same step. The parameter values are entered at a later step.
Creating the capsule for the Page discipline component The Page discipline component inherits the Page parameter from the abstractPage. A constellation could result due to the capsuling through which the system would not be able to differentiate between the component and the Page parameter anymore. A error would be reported and the capsule as a result could not be saved. In addition, the type of the parameter has to be switched from String to Integer. Therefore the Page parameter first has to be renamed.
    1. In the ECAD.Parameter unit, open the Page parameter.
    2. Change the name to PageNo.
