    OB1_RESERVED_2 : BYTE ; //Reserved for system

    OB1_PREV_CYCLE : INT ; //Cycle time of previous OB1 scan (milliseconds)

    OB1_MIN_CYCLE : INT ; //Minimum cycle time of OB1 (milliseconds)

    OB1_MAX_CYCLE : INT ; //Maximum cycle time of OB1 (milliseconds)

    OB1_DATE_TIME : DATE_AND_TIME ; //Date and time OB1 started

    END_VAR

    

    BEGIN

    

    NETWORK

    TITLE = Organisation

    

    CALL "FC_ASI_CopyInputs" ;

    CALL "FC_Org_Functions" ;

    

    NETWORK

    TITLE =Call Feeder

    

    (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Call}*)(*{END_LOOP}*)

    

    END_ORGANIZATION_BLOCK

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_edit_program_mod.title: Modifying program blocks
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_edit_program_mod.content:
# Modifying program blocks

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Modifying program blocks The OB_Cycle.AWL and FB_Sequence.AWL blocks must be adapted for use in EEC.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_export.title: Exporting SIMATIC STEP 7 program blocks
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_export.content:
# Exporting SIMATIC STEP 7 program blocks

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Exporting SIMATIC STEP 7 program blocks The following blocks are required for the program: Name | Block | Function  
---|---|---  
OB_Cycle | OB_Cycle.AWL | Organization of function blocks  
FC_Org_Functions | FC1.AWL |   
FC_ASI_CopyInputs | FC2.AWL |   
FB_Sequence | FB_Sequence.AWL |   
FB_Insert | FB_Insert.AWL | Controls the separator and checks the contents of the magazine  
FB_Move | FB_Move.AWL | Controls the X/Y-axes and the gripper  
FB_Inspect | FB_Inspect.AWL | Controls the workpiece position check  
Symbol table | Symboltable.sdf |   
FB_Errors | FB_Errors.AWL | Function block for error messages  
FR_Errors_Dec | FR_Errors_Dec.AWL | Fragment for error messages  
FR_Errors_NW | FR_Errors_NW.AEL | Fragment for error messages  
FR_SymbolsGeneral | FR_SymbolsGeneral.SDF | Fragment for symbol table  
FR_SymbolTable | FR_SymbolTable.SDF | Fragment for symbol table  
These blocks can be found in the zip file Tutorial_Step7.ZIP in the folder EC_Installationfolder\install\Tutorial\Step7. The block files must be unzipped in the folder EC_Installationfolder\resources\Tutorial\Step7.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_generate_code.title: Creating a STEP 7 program for Feeder
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_generate_code.content:
# Creating a STEP 7 program for Feeder

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a STEP 7 program for Feeder The task is to create a STEP 7 program based on the functional, mechatronic configuration Feeder. **** As shown in the following figure, the plug-socket principle will be used to insert the FB_Insert, FB_Move, and FB_Inspect function blocks into the FB_Sequence function block. The parameter for the STEP 7 discipline always has the name Caller assigned to it. The parameter value is the same as the name of the block where the socket is located, i.e., FB_Sequence for purposes of this tutorial. Because the optional Inspect function group is installed in the mechatronic, it should be possible to create two different programs.
     * With FB_Inspect
     * Without FB_Inspect
Information: A symbol file is prepared for the first task. The structure of the components is stored internally in the STEP 7 discipline. The following levels exist:

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_import_library.title: Importing T_Step7_Ui library into T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_import_library.content:
# Importing T_Step7_Ui library into T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Importing T_Step7_Ui library into T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem To add the functions for the STEP 7 discipline to the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem model, you will need to import the T_Step7_Ui library.
    1. Double-click to open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select the Imported libraries editor.
    3. Click .
The library finder now appears:
    1. Click [Search].
    2. Select the T_Step7_Ui library.
    3. Confirm with [OK].
    1. The modifications can be adopted by clicking the disk icon at the top left (or using the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S]).
In the following text, it is assumed that you save your modifications at the latest before opening the next object (diskette icon top left or by using the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S]), and if not, when you see the prompt click [Yes].

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_dc1.title: Adding discipline components to mechatronic components
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_dc1.content:
# Adding discipline components to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding discipline components to mechatronic components In the next step, you need to link the existing discipline-specific components to the functional components of the mechatronic model. This allows the STEP 7 structure to be automatically created from the mechatronic configuration. Use Drag & Drop to add the STEP 7 components SiemensCPU, OB_Cycle, FB_Sequence, and SymbolTable of the Feeder station as components.
    1. In T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem, open the Feeder station.
    1. Select the STEP 7 components SiemensCPU, OB_Cycle, FB_Sequence and SymbolTable.
    2. Drag & drop the STEP 7 components as a group in the Components field.
During instantiation, the inserted components are not arranged in a hierarchical level but according to the level configuration specified in the STEP 7 discipline library (see [Creating a STEP 7 program for Feeder](tutstep7_h_task1_generate_code.htm)).

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_dc2.title: Adding discipline components to mechatronic components
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_dc2.content:
# Adding discipline components to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding discipline components to mechatronic components The FB_Insert, FB_Inspect, and FB_Move function blocks still have to be inserted into the corresponding mechatronic components.
    1. Open the Insert function group in T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
    2. Insert the FB_Sequence function block.
    3. Open the Inspect function group.
    4. Insert the FB_Inspect function block.
    5. Open the Move function group.
    6. Insert the FB_Move function block.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_functions.title: Inserting functions as called POUs in OB_Cycle
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_functions.content:
# Inserting functions as called POUs in OB_Cycle

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting functions as called POUs in OB_Cycle Note POUs, or program organizational units, are used to structure programs. POUs of the type FB (Function Block) and FC (Function) are used here. The functions FC_ASI_CopyInputs and FC_Org_Functions are to be called as POUs of OB_Cycle.
    1. Double-click to open the OB_Cycle block.
    2. Select the Called POUs editor.
    3. Open the finder for POUs by clicking .
    4. Click [Search].
    5. Select the functions FC_ASI_CopyInputs and FC_Org_Functions.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
The functions are subsequently entered in the Called POUs editor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_values.title: Enter values for the DBNumber parameter
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_insert_values.content:
# Enter values for the DBNumber parameter

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Enter values for the DBNumber parameter The task is to assign a value for the DBNumber parameter:
    1. Open the FB_Sequence function block.
    2. Select the Parameters editor.
    3. Enter 1 as the value for the DBNumber parameter.
    4. Repeat steps 1 and 3 for the FB_Insert, FB_Move and FB_Inspect function blocks. Enter the values from the following table for the DBNumber parameter.
Function block | DBNumber | Comment  
---|---|---  
FB_Sequence | 1 |   
FB_Insert | 9999 | Multi-instance  
FB_Move | 9999 | Multi-instance  
FB_Inspect | 9999 | Multi-instance

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_file.title: Preparatory steps on a file level
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_file.content:
# Preparatory steps on a file level

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps on a file level Create the following folders on a file level (using Windows Explorer):
    
        C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringCenter\<Build-ID>\resources\Tutorial

    C:\Users\Public\EPLAN\EngineeringCenter\<Build-ID>\resources\Tutorial\Step7

[Entering the path to the resources into the initialization file](javascript:void\(0\);) This section can be skipped when the initialization file has already been edited for another discipline of the tutorial. The initialization (ec.ini) file has to be edited in the following way:
    1. Open the initialization file in an editor (for example Notepad).
    2. Expand the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath= with <EEC installation folder>\resources (see [de.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath](admin_r_vmargs_global_resourcePath_new.htm)).  

    3. Save the initialization file.
    4. Close the editor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_impo.title: Importing EOX files for SIMATIC STEP 7
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_impo.content:
# Importing EOX files for SIMATIC STEP 7

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Importing EOX files for SIMATIC STEP 7 Requirement:  
     * The file <EEC installation folder>\install\Tutorial\Step7\Tutorial_Step7.zip is unzipped in the same folder.
     * EEC is started.
Based on the initial situation, various EOX files that contain the corresponding libraries must be imported.
    1. The model is empty, i.e., only the system libraries are available (also see [Filter](eecbase_k_filter.htm)).
Import the following EOX files:
    
        <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Mechatronic\Mechatronic.eox

    <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Step7\Step7_Ui.eox

    <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Step7\Step7_IOGenerator_Ui.eox

    1. The model contains T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
Import the following EOX files:
    
        <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Step7\Step7_Ui.eox

    <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Step7\Step7_IOGenerator_Ui.eox

Read more
     * [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm)

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_mode.title: Configuring model variables
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_mode.content:
# Configuring model variables

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Configuring model variables The Instance Name Parameter For Discipline Structure model variable is used to create the program code. The value of a model variable is passed at runtime by a parameter with the same name. The value of the model variable is specified as follows:
    1. Select Model > Model Variables to open the model variables.
    1. Navigate to the model variable Disciplines > Instance Name Parameter For Discipline Structures.
    2. Enter the value DisciplineName.
    3. Save ([Ctrl] \+ [S]) and close the model variables.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_pref.title: Setting settings for the STEP 7 discipline
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_preparation_pref.content:
# Setting settings for the STEP 7 discipline

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Setting settings for the STEP 7 discipline In order for the EC to access the blocks that were unzipped and modified in the previous step, it is necessary to first enter the path of these files under the settings:
    1. Select Window > Settings.
The Settings (Preferences) window now appears:
    1. Select Disciplines > PLC > STEP 7 in the tree structure.
    2. Enter the path of the blocks that you want to unzip in the Source folder field.
    
        \resources\Tutorial\Step7

    1. Confirm with [OK].
Note Because this path has been changed, the STEP 7 discipline no longer works in the demo model! To work with the demo model, press [Restore default values] to restore the previous path.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test1_instantiat.title: Test 1 - Instantiation of SIMATIC STEP 7 discipline components
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test1_instantiat.content:
# Test 1 - Instantiation of SIMATIC STEP 7 discipline components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 1 - Instantiation of SIMATIC STEP 7 discipline components As with the mechatronic components, the STEP 7 discipline components can also be instantiated. This is done by dragging and dropping them from the library catalog view into the project tree, where they will appear under the discipline root node Step7:

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test2_generate_s.title: Test 2 - Generating instead of manually creating the current STEP 7 structure
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test2_generate_s.content:
# Test 2 - Generating instead of manually creating the current STEP 7 structure

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 2 - Generating instead of manually creating the current STEP 7 structure The structure should not be created manually but created.
    1. Select Mechatronic in the Project Catalog in the Feeder project.
    1. Select STEP 7 > Generate structure in the popup menu.
The same structure is created as above during manual instantiation. The only difference is the FB_Insert, FB_Inspect, and FB_Move function blocks are not yet contained in the structure. This will be done in the next step.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test3_generate_s.title: Test 3 - Creating the structure using the inserted discipline components
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test3_generate_s.content:
# Test 3 - Creating the structure using the inserted discipline components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 3 - Creating the structure using the inserted discipline components The complete structure should now be created.
    1. Select Mechatronic in the Project Catalog in the Feeder project.
    2. Select STEP 7 > Generate structure in the popup menu.
The function blocks that were previously inserted can also now be found in the structure. The next test is designed to show that the FB_Inspect function block is not displayed in the structure if the Inspect function group is disabled via disabler. For this purpose, the Option_Inspect_available parameter is set to false in the project explorer for the Feeder.
    1. Open the project component Feeder in the Project Catalog.
    2. Set the Option_Inspect_available parameter to false.
    3. Generate the STEP 7 structure again.
The FB_Inspect function block can be inserted and removed using the Option_Inspect_available parameter. The change does not take effect until the structure has been created again.

parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test4_generate_c.title: Generating the STEP 7 Program
parent_tutstep7_h_task1_test4_generate_c.content:
# Generating the STEP 7 Program

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating the STEP 7 Program This test is designed to show how to generate the STEP 7 program on the basis of the generated structure. Before you start, reset the Option_Inspect_available parameter to true.
    1. Generate the STEP 7 structure again.
    2. Select the Step7 root component.
    3. Select STEP 7 > Generate code in the popup menu.
The created files are stored in the following folder:
    
        EC-Installationsordner\workspace\Feeder\Step7\SiemensCPU

    1. Use the navigator view to view the folder contents:
    2. Open the navigator view.
The symbol table and AWL files of all blocks have been created. The test with the Option_Inspect_available parameter set to false creates the following results: The file FB_Inspect.AWL is no longer available. Double-click on an AWL file to open this in an internal EC editor. In the figure, you can see the created calls that have been inserted for the FB_Insert and FB_Move function blocks.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_ioaddress.title: Task 2 - Allocating addresses for the inputs and outputs
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_ioaddress.content:
# Task 2 - Allocating addresses for the inputs and outputs

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Task 2 - Allocating addresses for the inputs and outputs From the functional, mechatronic configuration Feeder, the addresses of the inputs and outputs are to be allocated to the available actuators and sensors.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_parameter.title: Allocating the parameters of the S7_313C component
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_parameter.content:
# Allocating the parameters of the S7_313C component

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Allocating the parameters of the S7_313C component For creating the input and output addresses (IO_Generation), the S7_313C component must first allocate the following parameters:
    1. Open the S7_313C component.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. Drag the following parameters from the Parameter unit into the editor.
     * IO_BitRange
     * IO_InputBlockSize
     * IO_InputStartAddress
     * IO_InputStartAddressFormat
     * IO_OutgoingBus
     * IO_OutputBlockSize
     * IO_OutputStartAddress
     * IO_OutputStartAddressFormat
     * IO_Place
     * IO_Places
     * Bit0_IX to Bit23_IX
     * Bit0_QX to Bit15_QX

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_structure.title: Allocating structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus, Bit0_QX, and Bit0_IX to the actuators and sensors
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_assign_structure.content:
# Allocating structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus, Bit0_QX, and Bit0_IX to the actuators and sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Allocating structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus, Bit0_QX, and Bit0_IX to the actuators and sensors The parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus and Bit0_IX must be allocated to all sensors and IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus and Bit0_QX must be allocated to all actuators.
    1. Open the Cylinder actuator.
    2. Select the Parameters editor.
    3. Use drag&drop to insert the parameters and  IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus and Bit0_QX into the editor.
    4. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for the Valve actuator.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 3 with the parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, IO_IncomingBus and Bit0_QX for the sensors Positionsensor and Pressuresensor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_compare.title: Comparison with sample solution
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_compare.content:
# Comparison with sample solution

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Comparison with sample solution To compare the results achieved with the sample solution, the following EOX file is imported (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)):
    
        EC-Installationsordner\install\Tutorial\Step7_Scope2.eox.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_mc.title: Creating the mechatronic component for the controller
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_mc.content:
# Creating the mechatronic component for the controller

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the mechatronic component for the controller The SiemensCPU controller requires a mechatronic component that can be used to calculate parameter values during the generation of the addresses.
    1. Select the ControllerComponents unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > Mechatronic > Functionunit.
    3. In the Name field, enter the text S7_313C.
    4. Open the Interfaces editor.
    1. Click .
    2. Click [Search].
    3. Select the IController interface.
    4. Confirm with [OK].
Due to the interdependencies, the ICoupler and ITerminal interfaces are also inserted.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_parameter.title: Creating parameters for input and output addresses
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_parameter.content:
# Creating parameters for input and output addresses

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating parameters for input and output addresses Parameters of type IX and OX must be created for the input and output addresses.
    1. Select the Parameter unit.
    2. Select New > Parameter in the shortcut menu.
    3. In the Name field, enter the text Bit0_IX.
    4. In the Type field, enter the text IX.
    5. Save the parameter.
    6. Repeat steps 2 to 5 for the parameters with names Bit1_IX to Bit23_IX (all with type IX).
    7. Repeat steps 2 to 5 for the parameters with names Bit0_QX to Bit15_QX and enter the type QX for these parameters.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_parameter.title: Creating parameters for model variables
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_parameter.content:
# Creating parameters for model variables

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating parameters for model variables Parameters must now be created for model variables for the parameters names defined in the [Defining parameter names for model variables](tutstep7_h_task2_define_parametername.htm) section:
    1. Select the Parameter unit.
    2. Select New > Parameter in the shortcut menu.
    3. In the Name field, enter the text IO_BitRange. The preset type String is set.
    4. Save the parameter.
    5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for the following parameters (all with type String):
Parameter name  
---  
IO_IncomingBus  
IO_InputBlockSize  
IO_InputStartAddressFormat  
IO_InputStartAddress  
IO_OutgoingBus  
IO_OutputBlockSize  
IO_OutputStartAddressFormat  
IO_OutputStartAddress  
IO_Place  
IO_Places

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_unit_for_.title: Creating the ControllerComponents unit
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_create_unit_for_.content:
# Creating the ControllerComponents unit

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the ControllerComponents unit For the control, a mechatronic component is created that is to be stored in a separate unit.  
    1. Select the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select New > Unit in the shortcut menu.
    3. In the Name field, enter the text ControllerComponents.
    4. Save the unit.
The new ControllerComponents unit is arranged parallel to the Mechatronic and Step7 units:

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_define_parameter.title: Defining parameter names for model variables
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_define_parameter.content:
# Defining parameter names for model variables

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Defining parameter names for model variables Model variables are required when creating the structure and the addresses. The names of the model variables for certain tasks are defined in EEC and parameters with the same names are created in the model. The values of these parameters influence the generation process. The model variables are defined as follows:
    1. Select Model > Model Variables to open the model variables.
    2. Click on the I/O Generator area.
3\. Define the following model variables: Name | Value | Description  
---|---|---  
Bit Range ParameterName | IO_BitRange | The name of the parameter that specifies the bit range of the bus.  
Incoming Bus Line Parameter Name | IO_IncomingBus | The name of the parameter that specifies the incoming bus of a controller device. Only the basic controller object has no incoming bus.  
Input Block Size Parameter Name | IO_InputBlockSize | The name of the parameter that specifies the input block size of a control device.  
Input Start Address Format Parameter Name | IO_InputStartAddressFormat | The name of the parameter that specifies the input start address format of a control device.  
Input Start Address Parameter Name | IO_InputStartAddress |   
Outgoing Bus Line Parameter Name | IO_OutgoingBus | The name of the parameter that specifies the outgoing bus of a control device.  
Output Block Size Parameter Name | IO_OutputBlockSize | The name of the parameter that specifies the output block size of a control device.  
Output Start Address Format Parameter Name | IO_OutputStartAddressFormat | The name of the parameter that specifies the output start address format of a control device.  
Output Start Address Parameter Name | IO_OutputStartAddress |   
Place Parameter Name | IO_Place | The name of the parameter that specifies the location of a control device.  
Places Parameter Name | IO_Places | The name of the parameter that specifies the locations that are searched using the sensors/actuators connected to the control device.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_edit_levelconfig.title: Changing the level configuration for a station
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_edit_levelconfig.content:
# Changing the level configuration for a station

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Changing the level configuration for a station For the future configuration, the level configuration of the Station component must be changed, so that a Function unit can be inserted below the Station in addition to the Function group. The level configuration for the Station component is changed as follows:
    1. Open the Station architecture component.
    2. Open the Level configuration editor.
    3. Click .
The Finder dialog is opened:
    1. Press the [Enter] button to list all available components.
    2. Select the Function unit component in the Finder.
    3. Confirm with [OK].

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_import_t_iogener.title: Importing T_IOGenerator_Ui library into mechatronic modules
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_import_t_iogener.content:
# Importing T_IOGenerator_Ui library into mechatronic modules

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Importing T_IOGenerator_Ui library into mechatronic modules To add the functions for the IO_Generator to the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem model, you will need to import the T_IOGenerator_Ui library.  
    1. Double-click to open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select the Imported libraries editor.
    3. Click .
The library finder now appears:
    1. Click [Search].
    2. Select the T_IOGenerator_Ui library.
    3. Confirm with [OK].

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_formulas1.title: Entering formulas for the parameters of the S7_313C component
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_formulas1.content:
# Entering formulas for the parameters of the S7_313C component

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Entering formulas for the parameters of the S7_313C component The parameters of the S7_313C component require fixed values and formulas, with whose help the parameter values of the project components are determined.
    1. Open the S7_313C component.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. Enter the following formula for the Bit0_IX parameter:
    
        ='I' + $IO_InputStartAddress

    1. Repeat step 3 for the parameters Bit1_IX to Bit23_IX with the following formula (the bit address must be increased by 1 each time):
    
        ='I' + $IO_InputStartAddress.addressAdd(1, 0, 8)

    ...

    ='I' + $IO_InputStartAddress.addressAdd(23, 0, 8)

    1. Repeat step 3 for the parameter Bit0_QX with the following formula:
    
        ='O' + $IO_OutputStartAddress

    1. Repeat step 3 for the parameters Bit1_QX to Bit15_QX with the following formula (the bit address must be increased by 1 each time):
    
        ='O' + $IO_OutputStartAddress.addressAdd(1, 0, 8)

    ...

    ='O' + $IO_OutputStartAddress.addressAdd(15, 0, 8)

    1. Enter these fixed values for the following parameters:
Parameter name | Value  
---|---  
IO_BitRange | 8  
IO_InputBlockSize | 24  
IO_InputStartAddress | 0.0  
IO_InputStartAddressFormat | x.0  
IO_OutgoingBus | IO  
IO_OutputBlockSize | 16  
IO_OutputStartAddress | 0.0  
IO_OutputStartAddressFormat | x.0  
IO_Place | PLC  
IO_Places | PLC

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_formulas2.title: Entering formulas for the structure parameters of the installed actuators and sensors
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_formulas2.content:
# Entering formulas for the structure parameters of the installed actuators and sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Entering formulas for the structure parameters of the installed actuators and sensors The installed actuators and sensors obtain the values for the structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, and IO_IncomingBus from their parent mechatronic component, here the Function unit.
    1. Open the Axis Functionunit.
    2. Select the installed Cylinder component.
The editor for the parameters of the installed Cylinder component will be displayed.
    1. Select the parameters IO_Place, IO_Places and IO_IncomingBus.
    2. Select the Add Interface Parameter item from the popup menu.
The parameter values are then filled with the formulas =mc.$IO_IncomingBus, =mc.$IO_Place, and =mc.$IO_Places.
    1. Repeat Steps 1 to 4 for the installed sensors of this Function unit and for all other sensors and actuators installed in Function units.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_s7_313c.title: Installing the S7_313C ControllerComponent in Feeder Station
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_s7_313c.content:
# Installing the S7_313C ControllerComponent in Feeder Station

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing the S7_313C ControllerComponent in Feeder Station Previously the SiemensCPU controller was installed directly in the Feeder station (see [Adding discipline components to mechatronic components](tutstep7_h_task1_insert_dc1.htm)). Instead of the SiemensCPU controller, now the S7_313C mechatronic component is installed in the Feeder station. The SiemensCPU controller is first uninstalled. Uninstalling the SiemensCPU controller:  
    1. Open the Feeder station.
    2. Select the SiemensCPU controller in the component editor.
    3. Click on the Delete component symbol or press the [Del] button.
Installing the S7_313C component in the Feeder station
    1. Open the Feeder station.
    2. Drag the S7_313C component into the component editor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_siemenscp.title: Installing the SiemensCPU controller in the mechatronic component
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_siemenscp.content:
# Installing the SiemensCPU controller in the mechatronic component

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing the SiemensCPU controller in the mechatronic component The SiemensCPU discipline component is installed in the S7_313C mechatronic component:
    1. Open the S7_313C component.
    1. Drag the SiemensCPU discipline component from the library catalog to the component editor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_values.title: Entering values for structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, and IO_IncomingBus in Function groups
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_insert_values.content:
# Entering values for structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, and IO_IncomingBus in Function groups

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Entering values for structure parameters IO_Place, IO_Places, and IO_IncomingBus in Function groups Previously, formulas were entered for the structure parameters for the installed actuators and sensors. By means of these formulas, these actuators and sensors obtain their values from the parent Function unit. The Function units obtain these values, in turn, from their corresponding parent Function group. First, the formulas are entered into all installed Function units:
    1. Open the Insert Function group.
    2. Select the installed Separator Function unit.
    3. Select the parameters IO_IncomingBus, IO_Place, and IO_Places.
    4. Select the Add Interface Parameter item from the popup menu.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the installed Stack Function unit.
    6. Open the Parameter editor of the Insert Function group.
    7. For the IO_IncomingBus parameter, enter the value IO.
    8. For the IO_Place and IO_Places parameters, enter the value PLC.
    9. Repeat steps 1 to 8 for the Inspect Function group with the installed Orientationinspector Function unit and for the Move Function group with the installed X_Axis, Y_Axis, and Gripper Function units.

parent_tutstep7_h_task2_test5_generate.title: Test 5 - Creating input and output addresses
parent_tutstep7_h_task2_test5_generate.content:
# Test 5 - Creating input and output addresses

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 5 - Creating input and output addresses This test is intended to check whether all of the addresses can be allocated for the inputs and outputs with reference to the parameter values and formulas that were entered in the previous sections. Importing the T_IOGenerator_UI library adds commands for creating (GenerateFieldbusCommand), resetting (ResetFieldbusCommand), and updating (UpdateFieldbusCommand) to the mechatronic module. The commands are called up by means of the popup menu for the mechatronic root object of the project, here Mechatronic.
    1. Select the project component Mechatronic.
    2. Select the item IO_Generator > GenerateFieldbusCommand from the popup menu.
The correct allocation of the addresses can be checked by opening all of the project components of type Actuator and Sensor. The following figure shows the address allocated to the project component Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Separator.Cylinder.Valve_1: The project component Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Separator.Cylinder.Valve_1 is after the Cylinder the second actuator in the project structure and is thus given the address O0.1.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_compare.title: Comparison with sample solution
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_compare.content:
# Comparison with sample solution

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Comparison with sample solution To compare the results achieved with the sample solution, the following EOX file is imported (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)):
    
        EC-Installationsordner\install\Tutorial\Step7_Scope3.eox.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_create_fr_symbol.title: Creating the resource file FR_SymbolTable
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_create_fr_symbol.content:
# Creating the resource file FR_SymbolTable

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the resource file FR_SymbolTable Two resource files are needed that can be created with any editor.
    1. Create the file FR_SymbolTable.sdf in the folder ..\installation folder\resources\Step7\.
    2. Write the following content into the file:
    
        FRAGMENT "FR_SymbolTable"

    VAR

    END_VAR

    BEGIN

    "M8B_Symbol_M8E","M8B_Address_M8E","M8B_DataType_M8E","M8B_Comment_M8E"

    END_FRAGMENT

    1. Save and close the file.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_create_fr_symbol.title: Creating new fragments FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_create_fr_symbol.content:
# Creating new fragments FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating new fragments FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral The FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral fragments will provide the required contents at a later time when the symbol table is created. First the fragments are created and then installed and parameterized in the actuators and sensors in additional steps. Create the fragment FR_SymbolTable in unit Step7:
    1. Select the Step7 unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > STEP 7 > Source > Fragment.
    3. In the Resource location panel, click Import.
    4. Select the file FR_SymbolTable.sdf in the following dialog.
    5. Confirm with [Open].
Create the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral in unit Step7:
    1. Select the Step7 unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > STEP 7 > Source > Fragment.
    3. In the Resource location panel, click Import.
    4. Select the file FR_SymbolsGeneral.sdf in the following dialog.
    5. Confirm with [Open].

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_edit_symboltable.title: Changing the symbol table resource file
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_edit_symboltable.content:
# Changing the symbol table resource file

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Changing the symbol table resource file The symbol table resource file will be filled with generic content. For this purpose, a directive must be inserted into the resource file instead of the previous symbol information.
    1. Select the SymbolTable symbol table.
    2. Select Edit POU > Fragment from the popup menu.
    3. Write the following content into the file:
    
        (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Call}*)(*{END_LOOP}*)

With the help of this instruction, the parser of the PLC discipline is iterated over all Call callups and the contents of the callups are added into the symbol table.
    1. Save the changes.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_generate_symbolt.title: Task 3 - Creating the symbol table
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_generate_symbolt.content:
# Task 3 - Creating the symbol table

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Task 3 - Creating the symbol table The task is to create the symbol table based on the functional, mechatronic configuration Feeder. In the previous sections, an enclosed symbol table was used, but now it will be shown how this table can be created from the mechatronic configuration.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_caller.title: Inserting and configuring the Caller parameter in FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_caller.content:
# Inserting and configuring the Caller parameter in FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting and configuring the Caller parameter in FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral The fragments FR_SymbolTable and FR_SymbolsGeneral also require the parameter Caller to be integrated in the discipline structure by means of the Plug-Socket principle. Inserting the Caller parameter:
    1. Open the fragment FR_SymbolTable.
    2. Select the Parameters editor.
    3. Drag the Caller parameter from the Parameter unit into the editor.
    4. For the Caller parameter, enter the value SymbolTable.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral.

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_formulas.title: Inserting formulas into the parameters of installed components
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_formulas.content:
# Inserting formulas into the parameters of installed components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting formulas into the parameters of installed components Formulas must also be inserted for the parameters of the installed components:
    1. Open the Axis Functionunit.
    2. Select the installed Cylinder component.
    3. Enter the following formula for the Symbol parameter:
    
        =mc.$Symbol_Cylinder

    1. Repeat steps 2 and 3 with the following data:
Installed component | Parameter | Value  
---|---|---  
Position_1 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_1  
Position_2 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_2  
Position_3 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_3  
Position_4 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_4  
    1. Repeat steps 1 and 3 for the following Function units:
Functionunit | Installed component | Parameter | Value  
---|---|---|---  
Gripper | Valve | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Valve  
Pressuresensor | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Pressuresensor  
Orientationinspector | Positionssensor_optical | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_1  
Separator | Cylinder | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Cylinder  
Position_1 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_1  
Position_2 | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_2  
Stack | Positionssensor_optical | Symbol | =mc.$Symbol_Position_1

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.title: Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral in the Feeder Station
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.content:
# Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral in the Feeder Station

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral in the Feeder Station Analogous to the "Installing the configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in actuators and sensors" section, the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral is now installed into the Feeder Station and the parameters are provided with values.  
    1. Open the Feeder station.
    2. Drag the fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral from the Step7 unit into the component editor.
    3. Select the installed fragment FR_SymbolsGeneral.
    4. Enter the following formulas and values for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Bit0 | =mc.$Bit0_IX  
Bit1 | =mc.$Bit1_IX  
Bit2 | =mc.$Bit2_IX  
Bit3 | =mc.$Bit3_IX  
Bit4 | =mc.$Bit4_IX  
Bit5 | =mc.$Bit5_IX  
Bit6 | =mc.$Bit6_IX  
Bit7 | =mc.$Bit7_IX  
Bit8 | =mc.$Bit8_IX

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.title: Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in actuators and sensors
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.content:
# Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in actuators and sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in actuators and sensors The fragment FR_SymbolTable is installed in all actuators and sensors and the parameters contained therein are provided with values.  
    1. Open the component Valve in the Actuators unit.
    2. Drag the fragment FR_SymbolTable from the Step7 unit into the component editor.
    3. Select the installed fragment FR_SymbolTable.
    4. Enter the following formulas and values for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Symbol | =mc.$Symbol  
Address | =mc.$Bit0_QX  
DataType | BOOL  
Comment | =mc.mc.mc.name+'_'+mc.mc.name+'_'+mc.name  
    1. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the components Position sensor_inductive, Position sensor_optical, and Pressure sensor and enter the following formulas and values for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Symbol | =mc.$Symbol  
Address | =mc.$Bit0_IX  
DataType | BOOL  
Comment | =mc.mc.mc.name+'_'+mc.mc.name+'_'+mc.name

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.title: Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in the Stack Function unit
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_fr_symbol.content:
# Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in the Stack Function unit

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing and configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in the Stack Function unit Analogous to the Installing the configuring the fragment FR_SymbolTable in actuators and sensors section, the fragment is now installed into the Stack Function unit and the parameters are provided with values.  
    1. Open the Stack function unit.
    2. Drag the fragment FR_SymbolTable from the Step7 unit into the component editor.
    3. Select the installed fragment FR_SymbolTable.
    4. Enter the following formulas and values for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Symbol | F_Pulse_PartInStack  
Address | M13.1  
DataType | BOOL  
Comment | Pulse Part in Stack

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_parameter.title: Inserting and configuring the Symbol parameter in actuators and sensors
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_parameter.content:
# Inserting and configuring the Symbol parameter in actuators and sensors

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting and configuring the Symbol parameter in actuators and sensors The parameter Symbol, whose value is determined by formula from the installed components of the previous step, must still be inserted into the components Valve, Cylinder, and Position sensor and provided with formulas. Installing the Symbol parameter in actuators and sensors:  
    1. Open the component Valve in the Actuators unit.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. Drag the parameter Symbol from the Parameter unit into the editor.
    4. Enter the following formula for the parameters:
    
        =mc.$Symbol+'_'+name

    1. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the components Cylinder, Positionsensor and Pressuresensor with the parameter Symbol and the following formula:
    
        =mc.$Symbol

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_symbolnam.title: Entering symbol names into the parameters of installed Function units
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_insert_symbolnam.content:
# Entering symbol names into the parameters of installed Function units

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Entering symbol names into the parameters of installed Function units The Function units installed in Function groups contain parameters for allocating symbols that must be entered in the following step.
    1. Open the Insert Function group.
    2. Select the installed Separator component.
    3. Enter the following values for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Symbol_Cylinder | O_Separator  
Symbol_Position_1 | I_Separator_Pos_1  
Symbol_Position_2 | I_Separator_Pos_2  
    1. Repeat steps 2 and 3 for the installed Stack component.
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Symbol_Position_1 | I_Stack_Part_In_Pos  
    1. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for the following installed Function groups:
Functiongroup | Installed Function unit | Parameter | Value  
---|---|---|---  
Inspect | Orientationinspector | Symbol_Position_1 | I_Inspect_Orientation  
Move | X_Axis | Symbol_Cylinder | O_XAxis  
Symbol_Position_1 | I_XAxis_Position_1  
Symbol_Position_2 | I_XAxis_Position_2  
Symbol_Position_3 | I_XAxis_Position_3  
Symbol_Position_4 | I_XAxis_Position_4  
Y_Axis | Symbol_Cylinder | O_YAxis  
Symbol_Position_1 | I_YAxis_Position_1  
Symbol_Position_2 | I_YAxis_Position_2  
Symbol_Position_3 | I_YAxis_Position_3  
Symbol_Position_4 | I_YAxis_Position_4  
Gripper | Symbol_Valve | O_Gripper  
Symbol_Pressuresensor | I_Gripper_Pressure

parent_tutstep7_h_task3_test6_generate.title: Test 6 - Generating the symbol table and input and output addresses
parent_tutstep7_h_task3_test6_generate.content:
# Test 6 - Generating the symbol table and input and output addresses

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 6 - Generating the symbol table and input and output addresses This test is intended to check whether all of the addresses can be allocated for the inputs and outputs and whether the symbol table can be created with reference to the parameter values and formulas that were entered in the previous sections. Creating:  
    1. Select the root object Mechatronic in the Feeder project.
    2. Select IO-Generator > GenerateFieldbusCommand from the popup menu.
    3. Select STEP 7 > Generate Structure from the popup menu.
Then check whether the addresses for the inputs and outputs have been correctly assigned (see [Test 5 - Creating input and output addresses](tutstep7_h_task2_test5_generate.htm)). The result of the discipline structure should look as follows: Then check the created symbol table:
    1. Open the SymbolTable symbol table in the project.
    2. Open the File editor.

parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_fb_errors.title: Creating the new function block FB_Errors and fragments FR_Errors_Dec and FR_Errors_NW
parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_fb_errors.content:
# Creating the new function block FB_Errors and fragments FR_Errors_Dec and FR_Errors_NW

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating the new function block FB_Errors and fragments FR_Errors_Dec and FR_Errors_NW First the fragments and the function block are created and configured in subsequent steps. Create the function block FB_Errors in unit Step7:
    1. Select the Step7 unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > STEP 7 > Source > Function block.
    3. In the Resource location panel, click [Import].
    4. Select the file FB_Errors.AWL in the following dialog.
    5. Confirm with [Open].
Create the fragment FR_Errors_Dec in unit Step7:
    1. Select the Step7 unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > STEP 7 > Source > Fragment.
    3. In the Resource location panel, click [Import].
    4. Select the file FR_Errors_Dec.AWL in the following dialog.
    5. Confirm with [Open].
Create the fragment FR_Errors_NW in unit Step7:
    1. Select the Step7 unit.
    2. From the popup menu, select New > STEP 7 > Source > Fragment.
    3. In the Resource location panel, click [Import].
    4. Select the file FR_Errors_NW.AWL in the following dialog.
    5. Confirm with [Open].

parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_parameter.title: Creating Actuator and Sensor parameters
parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_parameter.content:
# Creating Actuator and Sensor parameters

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating Actuator and Sensor parameters Lists of actuators and sensors that are accessed in the formulas of the previous steps are created with the new Actuator and Sensor parameters to be created:
    1. Select the Step7.Parameter unit.
    2. Select New > Parameter in the shortcut menu.
    3. In the Name field, enter the text Actuator.
    4. In the Type field, enter the text List.
    5. Repeat steps 2 to 4 for the Sensor actuator.

parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_resource_.title: Creating resource files
parent_tutstep7_h_task4_create_resource_.content:
# Creating resource files

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating resource files Three resource files are needed that can be created with any editor.
    1. Create the file FB_Errors.AWL in the folder ..\installation folder\resources\Step7\.
    2. Write the following content into the file:
    
        FUNCTION_BLOCK "FB_Errors"

    TITLE =

    VERSION : 0.1

    VAR

    (*{LOOP:dos('FR_Errors_Dec')}*)(*{Call}*)(*{END_LOOP}*)

    END_VAR

    BEGIN

    (*{LOOP:dos('FR_Errors_NW')}*)(*{Call}*)(*{END_LOOP}*)

    END_FUNCTION_BLOCK

    1. Save and close the file.
    2. Create the file FB_Errors_Dec.AWL in the folder ..\installation folder\resources\Step7\.
    3. Write the following content into the file:
    
        FRAGMENT "FR_Errors_Dec"

    VAR

    END_VAR

    BEGIN

    Error_M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E : BOOL;

    TON_Error_M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E : "TON";

    END_FRAGMENT

    1. Save and close the file.
    2. Create the file FB_Errors_NW.AWL in the folder ..\installation folder\resources\Step7\.
    3. Write the following content into the file:
    
        FRAGMENT "FR_Errors_NW"

    VAR

    END_VAR

    BEGIN

    NETWORK

    TITLE -Error M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E Part 1/2

    (*{IF $Output_2}*)

    (*{1 Output, 2 Input}*)

    U( ,

    U M8B_Output_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    O ,

    UN M8B_Output_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    O ,

    U M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    U M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    O ,

    UN M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    ) ,

    (*{ELSE}*)

    (*{IF mc.$Sensor.size -1}*)

    (*{2 Output, 1 Input}*)

    U( ,

    U M8B_Output_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    O ,

    U M8B_Output_2_M8E;

    U M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    ) ,

    (*{ELSE}*)

    (*{2 Output, 2 Input}*)

    U( ,

    U M8B_Output_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    O ,

    U M8B_Output_2_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    O ,

    U M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    U M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    O ,

    UN M8B_Input_1_M8E;

    UN M8B_Input_2_M8E;

    ) ,

    (*{END_IF}*)

    (*{END_IF}*)

    - L 0.0,

    BLD 103,

    CALL #TON_Error_M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E (

    IN :- L 0.0,

    PT :- T#500MS),

    NOP 0,

    NETWORK

    TITLE -Error M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E Part 2/2

    U #TON_Error_M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E.0,

    - #Error_M8B_ErrorNumber_M8E,

    END_FRAGMENT

    1. Save and close the file.

parent_tutstep7_h_task4_insert_parameter.title: Inserting and configuring Actuator and Sensor parameters into the Axis and Separator Function units
parent_tutstep7_h_task4_insert_parameter.content:
# Inserting and configuring Actuator and Sensor parameters into the Axis and Separator Function units

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting and configuring Actuator and Sensor parameters into the Axis and Separator Function units The Actuator and Sensor parameters are now installed in the Axis and Separator Function units, in order to determine lists of symbols of all sensors and actuators therein. Installing the Actuator and Sensor parameters in Axis providing them with parameter values:  
    1. Open the Axis Functionunit.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. Drag the parameters Actuator and Sensor from the Step7.Parameter unit into the editor.
    4. Enter the following formulas for the parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Actuator | =rmos('Valve').$Symbol  
Sensor | =rmos('Sensor').$Symbol  
    1. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the Separator Function unit.

parent_tutstep7_h_task4_insert_parameter.title: Inserting and configuring the parameter in the new function block and fragments
parent_tutstep7_h_task4_insert_parameter.content:
# Inserting and configuring the parameter in the new function block and fragments

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Inserting and configuring the parameter in the new function block and fragments The fragments FR_Error_Dec and FR_Errors_NW, as well as the function block FB_Errors, also require the parameter Caller to be integrated in the discipline structure by means of the Plug-Socket principle. The function block FB_Errors also requires the parameter DiscipineName. These parameters are immediately provided with values. Inserting the parameters:
    1. Open the fragment FR_Errors_Dec.
    2. Open the Parameter editor.
    3. Drag the Caller parameter from the Parameter unit into the editor.
    4. For the Caller parameter, enter the value FB_Errors.
    5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for the parameter ErrorNumber with the value =dc.dos('FR_Errors_Dec').indexOf(this)+1
    6. Repeat steps 1 to 5 for the fragment FR_Errors_NW with the following parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Caller | FB_Errors  
ErrorNumber | =dc.dos('FR_Errors_NW').indexOf(this)+1  
Output_1 | =mc.$Actuator.first  
Output_2 | =if mc.relRef('Cylinder').$Option_Two_Valves then mc.$Actuator.at(1) else false endif  
Input_1 | =mc.$Sensor.first  
Input_2 | =if mc.$Sensor.size>1 then mc.$Sensor.at(1) else false endif  
    1. Repeat steps 1 to 5 for the function block FB_Errors with the following parameters:
Parameter | Value  
---|---  
Caller | FB_Errors  
DisciplineName | =name+'DB'  
Number | 2

parent_tuttext_h_create_text_discipline_.title: Splitting the target file into text resources
parent_tuttext_h_create_text_discipline_.content:
# Splitting the target file into text resources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Splitting the target file into text resources Before you can define text discipline components, it is necessary to create the text fragments required for this purpose. The predefined target file has to be split up into text fragments in a way that allows the target file to be recreated from them:
    1. Open ListOfComponents.txt located in the install\Tutorial\ListOfComponents sub-folder using a text editor.
    2. Replace all lines except the first two (heading and underlining) with the line (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Content}*)(*{END_LOOP}*). The line contains three commands in the form of (*{â¦}*). The first and last commands act as a loop construct that iterates across all directly contained discipline components (dos = "discipline objects"). The middle command is responsible for their content.
    3. Save the open text file as Body.txt in the folder Installation folder\resources\Tutorial\ListOfComponents.
    4. Repeat the first step.
    5. Delete the first two lines and replace Actuators with the parameter #{Heading}.
    6. Replace the list and everything else with the same line used in step 2: (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Content}*)(*{END_LOOP}*).
    7. Save the open text file as Chapter.txt in the folder Installation folder\resources\Tutorial\ListOfComponents.
    8. Repeat the first step.
    9. Delete everything except for one list line with line break. Replace the number with the #{Pos} parameter and the descriptor with the #{Descriptor}.
    10. Save the open text file Line.txt in the folder Installation folder\resources\Tutorial\ ab ListOfComponents.

You should now see the following in the image on the lower right: If you activate the Mechatronic Component check box in settings under General > Representation > Label Decorations, the discipline components will also be shown under Mechatronic. The absolute name is shown in square brackets in the discipline panel.
