parent_tuttext_h_extend_mechatronic_with.title: Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components
parent_tuttext_h_extend_mechatronic_with.content:
# Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components In the first step, the text components Body and Chapter have to be linked with mechatronic components:
    1. Double-click to open the component Feeder in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library in the Stations unit and move the Body text component from Library Catalog view to the Components editor section.
    2. Move Chapter twice from the Library Catalog view to Components editor panel.
    3. Rename Chapter as ActuatorsChapter (switch to editor mode by clicking again on the selected component or by pressing [F2]).
    4. Rename Chapter2 as SensorsChapter.
    1. Again click ActuatorsChapter to mark it. The accompanying parameter table is now displayed.
    2. Enter the value Actuators in the table in the parameter line Heading.
    3. Repeat the final two steps for SensorsChapter and the Sensors value.

parent_tuttext_h_extend_mechatronic_with.title: Adding lines to mechatronic components
parent_tuttext_h_extend_mechatronic_with.content:
# Adding lines to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding lines to mechatronic components Add the Text component Line to all non-abstract actuators and sensors that are located in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library in the Mechatronic unit and the Actuators and Sensors subunits:
     * Valve
     * Cylinder
     * Pressuresensor
     * Positionsensor_inductive
     * Positionsensor_optical
The parameter values for Pos and Descriptor of the component Line are to be calculated with these formulas: Parameter | Formula  
---|---  
Descriptor | 
    
        =mroot.name + mc.absoluteName.substring(mroot.absoluteName.size, mc.absoluteName.size - 1)  
  
Pos. | 
    
        =dc.dos.indexOf(this) + 1  
  
For Descriptor, the absolute name (e.g., Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Stack.Positionsensor_optical) is to be used to determine the sub string e.g., Feeder.Insert.Stack.Positionsensor_optical. To do so, the result of mroot.name (e.g., Feeder) is added to the result of the mc.absoluteName.substring method. The length of the absolute name (mroot.absoluteName.size = e.g. Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder = 41) and the length of the name starting at the parent component (mc.absoluteName.size-1 = e.g. Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Stack.PositionÂ­sensor_optical = 84) is transferred to the substring method. The position of the discipline component for Pos is determined from the list of discipline components. dc.dos is used to identify a list of all children of the parent discipline components and indexOf(this) to determine its own position. Since the counter in lists begins at 0 (zero), a 1 is added.

parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Mechatronic model: Generating non-functional structures
parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Mechatronic model: Generating non-functional structures

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Mechatronic model: Generating non-functional structures Hierarchical discipline structures, which are different not in that elements are omitted from the mechatronic structure, are defined using Plug parameters and Socket parameters. If a discipline component does not have a plug parameter, it is subordinated in the mechatronic structure of the next discipline components to the same or parent level that permits this through the definition of sublevels. If a discipline component does have a plug parameter, it is placed below the discipline components that have a socket parameter with the same value as its plug parameter:

parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Creating plug and socket parameters
parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Creating plug and socket parameters

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating plug and socket parameters Before the plugs and sockets can be assigned to the discipline components, these must first be created as parameters:
    1. Select the Parameter unit in T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.
    2. Select New > Engineering > Parameter in the popup menu.
    3. Enter Plug in the Name field.
    4. EnterPlug as the type: The type can either be found by using the Finder  or be entered directly.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the parameter with Socket as the name and type.

parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Adding plugs and sockets to Text discipline components
parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Adding plugs and sockets to Text discipline components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding plugs and sockets to Text discipline components Using the corresponding sockets for chapters and plugs for lines, it is now possible to assign the lines to the correct chapters:
    1. Open the text component Chapter in the ListOfComponents unit.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Click the Add parameter symbol and select the Socket parameter in the Finder. Alternatively, you can also use Drag & Drop to add the Socket parameter.
    4. Repeat Steps 1 to 3 with the text component Line and the parameter Plug.
    1. Double-click to open the component Feeder in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library in the Stations unit.
    2. Click to mark the ActuatorsChapter component. The corresponding parameter table now opens.
    3. Enter Actuators as the value for the Socket parameter.
    4. Repeat the previous two steps to add the Sensors value to the SensorsChapter.
    1. Repeat steps 5 to 7 with Valve and Cylinder for the installed component Line with the plug parameter value Actuators.
    1. Repeat steps 5 to 7 with Pressuresensor, Positionsensor_optical and Positionsensor_inductive for the installed component Line with the plug parameter value Sensors.

parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Generation of finished ListOfComponents
parent_tuttext_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Generation of finished ListOfComponents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generation of finished ListOfComponents To create and view the ListOfComponents, open the Mechatronic popup menu in the Project catalog and select ListOfComponents > Generate structure and open: The actuators and sensors are all now contained in the correct chapter. By using formulas instead of predefined plug and socket values, it is possible to create any required discipline-specific structures.

parent_tuttext_h_preparation_steps_eec.title: Preparatory steps in EEC
parent_tuttext_h_preparation_steps_eec.content:
# Preparatory steps in EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps in EEC [Importing EOX files for the Text discipline](javascript:void\(0\);) Before you can edit the Text discipline, the T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem libraries created in the chapter âMechatronicâ must be available. If the T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem libraries are not available, please import the following EOX file:
    
        <EEC installation folder>\install\Tutorial\Mechatronic\Mechatronic.eox.

Read more
     * [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm)

parent_tuttext_h_preparation_steps_files.title: Preparatory steps on a file level
parent_tuttext_h_preparation_steps_files.content:
# Preparatory steps on a file level

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps on a file level Create the following folders on a file level (using Windows Explorer):
    
        <EEC_installation folder>\resources\Tutorial

    <EEC_installation folder>\resources\Tutorial\ListOfComponents

[Entering the path to the resources into the initialization file](javascript:void\(0\);) The initialization (ec.ini) file has to be edited in the following way:
    1. Open the initialization file in an editor (for example Notepad).
    2. Expand the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath= with <EEC installation folder>\resources (see [de.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath](admin_r_vmargs_global_resourcePath_new.htm)).  

    3. Save the initialization file.
    4. Close the editor.

parent_tuttext_h_task_generate_loc.title: Task: Generate a ListOfComponents
parent_tuttext_h_task_generate_loc.content:
# Task: Generate a ListOfComponents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Task: Generate a ListOfComponents The task is to create a ListOfComponents with the following structure based on the functional, mechatronic of a plant. To achieve this goal, this chapter will begin by describing how to create a new ListOfComponents discipline on the basis of the Text discipline. It then goes on to show how to create a reusable text block and how to check the modular system components of the list of components using a test configuration. The connection of this new discipline to the mechatronic model will be examined in the next chapter.

parent_tuttext_h_text.title: Text discipline
parent_tuttext_h_text.content:
# Text discipline

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Text discipline After the discipline model has been created, the result can be compared with the sample solution (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)).

parent_tutword_h_create_formulas.title: Creating formulas for image objects in the parameters
parent_tutword_h_create_formulas.content:
# Creating formulas for image objects in the parameters

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating formulas for image objects in the parameters The text #{Image:Logo} is now shown instead of the logo on the cover sheet of the document. A formula that returns the image of the Eplan logo is now used for the Logo parameter.
    1. Open the Feeder station.
    2. Select the installed components ListOfComponents.
    3. Enter the =type('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Images.Logo').image formula for the Logo parameter in the Value column.
With type(âT_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Images.Logoâ), the formula identifies the image object Logo and returns the image using the Image method. In the Word component Row, the Icon parameter is contained in the first column of the table. Only the text #{Image:Icon} was previously displayed. A formula is added for the Icon parameter in the component Row. This identifies an image in the Actuators_Sensors_Imageregister image register that matches the type of the current component.
    1. Open the component Row.
    2. Enter the =type('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Images.Actuators_Sensors_Imageregister').image(mc.type) formula as the value for the Icon parameter:
    3. Save the component Row.
    4. All the formulas for the image objects are created as a result.

parent_tutword_h_create_image_objects.title: Adding ImageObjects to the mechatronic model
parent_tutword_h_create_image_objects.content:
# Adding ImageObjects to the mechatronic model

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding ImageObjects to the mechatronic model The Eplan logo and, in the tables of the actuators and sensors in the first column, the symbols of the components are to be displayed on the cover sheet below the title. Instead of permanently installing these figures they are to be referenced by means of a formula. The GIF file Logo.gif () that is directly referenced serves as an image object for the Eplan logo. The images Actuator.gif() and Sensor.gif () previously specified should be used as the image objects for actuators and sensors. The image objects are assigned to the relevant type of the component in the image register. A formula can access this image register and determine the matching image.

parent_tutword_h_create_image_objects1.title: Creating image objects and image registers
parent_tutword_h_create_image_objects1.content:
# Creating image objects and image registers

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating image objects and image registers The images for the tutorial have to be copied into the folder <Installation path>/resources/MediaSources.
    1. Copy the files Aktor.gif, Logo.gif and Sensor.gif from the folder <Installation path>/install/icon-collection into the folder <Installation path>/resources/MediaSources.
Image objects are created in a new unit.
    1. Create the Images unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select the Images unit.
    3. Select New > Media sources > Image in the shortcut menu of the unit.
    4. Enter Actuator in the Name field.
    5. Click Import.
    6. Select the resources/MediaSources\Aktor.gif image. The path for this image is saved in the Resource path field.
    7. Repeat Steps 2 to 6, assign the Sensor name to the new image object and select the image Sensor.gif.
    8. Repeat Steps 2 to 6, assign the Logo name to the new image object and select the image Logo.gif.
The image register is created subsequently:
    1. Select the Images unit.
    2. Select New > Media sources > Image register in the popup menu of the unit.
    3. Enter Actuators_Sensors_Imageregister in the Name field.
    4. Open the Image assignment editor page.
    5. Click .
    6. Select the component Actuators from T_Mechatronic_Architecture.Levelcomponents.
    7. Select the image object Actuator in the Library component image field and in the Project component image field.
    8. Repeat steps 5 to 7 and select Sensor from T_Mechatronic_Architecture.Levelcomponents as well as the image object Sensor.
    9. Save the image register.
The result should correspond to the following image:

parent_tutword_h_create_word_components_.title: Creating discipline components for Word
parent_tutword_h_create_word_components_.content:
# Creating discipline components for Word

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating discipline components for Word A three-level hierarchy consisting of Body, Chapter, and Row is created as a result of splitting the target file. This hierarchy is now defined in a new unit.
    1. Begin by creating the Word_Components unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select the Word_Components unit.
    3. From the popup menu select New > Word > Main Document.
    4. Click [Import] and select the Body.docx file in the file selection dialog that subsequently appears.
The file name is entered automatically in the Name field.
    1. Repeat steps 2 to 4, but select Fragment document in step 3. Select the Word files Chapter.docx for the Word components with the name Chapter and Row.docx for the Word component with the name Row.
When importing the Word files, the parameters contained in them are automatically detected and are created in the Word.Parameter unit:

parent_tutword_h_create_word_components_.title: Splitting the target file into Word resources
parent_tutword_h_create_word_components_.content:
# Splitting the target file into Word resources

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Splitting the target file into Word resources Before you can define Word components, it is necessary to create the documents required for this purpose. The predefined target file has to be split up into a main document and several fragment documents in a way that allows the target file to be recreated from them.
    1. Open the ListOfComponents.docx file located in the <EEC installation folder>\install\Tutorial\Word folder.
    2. Replace the heading List of Components with #(Heading) on the first page.
The number string #{ instructs the parser to treat the following text up to character } as the name of a parameter and to replace with the parameter value.
    1. Also replace the image with #(Image:Logo) on the first page.
The parameter type is specified here as Image in front of the name of a parameter. Without this information String would be used as the type and, as a result, no image returned. Do not make any changes to the table of contents.
    1. Delete everything below the second heading 1.1 Actuators on the third page and replace Actuators with (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Content}*)(*{END_LOOP}*).
The line contains three commands in the form of (*{â¦}*). The first and last commands act as a loop construct that iterates across all directly contained discipline components (dos = **d** iscipline **o** bject**s**). The middle command is responsible for their content.
    1. Save the document as Body.docx in the folder resources\Office\Word\Tutorial.
    2. Repeat the first step.
    3. Delete pages 1 and 2, the heading 1 Actuators and Sensors, the heading 1.2 Sensors the Sensors table.
    4. Replace the heading 1.1 Actuators with #{Heading}.
    5. Replace the word Actuators with #{Heading} in the table header.
    6. Replace the image for actuator () with (*{LOOP:dos}*) in the first row, the image for actuator () with (*{Content}*) in the second row, and the image for actuator () with (*{END_LOOP}*) in the third row in the Symbol column.
    7. Delete all table rows below this (positions 4 to 7).
    8. Delete the contents from the table cells below Position and Description.
    9. Add a second blank row below the table.
    10. Save the document as Chapter.docx.
    11. Repeat the first step.
    12. Delete pages 1 and 2, all headings, and the bottom table.
    13. Delete everything except for the final row in the remaining table.
    14. Replace the image for actuator () with the #{Image:Icon} parameter, the number of the position with the #{Pos} parameter, and the descriptor with the #{Descriptor} parameter in the remaining table row.
    15. Save the document as Row.docx.

parent_tutword_h_expert.title: Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points
parent_tutword_h_expert.content:
# Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Upgrading mechatronic model to extension points The upgrade of the model to extension points is targeted towards experienced users who are already familiar with the basic functions. The currently structured model serves as the starting point. If the model has not been worked through, the Word.eox sample file has to be imported and the EEC preferences and model variables have to be adapted. The concept of extension points is based on the idea that only such components are installed on an extension point that import an interface by the same name and that are visible for the project. Those discipline components are visible whose libraries have been imported into the project. The number of components to be inserted is specified separately for each extension point or is controlled by formulas. The insertion of components is triggered by updating the extension points. The model is upgraded to extension points by means of the following steps: [Deleting a project](tutword_h_expert_delete_project.htm) [Expanding the architecture](tutword_h_expert_extend_architecture.htm) [Creating a library for interfaces](tutword_h_expert_create_interface_lib.htm) [Creating a library for the image objects](tutword_h_expert_create_image_lib.htm) [Creating a library for the Word discipline](tutword_h_expert_create_discipline_lib.htm) [Importing a library for interfaces](tutword_h_expert_import_interface_lib.htm) [Enclosing discipline components in mechatronic components](tutword_h_expert_encapsulate_discipline_comps.htm) [Installing extension points into mechatronic components](tutword_h_expert_extent_mech_by_extensionpoints.htm) [Parameterizing encapsulating mechatronic components](tutword_h_expert_correct_formulas.htm) [Creating actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system](tutword_h_expert_create_action_for_import_of_discipline.htm) [Testing upgrade](tutword_h_expert_test.htm) [Comparison with sample solution](tutword_h_task2_compare.htm)

parent_tutword_h_expert_correct_formulas.title: Parameterizing encapsulating mechatronic components
parent_tutword_h_expert_correct_formulas.content:
# Parameterizing encapsulating mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Parameterizing encapsulating mechatronic components All formulas have been lost due to the encapsulation of the discipline components and the usage of interface parameters. In addition all values can now be determined in the mechatronic structure. [Parameterizing M_Body](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_Body.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the formula =mc.name for the Heading parameter.
    4. Enter the formula =type('T_Images.Logo').image for the Logo parameter.
    5. Enter Chapter as the value for the Socket parameter.
    6. Save the component.
[Parameterizing M_Chapter](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_Chapter.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the following formula for the Heading parameter:
    
        =switch mc.mos('T_Office_Word.Wrapper.M_Chapter').indexOf(this)

    {

    	case 0 : 'Actuators'

    	case 1 : 'Sensors'

    	else : 'Not defined'

    }

    1. Enter Chapter as the value for the Plug parameter.
    2. Enter the formula =$Heading for the Socket parameter.
    3. Save the component.
[Parameterizing M_Row](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component M_Row.
    2. Switch to the Parameter editor page.
    3. Enter the formula =mroot.name + mc.absoluteName.substring(mroot.absoluteName.size, mc.absoluteName.size - 1) for the Descriptor parameter.
    4. Enter the formula =type('T_Images.Actuators_Sensors_Imageregister').image(mc.type) for the Icon parameter.
    5. Enter the following formula for the Plug parameter:
    
        =switch mc.isInstanceOf('T_Mechatronic_Architecture.Levelcomponents.Actuator')

    {

    	case true : 'Actuators'

    	case false : 'Sensors'

    	else: 'Not defined'

    }

    1. Enter the following formula for the Pos. parameter:
    
        =switch mc.type.superType.name

    {

    	case 'Actuator': mroot.rmos('Actuator').indexOf(this.mc) + 1

    	case 'Sensor': mroot.rmos('Sensor').indexOf(this.mc) + 1

    	case 'Positionsensor': mroot.rmos('Positionsensor').indexOf(this.mc) + 1

    	else: 'Not defined'

    }

    1. Save the component.
Explanation of the formula for Plug: First of all, it is a Switch-Case request. The expression for Switch is compared with expressions for Case. In the case of the Switch expression it is determined whether the parent component is an instance of the type Actuator. The Switch expression can take on the values "true", "false" or "null". If the determined value corresponds with the first Case expression "true", the value "Actuators" is used. If the determined value corresponds with the second Case expression "false", the value "Sensors" is used. If none of the two expressions applies, "Not defined" is used. Explanation of the formula for Pos: First of all, it is a Switch-Case request. The expression for Switch is compared with expressions for Case. In the case of the Switch expression it is determined whether which super type the parent component corresponds to. The Switch expression can take on the values "Actuator", "Sensor" or "Position sensor". If the determined value corresponds to the first Case expression "Actuator", a list of all instances of "Actuator" is created and in it the position of the parent component is determined. Because Collections always begin with Index 0, you still have to add "1". If the determined value corresponds to the second Case expression "Sensor", a list of all instances of "Sensor" is created and in it the position of the parent component is determined. If the determined value corresponds to the third Case expression "Position sensor", a list of all instances of "Position sensor" is created and in it the position of the parent component is determined. If none of the three expressions applies, "Not defined" is used.

parent_tutword_h_expert_create_action_fo.title: Creating actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system
parent_tutword_h_expert_create_action_fo.content:
# Creating actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating actions for the import of the discipline library into the modular system The upgrade to extension points has the advantage that during the configuration of a machine or plant only mechatronic components are involved and the data model therefore remains smaller. Only when the documents of a discipline have to be created for the completely configured machine or plant, are the discipline components installed per import of the discipline library and update of the extension points. Both the import as well as the update are to be triggered through actions by means of buttons of a user interface. The actions are based on a command and a SequenceSelectionAction that are created in the following steps. [Creating a command for the import of a library](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select New > Engineering > Unit in the popup menu.
    3. Specify the Actions name.
    4. Select the new Actions unit.
    5. Select New > Object... from the popup menu.
    6. Click Object > Command.
    7. Confirm with [Finish].
    8. Specify the ImportLibCommand name.
    9. Switch to the Methods editor page.
    10. Click .
    11. The New Name has to be entered in the table and the Name field.
    12. Specify execute as the name.
    13. In the drop-down list select LanguageSequence.
    14. In the Arguments area click on .
    15. The New Argument with the Engineering.Object type is entered in the table of the arguments.
    16. Enter root as the name.
    17. Enter Engineering.Root as the type.
    18. Repeat Steps 13 to 16 with the name libNames and the type Engineering.List.
    19. The new arguments are entered as values for the signature of the method. It is now an execute method that requires the root node of the mechatronic and the list of the libraries to be imported as arguments.
Below a table is shown with the specification of type names, method names and method arguments.
    1. Click  above the table.
    2. In the Select method dialog, click Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > ImportLibrariesCommand.
    3. Confirm with [Finish].
    4. Repeat Steps 18 to 20 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > SaveObjectCommand.
    5. Save the command.
    6. This concludes the creation of the command for the import of a library. With this command the framework commands for the import of libraries and the subsequent saving are performed.
[Creating SequenceSelectionAction for the update of extension points](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the Actions unit.
    2. Select New > Object in the popup menu.
    3. Click Object > Action > SelectionAction > SequenceSelectionAction.
    4. Confirm with [Finish].
    5. Specify the UpdateExtensionPointsAction name.
    6. Specify Update Extension Points in the Displayed Name field.
    7. Switch to the Methods editor page.
    8. Click .
    9. Click Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > StartFormulaCacheCommand.
    10. Confirm with [Finish].
    11. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > UpdateExtensionsCommand.
    12. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > StopFormulaCacheCommand.
    13. Repeat Steps 8 to 10 with Object > Command > FrameworkCommand > SaveObjectCommand.
    14. Switch to the Attributes editor page.
    15. Specify Component in the Selected object field.
    16. Save the SequenceSelectionAction.
    17. This completes the creation of the SequenceSelectionAction for updating the extension points. With this SequenceSelectionAction the Framework commands for starting the formula cache, for updating extension points, for stopping the formula cache and the subsequent saving are performed consecutively.
The created actions are then linked with interfaces in a small user interface. The user interface (Form-UI) is created for the component Feeder and therefore is available directly after the creation of a project. [Creating Form-UI for Feeder](javascript:void\(0\);) With the user interface you can not only set up the interfaces for the import of the library but you can also make the option for checking workpiece available for selection.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Change to the UI configuration editor page.
    3. Change to the Source tab.
    4. In the <form> tag specify the Feeder title and the ID feeder.
    5. Insert the following lines after the opening tag <form>:
    
        <line>

    	<label font="Arial-20-bold">Feeder configuration</label>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<gap hSizePx="10"/>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<input receiver="=parameter('Option_Inspect_available')" type="check" ></input>

    	<label font="Arial-12">Add workpiece inspection</label>

    </line>

    <line>

    	<gap hSizePx="10"/>

    </line>

    <action name="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Actions.ImportLibCommand" 

    	arguments="=List{this.mroot.parent, List{'T_Office_Word'}}" 

    	type="button" 

    	text="Import Word Discipline">

    </action>

    <action name="T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Actions.UpdateExtensionPointsAction" 

    	arguments="=mroot" 

    	type="button" 

    	text="Update extensions">

    </action>

Through the first three rows the user interface receives the Feeder configuration header. The header is followed by three rows to create space. Below that is a row with a check box, followed by a text. The check box influences the Option_Inspect_available parameter that amongst others is used for the Disabler of the function groups Inspect and Discard. Three further lines follow to create space to the buttons. This is followed by two buttons that can each perform actions. The first button triggers the action to import the library T_Office_Word. With the second button the action for updating the extension points is performed. [Setting the default value for the Option_Inspect_available parameter](javascript:void\(0\);) The check box for the Option_Inspect_available parameter in the user interface should always have a defined state at the beginning of the configuration. In this case the check box should not be selected, so that the value is false.
    1. Open the Option_Inspect_available parameter.
    2. Switch to the Formula editor page.
    3. Enter false in the Value field.
    4. Save the parameter.
    5. This value is now specified as the default value. As a result the Add workpiece inspection option will not be selected when opening the user interface for the first time.
Read more
     * [Form-UI](refformui_r_formui.htm)
     * [action](refformui_r_action.htm)

parent_tutword_h_expert_create_disciplin.title: Creating a library for the Word discipline
parent_tutword_h_expert_create_disciplin.content:
# Creating a library for the Word discipline

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a library for the Word discipline Previously, the discipline components were inserted in the mechatronic modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem below the Word_Components unit. The discipline components is now to be collected in their own library. Create a library with the name T_Office_Word, which imports the system library Word and the libraries already created Word_Ui, T_Mechatronic_Architecture, T_Images and T_Interfaces. See [Creating libraries](eecbase_k_libraries.htm). [Removing discipline components](javascript:void\(0\);) As long as instances of the discipline components are still installed in the mechatronic components, they cannot be moved into the new libraries T_Office_Word. Therefore all instances have to be removed first.
    1. Open the component Cylinder.
    2. Select the discipline component Row.
    3. Delete the discipline components with .
    4. Repeat Steps 1 to 3 for Valve, Posistionsensor_inductive, Positionsensor_optical and Pressuresensor.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for Feeder with the instances ListOfComponents, Actuators_Table and Sensors_Table.
Repeat Steps 1 to 3 for Valve, Posistionsensor_inductive, Positionsensor_optical and Pressuresensor. [Moving discipline components and parameters in the T_Office_Word library](javascript:void\(0\);) Making the T_Office_Word library visible for T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Click the symbol [Add library].
    4. In the Library finder dialog click [Search].
    5. Select the T_Office_Word library.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. Save the library.
    8. The visibility is established.
Moving discipline components and parameters
    1. In the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library select the units Word, and Word_Components.
    2. Drag the units into the T_Office_Word library.
Undoing visibilities
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Select the T_Office_Word library.
    4. Press the key [Del].
    5. Select the Word_Ui library.
    6. Press the key [Del].
    7. Save the library.

parent_tutword_h_expert_create_image_lib.title: Creating a library for the image objects
parent_tutword_h_expert_create_image_lib.content:
# Creating a library for the image objects

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a library for the image objects Previously, the image objects were directly inserted in the mechatronic modular system T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem below the Images unit. These components are now to be collected in their own library. Create a library with the name T_Images, which imports the system library MediaSource and the library T_Mechatronic_Architecture. See [Creating libraries](eecbase_k_libraries.htm). [Moving image objects in the T_Images library](javascript:void\(0\);) Making the T_Images library visible for T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Click the symbol [Add library].
    4. In the Library finder dialog click [Search].
    5. Select the T_Images library.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. Save the library.
    8. The visibility is established.
Moving discipline components
    1. Select all the components in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library in the Images unit.
    2. Drag the components into the T_Images library.
    3. Delete the Images unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
Undoing visibilities The T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library now does not require the visibility to the T_Images library anymore. The visibility is switched on and switched off later during the configuration. The visibility to the MediaSource library can not be undone yet since there are references to this.
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Open the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Select the T_Images library.
    4. Press the key [Del].
    5. Save the library.

parent_tutword_h_expert_create_interface.title: Creating a library for interfaces
parent_tutword_h_expert_create_interface.content:
# Creating a library for interfaces

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating a library for interfaces The interfaces can be created directly in the mechatronic modular system. However, it is more practical to create them in a separate library and import these into all the libraries that require these interfaces. Note If another discipline of the tutorial has already been edited, the T_Interfaces already exists. In this case the existing interfaces only have to be added. The names of interfaces can be selected freely. But it has become common to let the names begin with a capital âIâ. Create a library for the interfaces with the name T_Interfaces that imports the system library Engineering. See [Creating libraries](eecbase_k_libraries.htm). How to create the interfaces:
    1. Call up the popup menu of the T_Interfaces library and select New > Engineering > Interface.
    2. Enter IBody in the Name field.
    3. Save the interface.
    4. Repeat Steps 1 to 3 with the following names:
       * IChapter
       * IRow
All interfaces that are required for the extension points are now defined for the Word discipline.

parent_tutword_h_expert_delete_project.title: Deleting a project
parent_tutword_h_expert_delete_project.content:
# Deleting a project

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Deleting a project When modifying the mechatronic modular system, dependencies to the instances in the project can become invalid and result in severe model errors. Before changes are performed to a modular system, the project should be removed. [Removing the Feeder project](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Select the Feeder project in the project catalog.
    2. Click [Del].
    3. Click [Finish].

parent_tutword_h_expert_encapsulate_disc.title: Enclosing discipline components in mechatronic components
parent_tutword_h_expert_encapsulate_disc.content:
# Enclosing discipline components in mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Enclosing discipline components in mechatronic components In the course of the tutorial discipline components were created once through a mass import. Then formulas (for example to calculate a plug) were entered for the parameter values. If a macro was changed, for example another parameter added and the discipline component synchronized with the changed macro, the parameter values were lost. For this reason each discipline component is enclosed (encapsulated) in a mechatronic component. In the mechatronic component the same parameters are created and the formulas inserted. The parameters of the discipline components then always reference the parameters (interface parameters) of the same name of the encapsulating mechatronic components. After a synchronization you then only have to insert the references on the interface parameter again. [Creating a unit for mechatronic components](javascript:void\(0\);) The encapsulating mechatronic components are to be created in a unit parallel to Word_Components.
    1. Select the T_Office_Word library.
    2. Select New > Unit in the shortcut menu.
    3. Specify the Wrapper name.
    4. Save the unit.
[Creating mechatronic components](javascript:void\(0\);) An encapsulating mechatronic component has to be created for each mechatronic component. Creating encapsulation for the discipline components
    1. Select the new Wrapper unit.
    2. In the popup menu select New > Mechatronics > Function.
    3. Specify the M_Body name.
    4. Switch to the Interfaces editor page.
    5. Click  to open the Finder dialog.
    6. Click [Search].
    7. Select the IBody.
    8. Confirm with [OK].
    9. Drag the Body discipline component into the component editor.
    10. Select the installed discipline component Body.
    11. Mark all parameters.
    12. Select Add interface temperature from the popup menu.
    13. Save the component.
    14. Repeat steps 1 to 13 with the names M_Chapter with the interface IChapter and the discipline component Chapter.
    15. Repeat steps 1 to 13 with the names M_Row with the interface IRow and the discipline component Row.
A reference to the parameter of the superior component (capsule) by the same name is now entered as the value. These parameters were inserted into the capsule during the same step. The parameter values are entered at a later step. [Adding plugs and sockets to discipline components](javascript:void\(0\);) To generate the Word discipline structure independent of the structure of the mechatronics, the discipline components have to be extended with plugs and sockets. This takes place in two steps:
    1. Extend the discipline components with the parameters Plug and Socket.
    2. Extend the encapsulating mechatronic components.
Extending Plugs and Sockets to discipline components:
    1. Open the component Body.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Drag the Socket parameter from the Word.Parameter unit in the editor.
    4. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for the Chapter component with the Plug and Socket parameters.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 3 for the Row with the parameter Plug.
Extending encapsulating mechatronic components:
    1. Open the component M_Body.
    2. Select the installed Body discipline component.
    3. In the editor open the popup menu of the Socket parameter.
    4. Select Adding interface temperature.
    5. Save the editor.
    6. Repeat steps 1 to 5 for the M_Chapter and M_Row components with the newly added Plug and Socket parameters.

parent_tutword_h_expert_extend_architect.title: Expanding the architecture
parent_tutword_h_expert_extend_architect.content:
# Expanding the architecture

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Expanding the architecture In the architecture the Actuator and Sensor components represent the lowest level. Every other type therefore requires an extension of the architecture. To be able to expand the architecture flexibly, you now create a Function component that may be installed in itself and all other components. [Creating the Function component](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Mark the Levelcomponents unit in the T_Mechatronic_Architecture library.
    2. In the popup menu select New > Object.
    3. Navigate to Object > Component > MechatronicComponent in the Create new library object dialog.
    4. Click [Finish].
    5. Enter Function in the Name field.
    6. Select the Level6.gif file in the Image field.
    7. Select the Abstract check box.
    8. Save the component.
[Expanding level configurations](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the new Function component.
    2. Switch to the Level configuration editor.
    3. Click  to open the Finder dialog.
    4. Click [Search].
    5. Highlight the Function component.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. Save the component.
    8. Repeat Steps 1 to 7 for all other level components.
    9. This way a component of the Function type can be installed in all components and also in itself.

parent_tutword_h_expert_extent_mech_by_e.title: Installing extension points into mechatronic components
parent_tutword_h_expert_extent_mech_by_e.content:
# Installing extension points into mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Installing extension points into mechatronic components You now have to install extensions points in all mechatronic components in which Word components have already been installed fixed. This affects the following mechatronic components:
     * Feeder,
     * Cylinder,
     * Valve,
     * Positionsensor_Inductive,
     * Pressuresensor,
     * Positionsensor_optical.
[Installing extension point for the main document](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Click  in the component editor to open the Finder dialog.
    3. Mark the Placeholder.
    4. Confirm with [Finish].
    5. Change the name from Placeholder to Bodies.
    6. Click [...] in the Type field to open the type finder.
    7. Enter the character I in the Name field.
    8. Click [Search].
    9. Select the IBody interface.
    10. Confirm with [OK].
    11. Enter the value 0..1 in the Number field.
    12. Mark the Extension point check box.
    13. In the Selection formula field, enter the value =1.
    14. Save the component.
In the future a component that implements the IBody interface is installed on the extension point. The quantity is limited to the value 1 and the selection formula only allows a single component. Later an instance of M_Body is placed on this extension point through the update of the extension point. [Installing extension point for two chapters](javascript:void\(0\);) Two chapters are to be inserted into the Word document, one for the actuators and one for the sensors. Previously the discipline component Chapter was installed twice in the Feeder. A placeholder for chapters is now installed at the same place.
    1. Open the Feeder component.
    2. Click  in the component editor to open the Finder dialog.
    3. Mark the Placeholder.
    4. Confirm with [Finish].
    5. Change the name from Placeholder to Chapters.
    6. Click [...] in the Type field to open the type finder.
    7. Enter the character I in the Name field.
    8. Click [Search].
    9. Select the IChapter interface.
    10. Confirm with [OK].
    11. Enter the value 0..2 in the Number field.
    12. Mark the Extension point check box.
    13. In the Selection formula field, enter the value =2.
    14. Save the component.
[Installing an extension point for a row in all sensors and actuators](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. Open the component Cylinder.
    2. Click  in the component editor to open the Finder dialog.
    3. Mark the Placeholder.
    4. Move the placeholder to the uppermost position with .
    5. Confirm with [Finish].
    6. Change the name from Placeholder to Rows.
    7. Click [...] in the Type field to open the type finder.
    8. Enter the character I in the Name field.
    9. Click [Search].
    10. Select the IRow interface.
    11. Confirm with [OK].
    12. Enter the value 0..1 in the Number field.
    13. Mark the Extension point check box.
    14. In the Selection formula field, enter the value =1.
    15. Save the component.
    16. Repeat Steps 1 to 15 for all other sensors and actuators.

parent_tutword_h_expert_import_interface.title: Importing a library for interfaces
parent_tutword_h_expert_import_interface.content:
# Importing a library for interfaces

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Importing a library for interfaces The previously created interfaces are required both in the library T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem as well as in T_Office_Word. Extension points for components that implement a specific interface are installed in the mechatronic modular system. In the T_Office_Word library, the discipline components are still encapsulated by mechatronic components that implement those interfaces and can therefore be placed on the corresponding extension points.
    1. Open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Switch to the editor page Imported libraries.
    3. Click  to open the Finder dialog.
    4. Click [Search] in the Finder dialog.
    5. Select the T_Interfaces library.
    6. Confirm with [OK].
    7. Save the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
The interfaces of T_Interfaces can now be applied in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem and T_Office_Word.

parent_tutword_h_expert_test.title: Testing upgrade
parent_tutword_h_expert_test.content:
# Testing upgrade

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Testing upgrade The test of the upgrade is intended to show that the configuration can be first performed without the participation of discipline components. After the configuration a discipline can be added by importing a library. [Creating a project](javascript:void\(0\);)
    1. In the Project Catalog click .
    2. Specify Feeder1 as the name.
    3. Select the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library. The T_Office_Word and T_Images libraries are not automatically marked and should also not be.
    4. Click [Next >].
    5. The Feeder level component is automatically marked.
    6. Confirm with [Finish].
The Feeder1 project now exists in the Project catalog.
    1. Select the Feeder1 project.
    2. In the popup menu select Expand all.
Depending on the filter settings all project components and the extension points are now shown in the tree view. For this tutorial we recommend the following objects per filter setting:
     * Non-active objects
     * Categories
     * AutomationWorX
     * CoDeSys
     * ECAD
     * STEP 7
[Configuring a project without the option "Add workpiece inspection"](javascript:void\(0\);) Now open the Form-UI to configure the project.
    1. Select the Feeder project component.
    2. Select Form-UIs > Feeder in the popup menu.
    3. By default the value of the parameter Option_Inspect_available is false and the Add workpiece inspection check box is not activated.
[Importing a discipline library](javascript:void\(0\);) Which libraries are involved in the Feeder1 project shall be checked before and after the import. Before:
    1. Open the Feeder1 project library.
    2. Change to the Imported libraries editor.
Imported libraries: Import a discipline library per Form-UI:
    1. Select the Feeder component.
    2. Select Form-UIs > Feeder in the popup menu.
    3. Click the button [Import Word Discipline].
    4. Save the Form-UI.
After:
    1. Open the Feeder1 library.
    2. Change to the Imported libraries editor.
Imported libraries: After the import the discipline components of the T_ECAD_P8 library are available. [Updating extension points](javascript:void\(0\);) In the tree view of the project catalog you can see that no discipline components are inserted on the extension points.
    1. Select the Feeder component.
    2. Select Form-UIs > Feeder in the popup menu.
    3. Click the [Update Extensions] button.
    4. Save the Form-UI.
In the tree view of the project catalog you now can see that discipline components are inserted on all extension points. Note Discipline components are also inserted on the hidden extension points. However, these are not considered during the generation of the discipline documentation. [Generating discipline documents](javascript:void\(0\);) A Word document is generated from the existing discipline components with the following steps.
    1. Select the component Mechatronic.
    2. In the popup menu select Word > Generate Word Document.
Subsequently the structure of the Word components is created in the project catalog. It consists of the discipline components Body, two chapters with eight rows each.
    1. Select the component Mechatronic.
    2. In the popup menu select Word > Generate and open Word document.
Microsoft Word is opened and the generated document is displayed. The document contains a cover sheet, a table of contents and a page with tables of the actuators and sensors. Only the table of contents does not correspond to the current content. The table of contents for the field must be updated to this purpose.
    1. Close the Word document and exit Word.
    2. Select the component Mechatronic.
    3. In the popup menu select Word > Update Word document fields.
Microsoft Word is opened and the generated document is displayed. The table of contents now corresponds to the current content. [Configuring a project with the option "Add workpiece inspection"](javascript:void\(0\);) Open the Form-UI change again, to change the configuration.
    1. Select the Feeder component.
    2. Select Form-UIs > Feeder in the popup menu.
    3. Select the Add Workpiece Inspection check box.
    4. Save the Form-UI.
The Discard and Inspect components including extension points and discipline components placed on them are now included in the tree view of the Project catalog. Note The previous update of the extension points had an affect on the extension points of non-active components! Because of the filtering these have not been visible since then. [Re-generating a discipline component](javascript:void\(0\);) A Word document is re-generated with the added discipline components with the following steps.
    1. Select the component Mechatronic.
    2. In the popup menu select Word > Generate Word Document.
Subsequently the structure of the Word components is recreated in the project catalog. It consists of the discipline components body, two chapters, eight rows below chapter and 11 rows below the second chapter.
    1. Select the component Mechatronic.
    2. In the popup menu select Word > Generate and open Word document.
Microsoft Word is opened and the generated document is displayed. The document contains a cover sheet, a table of contents and a page with tables of the actuators and sensors. Only the table of contents does not correspond to the current content. The table of contents for the field must be updated to this purpose.
    1. Close the Word document and exit Word.
    2. Select the component Mechatronic.
    3. In the popup menu select Word > Update Word document fields.
Microsoft Word is opened and the generated document is displayed. The table of contents now corresponds to the current content. [Summary](javascript:void\(0\);) The advantage that extension points entail have no effect for smaller models in connection with an individual discipline. The bigger the model becomes and the more disciplines are added, the bigger the advantage of extension points becomes. The mechatronic modular system remains slim and clear. Discipline libraries can be changed and extended independently of each other.

parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.title: Adding ListofComponents to the mechatronic model
parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.content:
# Adding ListofComponents to the mechatronic model

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding ListofComponents to the mechatronic model In the next step, you need to link the existing modular system for the ListOfComponents to the functional components of the mechatronic model. This allows the ListOfComponents to be automatically created from the mechatronic configuration.

parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.title: Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components
parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.content:
# Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding body and chapter to mechatronic components In the first step, you need to link the Word components for the body and chapter of the ListOfComponents with the mechatronic components:
    1. Double-click to open the component Feeder in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library in the Stations unit.
    2. Using Drag & Drop, drag the Body component once, and the Chapter component twice into the component panel of the editor.
    3. Rename Body to ListOfComponents.
    4. Rename Chapter and Chapter2 into ActuatorsTable and SensorsTable.
Tip Switch to editor mode by clicking the selected component again or by pressing [F2].

parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.title: Adding a row to mechatronic components
parent_tutword_h_extent_mechatronic_by_l.content:
# Adding a row to mechatronic components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding a row to mechatronic components Add the discipline component Row to all non-abstract components Actuator and Sensor:
     * Valve
     * Cylinder
     * Positionsensor_inductive
     * Positionsensor_optical
     * Pressuresensor
The task is to use formulas to calculate the parameter values of the Row component:
    1. To do so, double-click to open Row and switch to the Parameter editor page.
    1. Enter the formula =mroot.name + mc.absoluteName.substring(mroot.absoluteName.size, mc.absoluteName.size - 1) for the Descriptor parameter.
    1. Enter the formula =dc.dos.indexOf(this) + 1 for the Pos parameter.
Explanation of the formulas: For Descriptor, the absolute name (e.g., Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Stack.Positionsensor_optical) is to be used to determine the component (e.g., Feeder.Insert.Stack.Positionsensor_optical). To do so, the result of mroot.name (e.g., Feeder) is added to the result of the mc.absoluteName.substring  method. The length of the absolute name (mroot.absoluteName.size = e.g. Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder = 41) and the length of the name starting at the parent component (mc.absoluteName.size-1 = e.g. Feeder.Mechatronic.Feeder.Insert.Stack.Positionsensor_optical = 84) is transferred to the substring method. The position of the discipline component for Pos is determined from the list of discipline components. dc.dos is used to identify a list of all children of the parent discipline components and indexOf(this) to determine its own position. Since the counter in lists begins at 0 (zero), a 1 is added. Another point is added with \+ '.'.

parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Generating non-functional structures
parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Generating non-functional structures

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating non-functional structures Hierarchical discipline structures, which are different not in that elements are omitted from the mechatronic structure, are defined using plug parameters and socket parameters. If a Word component does not have a plug parameter, it is subordinated in the mechatronic structure of the next discipline component the parent level that permits this through the internal definition of sublevels. If a Word component does have a plug parameter, it is placed below the discipline components that have a socket parameter with the same value as its plug parameter.

parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Creating parameters for plugs and sockets
parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Creating parameters for plugs and sockets

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Creating parameters for plugs and sockets Before the plugs and sockets can be assigned to the Word components, these must first be created as parameters:
    1. Mark the Mechatronic.Word.Parameter unit in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. In the popup menu select New > Engineering > Parameter.
    3. Enter Plug as the name.
    4. Enter Plug as the type. The type can either be found by using the Finder  or be entered directly.
    5. Repeat steps 1 to 4 for the parameter with Socket as the name and type.

parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.title: Adding plugs and sockets to Word components
parent_tutword_h_generate_nonfunctional_.content:
# Adding plugs and sockets to Word components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Adding plugs and sockets to Word components The discipline component Row is installed (pre-instantiated) in the mechatronic components for sensors and actuators. So that the fragment documents are inserted correctly in the sections ActuatorsTable and SensorsTable when generating the ListOfComponents, these sections have to be extended with a parameter of the type Socket with the values Actuators and Sensors. The discipline components Row are to be expanded with a parameter of the type Plug. Depending on whether these discipline components are installed in an actuator or a sensor, the values Actuators or Sensors are to be entered for the parameters. Extend the discipline components ActuatorsTable and SensorsTable with the parameter Socket:
    1. Open the component Chapter.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Drag & Drop the Socket parameter into the parameter editor.
    4. Save the component.
    5. Open the Feeder component.
    6. Select the installed ActuatorsTable discipline component.
    7. Enter Actuators as the value for the Socket parameter.
    8. Repeat steps 5 to 7 for the SensorsTable and for the value Sensors.
Expand the discipline components Cylinder and Valve with the parameter Plug:
    1. Open in the component Row.
    2. Open the Parameters editor page.
    3. Drag & Drop the Plug parameter into the parameter editor.
    4. Save the component.
    5. Open in the component Cylinder.
    6. Select the installed Row discipline component.
    7. Enter Actuators as the value for the Plug parameter.
    8. Save the component.
    9. Repeat the steps 5 to 8 for the Valve component.
Extend the discipline components Positionsensor_inductive, Positionsensor_optical and Pressuresensor with the parameter Plug:
    1. Open in the Positionsensor_inductive component.
    2. Select the installed Row discipline component.
    3. Enter Actuators as the value for the Plug parameter.
    4. Save the component.
    5. Repeat Steps 1 to 4 for the Positionsensor_optical and Pressuresensor components.

parent_tutword_h_parameterize_discipline.title: Configuring Body and Chapter
parent_tutword_h_parameterize_discipline.content:
# Configuring Body and Chapter

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Configuring Body and Chapter The parameters of the installed discipline components Body and Chapter still have to be provided with formulas.
    1. Select the installed components ListOfComponents.
    2. Enter the =type('T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Images.Logo').image formula as the value for the Logo parameter.
This formula identifies the image that is saved in the image object T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem.Images.Logo.
    1. Enter the =this.name formula as the value for the Heading parameter:
This formula is used to determine the name of the current components (Feeder).
    1. Select the installed ActuatorsTable component.
    2. Enter Actuators as the value for the Heading parameter.
    3. Select the installed Sensors_Table component.
    4. Enter Sensors as the value for the Heading parameter.

parent_tutword_h_preparation_steps_eec.title: Preparatory steps in EEC
parent_tutword_h_preparation_steps_eec.content:
# Preparatory steps in EEC

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps in EEC The following steps are required to prepare the tutorial: [Import EOX files for Word tutorial](javascript:void\(0\);) Before the Word discipline can be edited, the T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem libraries created in the chapter [Mechatronic](tutmechatronic_h_mechatronic.htm) have to be available. If the T_Mechatronic_Architecture and T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem libraries are not available, please import the following EOX file:
    
        <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Mechatronic\Mechatronic.eox.

Predefined SequenceSelectionActions used to generate and open the discipline structure, the code, and the PDF file can be found in this EOX file:
    
        <EEC-Installationsordner>\install\Tutorial\Word\Word_Ui.eox.

Read more
     * [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm)
[Customizing a model variable](javascript:void\(0\);) Various model variables can be customized for Word. Only the Name for Unit for Word Parameter model variable has to be changed in order to create a connection to the existing parameters when importing the Word resources and to ensure that parameters of the same name are not created in a new unit.
    1. Select Model > Model variables.
    2. Navigate to Disciplines > Office > Word.
    3. Select the value true for the model variable Create Parameters On Demand.
    4. Select the value true for the model variable Create Parameter Units On Demand.
[Importing libraries](javascript:void\(0\);) To use the Word function in the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library, it is necessary to import the Word system library. Likewise, the Word_Ui and Word_Customizing libraries must be imported to use predefined SequenceSelectionActions. In addition, the MediaSource library has to be imported. This will allow you to insert image objects into the Word file. Import the EOX file <EEC installation folder>\install\Tutorial\Word\Word_Ui.eox. Read more
     * [Importing libraries/projects as an EOX file](eecbase_k_eox_exchange_library_import.htm)
Subsequently the libraries Word_Customizing and Word_Ui are available in the library catalog.
    1. Double-click to open the T_Mechatronic_ModularSystem library.
    2. Select the Imported libraries editor.
    3. Click .
The library finder now appears.
    1. Click [Search].
    2. Select the Word_Ui and MediaSource libraries. The libraries Word und Word_Customizing are imported through dependencies on classes contained in it.
    3. Confirm with [OK].
    1. Save the changes.
In the following text, it is assumed that you save your modifications at the latest before opening the next object (diskette icon top left or by using the shortcut key [Ctrl] \+ [S]), and if not, when you see the prompt click [Yes].

parent_tutword_h_preparation_steps_file.title: Preparatory steps on a file level
parent_tutword_h_preparation_steps_file.content:
# Preparatory steps on a file level

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Preparatory steps on a file level Create the following folder on a file level (using Windows Explorer):
    
        <EEC installation folder>\resources\Office\Word\Tutorial

[Entering the path to the resources into the initialization file](javascript:void\(0\);) This section can be skipped when the initialization file has already been edited for another discipline of the tutorial. The initialization (ec.ini) file has to be edited in the following way:
    1. Open the initialization file in an editor (for example Notepad).
    2. Expand the specification for -Dde.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath= with <EEC installation folder>\resources (see [de.eplan.eec.global.resourcePath](admin_r_vmargs_global_resourcePath_new.htm)).
    3. Save the initialization file.
    4. Close the editor.

parent_tutword_h_task_generate_loc.title: Task: Generate ListOfComponents
parent_tutword_h_task_generate_loc.content:
# Task: Generate ListOfComponents

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Task: Generate ListOfComponents The task is to create a ListOfComponents with the following structure based on the functional, mechatronic of a plant. To achieve this target, this chapter will begin by creating a modular system from a reusable main document and a reusable fragment document on the basis of the Office discipline. It then goes on to show how to check the modular system components of the list of components by means of a test configuration. The relationship of this new discipline to the mechatronic model is then examined.

parent_tutword_h_test1.title: Test 1 - Instantiate Word components
parent_tutword_h_test1.content:
# Test 1 - Instantiate Word components

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Test 1 - Instantiate Word components As with the mechatronic components, the Word components can also be instantiated. This is done by dragging and dropping them from the library catalog view into the project tree, where they will appear under the Word discipline root node:

parent_tutword_h_test2.title: Generating the current status of the component list
parent_tutword_h_test2.content:
# Generating the current status of the component list

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating the current status of the component list Generate the current status of the component list.
    1. In the Project Explorer select the Mechatronic component.
    2. In the popup menu select Word > Generate and open Word document.
This operation creates an error. The reason for this is that the component Image.Logo to be accessed in the model has not yet been created. The error message can be acknowledged by pressing [OK].
    1. In the popup menu select Word > Update Word document fields.
The discipline structure for Word is generated in parallel to Mechatronic. The structure consists of the project components ListOfComponents as instance of the component Body and two project components ActuatorsTable and SensorsTable as instances of the component Chapter. The created ListOfComponents document is saved to the Workspace folder Word\Word and looks as follows:
    1. The parameter specifications #{Heading} are replaced by their values in the ListOfComponents document.
    2. The components ActuatorsTable and SensorsTable are inserted.
    3. The logo on the cover sheet is missing.
    4. The table of contents is not up-to-date, although the contents that are shown appear to be correct.

parent_tutword_h_test3.title: Generating the current status of the component list
parent_tutword_h_test3.content:
# Generating the current status of the component list

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating the current status of the component list Generate the current status of the component list. This operation again creates an error. The reason for this is that the component Image.Logo to be accessed in the model has not yet been created. The error message can be acknowledged by pressing [OK]. The created component list looks as follows: The logo on the cover sheet is, however, missing and the table of contents is not up-to-date, although the contents that are shown appear to be correct. If the table of contents was updated before Chapter.docx was saved, the formula (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Content}*)(*{END_LOOP}*) is shown as chapter 1.1 as the chapter heading. The table rows for actuators and sensors are now respectively contained in the correct chapter. The images of the actuators and sensors are, however, still missing in the Symbol column. Note By using formulas instead of predefined plug and socket values, it is possible to create any required discipline-specific structures.

parent_tutword_h_test4.title: Generating the current status of the component list
parent_tutword_h_test4.content:
# Generating the current status of the component list

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Generating the current status of the component list To create and view the ListOfComponents, open the Mechatronic popup menu in the Project Catalog and select the SequenceSelectionAction Word > Generate and open Word document. The created ListOfComponents looks as follows: The Eplan logo is on the cover sheet and the symbols for actuator and sensor are shown in the tables. The table of contents is not up-to-date, although the contents that are shown appear to be correct. If the table of contents was updated before Chapter.docx was saved, the formula (*{LOOP:dos}*)(*{Content}*)(*{END_LOOP}*) is shown as chapter 1.1 as the chapter heading. The SequenceSelectionAction Update Word document fields is used to update the table of contents. Open the Mechatronic popup menu in the Project Catalog and select the SequenceSelectionAction Word > Update Word document fields. Subsequently the table of contents of the ListOfComponents is updated:

parent_tutword_h_word.title: Word
parent_tutword_h_word.content:
# Word

This functionality is only available for certain module packages. [Info / Copyright](license.htm) You are here:  Word Microsoft Word has to be installed on the Workplace computer in order to work through this part of the tutorial (see also [Software and hardware requirements](admin_r_it_reqs_software.htm)). After the discipline model has been created, the result can be compared with the sample solution (see [Comparison with sample solution](tutorial_h_compare_with_example_solution.htm)).
